description: Funds programs to reduce the impact of substance abuse and mental illness through grants, data collection, and policy coordination. Administers the Substance Abuse Prevention and Treatment Block Grant and Community Mental Health Services Block Grant.
beneficiaries: Individuals with substance use disorders and mental illness, state behavioral health agencies

name: Health Resources and Services Administration (HRSA), Health Programs
description: Funds programs improving health care access for underserved populations, including community health centers, the National Health Service Corps, Ryan White HIV/AIDS Program, and maternal and child health programs. Supports approximately 1,400 community health centers.
beneficiaries: Medically underserved populations, uninsured and underinsured individuals

name: Refugee and Entrant Assistance, Administration for Children and Families
description: Funds resettlement assistance for refugees, asylees, Cuban/Haitian entrants, and unaccompanied alien children, including cash and medical assistance, social services, and the Unaccompanied Children Program. Authorized by the Refugee Act of 1980.
beneficiaries: Refugees, asylees, unaccompanied migrant children

name: Aging and Disability Services Programs, Administration for Community Living
description: Funds Older Americans Act programs including home-delivered and congregate meals (Meals on Wheels), caregiver support, elder abuse prevention, and independent living services for older adults and people with disabilities. Serves approximately 11 million older adults.
beneficiaries: Older adults age 60+, people with disabilities, family caregivers

name: Program Management, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
description: Funds CMS's administrative costs for overseeing Medicare, Medicaid, CHIP, and the Health Insurance Marketplaces, including claims processing, program integrity, and quality improvement. Manages programs covering over 150 million Americans.
beneficiaries: Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries, health care providers

name: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality (AHRQ)
description: Funds research to improve the quality, safety, efficiency, and effectiveness of health care for all Americans. Produces evidence-based clinical practice guidelines, patient safety research, and health services research.
beneficiaries: Health care providers, patients, health policy researchers

name: Biomedical Advanced Research and Development Authority (BARDA)/Administration for Strategic Preparedness and Response (ASPR)
description: Funds development and procurement of medical countermeasures against chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, and emerging infectious disease threats. Played a central role in Operation Warp Speed for COVID-19 vaccine development.
beneficiaries: National public health preparedness, general population during health emergencies

name: Military Construction, Defense-Wide
description: Funds construction projects for defense agencies and activities not assigned to a specific military department, including Special Operations Command, defense health, and intelligence facilities.
beneficiaries: Defense agencies, special operations forces, defense-wide installations

name: Military Construction, Space Force
description: Funds planning, design, and construction of Space Force facilities including satellite control centers, space launch infrastructure, and mission support buildings at space-focused installations.
beneficiaries: U.S. Space Force installations and personnel

name: Military Construction, Army National Guard
description: Funds construction of readiness centers (armories), training facilities, and maintenance buildings for Army National Guard units across 54 states and territories.
beneficiaries: Army National Guard units and personnel

name: Military Construction, Army Reserve
description: Funds construction of Army Reserve centers, training facilities, and maintenance buildings to support the readiness of Reserve component forces.
beneficiaries: U.S. Army Reserve units and personnel

name: Disaster Relief Fund, Federal Emergency Management Agency
description: The primary funding source for federal disaster response and recovery under the Stafford Act, covering individual assistance, public assistance, hazard mitigation, and mission assignments to other federal agencies. Activated upon presidential disaster declarations.
beneficiaries: Disaster-affected individuals, communities, state and local governments

name: National Flood Insurance Fund, FEMA
description: Administers the National Flood Insurance Program providing federally backed flood insurance to homeowners, renters, and businesses in participating communities. Covers approximately 5 million policies with significant accumulated debt to Treasury.
beneficiaries: Property owners in flood-prone areas, floodplain communities

name: Environmental Programs and Management, EPA
description: Funds EPA's core regulatory and compliance programs including air quality, water quality, pesticides, toxic substances, and environmental justice activities. The agency's primary operating account for environmental protection and standard-setting.
beneficiaries: General public, environmental and public health

name: Leaking Underground Storage Tank Trust Fund, EPA
description: Funds cleanup of petroleum releases from underground storage tanks at gas stations and other facilities, and supports state tank programs. Funded by a 0.1-cent-per-gallon tax on motor fuels.
beneficiaries: Communities affected by underground storage tank contamination

name: National Institute of Mental Health, NIH
description: Funds research on mental disorders including depression, schizophrenia, PTSD, and autism spectrum disorder to develop new interventions and improve understanding of brain function. The lead federal agency for research on mental illness.
beneficiaries: Individuals with mental illness, mental health research community

name: National Institute on Drug Abuse, NIH
description: Funds research on drug use and addiction, including neuroscience of addiction, development of prevention and treatment strategies, and epidemiology of substance use disorders. Conducts critical research on the opioid crisis.
beneficiaries: Individuals with substance use disorders, addiction research community

name: National Institute of Diabetes and Digestive and Kidney Diseases, NIH
description: Funds research on diabetes, endocrine and metabolic diseases, digestive diseases, nutrition, obesity, and kidney/urologic diseases. Supports major clinical trials and the national kidney and pancreas transplant registries.
beneficiaries: Patients with diabetes, kidney disease, and digestive disorders

name: National Institute on Aging, NIH
description: Funds research on the aging process and age-related diseases, particularly Alzheimer's disease and related dementias. Received significant funding increases through the National Alzheimer's Project Act.
beneficiaries: Older adults, Alzheimer's patients and caregivers, aging research community

name: National Institute of Child Health and Human Development, NIH
description: Funds research on fertility, pregnancy, childhood development, intellectual and developmental disabilities, and population dynamics. Conducts research on maternal health, SIDS, and learning disabilities.
beneficiaries: Mothers, children, individuals with developmental disabilities

name: National Institute of Neurological Disorders and Stroke, NIH
description: Funds research on the causes, prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of neurological disorders and stroke including Parkinson's disease, epilepsy, traumatic brain injury, and ALS. Supports the BRAIN Initiative.
beneficiaries: Patients with neurological disorders, neuroscience research community

name: National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences, NIH
description: Funds research on how environmental exposures affect human health, including toxicology, epidemiology of environmental diseases, and the National Toxicology Program. Also manages the Superfund Research Program.
beneficiaries: Communities exposed to environmental hazards, environmental health researchers

name: Construction, Bureau of Reclamation
description: Funds construction and rehabilitation of water resource projects in the 17 western states including dams, canals, and hydropower facilities. Supports major projects for irrigation, municipal water supply, and water recycling.
beneficiaries: Western water users, irrigators, municipalities

name: Procurement, Defense-Wide
description: Funds procurement of equipment and supplies for defense agencies not assigned to a specific military service, including Special Operations Command, missile defense, and defense-wide information systems.
beneficiaries: Defense agencies, special operations forces

name: RDT&E, Defense-Wide
description: Funds research, development, test, and evaluation for defense-wide programs including DARPA advanced research, missile defense development, chemical/biological defense, and information systems technology.
beneficiaries: Defense innovation ecosystem, military technology advancement

name: Defense Health Program
description: Funds the Military Health System providing medical and dental care to active-duty service members, retirees, and dependents through military treatment facilities and the TRICARE purchased care network. Serves approximately 9.6 million beneficiaries.
beneficiaries: Active-duty military, retirees, and dependents using the Military Health System

name: Operation and Maintenance, Air Force Reserve
description: Funds day-to-day operating costs of the Air Force Reserve including training, base operations, equipment maintenance, and civilian technician pay. Supports the combat readiness of approximately 70,000 Reserve Airmen.
beneficiaries: Air Force Reserve units and personnel

name: Operation and Maintenance, Army Reserve
description: Funds day-to-day operating costs of the Army Reserve including training, equipment maintenance, base operations, and civilian support staff. Supports readiness of approximately 189,000 Army Reserve soldiers.
beneficiaries: U.S. Army Reserve units and personnel

name: National Guard Personnel, Air Force
description: Funds pay, allowances, and benefits for Air National Guard members during drill, annual training, and active-duty periods. Covers approximately 107,000 Air Guard members in 54 states and territories.
beneficiaries: Air National Guard members and their dependents

name: Operation and Maintenance, Air National Guard
description: Funds day-to-day operating costs of the Air National Guard including flying operations, equipment maintenance, base operations, and civilian technician pay. Supports fighter, airlift, tanker, and ISR missions.
beneficiaries: Air National Guard units and flying operations

name: Reserve Personnel, Army
description: Funds pay, allowances, and benefits for Army Reserve members during drill weekends, annual training, and periods of active duty or mobilization.
beneficiaries: U.S. Army Reserve members and their dependents

name: Reserve Personnel, Navy
description: Funds pay, allowances, and benefits for Navy Reserve members during drill, annual training, and active-duty periods. Covers approximately 59,000 Selected Reserve sailors.
beneficiaries: Navy Reserve members and their dependents

name: Reserve Personnel, Marine Corps
description: Funds pay, allowances, and benefits for Marine Corps Reserve members during drill, annual training, and active-duty periods. Covers approximately 36,000 Selected Marine Corps Reservists.
beneficiaries: Marine Corps Reserve members and their dependents

name: Reserve Personnel, Air Force
description: Funds pay, allowances, and benefits for Air Force Reserve members during drill weekends, annual training, and mobilization periods. Supports approximately 70,000 Air Force Reserve members.
beneficiaries: Air Force Reserve members and their dependents

name: Weapons Procurement, Navy
description: Funds procurement of Navy weapons systems including missiles (Tomahawk, SM-6, LRASM), torpedoes, guns, ammunition, and associated equipment for surface ships, submarines, and aircraft.
beneficiaries: U.S. Navy combat forces, defense industrial base

name: Missile Procurement, Air Force
description: Funds procurement of Air Force missile systems including air-to-air missiles (AIM-9X, AIM-120), air-to-ground munitions (JASSM), and space launch vehicles.
beneficiaries: U.S. Air Force combat and space launch operations

name: Procurement of Ammunition, Army
description: Funds procurement of all classes of Army ammunition including small arms, medium caliber, tank ammunition, mortar rounds, artillery shells, and demolitions. Maintains ammunition production base capacity.
beneficiaries: U.S. Army combat units, ammunition industrial base

name: Procurement of Ammunition, Navy and Marine Corps
description: Funds procurement of ammunition and ordnance for the Navy and Marine Corps including naval gun ammunition, bombs, rockets, and small arms ammunition.
beneficiaries: U.S. Navy and Marine Corps combat forces

name: Procurement of Ammunition, Air Force
description: Funds procurement of Air Force munitions including bombs (JDAM), missiles, cartridges, and other expendable ordnance items for combat and training.
beneficiaries: U.S. Air Force combat and training operations

name: Defense Working Capital Funds
description: Revolving funds that finance business-like activities across DoD including depot maintenance, supply management, transportation, and information services on a customer-reimbursement basis. Operates with approximately $90 billion in annual revenue.
beneficiaries: All DoD components purchasing logistics and support services

name: Drug Interdiction and Counter-Drug Activities, Defense
description: Funds DoD's support to federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies for counter-drug operations, including detection and monitoring, intelligence sharing, and training. Provides military assets and expertise for drug interdiction.
beneficiaries: Federal and state drug enforcement agencies, border security

name: Office of the Inspector General, Department of Defense
description: Funds independent oversight of DoD programs and operations through audits, investigations, and evaluations to promote efficiency and prevent fraud, waste, and abuse. Largest inspector general office in the federal government.
beneficiaries: Taxpayers, DoD accountability and efficiency

name: Chemical Agents and Munitions Destruction, Defense
description: Funds the safe destruction of the U.S. chemical weapons stockpile in compliance with the Chemical Weapons Convention. Program nearing completion with destruction of remaining mustard and nerve agent stocks.
beneficiaries: Communities near chemical weapons storage sites, international treaty compliance

name: National Science Foundation, Education and Human Resources
description: Funds STEM education programs from K-12 through postdoctoral levels, including research experiences for undergraduates, graduate research fellowships, and programs to broaden participation in science and engineering.
beneficiaries: STEM students and educators at all levels, underrepresented groups in STEM

name: National Science Foundation, Major Research Equipment and Facilities Construction
description: Funds the acquisition, construction, and commissioning of major research facilities and equipment including telescopes, research vessels, and large-scale computing infrastructure costing over $100 million.
beneficiaries: Scientific research community, major research facility users

name: Salaries and Expenses, Bureau of Indian Education
description: Funds the operation of approximately 183 elementary and secondary schools and dormitories serving American Indian and Alaska Native students on or near reservations. Also supports tribal colleges and universities.
beneficiaries: American Indian and Alaska Native students in BIE-funded schools

name: Indian Housing Block Grant, HUD
description: Provides formula-based housing assistance grants to Indian tribes and tribally designated housing entities under the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act (NAHASDA). Replaced multiple earlier HUD Indian housing programs.
beneficiaries: American Indian and Alaska Native families needing affordable housing

name: HOME Investment Partnerships Program, HUD
description: Provides formula grants to states and localities for a wide range of affordable housing activities including construction, rehabilitation, rental assistance, and homebuyer assistance for low-income households. The largest federal block grant for affordable housing.
beneficiaries: Low-income renters and homebuyers

name: Housing Opportunities for Persons with AIDS (HOPWA), HUD
description: Provides housing assistance and supportive services to low-income persons living with HIV/AIDS and their families. Funds tenant-based rental assistance, short-term supported housing, and housing information services.
beneficiaries: Low-income individuals and families living with HIV/AIDS

name: Homeless Assistance Grants, HUD
description: Funds programs to assist individuals and families experiencing homelessness through emergency shelter, transitional housing, permanent supportive housing, and rapid re-housing under the Continuum of Care program. Authorized by the McKinney-Vento Act.
beneficiaries: Individuals and families experiencing or at risk of homelessness

name: Federal Pell Grants, Department of Education
description: Provides need-based grants to low-income undergraduate students to promote post-secondary education access, with maximum awards adjusted annually. The largest federal grant program for higher education, serving approximately 7 million students.
beneficiaries: Low-income undergraduate students

name: Impact Aid, Department of Education
description: Provides payments to school districts that are financially burdened by federal activities, including districts with large amounts of tax-exempt federal land or that serve children of military families and those living on Indian lands.
beneficiaries: School districts affected by federal presence, military-connected students

name: English Language Acquisition Programs, Department of Education
description: Provides formula grants to states for programs serving English learners and immigrant students in K-12 schools. Authorized under Title III of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act.
beneficiaries: English learner and immigrant students in public schools

name: Student Aid Administration, Department of Education
description: Funds the administrative costs of Federal Student Aid, the largest provider of student financial aid in the nation, managing a loan portfolio exceeding $1.6 trillion and processing approximately 18 million FAFSA applications annually.
beneficiaries: Student loan borrowers, financial aid applicants

name: School Improvement Programs, Department of Education
description: Funds a collection of formula and competitive grant programs supporting state and local education reform efforts, including 21st Century Community Learning Centers, charter schools, and magnet schools programs.
beneficiaries: K-12 students, underperforming schools, after-school program participants

name: Job Corps, Department of Labor
description: Operates the nation's largest residential education and job training program for at-risk youth ages 16-24, providing academic and vocational training, health care, and job placement at approximately 121 centers. Authorized under the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act.
beneficiaries: At-risk and disadvantaged youth ages 16-24

name: Employment and Training Administration, Department of Labor
description: Funds workforce development programs including Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act formula grants to states, adult and dislocated worker training, apprenticeship, and trade adjustment assistance.
beneficiaries: Job seekers, dislocated workers, employers, state workforce agencies

name: Unemployment Trust Fund, Department of Labor
description: Holds state and federal unemployment tax revenues and funds unemployment insurance benefit payments through state programs, as well as federal administrative costs. Extended benefits are triggered during periods of high unemployment.
beneficiaries: Unemployed workers receiving state unemployment insurance benefits

name: Mine Safety and Health Administration, Department of Labor
description: Funds MSHA's enforcement of mine safety and health standards through inspections, investigations, and training at the nation's coal and metal/non-metal mines. Created by the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977.
beneficiaries: Mine workers, mining industry safety

name: Occupational Safety and Health Administration, Department of Labor
description: Funds OSHA's enforcement of workplace safety and health standards through inspections, citations, training, and compliance assistance. Covers approximately 130 million workers at 8 million worksites.
beneficiaries: Workers in covered industries, workplace safety

name: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP), HHS
description: Provides block grants to states for heating and cooling assistance, crisis intervention, and weatherization for low-income households. Serves approximately 5-6 million households annually during peak energy cost periods.
beneficiaries: Low-income households, especially elderly and disabled, facing energy costs

name: Child Support Enforcement, ACF, HHS
description: Provides federal matching funds to states for establishing paternity, locating absent parents, establishing and enforcing child support orders, and collecting payments. Collects approximately $34 billion annually in child support.
beneficiaries: Children and custodial parents owed child support

name: Social Services Block Grant (Title XX), HHS
description: Provides flexible block grants to states for a broad range of social services including child care, child protective services, adult protective services, and services for people with disabilities. States have wide discretion in fund usage.
beneficiaries: Low-income individuals and families, vulnerable populations needing social services

name: Head Start, ACF, HHS
description: Provides comprehensive early childhood education, health, nutrition, and parent involvement services to low-income children ages birth to five and pregnant women. Serves approximately 800,000 children through locally operated programs.
beneficiaries: Low-income preschool children and their families

name: Payments to States for Child Care and Development Block Grant
description: Provides federal funds to states for child care subsidies enabling low-income parents to work or attend education and training. Also funds quality improvement activities and health and safety improvements for child care settings.
beneficiaries: Low-income working families needing child care assistance

name: Ryan White HIV/AIDS Program, HRSA
description: Provides comprehensive HIV/AIDS care and treatment services to low-income, uninsured, and underinsured people living with HIV. Funds the AIDS Drug Assistance Program (ADAP) providing antiretroviral medications to over 260,000 individuals.
beneficiaries: Low-income individuals living with HIV/AIDS

name: Community Health Centers (Section 330), HRSA
description: Funds approximately 1,400 community health centers providing primary care, dental, behavioral health, and pharmacy services to medically underserved communities regardless of ability to pay. Serves approximately 30 million patients annually.
beneficiaries: Uninsured, underinsured, and underserved populations

name: National Health Service Corps, HRSA
description: Provides scholarships and loan repayment to health professionals who serve in health professional shortage areas (HPSAs) in exchange for a commitment to practice in underserved communities. Supports approximately 20,000 providers.
beneficiaries: Health professionals with student debt, underserved communities lacking providers

name: Title X Family Planning Program, HHS
description: Funds a network of approximately 4,000 family planning service sites providing contraception, STI testing, cancer screening, and other preventive health services to low-income and uninsured individuals. The sole federal program dedicated to family planning.
beneficiaries: Low-income women and men seeking family planning and reproductive health services

name: Maternal and Child Health Block Grant (Title V), HRSA
description: Provides formula grants to states for maternal and child health services, children with special health care needs, and related programs. The oldest federal-state partnership in public health, supporting newborn screening, prenatal care, and child health programs.
beneficiaries: Pregnant women, mothers, infants, children, and children with special health care needs

name: National Institutes of Health, Buildings and Facilities
description: Funds the construction, renovation, and maintenance of NIH research facilities on the main Bethesda campus and other NIH-owned locations. Supports the physical infrastructure needed for the intramural research program.
beneficiaries: NIH intramural research programs, campus infrastructure

name: Commodity Assistance Program, USDA
description: Funds the purchase and distribution of USDA commodities for domestic food assistance programs including The Emergency Food Assistance Program (TEFAP), the Commodity Supplemental Food Program, and commodity purchases for schools.
beneficiaries: Food banks, low-income individuals, school meal programs

name: Food Safety and Inspection Service, USDA
description: Funds USDA's inspection of domestic and imported meat, poultry, and processed egg products to ensure safety, wholesomeness, and proper labeling. Employs approximately 7,800 inspectors at over 6,500 establishments.
beneficiaries: Consumers, meat and poultry industry

name: Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service, USDA
description: Funds programs to protect U.S. agriculture from plant and animal pests and diseases, regulate genetically engineered organisms, and manage wildlife damage. Responds to outbreaks such as avian influenza and citrus greening.
beneficiaries: Agricultural producers, livestock industry, food supply security

name: Natural Resources Conservation Service, USDA
description: Provides technical and financial assistance to farmers and landowners for conservation practices through programs including EQIP, CSP, and conservation planning. Originally established as the Soil Conservation Service.
beneficiaries: Farmers, ranchers, landowners, soil and water conservation

name: Agricultural Research Service, USDA
description: USDA's principal in-house research agency conducting basic and applied research in animal and crop production, food safety, nutrition, and natural resources at over 90 locations. The largest agricultural research organization in the world.
beneficiaries: Agricultural producers, food industry, scientific community

name: Rural Development Programs, USDA
description: Provides loans, grants, and technical assistance for rural community facilities, business development, and essential services. Includes the Community Facilities Program, Rural Business-Cooperative Service, and rural broadband programs.
beneficiaries: Rural communities, businesses, and essential service providers

name: Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy, DOE
description: Funds research, development, and deployment of clean energy technologies including solar, wind, geothermal, bioenergy, advanced vehicles, building efficiency, and advanced manufacturing. Received major boosts from the IIJA and IRA.
beneficiaries: Clean energy industry, consumers, climate and energy security

name: Environmental and Other Defense Activities, DOE
description: Funds cleanup of contaminated sites from decades of nuclear weapons production and government-sponsored nuclear energy research, including the Hanford and Savannah River sites. One of the world's largest environmental remediation programs.
beneficiaries: Communities near former nuclear production sites, environmental remediation

name: Nuclear Energy Programs, DOE
description: Funds research and development of advanced nuclear energy technologies including small modular reactors, advanced reactor designs, nuclear fuel cycle research, and management of spent nuclear fuel and high-level radioactive waste.
beneficiaries: Nuclear energy industry, energy security, climate goals

name: Power Marketing Administrations (Southeastern, Southwestern, Western Area)
description: Three DOE power marketing administrations that market and transmit wholesale electricity generated at federal hydroelectric dams to preference customers including rural cooperatives and municipal utilities across their respective regions.
beneficiaries: Rural electric cooperatives, municipal utilities, wholesale power customers

name: Federal Energy Regulatory Commission
description: Regulates interstate transmission of electricity, natural gas, and oil, licenses hydroelectric projects, and oversees wholesale electricity markets. Self-funded through annual charges and filing fees assessed on regulated entities.
beneficiaries: Energy consumers, regulated utilities, wholesale energy markets

name: Small Business Administration, Salaries and Expenses
description: Funds SBA's operating costs for administering small business programs including guaranteed lending (7(a) and 504 loans), contracting assistance, business counseling, and small business advocacy. Supports approximately 33 million small businesses.
beneficiaries: Small business owners and entrepreneurs

name: Legal Services Corporation
description: Provides grants to independent nonprofit legal aid programs delivering civil legal assistance to low-income Americans in matters including housing, family law, consumer issues, and public benefits. Serves approximately 1.8 million people annually.
beneficiaries: Low-income Americans needing civil legal assistance

name: Corporation for National and Community Service (AmeriCorps)
description: Funds national service programs including AmeriCorps State and National, AmeriCorps VISTA, AmeriCorps NCCC, and Senior Corps, engaging Americans in community service in exchange for education awards. Supports approximately 200,000 members and volunteers annually.
beneficiaries: AmeriCorps members, communities served by national service programs

name: Corporation for Public Broadcasting
description: Provides federal funding to public television and radio stations, supporting programming, station operations, and interconnection services for the public broadcasting system. Funds approximately 1,500 locally managed public media stations.
beneficiaries: Public media audiences, local public television and radio stations

name: National Endowment for the Arts
description: Provides grants to nonprofit arts organizations, state arts agencies, and individual artists to support artistic excellence, creativity, and public access to the arts. The largest annual funder of the arts in the United States.
beneficiaries: Arts organizations, artists, communities served by funded arts programs

name: National Endowment for the Humanities
description: Provides grants for humanities research, education, preservation, and public programs in areas including history, literature, philosophy, languages, and archaeology. Supports state humanities councils in every state and territory.
beneficiaries: Scholars, educators, cultural institutions, general public

name: Institute of Museum and Library Services
description: Provides federal grants and policy development for the nation's approximately 35,000 museums and 120,000 libraries. Administers the Library Services and Technology Act and Museum Services Act funding.
beneficiaries: Public libraries, museums, library and museum patrons

name: Election Assistance Commission
description: Provides grants and guidance to state and local election administrators for election administration improvements, voting system certification, and election security. Created by the Help America Vote Act following the 2000 election.
beneficiaries: State and local election officials, voters

name: Government Accountability Office
description: Funds GAO's audits, evaluations, and investigations of federal programs and expenditures on behalf of Congress. Known as the 'congressional watchdog,' it produces approximately 700 reports annually identifying billions in potential savings.
beneficiaries: Congress, taxpayers, federal program accountability

name: Office of Personnel Management, Salaries and Expenses
description: Funds OPM's administration of the federal civil service including hiring policy, classification, employee benefits, retirement, background investigations, and workforce management for approximately 2.1 million federal civilian employees.
beneficiaries: Federal civilian employees, job applicants, retirees

name: General Services Administration, Acquisition Services Fund
description: A revolving fund financing GSA's procurement services including Federal Supply Schedules, assisted acquisition, and technology solutions for federal agencies. Provides government-wide contracts generating significant volume discounts.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies purchasing goods and services

name: Payments to Health Care Trust Funds, CMS, HHS
description: Treasury transfers general revenue funds to the Medicare Hospital Insurance (Part A) and Supplementary Medical Insurance (Part B/D) trust funds. Covers the gap between dedicated revenues and program costs.
beneficiaries: Medicare enrollees (65+, disabled, ESRD patients)

name: Payments to Military Retirement Fund, Defense
description: Treasury payment to the Military Retirement Fund covering the unfunded liability for military retirement benefits accrued before the fund's creation. Ensures actuarial soundness of the retirement system.
beneficiaries: Military retirees and survivors

name: Refundable Premium Assistance Tax Credit, IRS, Treasury
description: Funds the premium tax credits under the Affordable Care Act for individuals purchasing health insurance on marketplace exchanges. Refundable portion paid to insurers on behalf of eligible enrollees.
beneficiaries: Low- and moderate-income individuals/families purchasing ACA marketplace plans

name: Medicare Prescription Drug Account, CMS, HHS
description: Finances the Medicare Part D prescription drug benefit enacted by the Medicare Modernization Act. Funded primarily through beneficiary premiums and general revenue transfers.
beneficiaries: Medicare beneficiaries enrolled in Part D drug plans

name: Federal Direct Student Loan Program, Education
description: Provides federal student loans directly from the Treasury to eligible students and parents for postsecondary education. Includes Stafford (subsidized/unsubsidized), PLUS, and consolidation loans.
beneficiaries: College and graduate students, parents of undergraduates

name: Medical Services, Veterans Health Administration, VA
description: Funds inpatient, outpatient, and mental health care services at VA medical centers and clinics nationwide. The largest single VA appropriation supporting direct health care delivery.
beneficiaries: Eligible veterans enrolled in VA health care

name: Payment Where Earned Income Credit Exceeds Liability for Tax, IRS
description: Funds the refundable portion of the Earned Income Tax Credit when the credit exceeds a filer's income tax liability. One of the largest anti-poverty programs in the federal budget.
beneficiaries: Low- to moderate-income working individuals and families

name: Federal-Aid Highways, Liquidation of Contract Authorization, FHWA
description: Provides cash to liquidate obligations incurred under contract authority for the federal-aid highway program. Funded primarily from the Highway Trust Fund's highway account.
beneficiaries: State and local highway agencies, motorists, commercial transportation

name: Payments to Social Security Trust Funds, SSA
description: General fund transfers to the OASI and DI trust funds covering items such as military service credits, taxation of benefits revenue, and special COVID-era credits. Ensures trust fund solvency for legislatively mandated obligations.
beneficiaries: Social Security beneficiaries (retirees, disabled, survivors)

name: Payment to Civil Service Retirement and Disability Fund, OPM
description: Treasury payment covering the government's share of retirement costs for federal civilian employees under CSRS and FERS, including the unfunded liability amortization. The largest single intragovernmental transfer for civilian retirement.
beneficiaries: Federal civilian retirees and their survivors

name: Military Personnel, Army
description: Funds pay, allowances, bonuses, and benefits for active-duty Army enlisted and officer personnel. Covers basic pay, BAH, BAS, special/incentive pays, and permanent change of station travel.
beneficiaries: Active-duty Army service members

name: Research, Development, Test and Evaluation, Air Force
description: Funds Air Force research, development, testing, and evaluation of weapons systems, technology, and equipment. Covers basic research through operational system development and live-fire testing.
beneficiaries: Air Force, defense contractors, national security

name: Defense Health Program, Defense
description: Consolidated account funding the TRICARE military health system, including direct care at military treatment facilities and purchased care from civilian providers. Covers medical and dental services for active duty, dependents, and retirees.
beneficiaries: Active-duty members, military retirees, and dependents

name: State and Tribal Assistance Grants, EPA
description: EPA's largest grant program funding state, tribal, and local governments for water infrastructure (drinking water and clean water state revolving funds) and environmental program implementation. Includes categorical grants for pollution control programs.
beneficiaries: State/tribal/local governments, public water systems, communities

name: Unemployment Trust Fund - Treasury Managed, Labor
description: Holds federal and state unemployment tax receipts and invests them in special Treasury obligations. States draw from their individual accounts to pay unemployment insurance benefits.
beneficiaries: Unemployed workers eligible for state/federal unemployment benefits

name: Taxation on OASDI Benefits, HI, CMS, HHS
description: Transfers revenue from federal income taxation of Social Security benefits to the Medicare Hospital Insurance trust fund. Enacted as part of the 1993 Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act.
beneficiaries: Medicare Part A beneficiaries

name: Disaster Relief Fund, FEMA, Homeland Security
description: Primary federal account for disaster response and recovery, funding FEMA's public and individual assistance programs after presidential disaster declarations. Receives both regular appropriations and emergency supplemental funding.
beneficiaries: Disaster-affected individuals, communities, state/local governments

name: Research, Development, Test and Evaluation, Defense-Wide
description: Funds RDT&E activities managed by defense agencies and the Office of the Secretary of Defense, including DARPA, MDA, and DTRA programs. Covers cross-cutting research not assigned to a specific military department.
beneficiaries: Defense agencies, warfighters, national security

name: Military Personnel, Navy
description: Funds pay, allowances, bonuses, and benefits for active-duty Navy enlisted and officer personnel. Covers basic pay, housing and subsistence allowances, and special/incentive pays.
beneficiaries: Active-duty Navy service members

name: Military Personnel, Air Force
description: Funds pay, allowances, bonuses, and benefits for active-duty Air Force and Space Force personnel. Covers basic pay, BAH, BAS, special pays, and PCS travel.
beneficiaries: Active-duty Air Force and Space Force service members

name: Child Nutrition Programs, Food and Nutrition Service, USDA
description: Funds the National School Lunch Program, School Breakfast Program, Child and Adult Care Food Program, and other child feeding initiatives. Provides cash reimbursements and commodity support to participating institutions.
beneficiaries: Children in schools, childcare, and summer feeding programs

name: Other Procurement, Air Force
description: Funds Air Force procurement of vehicles, communications/electronics equipment, and other support items not covered by aircraft or missile procurement. Includes classified programs and base support equipment.
beneficiaries: Air Force units and national defense

name: Medical Community Care, Veterans Health Administration, VA
description: Funds veteran health care purchased from non-VA community providers under the MISSION Act. Covers care when VA facilities lack capacity, capability, or geographic accessibility.
beneficiaries: Veterans requiring care from community (non-VA) health providers

name: Acquisition Services Fund, GSA
description: GSA revolving fund providing federal agencies with IT solutions, professional services, and assisted acquisition services on a fee-for-service basis. Successor to the former IT Fund, operating without annual appropriations.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies purchasing IT and professional services

name: Research, Development, Test and Evaluation, Navy
description: Funds Navy and Marine Corps RDT&E for weapons systems, shipboard systems, marine technology, and advanced concepts. Covers basic research through operational systems development.
beneficiaries: Navy/Marine Corps, defense industry, national security

name: Payment Where Child Tax Credit Exceeds Liability for Tax, IRS
description: Funds the refundable (additional) portion of the Child Tax Credit when it exceeds a filer's tax liability. Significantly expanded temporarily by the 2021 American Rescue Plan.
beneficiaries: Families with qualifying children, especially lower-income households

name: Universal Service Fund, FCC
description: Off-budget fund financed by telecom carrier assessments to support affordable telecommunications in underserved areas. Includes E-Rate (schools/libraries), Lifeline, Rural Health Care, and High-Cost programs.
beneficiaries: Rural/low-income consumers, schools, libraries, rural health providers

name: Research, Development, Test and Evaluation, Army
description: Funds Army RDT&E for ground combat vehicles, weapons, aviation, soldier systems, and network technologies. Covers basic and applied research through full-scale system development and testing.
beneficiaries: Army units, defense contractors, national security

name: Broadband Equity Access and Deployment Program, NTIA, Commerce
description: $42.45 billion program created by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act to fund broadband infrastructure deployment in unserved and underserved areas. Distributes formula grants to states and territories.
beneficiaries: Unserved/underserved communities, states, territories, broadband providers

name: Federal Crop Insurance Corporation Fund, Risk Management Agency, USDA
description: Funds the federal crop insurance program, paying premium subsidies and administrative/operating expense reimbursements to private insurance companies. Covers underwriting losses when indemnities exceed premiums.
beneficiaries: Farmers, ranchers, and approved private crop insurance companies

name: Operations and Support, CBP, Homeland Security
description: Primary appropriation for Customs and Border Protection's operations including border security, trade enforcement, and immigration inspection. Covers personnel compensation, technology, and air/marine operations.
beneficiaries: CBP workforce, border communities, lawful trade and travel

name: Deposit Insurance Fund, FDIC
description: Insures bank deposits up to $250,000 per depositor per institution, funded by risk-based assessments on insured depository institutions. Covers losses from bank failures and resolution costs.
beneficiaries: Depositors at FDIC-insured banks and savings institutions

name: Department of Defense Military Retirement Fund
description: Trust fund paying retired pay and survivor benefits for military retirees, financed by DoD contributions, Treasury payments for the unfunded liability, and investment earnings. Operates on an accrual accounting basis.
beneficiaries: Military retirees, survivors, and their families

name: Employees Health Benefits Fund, OPM
description: Revolving fund financing the Federal Employees Health Benefits Program, the largest employer-sponsored health plan in the U.S. Funded by employee and government premium contributions.
beneficiaries: Federal employees, retirees, and their dependents

name: Operation and Maintenance, Navy
description: Funds Navy day-to-day operating and training costs including ship and aircraft maintenance, base operations, and civilian personnel. The Navy's largest annual appropriation by obligation.
beneficiaries: Navy fleet readiness and operational forces

name: Operation and Maintenance, Army
description: Funds Army training, equipment maintenance, base operations, logistics, and civilian workforce. Sustains readiness of active-duty Army units and installations worldwide.
beneficiaries: Active-duty Army units and installations

name: Operation and Maintenance, Air Force
description: Funds Air Force flying operations, depot maintenance, base support, civilian personnel, and logistics. Sustains combat readiness of air and space forces.
beneficiaries: Air Force operational units and installations

name: Operation and Maintenance, Defense-Wide
description: Funds operating costs for defense agencies and OSD activities including SOCOM, DISA, DCMA, and defense-wide initiatives. Covers personnel, logistics, and program operations across multiple agencies.
beneficiaries: Defense agencies, combatant commands, special operations forces

name: Cost of War Toxic Exposures Fund, VA
description: Created by the PACT Act to fund health care and benefits for veterans exposed to burn pits, Agent Orange, and other toxic substances. Designated as mandatory spending outside discretionary caps.
beneficiaries: Veterans with toxic exposure-related conditions

name: Education for the Disadvantaged, Education
description: Funds Title I grants to local educational agencies serving high concentrations of children from low-income families. The largest federal K-12 education program supporting academic achievement in high-poverty schools.
beneficiaries: Low-income students and high-poverty school districts

name: Operations and Support, ICE, Homeland Security
description: Primary appropriation for Immigration and Customs Enforcement covering homeland security investigations, enforcement and removal operations, and detention. Funds personnel, detention beds, and investigative operations.
beneficiaries: ICE workforce, immigration enforcement system

name: Science, Energy Programs, DOE
description: Funds DOE's Office of Science, the nation's largest sponsor of basic research in the physical sciences. Supports national laboratories, large-scale user facilities, and research in energy, computing, and nuclear physics.
beneficiaries: Researchers, national laboratories, universities, scientific community

name: Medical Support and Compliance, VHA, VA
description: Funds VA health care administration, oversight, quality management, and compliance activities. Covers health informatics, medical research support, and management infrastructure.
beneficiaries: VA health system administration and indirectly all VA patients

name: Operating Expenses, Coast Guard, Homeland Security
description: Funds Coast Guard day-to-day operations including search and rescue, maritime law enforcement, port security, and aids to navigation. Covers military and civilian personnel, maintenance, and logistics.
beneficiaries: Coast Guard personnel, mariners, port communities

name: Operations and Support, TSA, Homeland Security
description: Funds Transportation Security Administration passenger and baggage screening, Federal Air Marshal Service, and surface transportation security. Created after September 11 to secure the nation's transportation systems.
beneficiaries: Air travelers, transportation systems, TSA workforce

name: Community Development Fund (CDBG), HUD
description: Formula and competitive grants to states and local governments for community development activities including housing rehabilitation, infrastructure, and economic development. Primarily targets low- and moderate-income communities.
beneficiaries: Low/moderate-income communities, cities, counties, states

name: General Operating Expenses, VBA, VA
description: Funds the Veterans Benefits Administration's claims processing, compensation and pension administration, education benefits processing, and loan guaranty oversight. Covers staffing and IT for all VBA programs.
beneficiaries: Veterans and dependents applying for or receiving VA benefits

name: Foster Care and Permanency Payments, ACF, HHS
description: Open-ended entitlement funding maintenance payments for children in foster care, adoption assistance, and kinship guardianship assistance under Title IV-E of the Social Security Act. Federal share is based on state Medicaid matching rates.
beneficiaries: Children in foster care, adoptive families, kinship guardians

name: Exchange Stabilization Fund, Treasury
description: Treasury fund for foreign exchange market intervention and international monetary stabilization, capitalized from gold revaluation profits. Also used for emergency lending (e.g., backed TARP and money market guarantees in 2008).
beneficiaries: U.S. financial system stability, international monetary order

name: Salaries and Expenses, Federal Prison System, BOP
description: Funds operation of the federal prison system including custody, care, and correctional programs for federal inmates across 122+ institutions. Covers staff compensation, inmate medical care, food, and facility operations.
beneficiaries: Federal inmates, correctional staff, public safety

name: Concurrent Receipt Accrual Payments to Military Retirement
description: Treasury payments to the Military Retirement Fund to cover concurrent receipt of military retirement pay and VA disability compensation. Authorized by the FY2004 NDAA eliminating the offset for qualifying retirees.
beneficiaries: Military retirees with combat-related or qualifying VA disability ratings

name: Readjustment Benefits, VBA, VA (GI Bill)
description: Funds the Post-9/11 GI Bill, Montgomery GI Bill, and other readjustment programs including vocational rehabilitation and employment services. One of the largest mandatory spending accounts at VA.
beneficiaries: Veterans, service members, and eligible dependents pursuing education/training

name: Federal Family Education Loan Program Account, Education
description: Manages the legacy portfolio of federally guaranteed student loans originated by private lenders before the program ended in 2010. Now in wind-down, covering default claims, consolidation, and interest subsidies on outstanding loans.
beneficiaries: Borrowers with outstanding FFEL loans, guaranty agencies

name: Revolving Fund, Corps of Engineers
description: Finances Army Corps of Engineers work performed for non-federal entities and other federal agencies on a reimbursable basis. Covers environmental remediation, water testing, and engineering services.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies, state/local governments, and other customers of Corps services

name: DoD Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund
description: Accrual fund financing TRICARE-for-Life benefits for Medicare-eligible military retirees, dependents, and survivors. Established by the FY2001 NDAA with contributions from DoD and Treasury.
beneficiaries: Medicare-eligible military retirees, dependents, and survivors age 65+

name: Salaries and Expenses, FBI, Justice
description: Primary appropriation for the Federal Bureau of Investigation covering counterterrorism, counterintelligence, cybercrime, and criminal investigative programs. Funds special agents, intelligence analysts, and forensic capabilities.
beneficiaries: Federal law enforcement, public safety, national security

name: Reserve Personnel, Army/Navy/Marine Corps/Air Force
description: Funds pay and allowances for Selected Reserve and Individual Ready Reserve members during drill, annual training, and active-duty tours. Separate appropriation for each service's reserve component.
beneficiaries: Reserve component service members across all branches

name: Procurement of Ammunition, Army/Navy/Air Force
description: Funds procurement of conventional ammunition, munitions, and related items for each service. Covers small arms, artillery, bombs, and other expendable ordnance.
beneficiaries: Military service members and combat forces

name: Office of the Inspector General, DoD
description: Funds the DoD IG's independent oversight through audits, investigations, evaluations, and hotline operations. Provides accountability for the largest departmental budget in the federal government.
beneficiaries: DoD leadership, Congress, taxpayers

name: Military Construction, Army/Navy/Air Force/Defense-Wide/Space Force/Guard/Reserve
description: Funds planning, design, and construction of military facilities including barracks, training ranges, maintenance facilities, and operational buildings. Separate appropriations for each service and component.
beneficiaries: Military service members, installations, and local communities

name: Family Housing Construction and O&M, Army/Navy/Air Force
description: Funds construction of new military family housing and operation/maintenance of existing units including utilities, furnishings, and privatization support. Separate accounts for each service.
beneficiaries: Military families residing in on-base housing

name: Defense Intelligence Agency
description: Funds the principal producer of foreign military intelligence for DoD, providing analysis, HUMINT collection, and technical intelligence to warfighters and policymakers. Budget details are largely classified.
beneficiaries: Military commanders, policymakers, intelligence community

name: National Security Agency
description: Funds signals intelligence collection, cryptologic activities, and cybersecurity operations for the nation. Budget is classified within the National Intelligence Program and Military Intelligence Program.
beneficiaries: National security community, military operations, cybersecurity defense

name: Federal Housing Finance Agency
description: Independent regulator overseeing Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, and the Federal Home Loan Banks. Created by HERA 2008, replacing OFHEO and the FHFB.
beneficiaries: Housing GSEs, mortgage markets, homeowners

name: Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC)
description: Treasury office administering and enforcing U.S. economic and trade sanctions programs against targeted countries, entities, and individuals. Maintains the Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) list.
beneficiaries: U.S. national security and foreign policy objectives

name: United States Mint
description: Treasury bureau manufacturing and distributing circulating coinage, bullion, and numismatic products. Self-funded through seigniorage (difference between face value and production cost) and product sales.
beneficiaries: U.S. economy, coin collectors, bullion investors

name: Bureau of Engraving and Printing
description: Treasury bureau designing, manufacturing, and supplying Federal Reserve notes (paper currency) and other security documents. Self-funded revolving fund financed by payments from the Federal Reserve.
beneficiaries: Federal Reserve System, U.S. economy, currency users

name: Operations Support, IRS
description: Funds IRS infrastructure including IT, facilities, printing, postage, and shared services that support all IRS operations. Reorganized from prior IRS accounts under the IRS Restructuring and Reform Act implementation.
beneficiaries: IRS workforce and indirectly all U.S. taxpayers through functional tax administration

name: Enforcement, IRS
description: Funds IRS examination, collection, criminal investigation, and regulatory enforcement activities to ensure tax compliance. Covers audits, investigations of tax fraud, and legal counsel for enforcement actions.
beneficiaries: U.S. Treasury (revenue collection) and compliant taxpayers through deterrence of tax evasion

name: Business Systems Modernization, IRS
description: Dedicated capital account for modernizing IRS legacy information technology systems, including taxpayer-facing digital services. Created under the IRS Restructuring and Reform Act of 1998.
beneficiaries: Taxpayers through improved digital services; IRS through modernized IT infrastructure

name: Customs Duties Collections, CBP
description: One of the oldest federal revenue accounts, collecting tariffs and duties on imported goods at U.S. ports of entry. Administered by Customs and Border Protection within DHS since 2003, successor to the original U.S. Customs Service.
beneficiaries: U.S. Treasury (revenue); domestic industries protected by tariffs

name: Indian Health Service
description: Provides federally funded health care to members of federally recognized American Indian and Alaska Native tribes through a network of hospitals, clinics, and contracted tribal health programs. Established by the Transfer Act of 1954, effective 1955.
beneficiaries: Approximately 2.6 million American Indians and Alaska Natives in federally recognized tribes

name: CDC Immunization Programs
description: Funds vaccine purchase, distribution, surveillance, and immunization infrastructure through programs like Vaccines for Children (VFC) and Section 317 grants. Supports state and local immunization efforts and outbreak response.
beneficiaries: Children, adults, and communities nationwide; uninsured and underinsured populations for vaccine access

name: CDC HIV/AIDS Prevention
description: Funds surveillance, testing, prevention interventions, and public education to reduce HIV transmission in the United States. Includes funding to state and local health departments and community-based organizations.
beneficiaries: Populations at elevated HIV risk; people living with HIV; state and local public health agencies

name: CDC Chronic Disease Prevention
description: Funds programs addressing heart disease, cancer, diabetes, tobacco use, and other chronic conditions through surveillance, prevention grants, and health promotion. Operates through the National Center for Chronic Disease Prevention and Health Promotion.
beneficiaries: General U.S. population; communities with high chronic disease burden; state health departments

name: FDA Salaries and Expenses
description: Primary operating account for the Food and Drug Administration, funding regulation of food safety, pharmaceuticals, medical devices, biologics, tobacco, and cosmetics. Supplemented significantly by industry user fees since 1992.
beneficiaries: U.S. consumers of food, drugs, medical devices, and regulated products

name: SAMHSA Programs
description: Funds the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration's grant programs including the Substance Abuse Prevention and Treatment Block Grant and Community Mental Health Services Block Grant. Created by the ADAMHA Reorganization Act of 1992.
beneficiaries: Individuals with substance use disorders and mental illness; state behavioral health agencies; community treatment providers

name: HRSA Health Programs
description: Umbrella operating account for the Health Resources and Services Administration, funding workforce development, primary care access, maternal/child health, and health systems in underserved areas. HRSA was established as a PHS agency in 1982.
beneficiaries: Medically underserved populations, rural communities, health professionals in shortage areas

name: Refugee and Entrant Assistance, ACF, HHS
description: Funds resettlement services for refugees, asylees, Cuban/Haitian entrants, and unaccompanied migrant children, including cash assistance, medical screening, and social integration. Authorized by the Refugee Act of 1980.
beneficiaries: Refugees, asylees, unaccompanied alien children, Cuban/Haitian entrants, victims of trafficking

name: Aging and Disability Services, ACL
description: Funds programs under the Older Americans Act including home-delivered and congregate meals, caregiver support, senior community service employment, and disability services. ACL was established in 2012 consolidating the Administration on Aging.
beneficiaries: Older adults (60+), family caregivers, people with disabilities

name: CMS Program Management
description: Administrative operating account for the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, funding program oversight, claims processing, fraud prevention, survey and certification of providers, and ACA marketplace operations. Separate from benefit payments.
beneficiaries: Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries indirectly through program integrity and administration

name: BARDA/ASPR
description: The Biomedical Advanced Research and Development Authority, within the Assistant Secretary for Preparedness and Response, develops medical countermeasures against chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, and pandemic threats. Created by the Pandemic and All-Hazards Preparedness Act of 2006.
beneficiaries: U.S. population (national health security); pharmaceutical/biotech firms developing countermeasures

name: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
description: Block grant helping low-income households pay heating and cooling bills and weatherize homes, distributed to states based on climate and poverty formulas. Authorized by the Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Act of 1981.
beneficiaries: Low-income households, especially elderly, disabled, and families with young children facing high energy costs

name: Child Support Enforcement, ACF
description: Federal matching funds for state child support enforcement programs including paternity establishment, support order establishment, and collections. Created as Title IV-D of the Social Security Act in 1975.
beneficiaries: Custodial parents and children owed child support; state IV-D agencies

name: Social Services Block Grant (Title XX)
description: Flexible block grant allowing states to fund a wide range of social services including child care, protective services, foster care, and services for disabled adults. Authorized under Title XX of the Social Security Act.
beneficiaries: Low-income individuals and families; children at risk of abuse/neglect; elderly and disabled adults

name: Head Start, ACF
description: Provides comprehensive early childhood education, health, nutrition, and family support services to low-income children ages 0-5 and their families. Launched as part of President Johnson's War on Poverty.
beneficiaries: Low-income preschool children and their families; pregnant women (Early Head Start)

name: Child Care and Development Block Grant
description: Primary federal program subsidizing child care for low-income working families, distributed as block grants to states with substantial state flexibility. Reauthorized and expanded by the CCDBG Act of 2014.
beneficiaries: Low-income working families with children under age 13; child care providers

name: Title X Family Planning Program
description: The sole federal program dedicated to providing comprehensive family planning and preventive health services, including contraception, STI screening, and cancer screening. Authorized under Title X of the Public Health Service Act.
beneficiaries: Low-income and uninsured individuals seeking reproductive health services; approximately 4 million patients annually

name: Maternal and Child Health Block Grant (Title V)
description: One of the oldest federal-state partnership programs, funding maternal and child health services, children with special health care needs, and public health infrastructure. Originally part of the Social Security Act of 1935.
beneficiaries: Pregnant women, mothers, infants, children, and children with special health care needs

name: NIH Buildings and Facilities
description: Capital account funding construction, renovation, and maintenance of NIH research facilities, primarily on the Bethesda, Maryland campus. Supports the physical infrastructure for intramural biomedical research.
beneficiaries: NIH intramural researchers; biomedical research enterprise

name: Environmental Cleanup Programs, DOE
description: Funds cleanup of radioactive and hazardous waste at former nuclear weapons production and energy research sites across the DOE complex, managed by the Office of Environmental Management. One of the largest cleanup programs in the world.
beneficiaries: Communities near contaminated DOE sites; environment; cleanup workforce

name: Small Business Administration Salaries and Expenses
description: Operating account for the SBA funding administration of small business loan guaranty programs, counseling, contracting assistance, and advocacy. SBA was created by the Small Business Act of 1953.
beneficiaries: Small businesses, entrepreneurs, and SBA staff

name: AmeriCorps/Corporation for National and Community Service
description: Funds national service programs including AmeriCorps State and National, AmeriCorps VISTA, AmeriCorps NCCC, and Senior Corps, engaging Americans in community service in exchange for education awards. Created by the National and Community Service Trust Act of 1993.
beneficiaries: Service members (education awards), communities receiving volunteer service, nonprofits hosting members

name: OPM Salaries and Expenses
description: Operating account for the Office of Personnel Management, which manages the federal civilian workforce including hiring policy, background investigations, employee benefits administration, and retirement services. OPM was established when the Civil Service Commission was reorganized in 1979.
beneficiaries: Federal civilian employees, retirees, and federal agencies

name: GSA Acquisition Services Fund
description: Revolving fund through which GSA procures goods and services (IT, vehicles, supplies, telecommunications) in bulk for federal agencies, leveraging economies of scale. Operates on a fee-for-service cost-recovery basis.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies purchasing goods and services; taxpayers through procurement savings

name: NIH National Institute of Mental Health
description: Funds research on mental disorders including depression, schizophrenia, anxiety, PTSD, and autism spectrum disorder, spanning basic neuroscience through clinical trials and services research. The lead federal agency for research on mental illnesses.
beneficiaries: Individuals with mental health conditions; researchers; clinicians through improved treatment evidence

name: NIH National Institute on Drug Abuse
description: Funds research on drug use and addiction including neuroscience of addiction, prevention, treatment development, and public health consequences. Leads federal research on the opioid crisis and emerging drug threats.
beneficiaries: Individuals with substance use disorders; researchers; public health agencies; treatment providers

name: NIH NIDDK (National Institute of Diabetes and Digestive and Kidney Diseases)
description: Funds research on diabetes, endocrine and metabolic diseases, digestive diseases, nutrition, obesity, and kidney/urologic/hematologic diseases. Supports major clinical trials and research networks for these conditions.
beneficiaries: Patients with diabetes, kidney disease, digestive disorders, and related conditions; researchers

name: NIH National Institute on Aging
description: Leads federal research on aging and age-related diseases, particularly Alzheimer's disease and related dementias, including biology of aging, behavioral research, and geriatrics. Received major funding increases for Alzheimer's research since 2015.
beneficiaries: Older adults, Alzheimer's patients and caregivers, aging researchers

name: NIH NICHD (Eunice Kennedy Shriver National Institute of Child Health and Human Development)
description: Funds research on fertility, pregnancy, childhood development, intellectual and developmental disabilities, and rehabilitation science. Established at President Kennedy's direction to study the processes of human development.
beneficiaries: Infants, children, pregnant women, individuals with developmental disabilities, researchers

name: NIH NINDS (National Institute of Neurological Disorders and Stroke)
description: Funds research on the brain, spinal cord, and nervous system disorders including stroke, epilepsy, Parkinson's, ALS, traumatic brain injury, and multiple sclerosis. Originally the National Institute of Neurological Diseases and Blindness.
beneficiaries: Patients with neurological disorders; neuroscience researchers; clinicians

name: NIH NIEHS (National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences)
description: Funds research on how environmental exposures (chemicals, pollutants, radiation, climate) affect human health and disease, including the Superfund Research Program. Located in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
beneficiaries: Communities exposed to environmental hazards; environmental health researchers; regulatory agencies using NIEHS evidence

name: Bureau of Reclamation Construction
description: Funds construction and rehabilitation of water infrastructure projects (dams, reservoirs, canals, water treatment facilities) in the 17 western states. Established by the Reclamation Act of 1902 to develop arid western lands through irrigation.
beneficiaries: Western U.S. water users including irrigators, municipalities, tribes, and hydropower consumers

name: NSF Education and Human Resources
description: Funds STEM education programs from K-12 through graduate level, including teacher preparation, undergraduate research experiences, graduate fellowships, and broadening participation of underrepresented groups. NSF's education directorate.
beneficiaries: STEM students at all levels, educators, researchers in STEM education, underrepresented groups in science

name: NSF Major Research Equipment and Facilities Construction
description: Funds construction of large-scale, cutting-edge research infrastructure such as major telescopes, particle detectors, research vessels, and cyberinfrastructure. Supports facilities too large for individual research grants.
beneficiaries: Research community using large-scale facilities; universities; national and international scientific collaborations

name: Bureau of Indian Education
description: Operates and funds approximately 180 elementary and secondary schools and dormitories serving Native American students on or near reservations, plus supports tribal colleges. One of only two federal school systems in the U.S.
beneficiaries: American Indian and Alaska Native students in BIE-funded schools; tribal colleges and universities

name: HOME Investment Partnerships, HUD
description: Formula block grant to states and localities for building, buying, and rehabilitating affordable housing for low-income households, including rental and homeowner assistance. Created by the Cranston-Gonzalez National Affordable Housing Act of 1990.
beneficiaries: Low-income renters and homebuyers; state and local governments; nonprofit housing developers

name: HOPWA (Housing Opportunities for Persons with AIDS), HUD
description: The only federal program dedicated to housing assistance for people living with HIV/AIDS and their families, providing rental assistance, short-term housing, and supportive services. Authorized by the AIDS Housing Opportunity Act.
beneficiaries: Low-income persons living with HIV/AIDS and their families

name: Impact Aid, Education
description: Compensates school districts for lost property tax revenue due to federal presence (military bases, Indian lands, federal facilities) and educates federally connected children. One of the oldest federal education programs.
beneficiaries: School districts with significant federal land or military installations; children of military families and those on Indian lands

name: English Language Acquisition, Education
description: Formula grants to states for programs serving English learners (ELs) in K-12 schools, supporting language instruction and academic achievement. Originally the Bilingual Education Act (Title VII), now Title III of ESEA/ESSA.
beneficiaries: English learner students in public schools; immigrant children and youth; schools with EL populations

name: Student Aid Administration, Education
description: Funds the administrative costs of operating the federal student financial aid programs (Pell Grants, Direct Loans, work-study), including application processing, loan servicing, and compliance. Operates Federal Student Aid as a PBO.
beneficiaries: College students and borrowers; postsecondary institutions; loan servicers

name: School Improvement Programs, Education
description: Funds various K-12 education programs supporting school improvement, including 21st Century Community Learning Centers, education for homeless children, rural education, and other formula and competitive grants under ESEA/ESSA.
beneficiaries: K-12 students in underperforming or high-need schools; school districts; states

name: Job Corps, Labor
description: Residential education and vocational training program for at-risk youth ages 16-24, providing academic instruction, career technical training, and social skills development at approximately 120 centers nationwide. Created by the Economic Opportunity Act of 1964.
beneficiaries: Disadvantaged and at-risk youth ages 16-24 lacking education or job skills

name: Employment and Training Administration, Labor
description: Funds federal workforce development programs including WIOA formula grants to states, Trade Adjustment Assistance, apprenticeship, unemployment insurance administration, and employment services. Current framework authorized under WIOA (2014).
beneficiaries: Job seekers, dislocated workers, employers, state workforce agencies, One-Stop Career Centers

name: Mine Safety and Health Administration, Labor
description: Funds enforcement of mine safety and health standards in all U.S. mines (coal and metal/nonmetal) through inspections, investigations, training, and standard-setting. Established by the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977.
beneficiaries: Mine workers (approximately 250,000); mining companies through safety compliance guidance

name: OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration), Labor
description: Funds workplace safety and health inspections, standard-setting, whistleblower protections, compliance assistance, and training grants. Created by the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970.
beneficiaries: Workers in private industry and some public sector; employers through compliance assistance

name: Child Nutrition Programs, USDA
description: Umbrella account funding the National School Lunch Program, School Breakfast Program, Child and Adult Care Food Program, Summer Food Service, and Fresh Fruit and Vegetable Program through federal reimbursements to schools and institutions. Originally authorized by the National School Lunch Act of 1946.
beneficiaries: Children in schools, child care, and summer programs; low-income families; schools and care institutions

name: WIC Program (Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants, and Children)
description: Provides supplemental foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals to low-income pregnant/postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5. Piloted in 1972, made permanent in 1975.
beneficiaries: Low-income pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children under age 5 (approximately 6 million participants)

name: National School Lunch Program
description: Provides free and reduced-price lunches to eligible children in participating schools and residential child care institutions through per-meal federal reimbursements and commodity donations. The largest child nutrition program, serving about 30 million children daily.
beneficiaries: School-age children, particularly low-income students qualifying for free or reduced-price meals

name: School Breakfast Program
description: Provides federal reimbursement for breakfast served in schools, with higher rates for free and reduced-price meals for low-income students. Piloted in 1966, made permanent in 1975, serves approximately 15 million children daily.
beneficiaries: School-age children, especially low-income students; schools in low-income areas with severe need

name: Wildland Fire Management, Forest Service
description: Funds wildfire suppression, fuels management, prescribed burning, preparedness, and post-fire rehabilitation on National Forest System lands and adjacent areas. One of the largest and most volatile USDA accounts due to fire season costs.
beneficiaries: Communities in the wildland-urban interface; national forests; firefighters; affected ecosystems

name: Management of Lands and Resources, BLM
description: Primary operating account for the Bureau of Land Management funding administration of 245 million acres of public lands including grazing, energy/mineral leasing, recreation, wild horse management, and resource protection.
beneficiaries: Ranchers, energy companies, recreationists, and communities near BLM-managed public lands

name: Resource Management, Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Primary operating account for the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service funding management of the National Wildlife Refuge System, endangered species conservation, migratory bird management, fisheries, and law enforcement.
beneficiaries: Wildlife species, hunters, anglers, wildlife watchers, and the general public

name: Operation of the National Park System, NPS
description: Primary operating account for the National Park Service funding daily operations, maintenance, resource protection, visitor services, and law enforcement across 400+ national park units. NPS was established by the Organic Act of 1916.
beneficiaries: Park visitors (300+ million annually), local gateway communities, cultural and natural heritage

name: NOAA Operations, Research, and Facilities
description: Primary operating account for NOAA funding the National Weather Service, fisheries management, ocean and coastal research, satellite operations, and marine conservation. Supports weather forecasting, climate science, and marine stewardship.
beneficiaries: General public (weather/climate services), commercial fishermen, mariners, coastal communities, aviation

name: Census Bureau Operations
description: Funds the Census Bureau's periodic and current surveys, the decennial census, the American Community Survey, and economic censuses that provide essential data for congressional apportionment and federal fund allocation.
beneficiaries: Congress, federal/state/local governments, businesses, researchers, and the public through demographic and economic data

name: Patent and Trademark Office
description: Almost entirely fee-funded agency that examines and grants patents and registers trademarks, protecting intellectual property rights and promoting innovation. Operates as a performance-based organization within the Department of Commerce.
beneficiaries: Inventors, businesses, trademark holders, and the innovation economy broadly

name: Bonneville Power Administration
