description: EPA's primary operating account funding regulatory and compliance programs across air quality, water quality, hazardous waste management, pesticides, toxic substances, environmental justice, and enforcement activities. EPA was created in 1970.
beneficiaries: General public (environmental and health protection), regulated industries, state environmental agencies, environmental justice communities

name: National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute, National Institutes of Health, HHS
description: Funds research on cardiovascular, pulmonary, blood, and sleep disorders, and manages the nation's blood supply research programs. Originally the National Heart Institute (1948), expanded to cover lung and blood diseases in 1972.
beneficiaries: Patients with heart disease, lung disease, blood disorders, and sleep disorders; cardiovascular researchers; clinical trial participants

name: Water and Related Resources, Bureau of Reclamation, Interior
description: Funds the Bureau of Reclamation's water infrastructure projects in the 17 western states, including dam and reservoir operations, water delivery systems, drought contingency, desalination, water recycling, and rural water projects. Reclamation was established by the Reclamation Act of 1902.
beneficiaries: Western farmers and irrigation districts, municipal water users, hydroelectric power consumers, tribal water settlements, endangered species

name: Working Capital Fund, International Cooperative Administrative Support Services, State
description: A revolving fund that provides shared administrative support services (such as facility maintenance, IT, shipping, local staff management) at U.S. embassies overseas, with costs shared among all U.S. government agencies present at each post.
beneficiaries: U.S. government agencies with overseas presence (State, USAID, DoD, Commerce, etc.), embassy staff

name: Homeless Assistance Grants, Community Planning and Development, HUD
description: Funds competitive and formula grants for homeless assistance including the Continuum of Care program, Emergency Solutions Grants, and rural homelessness programs. Provides funding for shelters, transitional housing, permanent supportive housing, and homelessness prevention. Originated under the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act of 1987.
beneficiaries: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness, homeless service providers, Continuums of Care, local governments

name: Special Benefits, Office of Workers Compensation Program, Labor
description: Provides workers' compensation benefits to federal civilian employees and certain other workers injured on the job, covering medical treatment, wage replacement, vocational rehabilitation, and death benefits under the Federal Employees' Compensation Act (FECA).
beneficiaries: Injured federal civilian employees, surviving dependents of federal workers killed on duty, postal workers

name: Employees' Life Insurance Fund, Office of Personnel Management
description: Operates the Federal Employees' Group Life Insurance (FEGLI) program, providing group term life insurance coverage to approximately 4 million federal employees and retirees. Established by the Federal Employees' Group Life Insurance Act of 1954.
beneficiaries: Federal employees and retirees enrolled in FEGLI, their designated beneficiaries and survivors

name: Payments to States from Receipts Under Mineral Leasing, Public and Acquired Military Lands, Office of Natural Resources Revenue, Interior
description: Distributes to states their statutory share (typically 50%) of revenues collected from mineral extraction (oil, gas, coal, etc.) on federal and acquired military lands within their borders. Mandated by the Mineral Leasing Act of 1920.
beneficiaries: State governments (especially Wyoming, New Mexico, Colorado, Utah, North Dakota), schools, and roads funded by mineral revenues

name: Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund - Treasury Managed, HHS
description: A Treasury-managed component of the Medicare Part A (Hospital Insurance) trust fund, handling interest earnings, investment management, and certain administrative transfers for the HI trust fund established by Medicare legislation in 1965.
beneficiaries: Medicare Part A beneficiaries (those 65+ and certain disabled), hospitals, skilled nursing facilities

name: Capital Investments Grants, Federal Transit Administration, Transportation
description: Provides competitive federal funding for major transit capital investments including new fixed guideway systems (light rail, heavy rail, bus rapid transit), extensions, and core capacity improvements in existing systems. Known as the New Starts/Small Starts/Core Capacity program.
beneficiaries: Transit agencies building new rail and BRT systems, commuters in metropolitan areas, construction firms

name: Embassy Security, Construction, and Maintenance, State
description: Funds the construction, acquisition, maintenance, and security upgrades of U.S. diplomatic facilities worldwide. Created in response to the 1998 embassy bombings in Kenya and Tanzania that highlighted the need for more secure diplomatic compounds.
beneficiaries: State Department employees overseas, U.S. diplomatic missions, construction and security contractors

name: Child Care Entitlement to States, Administration for Children and Families, HHS
description: Provides mandatory/entitlement funding to states for child care assistance to low-income families, particularly those transitioning from welfare to work. Created under the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act of 1996 (welfare reform).
beneficiaries: Low-income families receiving or transitioning off TANF, working-poor families, child care providers

name: Higher Education, Department of Education
description: Funds federal higher education programs including TRIO (upward bound, talent search, student support services), GEAR UP, Title III/V institutional aid to minority-serving institutions (HBCUs, HSIs, Tribal Colleges), and international education. Authorized under the Higher Education Act of 1965.
beneficiaries: Low-income and first-generation college students, HBCUs, Hispanic-Serving Institutions, Tribal Colleges, TRIO/GEAR UP participants

name: Bonneville Power Administration Fund, Power Marketing Administration, Energy
description: Operates the Bonneville Power Administration, which markets wholesale electric power from 31 federal hydroelectric dams on the Columbia River system in the Pacific Northwest, plus transmission over 15,000 miles of high-voltage lines.
beneficiaries: Pacific Northwest electric utilities, industrial customers, ratepayers in OR/WA/ID/MT, salmon recovery programs

name: SPR Petroleum Account, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds the operation, maintenance, and petroleum acquisition for the Strategic Petroleum Reserve, the world's largest government-owned emergency crude oil stockpile stored in underground salt caverns along the Gulf Coast. Created by the Energy Policy and Conservation Act of 1975.
beneficiaries: U.S. consumers and economy (energy security), oil markets during supply disruptions, Gulf Coast storage facility workers

name: Grants-In-Aid for Airports, Liquidation of Contract Authorization, Airport and Airway Trust Fund, FAA, Transportation
description: Provides federal grants to public-use airports for planning, construction, and improvement of runways, taxiways, terminals, and safety/security infrastructure through the Airport Improvement Program (AIP). Funded by the Airport and Airway Trust Fund from aviation excise taxes.
beneficiaries: Public-use airports (especially small and rural), airport operators, airline passengers, general aviation users

name: Safety, Security and Missions Services, National Aeronautics and Space Administration
description: Duplicate entry (slight naming variation) for NASA's institutional and mission support account. Covers center operations, environmental remediation, security, agency IT, and construction of facilities across NASA's 10 field centers.
beneficiaries: NASA centers and employees, facility maintenance and construction contractors

name: Capital Investment Grants, Federal Transit Administration, Transportation
description: Duplicate entry (slight naming variation) for FTA capital investment grants. Supports the project development pipeline for major transit projects through engineering, final design, and full funding grant agreement stages.
beneficiaries: Transit agencies, metropolitan commuters, communities along new transit corridors

name: Hazardous Substance Superfund - Treasury Managed, Environmental Protection Agency
description: Funds the cleanup of the nation's most contaminated hazardous waste sites (Superfund sites) on the National Priorities List. Created by CERCLA (Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act) of 1980, commonly known as Superfund.
beneficiaries: Communities near contaminated sites, environmental remediation contractors, state environmental agencies, public health (reduced toxic exposure)

name: Facilities and Equipment, Airport and Airway Trust Fund, FAA, Transportation
description: Funds FAA capital investments in the air traffic control system including radar and surveillance equipment, navigation aids, communication systems, weather systems, automation, and NextGen air traffic modernization technologies.
beneficiaries: Airlines, general aviation, air traffic controllers, airline passengers, aviation safety

name: National Institute of General Medical Sciences, National Institutes of Health, HHS
description: Funds basic biomedical research that is not targeted at specific diseases, including cell biology, genetics, biophysics, pharmacology, and computational biology. Also administers the IDeA program supporting research in states with historically low NIH funding.
beneficiaries: Basic science researchers at universities, graduate students and postdocs, IDeA-eligible states (primarily rural and underserved)

name: United States Mint Public Enterprise Fund, Treasury
description: Operates the U.S. Mint as a public enterprise revolving fund, covering all costs of manufacturing and distributing circulating coins, bullion coins (American Eagle, Buffalo), numismatic products, and Congressional gold medals. Established as a PEF by the United States Mint Reauthorization Act of 1995.
beneficiaries: Federal Reserve Banks (circulating coin supply), coin collectors, bullion investors, the general public

name: Interior Franchise Fund, Departmental Management
description: A revolving fund that provides shared administrative services (IT, financial management, acquisition, HR) to bureaus and offices within the Department of the Interior on a fee-for-service basis. Created under the Government Management Reform Act authority for franchise fund pilots.
beneficiaries: Department of Interior bureaus (BLM, NPS, FWS, USGS, BOR, BIA), and other federal agencies purchasing shared services

name: Program Management, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, Health and Human Services
description: Funds the administrative operations of CMS, including program integrity, claims processing, quality oversight, and contractor management for Medicare and Medicaid. Supports the agency's ability to administer health coverage for over 150 million Americans.
beneficiaries: CMS staff, Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries (seniors, disabled, low-income), Medicare Administrative Contractors, healthcare providers subject to CMS oversight

name: Operations and Support, United States Secret Service, Homeland Security
description: Funds the Secret Service's dual mission of protecting the President, Vice President, and other dignitaries, as well as investigating financial crimes including counterfeiting, cyber fraud, and identity theft. The account was restructured when Secret Service moved to DHS in 2003.
beneficiaries: Protected officials (President, VP, former presidents, candidates, foreign dignitaries), the general public (financial crime prevention), Secret Service agents and staff

name: Indian Health Services, Indian Health Service, Health and Human Services
description: Funds direct health care services for American Indians and Alaska Natives through a network of hospitals, clinics, and health stations operated by the Indian Health Service. Established by the Indian Health Care Transfer Act of 1954, fulfilling the federal government's trust responsibility to tribal nations.
beneficiaries: Approximately 2.6 million American Indians and Alaska Natives from 574 federally recognized tribes, IHS clinical staff, tribal health programs

name: Nuclear Energy, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds research, development, and demonstration of advanced nuclear energy technologies, including advanced reactor designs, nuclear fuel cycle research, and existing fleet sustainability. Supports DOE's mission to maintain nuclear power as a carbon-free energy source.
beneficiaries: National laboratories (INL, ANL, ORNL), nuclear energy industry, universities conducting nuclear research, utility companies operating nuclear plants

name: Operation of the National Park System, National Park Service, Interior
description: Funds the day-to-day operations of the 400+ units of the National Park System, including resource protection, visitor services, facility maintenance, law enforcement, and interpretation programs. The National Park Service was established by the Organic Act of 1916.
beneficiaries: Park visitors (over 300 million annually), NPS rangers and staff, local gateway communities, wildlife and natural/cultural resources

name: Homeless Assistance Grants, Community Planning and Development, Housing and Urban Development
description: Funds the Continuum of Care program, Emergency Solutions Grants, and other HUD programs to prevent and end homelessness through shelter, transitional housing, permanent supportive housing, and rapid re-housing. Originally established under the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act of 1987.
beneficiaries: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness, veterans, chronically homeless populations, local Continuum of Care organizations, homeless service providers and shelters

name: Public Health and Social Services Emergency Fund, Office of the Secretary, Health and Human Services
description: Provides flexible funding for HHS to prepare for and respond to public health emergencies including pandemics, bioterrorism, and natural disasters. Supports the Biomedical Advanced Research and Development Authority (BARDA), the Strategic National Stockpile, and pandemic preparedness activities.
beneficiaries: General public (pandemic/emergency preparedness), hospitals, state and local health departments, pharmaceutical and biotech companies developing medical countermeasures

name: Mental Health, Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Funds programs to prevent and treat mental illness and substance use disorders, including the Community Mental Health Services Block Grant, the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline, and programs addressing serious mental illness. SAMHSA was established in 1992 under the ADAMHA Reorganization Act.
beneficiaries: Individuals with mental health and substance use disorders, community mental health centers, state mental health authorities, crisis intervention services, children and adolescents with serious emotional disturbance

name: Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund - Treasury Managed, Corps of Engineers - Civil
description: Funded by the Harbor Maintenance Tax (a per-value ad valorem tax on commercial cargo at U.S. ports), this trust fund supports the maintenance dredging, jetty repair, and upkeep of federal navigation channels and harbors. Created by the Water Resources Development Act of 1986.
beneficiaries: Commercial shipping companies, port authorities, coastal communities, importers/exporters, U.S. Army Corps of Engineers

name: Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Contribution, Army
description: The Army's annual contribution to the Department of Defense Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund (MERHCF), which pre-funds the health care costs of military retirees and their dependents who are eligible for Medicare. Established by the National Defense Authorization Act for FY2001.
beneficiaries: Retired Army service members and their dependents who are Medicare-eligible (age 65+), TRICARE for Life participants

name: Bureau of Engraving and Printing Fund, Treasury
description: A revolving fund that finances the Bureau of Engraving and Printing's operations in designing and manufacturing U.S. currency (Federal Reserve Notes), security documents, and other government printed materials. BEP recoups costs by selling its products to the Federal Reserve and other agencies.
beneficiaries: Federal Reserve System (primary customer), the general public (currency users), BEP employees at Washington DC and Fort Worth facilities

name: Salaries and Expenses, United States Attorneys, Justice
description: Funds the 94 U.S. Attorney offices across the country, which serve as the principal litigators of the federal government, prosecuting federal crimes, defending the government in civil cases, and collecting debts owed to the United States. The offices were formalized under the Department of Justice Act of 1870.
beneficiaries: Federal prosecutors (AUSAs), victims of federal crimes, the federal judiciary system, communities benefiting from federal law enforcement

name: National Institute of Neurological Disorders and Stroke, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Funds biomedical research on brain and nervous system disorders including stroke, epilepsy, Parkinson's disease, Alzheimer's disease, traumatic brain injury, and spinal cord injury. NINDS is the leading federal funder of neurological research, established in 1950.
beneficiaries: Patients with neurological disorders, neuroscience researchers, academic medical centers, pharmaceutical companies developing neurological therapies

name: Office of the Director, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Funds the central leadership and coordination activities of NIH, including the Common Fund for trans-NIH strategic initiatives, the All of Us precision medicine research program, and other cross-cutting research priorities. The OD sets overall NIH policy and direction.
beneficiaries: Biomedical researchers across all disciplines, All of Us research participants, the scientific community broadly, patients benefiting from cross-cutting research initiatives

name: Service and Supply Fund, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: A revolving fund that provides centralized administrative and support services to NIH institutes and centers on a reimbursable basis, including facilities management, IT services, procurement, and research support. Operates on a fee-for-service model.
beneficiaries: NIH institutes and centers, NIH researchers, contractors providing support services to the NIH campus

name: Energy Employees Occupational Illness Compensation Fund, Labor
description: Provides lump-sum compensation and medical benefits to current and former Department of Energy employees, contractors, and subcontractors (or their survivors) who became ill due to exposure to radiation, beryllium, or silica during nuclear weapons production. Created by the Energy Employees Occupational Illness Compensation Program Act of 2000.
beneficiaries: Former nuclear weapons production workers and uranium miners with occupational illnesses, their survivors, DOE contractor employees

name: Salaries and Expenses, Drug Enforcement Administration, Justice
description: Funds the DEA's operations in enforcing controlled substance laws and regulations, investigating drug trafficking organizations, managing the diversion control program, and supporting international counter-narcotics efforts. The DEA was established by executive order in 1973.
beneficiaries: DEA special agents and staff, communities affected by drug trafficking, law enforcement partners, the criminal justice system

name: National Forest System, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Funds the management, protection, and stewardship of 193 million acres of national forests and grasslands, including recreation management, wildlife habitat, timber sales, watershed protection, and road maintenance. The Forest Service was established within USDA in 1905.
beneficiaries: Recreational users, rural communities, timber industry, wildlife, watershed-dependent communities, Forest Service employees (rangers, firefighters)

name: Aging and Disability Services Programs, Administration for Community Living, Health and Human Services
description: Funds programs under the Older Americans Act and other authorities that support older adults and people with disabilities to live independently in their communities, including home-delivered meals (Meals on Wheels), senior centers, caregiver support, and disability rights protection. ACL was created in 2012 by consolidating several HHS offices.
beneficiaries: Older adults (especially those 60+), people with disabilities, family caregivers, Area Agencies on Aging, Meals on Wheels providers, senior centers, independent living centers

name: Defense Nuclear Nonproliferation, National Nuclear Security Administration, Energy
description: Funds programs to detect, prevent, and reverse the proliferation of nuclear weapons and materials worldwide, including securing vulnerable nuclear materials, monitoring nuclear detonations, converting research reactors from highly enriched uranium, and disposing of surplus weapons-grade plutonium. Part of NNSA established in 2000.
beneficiaries: Global security stakeholders, the IAEA, partner countries with nuclear materials, national laboratories (PNNL, SNL, LANL), the general public (nuclear threat reduction)

name: Ryan White HIV/AIDS Program, Health Resources and Services Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Funds a comprehensive system of HIV primary medical care, medications (AIDS Drug Assistance Program), and support services for low-income, uninsured, and underserved people living with HIV. Named after Ryan White and authorized by the Ryan White CARE Act of 1990.
beneficiaries: People living with HIV/AIDS who lack adequate health coverage (approximately 500,000+ annually), HIV/AIDS clinics, community health centers, ADAP participants

name: Health Care Fraud and Abuse Control Account, Health and Human Services
description: Funds coordinated federal, state, and local efforts to combat fraud, waste, and abuse in the Medicare and Medicaid programs, including the HHS Office of Inspector General's investigative activities and the Medicare Fraud Strike Force. Established by the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) of 1996.
beneficiaries: Medicare and Medicaid programs (cost savings), taxpayers, legitimate healthcare providers, Medicare beneficiaries, DOJ and HHS-OIG investigators

name: Research, Development, and Procurement, Administration for Strategic Preparedness and Response, Health and Human Services
description: Funds the development, procurement, and stockpiling of medical countermeasures (vaccines, therapeutics, diagnostics) for public health emergencies and national security threats. ASPR was elevated to an operating division within HHS in 2022, consolidating BARDA and Strategic National Stockpile functions.
beneficiaries: The general public (emergency preparedness), pharmaceutical and biotech companies developing countermeasures, hospitals, state health departments

name: Rural Electrification and Telecommunications Loans Program Account, Rural Utilities Service, Agriculture
description: Provides direct and guaranteed loans to rural electric cooperatives, telecommunications companies, and broadband providers to finance infrastructure in rural areas. Originally established by the Rural Electrification Act of 1936 to bring electricity to rural America.
beneficiaries: Rural electric cooperatives, rural telecommunications providers, rural broadband providers, rural residents and businesses lacking utility infrastructure

name: Securities and Exchange Commission
description: Funds the SEC's operations in regulating securities markets, protecting investors, enforcing securities laws, and overseeing public company disclosures, broker-dealers, investment advisers, and stock exchanges. Created by the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 in response to the 1929 stock market crash.
beneficiaries: Investors (retail and institutional), publicly traded companies, securities markets, broker-dealers, investment advisers, the financial system broadly

name: Payment Where American Opportunity Credit Exceeds Liability for Tax - Recovery Act, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds the refundable portion of the American Opportunity Tax Credit (AOTC) that exceeds a taxpayer's income tax liability, providing up to $1,000 in refundable credits for qualifying higher education expenses. Originally enacted as part of the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 and later made permanent.
beneficiaries: College students and their families (especially low- and moderate-income), postsecondary education institutions

name: National Institute of Diabetes and Digestive and Kidney Diseases, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Funds research on diabetes, endocrine and metabolic diseases, digestive diseases, nutritional disorders, kidney and urologic diseases, and hematologic diseases. NIDDK supports major clinical research networks and is a leading funder of type 1 and type 2 diabetes research.
beneficiaries: Patients with diabetes, kidney disease, obesity, and digestive disorders; biomedical researchers; academic medical centers; clinical trial participants

name: Salaries and Expenses, Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service, Agriculture
description: Funds APHIS operations in protecting U.S. agriculture from pests and diseases, including animal health surveillance, plant pest and disease prevention, wildlife damage management, veterinary diagnostics, and regulation of genetically engineered organisms. APHIS was established as a separate USDA agency in 1972.
beneficiaries: Farmers, ranchers, and agricultural producers; the livestock industry; international trade partners; wildlife; consumers (food safety)

name: CDC-Wide Activities and Program Support, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Health and Human Services
description: Funds cross-cutting public health activities at CDC including public health preparedness, global health security, health statistics and surveillance, laboratory science, and agency-wide program support. Provides the CDC Director flexibility for emerging health threats and core infrastructure.
beneficiaries: The general public (disease prevention), state and local health departments, global health partners, epidemiologists and public health researchers

name: Procurement, Acquisition and Construction, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, Commerce
description: Funds NOAA's major capital investments including weather satellites (GOES, JPSS), research vessels, weather radar systems, supercomputers for weather modeling, and facility construction. Supports the infrastructure that underpins weather forecasting and ocean observation.
beneficiaries: The general public (weather forecasting), aviation and maritime industries, farmers, emergency managers, NOAA scientists, satellite industry contractors

name: Victims Compensation Fund, General Legal Activities, Justice
description: Provides compensation to individuals (or their families) who suffered physical harm or death as a result of the September 11, 2001 terrorist attacks or from exposure to debris and toxins at crash sites. Reauthorized and extended by the Never Forget the Heroes Act of 2019 through 2090.
beneficiaries: 9/11 survivors, first responders (FDNY, NYPD, Port Authority), recovery workers, residents and workers in the exposure zones, families of those killed

name: Operations and Support, Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, Homeland Security
description: Funds CISA's mission to protect the nation's critical infrastructure and federal civilian networks from cyber threats, coordinate national cybersecurity efforts, and secure elections infrastructure. CISA was established as a standalone agency within DHS by the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency Act of 2018.
beneficiaries: Federal civilian agencies, critical infrastructure operators (energy, water, healthcare), state and local governments (election security), private sector cybersecurity partners

name: Surveys, Investigations and Research, U.S. Geological Survey
description: Funds USGS's scientific research and monitoring programs including earthquake and volcano monitoring, water resources assessment, biological surveys, mapping and geospatial data, mineral resources, and natural hazards research. USGS was established by Congress in 1879.
beneficiaries: The general public (natural hazard warnings), scientists and researchers, land and water managers, emergency managers, mining and energy industries, state geological surveys

name: Student and Administration, Education
description: Funds the Department of Education's administrative costs for managing federal student aid programs (the largest source of student financial aid in the country), including loan servicing, program oversight, financial management, and the Federal Student Aid office operations.
beneficiaries: Student loan borrowers, college students receiving financial aid, Federal Student Aid (FSA) staff, loan servicers

name: Food for Peace Title II Grants, Foreign Agricultural Service, Agriculture
description: Provides U.S. agricultural commodities as food aid to developing countries for emergency relief and development programs, administered primarily through USAID. Established by the Agricultural Trade Development and Assistance Act of 1954 (P.L. 480).
beneficiaries: Populations in food-insecure developing countries, humanitarian organizations (WFP, CARE, Catholic Relief Services), U.S. agricultural producers (commodity procurement)

name: Northeast Corridor Grants to the National Railroad Passenger Corporation, Transportation
description: Provides federal grants specifically for capital improvements, maintenance, and operations on Amtrak's Northeast Corridor (Boston-New York-Washington), the most heavily used passenger rail line in the country. Authorized under the Passenger Rail Investment and Improvement Act of 2008.
beneficiaries: Amtrak NEC riders, commuter rail agencies using the corridor (NJ Transit, MARC, SEPTA, Metro-North), Northeast Corridor communities, Amtrak employees

name: State and Private Forestry, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Funds Forest Service programs that provide financial and technical assistance to state forestry agencies, tribal governments, and private landowners for forest stewardship, urban forestry, wildfire prevention, forest health monitoring, and community forestry. Supports cooperative programs beyond National Forest lands.
beneficiaries: State forestry agencies, private forest landowners, tribal forest managers, urban communities (urban forestry), rural fire departments (volunteer fire assistance)

name: National Institute of Mental Health, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Funds research to understand, treat, and prevent mental disorders including depression, schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, anxiety disorders, autism spectrum disorder, and PTSD. NIMH is the lead federal agency for research on mental illness.
beneficiaries: People with mental health disorders, psychiatric researchers, clinical trial participants, academic institutions, mental health clinicians

name: Operation of Indian Programs, Bureau of Indian Affairs and Bureau of Indian Education, Interior
description: Funds the Bureau of Indian Affairs' responsibilities to federally recognized tribes including tribal government support, natural resource management, law enforcement, social services, and economic development on Indian reservations. BIA is one of the oldest federal agencies, established in 1824.
beneficiaries: Members of 574 federally recognized tribes, tribal governments, tribal courts, BIA law enforcement, Indian communities on reservations

name: Indian Health Facilities, Indian Health Service, Health and Human Services
description: Funds the construction, renovation, maintenance, and improvement of Indian Health Service hospitals, clinics, health stations, and staff quarters serving American Indian and Alaska Native communities. Also supports sanitation facilities (water and sewer) for tribal communities.
beneficiaries: American Indians and Alaska Natives, tribal health programs, IHS hospitals and clinics, tribal communities lacking water and sanitation infrastructure

name: Flood Control and Coastal Emergencies, Corps of Engineers, Civil
description: Funds the Army Corps of Engineers' emergency response to floods and coastal storms, including emergency levee repairs, sandbagging operations, pumping, and post-disaster rehabilitation of flood control works. Activated when the President declares a flood emergency.
beneficiaries: Communities in flood zones, property owners protected by federal levees and flood control structures, state and local emergency managers

name: Working Capital Fund, Justice
description: A revolving fund providing centralized administrative services to DOJ components on a reimbursable basis, including IT infrastructure, telecommunications, financial management systems, building operations, and shared litigation support. Achieves economies of scale across DOJ.
beneficiaries: DOJ components (FBI, DEA, ATF, BOP, USMS, EOUSA), DOJ employees, IT service contractors

name: Supply Fund, Veterans Affairs
description: A revolving fund that provides centralized supply chain, logistics, and procurement services for the Department of Veterans Affairs, including medical supplies, pharmaceuticals, equipment, and prosthetics distribution to VA medical centers and facilities.
beneficiaries: VA medical centers, veteran patients, VA healthcare staff, medical supply and pharmaceutical vendors

name: Career, Technical, and Adult Education, Education
description: Funds career and technical education (CTE) programs in secondary and postsecondary institutions through the Carl D. Perkins Act, as well as adult education and literacy programs. The federal role in vocational education dates to the Smith-Hughes Act of 1917.
beneficiaries: High school and community college CTE students, adult learners, workers seeking skills upgrades, state CTE agencies, community colleges, career centers

name: Federal Prisoner Detention, United States Marshals Service, Justice
description: Funds the housing, transportation, and care of federal prisoners and detainees in the custody of the U.S. Marshals Service prior to sentencing, including costs for contract detention facilities, state and local jail agreements, and medical care. The USMS is the nation's oldest federal law enforcement agency.
beneficiaries: Federal pretrial detainees, the U.S. Marshals Service, private detention facility operators, state and local jails holding federal detainees

name: Federal Prisoner Detention, Justice
description: The broader DOJ account for federal prisoner detention costs covering pre-trial and pre-sentencing custody of individuals in the federal criminal justice system. Funds contract facilities and intergovernmental agreements for detention services.
beneficiaries: Federal detainees awaiting trial or sentencing, the federal judiciary, detention facility operators, USMS

name: Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Contribution, Navy
description: The Navy's annual accrual payment to the DoD Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund, pre-funding the future health care costs of Navy and Marine Corps retirees and dependents who will become Medicare-eligible. Established by the FY2001 NDAA.
beneficiaries: Retired Navy and Marine Corps personnel and their dependents who are Medicare-eligible, TRICARE for Life beneficiaries

name: Build America Bond Payments - Recovery Act, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds the federal subsidy payments to state and local governments that issued Build America Bonds under the 2009 Recovery Act, where the Treasury makes direct payments equal to 35% of interest costs on these taxable municipal bonds. The program ran from April 2009 to December 2010 but payments continue for the life of outstanding bonds.
beneficiaries: State and local governments that issued BABs, municipal bond investors, taxpayers in issuing jurisdictions (lower borrowing costs for infrastructure)

name: Resource Management, United States Fish and Wildlife Service, Interior
description: Funds the FWS's core operations including management of the National Wildlife Refuge System (560+ refuges), endangered species conservation, migratory bird management, fisheries restoration, habitat conservation, and law enforcement against wildlife trafficking. FWS was established in its current form in 1940.
beneficiaries: Wildlife and endangered species, refuge visitors, hunters and anglers, state wildlife agencies, conservation organizations, migratory bird populations

name: Transit Infrastructure Grants, Federal Transit Administration, Transportation
description: Funds capital investments in public transit systems including bus and rail fleet replacement, transit facility construction and modernization, new fixed-guideway projects (light rail, BRT), and state of good repair. Rooted in the Urban Mass Transportation Act of 1964.
beneficiaries: Public transit agencies, transit riders (especially low-income and urban commuters), metropolitan areas, transit equipment manufacturers

name: National Infrastructure Investments, Office of the Secretary, Transportation
description: The competitive discretionary grant program originally known as TIGER grants (now RAISE grants - Rebuilding American Infrastructure with Sustainability and Equity), funding multimodal surface transportation projects of national or regional significance. Created in the 2009 Recovery Act.
beneficiaries: State and local governments, metropolitan planning organizations, transit agencies, port authorities, rural communities with significant transportation needs

name: State and Local Law Enforcement Assistance, Office of Justice Programs, Justice
description: Provides federal grants to state, local, and tribal law enforcement and criminal justice agencies for a range of programs including the Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grants, violence reduction initiatives, body-worn camera programs, and forensic science improvement. Originated with the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968.
beneficiaries: State and local police departments, prosecutors, courts, corrections agencies, crime victims, tribal law enforcement, community-based violence intervention programs

name: Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Contribution, Air Force
description: The Air Force's annual accrual contribution to the DoD Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund, pre-funding health care obligations for retired Air Force personnel and their Medicare-eligible dependents under TRICARE for Life.
beneficiaries: Retired Air Force service members and their dependents eligible for Medicare, TRICARE for Life participants

name: Franchise Fund, Veterans Affairs
description: A revolving fund providing shared administrative services to VA components on a fee-for-service basis, including financial management, human resources, IT services, and debt management. Created under the Government Management Reform Act franchise fund pilot authority.
beneficiaries: VA offices and administrations (VHA, VBA, NCA), VA employees, other federal agencies purchasing VA shared services

name: Operations and Support, Management Directorate, Departmental Management, Intelligence, Situational Awareness, and Oversight, Homeland Security
description: Funds DHS headquarters management and oversight functions including the Office of Intelligence and Analysis, departmental operations coordination, financial management, procurement, human capital, and the DHS Chief Information Officer. Supports the department-wide management backbone.
beneficiaries: DHS component agencies, DHS workforce, intelligence community partners, federal cybersecurity coordination

name: Airport Infrastructure Grants, Federal Aviation Administration, Transportation
description: Provides federal grants to airports (through the Airport Improvement Program and supplemental programs) for runway, taxiway, and terminal construction and improvement, safety and security enhancements, and noise mitigation. Originally rooted in the Federal Airport Act of 1946.
beneficiaries: Public-use airports (commercial and general aviation), airline passengers, airport authorities, surrounding communities (noise mitigation), aviation safety

name: Salaries and Expenses, Agricultural Research Service, Agriculture
description: Funds the USDA's primary intramural scientific research agency, operating over 90 research locations conducting studies on crop and animal production, food safety, nutrition, natural resource conservation, and agricultural sustainability. ARS was established in 1953.
beneficiaries: Farmers and ranchers, the food industry, consumers (food safety and nutrition), agricultural scientists, plant and animal breeders

name: Veterans Housing Benefit Program Fund, Veterans Benefits Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds the VA Home Loan Guaranty program which guarantees a portion of home mortgage loans made by private lenders to eligible veterans, active duty service members, and surviving spouses, enabling home purchases with no down payment and no private mortgage insurance. Established by the Servicemen's Readjustment Act (GI Bill) of 1944.
beneficiaries: Veterans and active duty service members purchasing homes, surviving spouses, private mortgage lenders, the housing market

name: Clean Vehicle Credit, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds the refundable tax credit for purchases of new and used clean vehicles (electric vehicles, plug-in hybrids, and fuel cell vehicles) as established by the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022, providing up to $7,500 for new and $4,000 for used clean vehicles meeting domestic content requirements.
beneficiaries: Consumers purchasing qualifying electric and clean vehicles, EV manufacturers and dealers, domestic battery and critical mineral supply chain

name: Federal Protective Service, Management Directorate, Departmental Management, Intelligence, Situational Awareness, and Oversight, Homeland Security
description: Funds the Federal Protective Service, which provides law enforcement and security services for over 9,000 federal buildings and facilities, including contract guard forces, criminal investigations, and risk assessments. FPS is funded through security fees charged to tenant federal agencies.
beneficiaries: Federal employees working in government buildings, federal agencies (as tenants), visitors to federal facilities, contract security guard force

name: International Narcotics Control and Law Enforcement, International Security Assistance, State
description: Funds U.S. assistance to foreign countries for counter-narcotics operations, law enforcement capacity building, judicial reform, anti-corruption programs, and rule of law development. Major recipients include countries in Latin America (Plan Colombia successor programs), Afghanistan, and other drug-source/transit countries.
beneficiaries: Partner country law enforcement and judicial systems, populations in drug-source countries, U.S. counter-narcotics interests, INL-funded implementing organizations

name: U.S. International Development Finance Corporation
description: Funds America's development finance institution, which provides loans, guarantees, equity investments, political risk insurance, and technical assistance to private sector projects in developing countries. Created by the BUILD Act of 2018 (operational 2019), consolidating OPIC and USAID's Development Credit Authority.
beneficiaries: Private sector companies investing in developing countries, populations in low- and middle-income countries, U.S. foreign policy and development interests

name: Operating Expenses of the United States Agency for International Development, Funds Appropriated to the President
description: Funds USAID's administrative and operational costs, including staff salaries, overseas mission operations, IT systems, security, and the infrastructure needed to manage U.S. foreign development assistance programs worldwide. USAID was established by executive order in 1961.
beneficiaries: USAID staff (domestic and overseas), foreign service officers, USAID missions in developing countries, development program management

name: Wildland Fire Management, Department-Wide Programs, Interior
description: Funds the Department of Interior's wildland fire suppression, hazardous fuels reduction, prescribed burning, and fire preparedness activities across BLM, NPS, FWS, and BIA lands. Restructured after the 2000 National Fire Plan in response to severe wildfire seasons.
beneficiaries: Communities in the wildland-urban interface, Interior agency firefighters, wildlife and ecosystem health, western states affected by wildfire

name: Fossil Energy, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds research and development on fossil energy technologies including carbon capture, utilization, and storage (CCUS), hydrogen from fossil sources, advanced coal and natural gas power systems, and the Strategic Petroleum Reserve operations. Increasingly focused on decarbonization of fossil energy use.
beneficiaries: Fossil energy industry (coal, oil, natural gas), national laboratories, CCUS technology developers, communities dependent on fossil energy, Strategic Petroleum Reserve

name: Naval Reactors, National Nuclear Security Administration, Energy
description: Funds the Naval Nuclear Propulsion Program, which designs, builds, operates, and maintains nuclear propulsion systems for the Navy's aircraft carriers and submarines. Oversees the entire lifecycle of naval nuclear reactors from R&D through decommissioning, with a perfect safety record since Admiral Rickover's leadership.
beneficiaries: U.S. Navy (submarine and carrier fleet), Bettis and Knolls national laboratories, naval shipyards, nuclear-trained Navy personnel

name: Office of Job Corps, Employment and Training Administration, Labor
description: Funds the Job Corps program, which provides free residential education and vocational training to at-risk youth ages 16-24 at over 120 centers nationwide. Established as part of the Economic Opportunity Act of 1964 under the War on Poverty, it is the nation's largest residential career training program for disadvantaged youth.
beneficiaries: At-risk and disadvantaged youth (16-24), Job Corps center operators (both federal and contractor-run), employers hiring Job Corps graduates

name: Contributions for International Peacekeeping Activities, State
description: Funds the United States' assessed contributions to United Nations peacekeeping operations worldwide, covering the U.S. share (approximately 25-27%) of UN peacekeeping budgets for missions in conflict zones. The U.S. is the largest financial contributor to UN peacekeeping.
beneficiaries: Populations in conflict zones with UN peacekeeping missions, the United Nations, U.S. foreign policy interests in international stability

name: Nonrecurring Expense Fund, Office of the Secretary, Health and Human Services
description: Collects unobligated balances from expiring HHS discretionary accounts and makes them available for one-time capital investments in IT modernization, facilities, and infrastructure projects across the department. Created by the Consolidated Appropriations Act of 2008.
beneficiaries: HHS operating divisions, HHS IT systems modernization, federal health program infrastructure

name: Eunice Kennedy Shriver National Institute of Child Health and Human Development, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Funds research on fertility, pregnancy, child development, intellectual and developmental disabilities, and rehabilitation science. NICHD conducts and supports research across the lifespan from conception through adulthood, including maternal health, SIDS, and population dynamics. Established in 1962 at the urging of Eunice Kennedy Shriver.
beneficiaries: Pregnant women, infants and children, people with intellectual and developmental disabilities, fertility patients, population health researchers

name: Refugee and Entrant Assistance, Administration for Children and Families, Health and Human Services
description: Funds the Office of Refugee Resettlement (ORR) which provides transitional assistance to refugees, asylees, Cuban/Haitian entrants, victims of trafficking, and unaccompanied migrant children, including cash assistance, medical screening, social services, and foster care. Authorized by the Refugee Act of 1980.
beneficiaries: Refugees and asylees admitted to the U.S., unaccompanied migrant children, trafficking victims, refugee resettlement agencies (VOLAGS), state refugee coordinators

name: Salaries and Expenses, United States Marshals Service, Justice
description: Funds the operations of the U.S. Marshals Service, the nation's oldest federal law enforcement agency, responsible for fugitive apprehension, court security, witness protection (WITSEC), prisoner transport, asset forfeiture, and serving federal court orders. Established by the Judiciary Act of 1789.
beneficiaries: Federal courts (judicial security), federal judges and prosecutors, witnesses in the WITSEC program, communities (fugitive apprehension), USMS deputies

name: National Institute on Drug Abuse, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Funds research on drug use and addiction, including the neuroscience of substance use disorders, development of addiction treatments and prevention strategies, and the health consequences of drug use including the opioid crisis. NIDA was established in 1974.
beneficiaries: People with substance use disorders, addiction researchers, clinicians treating addiction, communities affected by drug abuse, the opioid crisis response

name: Millennium Challenge Corporation
description: Funds the MCC, an independent U.S. foreign aid agency that provides large, time-limited grants (compacts) to developing countries that meet rigorous eligibility criteria for good governance, economic freedom, and investing in their people. Created by the Millennium Challenge Act of 2003 (operational 2004).
beneficiaries: Developing countries meeting MCC eligibility criteria, populations in compact countries (through infrastructure, agriculture, education investments), U.S. development policy

name: Expenses, Millennium Challenge Corporation
description: The administrative and program expense account for MCC covering compact development, due diligence, monitoring and evaluation, and threshold program implementation in eligible developing countries. Supports MCC's evidence-based approach to development assistance.
beneficiaries: MCC staff, partner country governments, program implementers, populations benefiting from MCC-funded projects

name: Salaries and Expenses, Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives, Justice
description: Funds ATF's operations in regulating the firearms and explosives industries, investigating firearms trafficking, arson, and explosives crimes, and enforcing federal alcohol and tobacco tax laws. ATF became a DOJ law enforcement agency in 2003 (previously Treasury), with roots in the 1972 establishment as a separate bureau.
beneficiaries: Public safety (firearms violence reduction), law enforcement partners, licensed firearms dealers, the explosives industry (regulation), communities affected by gun violence

name: Defense Counterintelligence and Security Agency Working Capital Fund, Defense
description: Funds the Defense Counterintelligence and Security Agency (DCSA), which conducts background investigations for security clearances across the federal government and provides industrial security oversight for classified defense contracts. DCSA was established in 2019 to replace the National Background Investigations Bureau.
beneficiaries: Federal employees and contractors requiring security clearances, DoD and other agencies (investigation services), defense industrial base, national security

name: Primary Health Care, Health Resources and Services Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Funds the Health Center Program (Section 330 of the Public Health Service Act) which supports approximately 1,400 community health centers providing comprehensive primary care to underserved populations regardless of ability to pay. These Federally Qualified Health Centers serve over 30 million patients annually.
beneficiaries: Uninsured and underserved patients, community health centers (FQHCs), rural and urban medically underserved communities, migrant farmworkers, homeless individuals

name: Conservation Operations, Natural Resources Conservation Service, Agriculture
description: Funds NRCS's technical assistance to farmers, ranchers, and private landowners in implementing conservation practices for soil health, water quality, and wildlife habitat on working agricultural lands. NRCS (originally the Soil Conservation Service) was established after the Dust Bowl in 1935.
beneficiaries: Farmers and ranchers, private landowners, soil and water conservation districts, the environment (soil, water, wildlife), rural communities

name: Training and Employment Services, Employment and Training Administration, Labor
description: Funds workforce development programs under the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA), including adult training, dislocated worker assistance, youth employment programs, and the American Job Center network. Supports workers in acquiring skills for in-demand occupations.
beneficiaries: Unemployed and underemployed workers, dislocated workers, youth, American Job Centers (One-Stop Career Centers), state workforce agencies, employers seeking skilled workers

name: Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health-NIH, National Institutes of Health, Department of Health and Human Services
description: Funds ARPA-H, a new agency modeled on DARPA that pursues high-risk, high-reward biomedical and health research breakthroughs that cannot be readily accomplished through traditional NIH funding mechanisms. Focuses on transformative health technologies including cancer detection, mRNA platforms, and precision medicine.
beneficiaries: Patients with difficult-to-treat diseases, biomedical researchers and innovators, biotech startups, academic institutions, the health technology sector

name: Clean Energy Demonstrations, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds large-scale demonstration projects for clean energy technologies including clean hydrogen hubs, carbon capture demonstrations, advanced nuclear, long-duration energy storage, and industrial decarbonization. Established by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (Bipartisan Infrastructure Law) of 2021.
beneficiaries: Clean energy technology developers, communities hosting demonstration projects, national laboratories, energy transition workers, industrial decarbonization efforts

name: Other Defense Activities, Environmental and Other Defense Activities, Energy
description: Funds the cleanup of legacy nuclear weapons production sites and other defense-related environmental remediation managed by DOE's Office of Environmental Management. Addresses radioactive and hazardous waste contamination at sites like Hanford, Savannah River, and Oak Ridge.
beneficiaries: Communities near former nuclear weapons production sites, environmental cleanup workers, environmental remediation contractors, the environment (groundwater, soil)

name: Surveys, Investigations, and Research, Geological Survey
description: A second fiscal year appropriation for USGS scientific activities including earthquake early warning systems, streamflow monitoring, landsat satellite operations, mineral resource assessments, and biological science supporting wildlife management and ecosystem health.
beneficiaries: The public (natural hazard preparedness), water resource managers, scientists, land managers, emergency responders, mining and energy industries

name: Working Capital Fund, Agriculture
description: A revolving fund providing centralized administrative services to USDA agencies on a reimbursable basis, including IT systems (including the USDA data center), financial management, telecommunications, procurement, and human resources support.
beneficiaries: USDA agencies and missions areas, USDA employees, IT service providers, shared services customers

name: Salaries and Expenses, General Legal Activities, Justice
description: Funds the DOJ's litigating divisions including the Civil Division, Civil Rights Division, Criminal Division, Antitrust Division, Tax Division, and Environment and Natural Resources Division, which handle the government's most significant legal matters. Covers attorney salaries, litigation costs, and legal operations.
beneficiaries: The federal government (legal representation), civil rights communities, antitrust enforcement, environmental protection, tax compliance, DOJ attorneys

name: Contribution to the International Development Association, Treasury
description: Funds the U.S. contribution to the International Development Association (IDA), the World Bank's concessional lending arm that provides grants and zero/low-interest loans to the world's poorest countries for poverty reduction and development. IDA was established in 1960; the U.S. is historically its largest contributor.
beneficiaries: The world's 75 poorest countries, populations in IDA-eligible nations (sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia), global development goals

name: Operation of Indian Education Programs, Bureau of Indian Education, Interior
description: Funds the Bureau of Indian Education's operation of approximately 180 schools and dormitories serving Native American students on or near reservations, as well as tribal colleges and universities and Johnson-O'Malley supplemental education programs. Fulfills the federal trust responsibility for Indian education.
beneficiaries: Native American and Alaska Native students (approximately 46,000), BIE-operated and tribally-controlled schools, tribal colleges, families on reservations

name: Veterans Electronic Health Record, Departmental Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds the VA's effort to deploy a modern electronic health record system (Oracle Cerner/now Oracle Health) to replace the legacy VistA system, aiming for interoperability with the Department of Defense's MHS GENESIS system for seamless care transitions between military and veteran status.
beneficiaries: Veterans receiving VA healthcare, VA clinicians and staff, Oracle Health (Cerner) as the contractor, DoD (interoperability)

name: Rental Assistance Program, Rural Housing Service, Agriculture
description: Provides rental subsidies to low-income tenants living in USDA-financed rural multifamily housing projects (Section 521 rental assistance), ensuring that tenants pay no more than 30% of their income toward rent. Administered by the Rural Housing Service within USDA.
beneficiaries: Low-income rural renters, elderly and disabled tenants in rural housing, owners of USDA-financed rural rental properties, rural communities

name: Training and Employment Service
description: Funds job training, employment services, and workforce development programs administered by the Employment and Training Administration under the Department of Labor.
beneficiaries: Unemployed and underemployed workers, dislocated workers, youth, veterans seeking employment assistance

name: Social Services Block Grant (SSBG)
description: Provides flexible federal funding to states for a wide range of social services including child care, protective services, home-based care, and community-based support. Established under Title XX of the Social Security Act.
beneficiaries: Low-income individuals and families, children, elderly, disabled persons

name: Assets Forfeiture Fund
description: Receives proceeds from the forfeiture of assets seized in federal criminal investigations and uses them to fund law enforcement operations, victim restitution, and equitable sharing with state/local agencies.
beneficiaries: Federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies; crime victims receiving restitution

name: Management of Lands and Resources
description: Funds the Bureau of Land Management's core operations including management of 245+ million surface acres of public lands, resource planning, cadastral surveys, and environmental assessments.
beneficiaries: General public, ranchers and grazing permittees, mining claimants, recreation users on public lands

name: Salaries and Expenses, Farm Service Agency
description: Covers administrative costs of the Farm Service Agency including salaries of field office staff who deliver farm commodity, conservation, credit, and disaster assistance programs.
beneficiaries: American farmers and ranchers accessing USDA program services

name: Operations and Support, FEMA
description: Funds FEMA's base operations, staffing, IT infrastructure, preparedness grants management, and readiness for disaster response coordination across the nation.
beneficiaries: General public in disaster-prone areas, state and local emergency management agencies

name: Hermit's Peak/Calf Canyon Fire Assistance Account
description: Created by the Hermit's Peak/Calf Canyon Fire Assistance Act to compensate individuals, businesses, and tribal nations for losses caused by the 2022 prescribed fire that escaped federal control in New Mexico.
beneficiaries: Residents, businesses, tribal communities, and local governments affected by the 2022 Hermit's Peak/Calf Canyon Fire in New Mexico

name: Operation of Indian Programs
description: Funds the Bureau of Indian Affairs' operation of programs for federally recognized tribes including tribal government support, social services, natural resources management, law enforcement, and education.
beneficiaries: Members of 574+ federally recognized American Indian and Alaska Native tribes

name: Abandoned Mine Reclamation Fund
description: Funded by fees on active coal production under the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977, used to reclaim lands and waters damaged by coal mining before 1977.
beneficiaries: Communities near abandoned coal mines, states and tribes with legacy mining environmental damage

name: Agricultural Credit Insurance Fund Program Account
description: Provides direct and guaranteed loans to farmers and ranchers who cannot obtain commercial credit, covering operating expenses, farm ownership, and emergency needs through the Farm Service Agency.
beneficiaries: Beginning, socially disadvantaged, and family farmers and ranchers unable to obtain private credit

name: United States Emergency Refugee and Migration Assistance Fund
description: Presidential emergency fund providing rapid-response humanitarian assistance for unexpected refugee crises worldwide, administered through the State Department.
beneficiaries: Refugees and displaced persons worldwide facing sudden humanitarian emergencies

name: Contributions to International Organizations
description: Pays assessed U.S. membership dues and contributions to international organizations such as the United Nations, NATO, WHO, and other multilateral bodies.
beneficiaries: International organizations; indirectly, global populations benefiting from multilateral cooperation

name: Clean Energy Demonstrations
description: Created by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (Bipartisan Infrastructure Law) to fund large-scale demonstrations of clean energy technologies including clean hydrogen hubs, carbon capture, advanced nuclear, and long-duration energy storage.
beneficiaries: Clean energy technology developers, utilities, communities near demonstration projects, workers in emerging energy sectors

name: Guarantees of Mortgage-Backed Securities Loan Guarantees, Ginnie Mae
description: Funds the Government National Mortgage Association's guarantee of mortgage-backed securities backed by federally insured or guaranteed loans (FHA, VA, USDA), ensuring capital flows into housing finance markets.
beneficiaries: Homebuyers using FHA/VA/USDA loans, mortgage lenders, investors in government-backed MBS

name: Treasury Franchise Fund
description: A revolving fund providing shared administrative services (IT, HR, procurement, financial management) on a fee-for-service basis to Treasury bureaus and other federal agencies.
beneficiaries: Treasury bureaus and other federal agencies purchasing shared services

name: Health Workforce
description: Supports training, education, and placement of health professionals in underserved areas through programs like the National Health Service Corps, nursing workforce development, and graduate medical education.
beneficiaries: Health professional students and trainees, medically underserved communities, rural health facilities

name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
description: Independent bureau created by the Dodd-Frank Act to regulate consumer financial products and services, enforce fair lending laws, and educate consumers. Funded through Federal Reserve transfers, not congressional appropriations.
beneficiaries: American consumers using financial products including mortgages, credit cards, student loans, and banking services

name: Veterans Housing Benefit Program Fund
description: Administers VA home loan guarantees enabling veterans to obtain mortgages with favorable terms (no down payment, no PMI). Originally created under the GI Bill.
beneficiaries: Veterans, active-duty service members, eligible surviving spouses purchasing or refinancing homes

name: Guarantees of Mortgage-Backed Securities Loan Guarantee, Ginnie Mae
description: Second budget line for Ginnie Mae's MBS guarantee program, likely reflecting a different fiscal year or subsidy reestimate.
beneficiaries: Homebuyers, mortgage lenders, MBS investors

name: Working Capital Fund, Departmental Management
description: A revolving fund providing centralized administrative services (IT, facilities, procurement) to offices within a department on a reimbursable basis, eliminating duplication of support functions.
beneficiaries: Departmental offices and bureaus receiving consolidated administrative support

name: Marketing Services, Agricultural Marketing Service
description: Funds the AMS's grading, inspection, certification, and market news services for agricultural commodities, including organic certification, commodity purchasing for federal feeding programs, and market oversight.
beneficiaries: Farmers, food processors, consumers, school lunch programs, commodity purchasers

name: Chronic Disease Prevention and Health Promotion
description: Funds CDC programs targeting chronic diseases including heart disease, cancer, diabetes, obesity, and tobacco use through surveillance, research, community-based prevention, and state grants.
beneficiaries: General public, particularly populations at risk for chronic diseases; state and local health departments

name: Food Safety and Inspection Service
description: Funds FSIS's mandatory continuous inspection of domestic and imported meat, poultry, and processed egg products to ensure they are safe, wholesome, and correctly labeled.
beneficiaries: American consumers; meat, poultry, and egg product industries

name: Management Fund, National Institutes of Health
description: Provides centralized funding for NIH-wide administrative support, facilities management, IT infrastructure, and cross-cutting research support services across the 27 institutes and centers.
beneficiaries: NIH institutes and centers; indirectly, biomedical researchers and patients benefiting from research

name: State and Private Forestry
description: Funds Forest Service programs that provide technical and financial assistance to state, tribal, and private landowners for forest management, wildfire prevention, urban forestry, and forest health protection.
beneficiaries: State forestry agencies, private forest landowners, tribal nations, urban communities

name: Operation and Maintenance, Navy Reserve
description: Funds day-to-day operating costs of Navy Reserve forces including training, equipment maintenance, base operations, facilities upkeep, and logistics support for reserve readiness.
beneficiaries: Navy Reserve personnel and units maintaining combat readiness

name: Telecommunications Relay Services Fund
description: Established under the Americans with Disabilities Act, funds the Telecommunications Relay Service that enables people with hearing or speech disabilities to communicate by telephone through relay operators and modern IP-based services.
beneficiaries: Individuals who are deaf, hard of hearing, or have speech disabilities

name: Tenant-Based Rental Assistance (Section 8 Vouchers)
description: Funds the Housing Choice Voucher program providing portable rental subsidies to very low-income families, enabling them to rent privately owned housing by paying the difference between 30% of tenant income and fair market rent.
beneficiaries: Very low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals renting in the private market

name: HIV/AIDS, Viral Hepatitis, STDs, and TB Prevention
description: Funds CDC's surveillance, prevention, testing, and public health response programs for HIV/AIDS, viral hepatitis B and C, sexually transmitted diseases, and tuberculosis.
beneficiaries: Populations at risk for or living with HIV/AIDS, viral hepatitis, STDs, and TB; state and local health departments

name: Innovation and Improvement, Education
description: Funds competitive grant programs supporting educational innovation, charter schools, magnet schools, evidence-based interventions, and teacher/principal quality improvement initiatives within the Department of Education.
beneficiaries: K-12 students, charter and magnet schools, teachers, school districts piloting innovative education approaches

name: Salaries and Expenses, Small Business Administration
description: Covers SBA's administrative costs including staff salaries, district office operations, counseling services, and oversight of loan programs, contracting assistance, and entrepreneurial development.
beneficiaries: Small business owners and entrepreneurs accessing SBA services, counseling, and loan programs

name: Forest Service Operations
description: Funds the administrative and operational costs of the U.S. Forest Service including regional and national office operations, strategic planning, law enforcement, and management of 193 million acres of National Forest System lands.
beneficiaries: General public using national forests, rural communities, Forest Service workforce

name: National Surface Transportation Finance Bureau, Highway Trust Fund Account
description: Created under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act to manage innovative financing tools for surface transportation projects, including TIFIA loans and other credit assistance from the Highway Trust Fund.
beneficiaries: State and local transportation agencies, toll authorities, transit agencies using federal credit assistance for infrastructure projects

name: Procurement, Construction, and Improvements, Coast Guard
description: Funds the Coast Guard's acquisition of vessels, aircraft, shore facilities, and other capital assets needed for maritime safety, security, and environmental protection missions.
beneficiaries: Coast Guard personnel; maritime industries; coastal communities protected by Coast Guard operations

name: Career, Technical, and Adult Education
description: Funds state formula grants under the Perkins Act for career and technical education, as well as adult education and literacy programs under the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act.
beneficiaries: High school and postsecondary CTE students, adult learners, workforce development programs

name: Financial Agent Services, Bureau of the Fiscal Service
description: Reimburses financial institutions and Federal Reserve Banks that serve as fiscal agents of the U.S. government, processing payments, collections, and managing Treasury securities.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies using fiscal agent services; indirectly, taxpayers and Treasury security holders

name: Fossil Energy, Energy Programs
description: Funds research, development, and demonstration of fossil energy technologies including carbon capture, utilization, and storage (CCUS), natural gas technologies, and unconventional oil and gas resource characterization.
beneficiaries: Energy industry researchers, fossil fuel sector workers, communities near fossil energy infrastructure

name: Native American Programs, HUD
description: Funds the Indian Housing Block Grant (IHBG) and other programs providing housing assistance to American Indian and Alaska Native families through the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act (NAHASDA).
beneficiaries: American Indian and Alaska Native families and tribal housing entities

name: Funds for Strengthening Markets, Income, and Supply (Section 32)
description: Uses customs receipts to purchase surplus agricultural commodities for domestic feeding programs, support farm prices, and fund USDA nutrition assistance. Authorized under Section 32 of the Act of August 24, 1935.
beneficiaries: Farmers facing surplus commodity prices, school lunch and food bank recipients

name: International Narcotics Control and Law Enforcement
description: Funds State Department programs to combat international drug trafficking, strengthen foreign criminal justice systems, support counternarcotics operations, and build law enforcement capacity in partner nations.
beneficiaries: Foreign law enforcement and judicial institutions; U.S. communities benefiting from reduced drug trafficking

name: General Fund Payment to National Surface Transportation and Innovative Finance Bureau Highway Trust Fund Account, Upward Reestimates
description: Covers upward subsidy reestimates for federal credit programs (like TIFIA) administered through the Highway Trust Fund, reflecting revised cost projections for outstanding transportation loans.
beneficiaries: Highway Trust Fund solvency; state and local transportation project sponsors

name: Facilities and Equipment, Airport and Airway Trust Fund, FAA
description: Funds modernization of the national airspace system including air traffic control facilities, navigation equipment, communication systems, and airport surveillance technology.
beneficiaries: Airlines, general aviation, air travelers, air traffic controllers

name: Medical Community Care, Veterans Health Administration
description: Funds the VA's Community Care program (successor to Veterans Choice) that authorizes veterans to receive health care from non-VA community providers when VA facilities cannot provide timely or accessible care.
beneficiaries: Veterans who live far from VA facilities or face long wait times for VA care

name: Scientific and Technical Research and Services, NIST
description: Funds core research and measurement science programs at the National Institute of Standards and Technology, including development of standards, calibration services, cybersecurity frameworks, and advanced manufacturing research.
beneficiaries: U.S. industries relying on measurement standards, technology companies, cybersecurity practitioners, manufacturers

name: Crime Victims Fund
description: Established by the Victims of Crime Act (VOCA), funded by federal criminal fines and penalties (not taxpayer dollars), provides grants to states for victim compensation and assistance programs.
beneficiaries: Victims of federal and state crimes; domestic violence shelters, rape crisis centers, child abuse programs

name: Assessment Funds, Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
description: Funds OCC operations through assessments levied on national banks and federal savings associations that OCC supervises — not taxpayer funded. Covers bank examination, supervision, and regulation.
beneficiaries: National banks and federal savings associations (regulated entities); bank depositors and consumers (protection)

name: HHS Service and Supply Fund
description: An intragovernmental revolving fund through which the HHS Program Support Center provides shared services (accounting, grants management, IT, human resources) to HHS operating divisions on a reimbursable basis.
beneficiaries: HHS operating divisions and staff offices receiving centralized administrative services

name: Military Personnel, Space Force
description: Funds pay, allowances, bonuses, permanent change of station moves, and other personnel costs for United States Space Force military members, the newest branch of the armed forces.
beneficiaries: Active-duty Space Force guardians and their families

name: National Railroad Retirement Investment Trust
description: Independent trust that manages and invests railroad retirement assets in a diversified portfolio of stocks, bonds, and other financial instruments to fund benefits for retired railroad workers.
beneficiaries: Retired railroad workers and their survivors receiving Railroad Retirement benefits

name: STEM Education, National Science Foundation
description: Funds NSF programs that strengthen science, technology, engineering, and mathematics education at all levels, including K-12, undergraduate, and graduate education, teacher training, and broadening participation.
beneficiaries: STEM students and educators at all levels, underrepresented minorities in STEM fields

name: Research and Education Activities, National Institute of Food and Agriculture
description: Funds competitive and capacity grants for agricultural research and higher education through NIFA, including the Agriculture and Food Research Initiative (AFRI), 1862/1890 land-grant university capacity funds, and research fellowships.
beneficiaries: Land-grant universities, agricultural researchers, food and nutrition scientists

name: Maternal and Child Health
description: HRSA-administered Title V Maternal and Child Health Block Grant and related programs providing health services, infrastructure, and systems-building for mothers, infants, children, and adolescents, including children with special health care needs.
beneficiaries: Pregnant women, mothers, infants, children, adolescents, and children with special health care needs

name: U.S. Coronavirus Refundable Credits
description: Funds outlays for COVID-19-related refundable tax credits including Employee Retention Credits, paid sick/family leave credits, and recovery rebates enacted under CARES Act, ARPA, and related legislation.
beneficiaries: Employers claiming pandemic-era retention credits; individuals receiving COVID stimulus payments

name: Treasury Forfeiture Fund
description: Receives proceeds from forfeitures by Treasury law enforcement bureaus (IRS-CI, Secret Service, CBP) and funds law enforcement operations, victim restitution, and equitable sharing with state/local partners.
