beneficiaries: Treasury law enforcement agencies; crime victims; state and local law enforcement partners

name: Foreign Service Retirement and Disability Fund
description: Mandatory funding for the retirement and disability pension system for Foreign Service officers and certain other State Department employees, analogous to the military retirement system.
beneficiaries: Retired Foreign Service officers and their survivors

name: Medical Services, Veterans Health Administration
description: Funds core inpatient and outpatient health care services delivered through the VA's network of medical centers, clinics, and Vet Centers to eligible veterans.
beneficiaries: Enrolled veterans receiving VA health care

name: Executive Office of the President
description: Funds the White House Office, National Security Council, Office of Management and Budget, Council of Economic Advisers, and other EOP entities supporting presidential decision-making and policy coordination.
beneficiaries: The President and senior advisors; indirectly, the nation through executive branch policy coordination

name: Quality Improvement Organizations, CMS
description: Funds contracts with Quality Improvement Organizations (QIOs) that review medical care provided to Medicare beneficiaries to ensure quality, appropriateness, and necessity of services.
beneficiaries: Medicare beneficiaries; health care providers receiving quality improvement technical assistance

name: Federal Aid to Wildlife Restoration (Pittman-Robertson)
description: Distributes excise taxes on firearms, ammunition, and archery equipment to states for wildlife habitat restoration, hunter education, and shooting range development under the Pittman-Robertson Act.
beneficiaries: State fish and wildlife agencies, hunters, recreational shooters, wildlife populations

name: Procurement, Acquisition and Construction, NOAA
description: Funds NOAA's acquisition of satellites, ships, aircraft, supercomputers, and construction of facilities essential for weather forecasting, ocean monitoring, and environmental observation.
beneficiaries: General public (weather forecasts), maritime industries, fisheries, environmental researchers

name: Contributions for International Peacekeeping Activities
description: Pays the U.S. assessed share of United Nations peacekeeping operations worldwide, funding uniformed peacekeepers, civilian staff, and logistics for UN missions in conflict zones.
beneficiaries: Populations in conflict zones protected by UN peacekeeping missions; the international peace and security framework

name: Home Investment Partnerships Program (HOME)
description: Provides formula grants to states and localities for building, buying, or rehabilitating affordable housing or providing rental assistance to low-income households. Created under the Cranston-Gonzalez National Affordable Housing Act.
beneficiaries: Low-income families and individuals; state and local housing agencies; nonprofit affordable housing developers

name: Wildland Fire Management, Interior
description: Funds the Department of the Interior's wildfire preparedness, suppression, fuels management, burned area rehabilitation, and hazardous fuels reduction across DOI-managed lands.
beneficiaries: Communities at risk from wildfire, DOI firefighters, public lands ecosystem health

name: Procurement, Construction, and Improvements, U.S. Customs and Border Protection
description: Funds CBP's acquisition of border surveillance technology, non-intrusive inspection equipment, facilities construction, tactical communications, and vehicle fleet upgrades.
beneficiaries: CBP agents and officers; border communities; national security

name: Corporate Capital Account, U.S. International Development Finance Corporation (DFC)
description: Provides capital for DFC (successor to OPIC) to make equity investments, provide loans, and offer political risk insurance to support private-sector investment in developing countries aligned with U.S. foreign policy.
beneficiaries: Private investors in developing countries, host country populations benefiting from development finance

name: Program Offices, Management and Administration, HUD
description: Funds salaries, administrative costs, and operations of HUD program offices responsible for overseeing housing, community development, and fair housing programs.
beneficiaries: HUD staff; indirectly, recipients of HUD-administered housing and community development programs

name: Ready Reserve Force, Maritime Administration
description: Maintains a fleet of cargo ships in reduced operating status ready for rapid activation to support military sealift during national emergencies or defense mobilization.
beneficiaries: Department of Defense (sealift capacity), national defense logistics

name: Housing for the Elderly (Section 202)
description: Provides capital advances and project rental assistance to nonprofit sponsors for the construction and operation of supportive housing for very low-income elderly persons.
beneficiaries: Very low-income elderly persons (age 62+) in need of affordable, accessible housing

name: Program Management, HRSA
description: Funds HRSA's central administrative and management costs including IT, evaluation, planning, and cross-cutting support for health programs serving underserved populations.
beneficiaries: HRSA program offices; indirectly, underserved communities receiving HRSA-funded health services

name: Non-Proliferation, Anti-Terrorism, Demining and Related Programs
description: Funds State Department programs to prevent weapons of mass destruction proliferation, build foreign counterterrorism capacity, clear landmines and unexploded ordnance, and reduce small arms threats.
beneficiaries: Foreign nations receiving nonproliferation and demining assistance; civilian populations in mined areas

name: Assistance for Europe, Eurasia, and Central Asia
description: Provides economic and democratic development assistance to countries of the former Soviet Union and Central/Eastern Europe, supporting market reforms, rule of law, civil society, and Euro-Atlantic integration.
beneficiaries: Countries and populations in Eastern Europe, Eurasia, and Central Asia undergoing democratic and economic transitions

name: Operating Expenses, Corporation for National and Community Service
description: Funds the operational costs of AmeriCorps programs including AmeriCorps State and National, VISTA, NCCC, and Senior Corps volunteer programs.
beneficiaries: AmeriCorps members, VISTA volunteers, Senior Corps participants, and communities they serve

name: STEM Education, NSF
description: Second budget line for NSF STEM education programs.
beneficiaries: STEM students, educators, and underrepresented groups in STEM

name: Periodic Censuses and Programs, Bureau of the Census
description: Funds the decennial census, American Community Survey, Economic Census, Census of Governments, and other periodic demographic and economic surveys mandated by the Constitution and Congress.
beneficiaries: General public (representation, redistricting); state and local governments (formula grant allocations); businesses and researchers (data users)

name: Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Contribution, Marine Corps
description: The Marine Corps' annual accrual payment into the DOD Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund (MERHCF), pre-funding future TRICARE For Life benefits for Medicare-eligible military retirees.
beneficiaries: Medicare-eligible Marine Corps retirees and their dependents receiving TRICARE For Life benefits

name: Construction, Rehabilitation, Operation, and Maintenance, Western Area Power Administration
description: Funds WAPA's construction and maintenance of high-voltage transmission systems that market and deliver federal hydroelectric power across 15 western and central states.
beneficiaries: Rural electric cooperatives, municipalities, and other preference customers purchasing federal hydropower in the western U.S.

name: Space Technology, NASA
description: Funds NASA's development and demonstration of transformative space technologies including in-space propulsion, entry/descent/landing systems, small spacecraft, and technology transfer to industry.
beneficiaries: NASA missions, aerospace industry, commercial space companies adopting NASA-developed technologies

name: Patient-Centered Outcomes Research Trust Fund
description: Established by the Affordable Care Act to fund the Patient-Centered Outcomes Research Institute (PCORI), which sponsors comparative clinical effectiveness research to help patients and clinicians make better-informed health care decisions.
beneficiaries: Patients, clinicians, and health systems using evidence from comparative effectiveness research

name: Contract Support Costs, Indian Health Service
description: Reimburses tribes and tribal organizations for administrative and overhead costs of operating health programs contracted or compacted from the Indian Health Service under the Indian Self-Determination Act.
beneficiaries: Tribal governments and organizations operating self-determination health programs

name: Procurement, Space Force
description: Funds the acquisition of Space Force weapon systems, satellites, launch services, ground-based space control systems, and associated space program equipment.
beneficiaries: U.S. Space Force, national security space capabilities, defense industry space contractors

name: HIV/AIDS Working Capital Fund, USAID
description: A revolving fund enabling USAID to efficiently procure and distribute HIV/AIDS commodities (antiretroviral drugs, test kits, condoms) for PEPFAR programs in developing countries.
beneficiaries: People living with HIV/AIDS in PEPFAR-supported countries

name: Economic Development Assistance Programs, EDA
description: Provides grants for infrastructure, technical assistance, and economic adjustment to distressed communities and regions experiencing high unemployment, low income, or sudden economic dislocations.
beneficiaries: Economically distressed communities, regions affected by plant closures or natural disasters, tribal communities

name: Veterans Electronic Health Record
description: Funds the VA's deployment of a new electronic health record system (Oracle Cerner/MHS GENESIS) to replace VistA, aiming to create a single seamless health record shared with the Department of Defense.
beneficiaries: Veterans and VA health care providers transitioning to a modern interoperable health record system

name: Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health (ARPA-H)
description: Modeled after DARPA, ARPA-H funds high-risk, high-reward biomedical and health research to drive breakthroughs in preventing, detecting, and treating cancer, Alzheimer's, diabetes, and other diseases.
beneficiaries: Biomedical researchers and patients who may benefit from transformative health breakthroughs

name: Medical and Prosthetic Research, VHA
description: Funds the VA's intramural biomedical, clinical, health services, and rehabilitation research programs conducted across VA medical centers, focused on conditions prevalent among veterans.
beneficiaries: Veterans, VA clinician-researchers, and the broader medical community benefiting from VA research findings

name: Transmission Facilitation Fund
description: Created by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act to enable DOE to facilitate the construction of new high-capacity electric transmission lines by providing loans, capacity contracts, or participating as an anchor customer.
beneficiaries: Electric utilities, renewable energy developers, electricity consumers in regions needing new transmission capacity

name: Retired Pay, Coast Guard
description: Mandatory funding for military retirement pensions for Coast Guard retirees, including regular retirement, disability retirement, and survivor benefit payments.
beneficiaries: Retired Coast Guard members and their surviving dependents

name: Construction, Minor Projects, VA
description: Funds construction, alteration, and improvement projects at VA facilities with individual project costs below the major construction threshold (typically under $20 million).
beneficiaries: Veterans and VA staff at medical centers, clinics, and national cemeteries undergoing facility improvements

name: Rural Housing Insurance Fund Program Account
description: Provides direct and guaranteed housing loans to rural residents through USDA's Rural Housing Service, including Section 502 single-family homeownership loans and Section 515 rental housing loans.
beneficiaries: Low- and moderate-income rural residents seeking homeownership or affordable rental housing

name: Emergency Relief Program, Federal Highway Administration
description: Reimburses states and localities for the repair or reconstruction of federal-aid highways and bridges damaged by natural disasters or catastrophic failures.
beneficiaries: State and local transportation departments; motorists and communities dependent on damaged highway infrastructure

name: General Administration, Departmental Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds the VA's central office administrative functions including the Secretary's office, IT, human resources, financial management, and policy coordination across VA's three administrations.
beneficiaries: VA headquarters staff; indirectly, all veterans served by VA programs

name: Census Working Capital Fund
description: A revolving fund that provides reimbursable shared services (IT, telecommunications, facilities) to the Census Bureau's programs, enabling efficient centralized support for survey and census operations.
beneficiaries: Census Bureau programs and operations

name: Emergency Relief Program, FHWA
description: Second budget line for federal-aid highway emergency repairs.
beneficiaries: State DOTs and communities with disaster-damaged highways

name: Salaries and Expenses, Nuclear Regulatory Commission
description: Funds NRC's regulatory and oversight activities for civilian nuclear power plants, nuclear materials, and radioactive waste, including licensing, inspection, enforcement, and safety research.
beneficiaries: Public safety near nuclear facilities; nuclear power plant operators; nuclear materials licensees

name: National Center for Advancing Translational Sciences (NCATS), NIH
description: Funds research to transform the translational science process, accelerating the development of diagnostics and therapeutics by reducing systemic bottlenecks from laboratory discovery to patient bedside.
beneficiaries: Biomedical researchers, patients awaiting new therapies, clinical trial networks including CTSA hubs

name: Rural Water and Waste Disposal Program Account
description: Provides direct loans, guaranteed loans, and grants through USDA's Rural Utilities Service for drinking water and waste disposal systems in rural communities with populations under 10,000.
beneficiaries: Rural communities lacking adequate water and waste disposal infrastructure

name: Aeronautics, NASA
description: Funds NASA's aeronautics research programs including advanced air vehicles, integrated aviation systems, transformative concepts, supersonic flight research, and unmanned aircraft systems integration.
beneficiaries: Aviation industry, airlines, air travelers, aircraft manufacturers, FAA

name: District of Columbia Federal Pension Fund
description: Federal trust fund established under the Balanced Budget Act of 1997 to fund pension obligations for DC police, firefighters, and teachers hired before 1987, when those pension systems were a federal responsibility.
beneficiaries: Retired DC police officers, firefighters, and teachers hired before July 1, 1997

name: Federal Payments to the Railroad Retirement Accounts
description: Mandatory federal payments into the Railroad Retirement system to cover financial interchange transfers from Social Security and windfall elimination provisions for railroad workers' retirement benefits.
beneficiaries: Retired railroad workers and their survivors

name: Federal Assistance, FEMA
description: Funds FEMA grant programs for state and local preparedness including the State Homeland Security Grant Program, Urban Area Security Initiative, and other preparedness grants.
beneficiaries: State and local governments, first responders, emergency management agencies building preparedness capacity

name: Airport Terminal Program, FAA
description: Created by the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law to fund grants for airport terminal development and modernization projects at commercial service airports across the country.
beneficiaries: Commercial airports, airline passengers, airport workers, local economies near airports

name: Creating Helpful Incentives to Produce Semiconductors (CHIPS) for America Fund
description: Established by the CHIPS and Science Act to provide direct financial incentives (grants, loans, loan guarantees) for semiconductor manufacturing, assembly, testing, and advanced packaging facilities in the United States.
beneficiaries: Semiconductor manufacturers investing in U.S. fabrication facilities; U.S. workers in the chip industry; national security and supply chain resilience

name: Disaster Relief Fund, FEMA
description: FEMA's primary account for financing disaster response and recovery under the Stafford Act, funding Individual Assistance, Public Assistance, Hazard Mitigation Grants, and other disaster operations following presidential disaster declarations.
beneficiaries: Individuals, families, businesses, and state/local governments in presidentially declared disaster areas

name: General Departmental Management, HHS
description: Funds the Office of the Secretary of HHS and departmental management offices responsible for policy coordination, budget, legislative affairs, public affairs, and cross-cutting management of HHS operating divisions.
beneficiaries: HHS leadership and management staff; indirectly, all populations served by HHS programs

name: Customs and Border Protection, Customs User Fees Account
description: Collects and expends user fees charged for customs processing of commercial cargo, passengers, and vessels to fund CBP inspection and trade facilitation services at ports of entry.
beneficiaries: International travelers and importers processed at U.S. ports of entry; CBP operations

name: Highway Traffic Safety Grants, Liquidation of Contract Authorization, NHTSA
description: Liquidates (pays out) previously authorized grant obligations to states for highway safety programs including impaired driving countermeasures, occupant protection, traffic records, and pedestrian/bicycle safety.
beneficiaries: State highway safety offices, motorists, pedestrians, and all road users

name: Highway Traffic Safety Grants, NHTSA
description: Second budget line for NHTSA highway safety grant liquidation.
beneficiaries: State highway safety programs and road users

name: Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Contribution, National Guard Personnel, Army
description: The Army National Guard's annual accrual payment into the MERHCF to pre-fund future TRICARE For Life benefits for Medicare-eligible retired Guard members.
beneficiaries: Medicare-eligible Army National Guard retirees and their dependents

name: Public Health Preparedness and Response, CDC
description: Funds CDC's programs for preparing the nation for public health emergencies including the Strategic National Stockpile, BioSense surveillance system, state/local preparedness cooperative agreements, and emergency operations.
beneficiaries: State and local health departments; general public in public health emergencies

name: General Administration, VA
description: Second budget line for VA departmental administration.
beneficiaries: VA headquarters operations and all veterans served by VA

name: Payment Where Certain Tax Credits Exceed Liability for Corporate Tax
description: Funds refundable payments to corporations when clean energy and other tax credits exceed their corporate tax liability, primarily related to Inflation Reduction Act clean energy investment and production credits.
beneficiaries: Corporations with clean energy tax credits exceeding their tax liability, particularly renewable energy developers

name: National Eye Institute, NIH
description: Funds research on the causes, prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of eye diseases and visual disorders including macular degeneration, glaucoma, diabetic retinopathy, and low vision.
beneficiaries: People with or at risk for eye diseases and vision disorders; vision researchers

name: Payment to the Resolution Funding Corporation
description: Mandatory payment covering interest on bonds issued by the Resolution Funding Corporation (REFCORP) created to finance the savings and loan crisis bailout of the late 1980s and early 1990s.
beneficiaries: Holders of REFCORP bonds; financial system stability (legacy S&L crisis obligation)

name: Judgements, Court of Claims (Indefinite)
description: Mandatory indefinite appropriation to pay court judgments and compromise settlements against the United States rendered by the Court of Federal Claims and appellate courts.
beneficiaries: Plaintiffs who have won judgments or settlements against the federal government

name: Salaries and Expenses, Rural Development, Agriculture
description: Covers administrative costs of USDA Rural Development staff who deliver housing, business, utility, and community facility programs across rural America through state and area offices.
beneficiaries: USDA Rural Development staff and the rural communities they serve

name: Innovation and Improvement, Department of Education
description: Second budget line for Education Department innovation and improvement grants.
beneficiaries: K-12 students, innovative schools, and education reform initiatives

name: U.S. Agency for Global Media
description: Independent federal agency overseeing U.S. international broadcasting entities including Voice of America, Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty, Radio Free Asia, Office of Cuba Broadcasting, and Middle East Broadcasting Networks.
beneficiaries: International audiences in countries with limited press freedom; U.S. public diplomacy interests

name: Research, Development, and Procurement, ASPR
description: Funds the Administration for Strategic Preparedness and Response (formerly BARDA) in developing and procuring medical countermeasures (vaccines, therapeutics, diagnostics) against chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, and emerging infectious disease threats.
beneficiaries: General public (pandemic and biodefense preparedness); pharmaceutical and biotech developers of medical countermeasures

name: Salaries and Expenses, Government Accountability Office
description: Funds the GAO's operations as Congress's nonpartisan auditing and investigative arm, conducting performance audits, financial audits, policy analyses, and legal opinions for Congress.
beneficiaries: Congress and the public through improved government accountability and efficiency

name: International Broadcasting Operations, U.S. Agency for Global Media
description: Funds day-to-day broadcasting operations of U.S. international media networks including content production, transmission, digital platforms, and journalist salaries.
beneficiaries: Global audiences receiving U.S.-funded independent news; press freedom worldwide

name: CDC-Wide Activities and Program Support
description: Funds cross-cutting CDC activities including public health workforce development, global health security, laboratory safety, health equity initiatives, and agency-wide program support.
beneficiaries: CDC workforce, state and local health departments, global health security partners

name: Compact of Free Association, Insular Affairs
description: Funds economic assistance grants to the Freely Associated States (Republic of the Marshall Islands, Federated States of Micronesia, Republic of Palau) under Compacts of Free Association negotiated with the U.S.
beneficiaries: Citizens of Marshall Islands, Federated States of Micronesia, and Palau

name: Compact of Free Association
description: Second budget line for Compact of Free Association assistance, possibly reflecting amended compact terms under the 2023 renegotiated compacts.
beneficiaries: Freely Associated States populations

name: Retirement Pay and Medical Benefits for Commissioned Officers, Public Health Service
description: Mandatory funding for retirement pensions and medical benefits for commissioned officers of the U.S. Public Health Service Commissioned Corps, one of the eight uniformed services.
beneficiaries: Retired PHS Commissioned Corps officers and their dependents

name: Immunization and Respiratory Diseases, CDC
description: Funds CDC's national immunization program including the Vaccines for Children Program, influenza surveillance and response, immunization infrastructure, and respiratory disease monitoring.
beneficiaries: Children, adults, and elderly receiving federally supported vaccinations; state immunization programs

name: Executive Office for Immigration Review, Justice
description: Funds the immigration court system within DOJ that adjudicates immigration cases including removal proceedings, asylum claims, and appeals through the Board of Immigration Appeals.
beneficiaries: Immigrants in removal proceedings, asylum seekers, the immigration adjudication system

name: Retirement Pay and Medical Benefits for PHS Commissioned Officers
description: Second budget line for PHS Commissioned Corps retirement benefits.
beneficiaries: Retired PHS officers and dependents

name: Agricultural Credit Insurance Fund Program Account, FSA
description: Second budget line for FSA agricultural loans to farmers.
beneficiaries: Beginning and underserved farmers and ranchers

name: Customs and Border Protection, Immigration User Fees
description: Collects and expends user fees from immigration inspection services at ports of entry to fund CBP's immigration inspection and enforcement operations.
beneficiaries: International travelers entering the U.S.; CBP immigration enforcement operations

name: Uranium Enrichment Decontamination and Decommissioning Fund
description: Funds cleanup and decommissioning of gaseous diffusion uranium enrichment plants (Oak Ridge, Paducah, Portsmouth) used during the Cold War, financed by assessments on domestic utilities and federal appropriations.
beneficiaries: Communities near former uranium enrichment sites; workers and residents exposed to contamination

name: FDA Working Capital Fund
description: A revolving fund providing shared administrative services (IT, facilities, HR) to FDA centers and offices on a reimbursable basis.
beneficiaries: FDA program offices; indirectly, consumers protected by FDA regulatory activities

name: Working Capital Fund, General Services Administration
description: A revolving fund through which GSA provides shared services including IT solutions, payroll processing, financial management, and other administrative support to federal agencies on a fee-for-service basis.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies purchasing GSA shared services

name: Pentagon Reservation Maintenance Revolving Fund
description: A revolving fund that finances the operation, maintenance, protection, repair, and renovation of the Pentagon Reservation including the Pentagon building, associated parking, and grounds.
beneficiaries: DOD personnel and visitors at the Pentagon complex

name: Refugee and Entrant Assistance, ACF
description: Funds resettlement assistance for refugees, asylees, Cuban/Haitian entrants, and unaccompanied alien children including cash assistance, medical screening, social services, and the Unaccompanied Children program.
beneficiaries: Refugees, asylees, unaccompanied migrant children, Cuban/Haitian entrants resettling in the U.S.

name: Management Fund, NIH
description: Second budget line for NIH-wide administrative and facilities support.
beneficiaries: NIH institutes, centers, and supported research enterprise

name: Working Capital Fund, GSA
description: Second budget line for GSA's shared services revolving fund.
beneficiaries: Federal agency customers

name: National Parks and Public Land Legacy Restoration Fund
description: Created by the Great American Outdoors Act to address the deferred maintenance backlog at National Park Service, BLM, Fish and Wildlife Service, Bureau of Indian Education, and Forest Service facilities, funded from energy development revenues.
beneficiaries: Visitors to national parks and public lands; maintenance workers; facilities at NPS, BLM, FWS, BIE, and USFS

name: Corporate Capital Account, DFC
description: Second budget line for DFC development finance capital.
beneficiaries: Private investors in developing countries and host populations

name: Defense Production Act Purchases
description: Funds government purchases and investments to create, maintain, or expand domestic production capacity for items essential to national defense under Title III of the Defense Production Act.
beneficiaries: Domestic manufacturers of defense-critical materials and components; national defense supply chain

name: Science and Technology, Environmental Protection Agency
description: Funds EPA's scientific research programs that provide the foundation for environmental regulations, including research on air quality, water quality, hazardous waste, pesticides, and climate change.
beneficiaries: EPA regulatory programs; communities affected by pollution; environmental researchers

name: Construction, FBI
description: Funds construction, renovation, and improvement of FBI facilities including field offices, laboratories, training facilities, and the ongoing headquarters building project.
beneficiaries: FBI personnel and operations; national security infrastructure

name: Educational and Cultural Exchange Programs, State
description: Funds the Fulbright Program, International Visitor Leadership Program, and other people-to-people exchanges managed by the Bureau of Educational and Cultural Affairs to build mutual understanding between the U.S. and other nations.
beneficiaries: Fulbright scholars, international exchange participants, U.S. students and academics, foreign leaders and professionals

name: Conservation Operations, Natural Resources Conservation Service
description: Funds NRCS's technical assistance to farmers, ranchers, and landowners for conservation planning and implementation of practices to protect soil, water, air, plants, and wildlife on private agricultural lands.
beneficiaries: Farmers and ranchers receiving conservation technical assistance; rural watersheds and ecosystems

name: Health Care Fraud and Abuse Control Account
description: Jointly administered by HHS-OIG and DOJ, funds investigations and prosecutions of health care fraud in Medicare, Medicaid, and other federal health programs. Returns significantly more money than it costs.
beneficiaries: Medicare and Medicaid programs (recovered funds); taxpayers; patients protected from fraudulent providers

name: Institute of Education Sciences, Department of Education
description: Funds the federal government's primary source of education research, statistics (NAEP, NCES), and evaluation, generating evidence to improve education outcomes and inform policy.
beneficiaries: Educators, policymakers, researchers, students benefiting from evidence-based education practices

name: Office of Job Corps, Employment and Training Administration
description: Funds the Job Corps program providing free residential education and vocational training to disadvantaged youth ages 16-24 at 121 centers nationwide, offering GED preparation, career technical training, and placement services.
beneficiaries: At-risk and disadvantaged youth ages 16-24 seeking education and job skills

name: Judgements, United States Courts (Indefinite)
description: Mandatory indefinite appropriation to pay judgments rendered by federal district courts and courts of appeals against the United States in civil cases.
beneficiaries: Plaintiffs who have won federal court judgments against the U.S. government

name: Servicemen's Group Life Insurance Fund
description: A trust fund holding premiums and paying death benefits under the Servicemembers' Group Life Insurance (SGLI) and Veterans' Group Life Insurance (VGLI) programs providing low-cost life insurance to service members and veterans.
beneficiaries: Active-duty service members, veterans, and their beneficiaries under SGLI and VGLI policies

name: Training and Employment Service, ETA
description: Second budget line for DOL workforce development programs.
beneficiaries: Unemployed and underemployed workers, job seekers

name: CDC Working Capital Fund
description: A revolving fund providing shared business services (IT, procurement, facilities, HR) to CDC centers and offices on a reimbursable basis to achieve economies of scale.
beneficiaries: CDC program offices and centers

name: Salaries and Expenses, Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service
description: Funds APHIS operations protecting U.S. agriculture and natural resources from pests and diseases, including animal health monitoring, plant quarantine, wildlife damage management, and veterinary diagnostics.
beneficiaries: U.S. agricultural producers, livestock industry, natural ecosystems, trade partners

name: Burden Sharing Contribution, Defense
description: Receives contributions from allied nations (primarily Japan, South Korea, and other host nations) that share the cost of stationing U.S. military forces on their territory under bilateral defense agreements.
beneficiaries: DOD operations at overseas bases; U.S.-allied defense cooperation

name: Operations, Research and Facilities, NOAA
description: Funds NOAA's core operations including the National Weather Service, National Marine Fisheries Service, National Ocean Service, and Office of Oceanic and Atmospheric Research for weather forecasting, fisheries management, and ocean/coastal services.
beneficiaries: General public (weather forecasts), fishing industry, coastal communities, maritime sector, climate researchers

name: National Guard and Reserve Equipment Account
description: Funds procurement of critical equipment for all Reserve Component forces (Army/Air National Guard, Army/Navy/Air Force/Marine Corps Reserves) to close equipment gaps with active-duty forces.
beneficiaries: National Guard and Reserve units across all service branches

name: Refundable Premium Assistance Tax Credit
description: Funds the refundable portion of the Premium Tax Credit under the Affordable Care Act, which subsidizes health insurance premiums for individuals and families purchasing coverage through Health Insurance Marketplaces.
beneficiaries: Low- and moderate-income individuals and families purchasing ACA Marketplace health insurance

name: National Archives and Records Administration
description: Independent agency that preserves and provides access to the nation's most important government records, historical documents, presidential libraries, and the Federal Register.
beneficiaries: Researchers, historians, genealogists, government agencies, and citizens accessing federal records

name: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Innovation (CMMI)
description: Created by the ACA to test innovative payment and service delivery models in Medicare, Medicaid, and CHIP that reduce costs while preserving or improving quality of care.
beneficiaries: Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries; health care providers participating in alternative payment models

name: Emerging and Zoonotic Infectious Diseases, CDC
description: Funds CDC programs to detect, prevent, and respond to emerging and zoonotic infectious disease threats including antibiotic resistance, foodborne diseases, vector-borne diseases, and healthcare-associated infections.
beneficiaries: General public; health care facilities combating antibiotic resistance; state epidemiologists

name: Nonrecurring Expenses Fund, Agriculture
description: Allows USDA to transfer unobligated balances from expired discretionary accounts to fund one-time IT modernization, facilities improvements, and other nonrecurring capital investments.
beneficiaries: USDA information technology and facilities modernization

name: Green and Resilient Retrofit Program for Multifamily Housing
description: Created by the Inflation Reduction Act to fund direct loans, grants, and contracts for energy and water efficiency improvements, climate resilience measures, and green retrofits in HUD-assisted multifamily housing.
beneficiaries: Residents of HUD-assisted multifamily housing; building owners implementing green retrofits

name: World Trade Center Health Program Fund
description: Funds medical monitoring and treatment for 9/11 responders, recovery workers, and survivors who became ill from exposure to toxins at the World Trade Center, Pentagon, and Shanksville crash sites. Permanently authorized under the Zadroga Act.
beneficiaries: 9/11 first responders, recovery and cleanup workers, and survivors with certified WTC-related health conditions

name: Non-Proliferation, Anti-Terrorism, Demining, State
description: Second budget line for State Department nonproliferation and demining programs.
beneficiaries: Foreign nations and civilian populations in conflict-affected areas

name: Injury Prevention and Control, CDC
description: Funds CDC research and programs to prevent injuries and violence including opioid overdose prevention, suicide prevention, traumatic brain injury, motor vehicle safety, child abuse prevention, and firearm injury surveillance.
beneficiaries: General public, particularly populations at risk for injuries, overdose, and violence

name: Public Health Scientific Services, CDC
description: Funds CDC's cross-cutting scientific infrastructure including laboratory science, health statistics (NCHS), epidemiology and laboratory capacity, surveillance systems, and workforce development.
beneficiaries: Public health system nationwide; epidemiologists, laboratorians, health statisticians

name: Operation of Indian Education Programs
description: Funds the Bureau of Indian Education's operation of 183 elementary and secondary schools and dormitories on reservations, as well as tribal college supplemental funding and Johnson-O'Malley education assistance.
beneficiaries: American Indian and Alaska Native students in BIE-operated schools on reservations

name: Green and Resilient Retrofit Program
description: Second budget line for IRA-funded green retrofits in HUD multifamily housing.
beneficiaries: HUD-assisted multifamily housing residents and owners

name: Salaries and Expenses, SBA
description: Second budget line for Small Business Administration administrative costs.
beneficiaries: Small business owners and entrepreneurs

name: Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection Fund
description: The CFPB's primary funding mechanism through Federal Reserve transfers (not congressional appropriations), capped at a percentage of the Federal Reserve System's operating expenses.
beneficiaries: American consumers of financial products and services

name: Revolving Fund, Undistributed SIBAC Chargebacks, OPM
description: A revolving fund within OPM for the Shared Information and Business Application Computing (SIBAC) program that distributes IT infrastructure and shared service costs across participating federal agencies through chargeback billing.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies using OPM shared IT services

name: Highway Infrastructure Programs, Federal Highway Administration
description: Provides grants for highway infrastructure projects including bridge replacement, freight corridors, and other capital improvements on the national highway system and local roads.
beneficiaries: State DOTs, local governments, motorists, freight transportation industry

name: Working Capital Fund, Labor
description: A revolving fund providing shared administrative services (IT, financial management, HR, procurement) to Department of Labor agencies on a reimbursable basis.
beneficiaries: DOL agencies and bureaus receiving shared administrative services

name: Rural Energy for America Program (REAP)
description: Provides grants and loan guarantees to rural small businesses and agricultural producers for renewable energy systems (solar, wind, biomass) and energy efficiency improvements.
beneficiaries: Rural agricultural producers and small businesses investing in renewable energy and energy efficiency

name: Extension Activities, National Institute of Food and Agriculture
description: Funds the Cooperative Extension System, Smith-Lever Act programs, and other extension activities delivering research-based knowledge to farmers, families, and communities through land-grant university extension services in every county.
beneficiaries: Farmers, rural communities, 4-H youth, families accessing land-grant university extension services

name: Salaries and Expenses, Rural Development
description: Second budget line for USDA Rural Development administration.
beneficiaries: Rural communities served by USDA Rural Development programs

name: Salaries and Expenses, Bureau of Labor Statistics
description: Funds BLS operations producing critical economic statistics including the Consumer Price Index, unemployment rate, employment projections, and productivity data that guide monetary policy, federal benefits adjustments, and economic analysis.
beneficiaries: Policymakers, Federal Reserve, businesses, workers, Social Security recipients (COLA adjustments), economists

name: Violence Against Women Prevention and Prosecution Programs
description: Funds grant programs under the Violence Against Women Act (VAWA) supporting law enforcement, prosecution, victim services, legal assistance, transitional housing, and prevention programs for domestic violence, sexual assault, dating violence, and stalking.
beneficiaries: Victims and survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, dating violence, and stalking; shelters, legal aid, law enforcement

name: Violence Against Women Programs
description: Second budget line for VAWA grant programs.
beneficiaries: Domestic violence and sexual assault survivors and service providers

name: Program Account, U.S. International Development Finance Corporation
description: Funds DFC's administrative expenses and credit subsidy costs for development finance programs including direct loans, loan guarantees, equity investments, political risk insurance, and feasibility studies.
beneficiaries: Private-sector investors in developing countries; populations in low-income countries benefiting from DFC-financed projects

name: Salaries and Expenses, Office of Personnel Management
description: Funds OPM's administrative costs for managing the federal civilian workforce including retirement and benefits administration, hiring policy, background investigations oversight, and human capital management.
beneficiaries: Federal civilian employees and retirees; federal agencies relying on OPM HR policy and services

name: Consumer Financial Civil Penalty Fund, CFPB
description: Receives civil monetary penalties collected by the CFPB from enforcement actions against financial institutions and uses them to compensate victims of consumer financial law violations or fund financial literacy programs.
beneficiaries: Consumers harmed by financial institution violations; financial literacy program participants

name: Supplemental Payments to UMWA Health Plans
description: Provides federal payments to supplement the United Mine Workers of America health benefit plans covering retired coal miners and their dependents when plan assets are insufficient.
beneficiaries: Retired coal miners and their dependents covered by UMWA health benefit plans

name: Joint DOD-VA Medical Facility Demonstration Fund
description: Funds joint Department of Defense and VA health care sharing initiatives including construction and operation of joint medical facilities that serve both active-duty military and veterans.
beneficiaries: Active-duty service members and veterans receiving care at joint DOD-VA facilities

name: State Small Business Credit Initiative, Treasury
description: Provides federal funds to states, territories, and tribal governments to expand small business lending and investment programs through partnerships with private lenders, including venture capital programs for underserved entrepreneurs.
beneficiaries: Small businesses, particularly those in underserved communities and minority-owned businesses accessing state-administered credit programs

name: Global Health, CDC
description: Funds CDC's international health programs including global disease detection, HIV/AIDS surveillance, malaria prevention, global health security capacity building, and Field Epidemiology Training Programs.
beneficiaries: Populations in developing countries; global health security infrastructure

name: Administrative Support Offices, Management and Administration, HUD
description: Funds HUD's administrative support functions including information technology, human resources, procurement, financial management, and field office operations.
beneficiaries: HUD staff and operations; indirectly, all HUD program beneficiaries

name: National Infrastructure Investments (RAISE/TIGER Grants)
description: Competitive discretionary grant program (now called RAISE, formerly TIGER/BUILD) funding multimodal surface transportation capital projects of national or regional significance that are difficult to fund through traditional programs.
beneficiaries: State and local governments, transit agencies, port authorities with innovative multimodal transportation projects

name: Airport Infrastructure Grants, FAA
description: Created by the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law to provide formula-based grants to airports for infrastructure needs including runways, taxiways, and safety-related improvements.
beneficiaries: Airports of all sizes, air travelers, aviation safety

name: National Institute of Arthritis and Musculoskeletal and Skin Diseases (NIAMS), NIH
description: Funds research on arthritis, musculoskeletal diseases (osteoporosis, lupus), and skin diseases to understand causes, develop treatments, and improve quality of life for affected individuals.
beneficiaries: People with arthritis, osteoporosis, lupus, musculoskeletal disorders, and skin diseases; rheumatology and dermatology researchers

name: National Human Genome Research Institute (NHGRI), NIH
description: Funds genomics research including the ethical, legal, and social implications of genomic advances, development of genomic technologies, and application of genomics to medicine and disease understanding.
beneficiaries: Genomics researchers, patients benefiting from precision medicine, populations studied in genomic diversity research

name: Office of Job Corps, ETA
description: Second budget line for Job Corps residential education and training centers.
beneficiaries: Disadvantaged youth ages 16-24

name: Section 241 Transactions Account, HHS
description: A transfer account under Section 241 of the Public Health Service Act that allows the HHS Secretary to use a portion of appropriations for research, evaluation, demonstrations, and public health training across HHS agencies.
beneficiaries: HHS programs receiving flexible evaluation and research funding

name: NIAMS, NIH
description: Second budget line for NIAMS musculoskeletal and skin disease research.
beneficiaries: Patients with arthritis and musculoskeletal conditions; researchers

name: Federal Reserve Bank Reimbursement Fund, Bureau of the Fiscal Service
description: Reimburses Federal Reserve Banks for fiscal agency services performed on behalf of the Treasury including processing government payments, maintaining Treasury securities systems, and cash management.
beneficiaries: Federal Reserve Banks performing Treasury fiscal agent duties; government payment recipients

name: Department of Defense Base Closure Account
description: Funds the environmental cleanup, property disposal, and community transition costs associated with military bases closed or realigned under Base Realignment and Closure (BRAC) rounds.
beneficiaries: Communities surrounding closed/realigned military bases; environmental remediation of former defense installations

name: Operations and Support, U.S. Customs and Border Protection
description: Funds CBP's day-to-day operations including Border Patrol agent and CBP officer salaries, border security technology, port of entry operations, and air and marine interdiction.
beneficiaries: National border security; communities along the border; international trade and travel

name: NHGRI, NIH
description: Second budget line for National Human Genome Research Institute.
beneficiaries: Genomics researchers and precision medicine patients

name: Distance Learning, Telemedicine and Broadband Program, RUS
description: Provides grants and loans through USDA's Rural Utilities Service to fund broadband infrastructure, distance learning connectivity, and telemedicine links in rural communities.
beneficiaries: Rural schools, hospitals, libraries, and residents gaining broadband and telemedicine access

name: Science and Technology, EPA
description: Second budget line for EPA scientific research.
beneficiaries: Environmental regulators, researchers, and affected communities

name: Federal Payment to the Federal District of Columbia Pension Fund
description: Federal government's annual contribution to the DC pension fund covering pre-1997 retirement obligations for District of Columbia police, firefighters, and teachers.
beneficiaries: Retired DC police, firefighters, and teachers hired before 1997

name: Mississippi River and Tributaries, Corps of Engineers
description: Funds the Army Corps of Engineers' flood control, channel improvement, and navigation projects along the Mississippi River and its tributaries under the comprehensive Mississippi River and Tributaries project authorized by the 1928 Flood Control Act.
beneficiaries: Communities in the Mississippi River floodplain, navigation industry, agricultural areas protected from flooding

name: Salaries and Expenses, OPM
description: Second budget line for OPM administration.
beneficiaries: Federal workforce and retirees

name: Watershed and Flood Prevention Operations, NRCS
description: Funds USDA's small watershed program under PL-566 providing technical and financial assistance for flood prevention, watershed protection, water quality improvement, and agricultural water management projects.
beneficiaries: Rural communities and agricultural areas at risk of flooding; local watershed project sponsors

name: Agricultural Credit Insurance Fund, FSA
description: Third budget line for FSA farm loan programs.
beneficiaries: Farmers and ranchers needing agricultural credit

name: Research and Education Activities, NIFA
description: Second budget line for NIFA agricultural research and education grants.
beneficiaries: Land-grant universities and agricultural researchers

name: Working Capital Fund, Bureau of Reclamation
description: A revolving fund supporting Reclamation's technical services, laboratory testing, and shared engineering capabilities provided to Reclamation projects and other federal agencies on a reimbursable basis.
beneficiaries: Bureau of Reclamation projects and partner agencies

name: Operations and Administration, International Trade Administration
description: Funds ITA's trade promotion, enforcement of trade laws (anti-dumping, countervailing duties), commercial diplomacy, and industry competitiveness analysis to support U.S. exports and fair trade.
beneficiaries: U.S. exporters, domestic manufacturers affected by unfair trade, industries seeking international market access

name: Primary Health Care Access, HRSA
description: Funds the Health Center Program (Community Health Centers, Federally Qualified Health Centers) providing comprehensive primary care to medically underserved populations regardless of ability to pay.
beneficiaries: Low-income, uninsured, and underserved patients receiving care at 1,400+ community health centers nationwide

name: Prison Industries Fund (UNICOR/Federal Prison Industries)
description: A revolving fund for Federal Prison Industries (UNICOR), a government corporation that employs federal inmates in manufacturing and services, providing job training while producing goods for federal agencies.
beneficiaries: Federal inmates gaining job skills; federal agencies purchasing UNICOR products; correctional system rehabilitation goals

name: Program Account, DFC
description: Second budget line for DFC administrative and program costs.
beneficiaries: Development finance beneficiaries in developing countries

name: Administrative Support Offices, HUD
description: Second budget line for HUD administrative support functions.
beneficiaries: HUD operations and staff

name: Rivers and Harbors Contributed and Advance Funds, Corps of Engineers
description: Receives contributions from non-federal sponsors (states, localities, port authorities) who advance funds or contribute to Army Corps of Engineers navigation and flood control projects as required cost-sharing partners.
beneficiaries: Ports, harbors, and communities co-funding Corps of Engineers water infrastructure projects

name: Procurement, Construction, and Improvements, DHS Management Directorate
description: Funds DHS headquarters-level procurement of IT systems, intelligence and surveillance capabilities, construction of consolidated facilities, and department-wide infrastructure improvements.
beneficiaries: DHS component agencies and the department's unified operational capabilities

name: Salaries and Expenses, Occupational Safety and Health Administration
description: Funds OSHA's workplace safety and health regulatory activities including inspections, standards development, enforcement, whistleblower protection, and compliance assistance for employers.
beneficiaries: Workers in regulated industries; employers receiving compliance assistance; workplace safety nationwide

name: Energy Community Revitalization Program, Interior
description: Created by the Inflation Reduction Act to fund economic revitalization of communities affected by fossil fuel industry transitions, including remediation of abandoned wells, mine lands, and brownfields on federal and tribal lands.
beneficiaries: Former fossil fuel-dependent communities, workers in transitioning energy industries, tribal nations

name: Community Oriented Policing Services (COPS)
description: Provides grants to state, local, and tribal law enforcement agencies to hire community policing officers, develop anti-crime technologies, and implement community policing strategies through the COPS Hiring Program.
beneficiaries: State and local police departments; communities benefiting from community-oriented policing strategies

name: Offshore Safety and Environmental Enforcement, Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement, Interior
description: Funds BSEE's regulatory oversight of offshore oil, gas, and renewable energy operations on the Outer Continental Shelf, including safety inspections, environmental compliance, and oil-spill response preparedness.
beneficiaries: Offshore energy workers, coastal communities, marine ecosystems, offshore energy industry operators subject to safety regulation

name: McGovern-Dole International Food for Education and Child Nutrition Program, Foreign Agricultural Service, Agriculture
description: Donates U.S. agricultural commodities and provides financial and technical assistance to support school feeding programs and child nutrition in food-insecure developing countries, improving literacy and school attendance.
beneficiaries: Children in low-income developing countries, foreign schools and educational institutions, U.S. agricultural commodity producers

name: National Labor Relations Board
description: Independent federal agency that enforces the National Labor Relations Act by conducting union representation elections and adjudicating unfair labor practice complaints against employers and unions.
beneficiaries: Private-sector employees exercising collective bargaining rights, labor unions, employers subject to NLRA jurisdiction

name: Salaries and Expenses, National Labor Relations Board
description: Funds the personnel compensation, travel, and operating costs of the NLRB, including its regional offices, administrative law judges, and the Office of the General Counsel.
beneficiaries: NLRB staff and regional office personnel, private-sector workers and employers involved in labor disputes

name: Office of Museum and Library Services: Grants and Administration, Institute of Museum and Library Services
description: Provides federal grants and policy leadership to support the nation's museums and libraries, funding digital inclusion, literacy, workforce development, and community engagement programs through IMLS.
beneficiaries: Public libraries, museums, tribal libraries, state library agencies, library and museum patrons across the United States

name: Operational Test and Evaluation, Defense
description: Funds the Director of Operational Test and Evaluation (DOT&E) to conduct independent testing and live-fire evaluation of major defense acquisition programs before full-rate production decisions.
beneficiaries: Department of Defense, Congress (oversight), military service members who depend on reliable weapons systems

name: Salaries and Expenses, Foreign Agricultural Service, Agriculture
description: Funds personnel and operations of the Foreign Agricultural Service, which promotes U.S. agricultural exports, collects global crop intelligence, and administers international food aid and trade programs.
beneficiaries: U.S. agricultural exporters, farmers and ranchers, foreign agricultural attaches, developing country food-aid recipients

name: Howard University, Education
description: Direct federal appropriation to Howard University, a historically Black university chartered by Congress, supporting its educational mission, research programs, hospital operations, and campus infrastructure.
beneficiaries: Howard University students, faculty, and staff; the broader African American academic and professional community

name: Title 17 Innovative Technology Loan Guarantee Program, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Provides federal loan guarantees under Title 17 of the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to accelerate deployment of innovative clean energy technologies including advanced nuclear, renewable energy, and carbon capture projects.
beneficiaries: Clean energy technology developers, project sponsors, private lenders, communities benefiting from clean energy deployment

name: Black Lung Disability Trust Fund, Treasury Managed, Labor
description: Provides disability benefits and medical treatment to coal miners totally disabled by pneumoconiosis (black lung disease) and their surviving dependents when no responsible mine operator can be identified.
beneficiaries: Disabled coal miners with black lung disease and their surviving dependents

name: Court Services and Offender Supervision Agency
description: Independent federal agency created by the National Capital Revitalization and Self-Government Improvement Act to provide pretrial services, community supervision, and offender reentry support in the District of Columbia.
beneficiaries: D.C. criminal defendants on pretrial release, D.C. offenders on probation/parole/supervised release, D.C. communities

name: Salaries and Expenses, Federal Payment to the Court Services and Offender Supervision Agency for the District of Columbia
description: Federal payment covering the salaries, benefits, and operating expenses of CSOSA and the D.C. Pretrial Services Agency, including community supervision officers and drug-testing programs.
beneficiaries: CSOSA employees, D.C. pretrial defendants, supervised offenders in the District of Columbia

name: Federal Student Loan Reserve Fund, Education
description: Holds reserve funds to cover estimated costs and cash flows associated with the Federal Direct Student Loan Program, including subsidized and unsubsidized loans, PLUS loans, and consolidation loans.
beneficiaries: College and university students and parents borrowing federal student loans

name: Lead Hazard Reduction, Office of Lead Hazard Control and Healthy Homes, Housing and Urban Development
description: Provides grants to state and local governments and private entities to identify and control lead-based paint hazards in privately owned low-income housing, reducing childhood lead poisoning.
beneficiaries: Low-income families with children in pre-1978 housing, state and local governments, lead abatement contractors

name: Operations and Research, National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, Transportation
description: Funds NHTSA's vehicle safety standards development, defect investigations, crash data collection (FARS, NASS), behavioral safety research, fuel economy standards (CAFE), and consumer information programs.
beneficiaries: All motor vehicle occupants and road users, automobile manufacturers (regulatory compliance), state highway safety offices

name: High Intensity Drug Trafficking Areas Program, Funds Appropriated to the President
description: Provides federal assistance to regional HIDTA task forces that coordinate multi-agency law enforcement efforts to disrupt drug trafficking and production in designated high-intensity areas across the nation.
beneficiaries: Federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies in HIDTA-designated regions, communities affected by drug trafficking

name: Cooperative Threat Reduction Account, Defense
description: Funds the Nunn-Lugar Cooperative Threat Reduction program to secure and dismantle weapons of mass destruction, related materials, and delivery systems in former Soviet states and other partner countries.
beneficiaries: Global security community, partner nation governments dismantling WMD, U.S. and allied populations protected from WMD proliferation

name: Regulatory Program, Corps of Engineers, Civil
description: Funds the Army Corps of Engineers' regulatory mission under the Clean Water Act Section 404 and Rivers and Harbors Act Section 10, reviewing and issuing permits for activities in U.S. waters and wetlands.
beneficiaries: Developers, landowners, and entities seeking permits for wetlands and waterway activities; aquatic ecosystems and wetland habitats

name: Office of Inspector General, Departmental Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds the VA Inspector General's independent oversight of VA programs and operations through audits, investigations, inspections, and reviews to prevent fraud, waste, abuse, and mismanagement.
beneficiaries: Veterans receiving VA services, VA employees (whistleblower protections), Congress and the public (oversight and accountability)

name: Natural Resource Damage Assessment and Restoration Fund, Office of the Secretary
description: Funds DOI's assessment of injuries to natural resources from oil spills and hazardous substance releases and restoration of damaged ecosystems under CERCLA and the Oil Pollution Act.
beneficiaries: Damaged ecosystems and wildlife, communities near contaminated sites, tribal nations with affected trust resources

name: Port Infrastructure Development Program, Maritime Administration, Transportation
description: Competitive grant program funding infrastructure improvements at coastal and inland ports to improve safety, efficiency, and environmental sustainability of port facilities and intermodal connections.
beneficiaries: Port authorities, terminal operators, coastal and inland port communities, freight supply chain stakeholders

name: Family Planning, Health Resources and Services Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Funds the Title X Family Planning Program providing comprehensive family planning services, contraceptive counseling, preventive health screenings, and reproductive health education through a network of clinics.
beneficiaries: Low-income and uninsured individuals seeking reproductive health care, Title X-funded clinics, community health centers

name: Contribution for Annuity Benefits, United States Secret Service, Homeland Security
description: Government contribution to the retirement annuity system for Secret Service agents who receive enhanced retirement benefits due to the physically demanding and hazardous nature of protective duties.
beneficiaries: Current and retired U.S. Secret Service special agents eligible for enhanced law enforcement retirement benefits

name: Office of the Inspector General, Office of the Secretary, Health and Human Services
description: Funds the HHS-OIG's oversight of Medicare, Medicaid, and over 100 HHS programs through audits, investigations, evaluations, and enforcement actions to combat fraud, waste, and abuse in health care and human services.
beneficiaries: Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries, taxpayers, HHS program participants, the health care system broadly

name: Payment to the Hazardous Substances Superfund, Environmental Protection Agency
description: Transfers general fund revenues to the Superfund trust fund used by EPA to clean up the nation's most contaminated hazardous waste sites where no responsible party can be identified or is unable to pay.
beneficiaries: Communities near Superfund sites, residents affected by hazardous waste contamination, environmental remediation workers

name: Board of Veterans Appeals, Departmental Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds the Board of Veterans' Appeals, which adjudicates appeals of VA benefit claim decisions made by regional offices, issuing final decisions on behalf of the Secretary of Veterans Affairs.
beneficiaries: Veterans and their dependents/survivors appealing denied or partially granted VA benefit claims

name: Departmental Administration, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds the Department of Energy's headquarters management, policy analysis, congressional liaison, public affairs, chief financial officer operations, and general administrative functions.
beneficiaries: DOE headquarters and field staff, the energy policy process, Congressional oversight

name: Defense Production Act Purchases, Defense
description: Funds Title III of the Defense Production Act, enabling DOD to invest in domestic industrial base capabilities, provide purchase commitments, and create assured supply chains for critical defense materials and technologies.
beneficiaries: Domestic defense industrial base manufacturers, military services requiring assured supply of critical materials

name: International Registered Traveler Program Fund, U.S. Customs and Border Protection, Homeland Security
description: Fee-funded account supporting CBP's trusted traveler programs (Global Entry, NEXUS, SENTRI, FAST) that allow pre-approved, low-risk travelers expedited clearance upon arrival in the United States.
beneficiaries: Pre-approved international travelers and frequent border crossers, CBP, airports and land ports of entry

name: Salaries and Expenses, Antitrust Division, Justice
description: Funds the DOJ Antitrust Division's enforcement of federal antitrust laws, including investigation and prosecution of anticompetitive mergers, price-fixing conspiracies, and monopolistic practices.
beneficiaries: American consumers and businesses harmed by anticompetitive conduct, competitive markets

name: Safety and Operations, Federal Railroad Administration, Transportation
description: Funds FRA's railroad safety inspection and enforcement programs, safety research, grade crossing improvement, rail safety technology development, and regulatory oversight of the railroad industry.
beneficiaries: Railroad passengers, railroad workers, communities near rail lines, freight and passenger railroad operators

name: Aviation Security Capital Fund, Transportation Security Administration, Homeland Security
description: Funds capital improvements for aviation security infrastructure at airports, including explosive detection systems, baggage screening equipment installation, and associated facility modifications.
beneficiaries: Air travelers, airports, TSA screeners, the commercial aviation industry

name: Global Health, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Health and Human Services
description: Funds CDC's global health security and disease surveillance programs, including international HIV/AIDS, malaria, tuberculosis, and emerging infectious disease response and capacity building in partner countries.
beneficiaries: Populations in developing countries affected by infectious diseases, global public health systems, U.S. population protected from imported diseases

name: Operations and Support, U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services, Homeland Security
description: Funds USCIS operations primarily through immigration fees, covering adjudication of immigration benefit applications including citizenship, green cards, work permits, asylum, and refugee processing.
beneficiaries: Immigration applicants (naturalization, permanent residency, work authorization, asylum seekers), USCIS adjudicators

name: Military Construction, Air National Guard
description: Funds construction, renovation, and improvement of Air National Guard facilities including hangars, operations buildings, training facilities, and base infrastructure at ANG installations nationwide.
beneficiaries: Air National Guard units and personnel, host communities near ANG installations

name: Expenses, Corps of Engineers, Civil
description: Funds the Army Corps of Engineers' civil works operations and maintenance for existing water resources infrastructure including locks, dams, harbors, flood control structures, and navigation channels.
beneficiaries: Inland waterway shippers, flood-prone communities, commercial navigation users, hydropower consumers

name: Capital Investment Fund of the United States Agency for International Development, Funds Appropriated to the President
description: Funds USAID's information technology systems, capital equipment, and infrastructure investments needed to manage and deliver international development and humanitarian assistance programs.
beneficiaries: USAID staff and operations, foreign assistance program management and oversight

name: Agricultural Quarantine Inspection User Fee Account, Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service, Agriculture
description: Fee-funded account supporting APHIS inspections of passengers, cargo, and conveyances arriving from foreign countries to prevent introduction of foreign agricultural pests and diseases.
beneficiaries: U.S. agriculture industry, importers and international travelers (inspection services), domestic ecosystems protected from invasive species

name: Working Capital Fund, Departmental Administration, Energy
description: Revolving fund providing shared administrative services to DOE programs on a reimbursable basis, including IT, financial management, human resources, and procurement services.
beneficiaries: DOE programs and offices receiving centralized administrative services

name: Salaries and Expenses, Wage and Hour Division, Labor
description: Funds the enforcement of the Fair Labor Standards Act (minimum wage, overtime), Family and Medical Leave Act, Davis-Bacon Act, and other worker protection statutes by WHD investigators.
beneficiaries: Workers subject to minimum wage, overtime, and family leave protections; employers seeking compliance guidance

name: Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Contribution, United States Coast Guard, Homeland Security
description: The Coast Guard's annual accrual payment to the DoD Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund, pre-funding future TRICARE for Life benefits for Coast Guard retirees eligible for Medicare.
beneficiaries: Medicare-eligible Coast Guard retirees and their dependents receiving TRICARE for Life benefits

name: Working Capital Fund, Transportation Systems Center, Transportation
description: Revolving fund for the Volpe National Transportation Systems Center in Cambridge, MA, providing fee-for-service technical, research, and analytical support to DOT and other federal agencies on transportation issues.
beneficiaries: DOT modal administrations and other federal agencies contracting for transportation research and technical services

name: Salaries and Expenses, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Funds the core management and policy offices of the Department of the Treasury, including the Secretary's immediate office, economic policy, tax policy, international affairs, and domestic finance staff.
beneficiaries: Treasury Department leadership and policy staff, the broader financial and economic policymaking process

name: Southern Nevada Public Land Management, Bureau of Land Management
description: Funded by proceeds from the sale of BLM-managed public land in the Las Vegas Valley under the Southern Nevada Public Land Management Act, used for parks, trails, conservation, and capital improvements in Clark County.
beneficiaries: Clark County and Southern Nevada communities, state and local parks, Lake Tahoe restoration, Nevada education system

name: Operations and Support, Intelligence, Analysis, and Operations Coordination, Departmental Management, Homeland Security
description: Funds DHS's Office of Intelligence and Analysis, which provides intelligence products and threat assessments to DHS components, state/local partners, and the Intelligence Community on homeland security threats.
beneficiaries: DHS leadership and components, state and local fusion centers, Intelligence Community, homeland security decision-makers

name: Investor Protection Fund, Securities and Exchange Commission
description: Established by the Dodd-Frank Act to fund SEC whistleblower awards and the SEC Office of Inspector General, financed by monetary sanctions collected in SEC enforcement actions.
beneficiaries: SEC whistleblowers reporting securities law violations, investors protected by SEC enforcement, SEC OIG

name: Choice Neighborhoods Initiative, Public and Indian Housing Programs, Housing and Urban Development
description: Competitive grants to transform distressed public and HUD-assisted housing neighborhoods into mixed-income communities through housing replacement, supportive services, and neighborhood improvement strategies.
beneficiaries: Residents of severely distressed public housing, local housing authorities, neighborhood organizations, mixed-income community developers

name: Food Safety and Inspection Service, Agriculture
description: Funds USDA's public health agency responsible for ensuring the safety of domestic and imported meat, poultry, and processed egg products through in-plant inspection, testing, and regulatory enforcement.
beneficiaries: American consumers of meat, poultry, and egg products; slaughter and processing plant operators; public health

name: Entrepreneurial Development Programs, Small Business Administration
description: Funds SBA's network of small business development centers, women's business centers, SCORE mentoring, and other technical assistance and training programs for entrepreneurs and small business owners.
beneficiaries: Small business owners, aspiring entrepreneurs, women-owned businesses, veteran entrepreneurs, underserved communities

name: Payment to the United States Territories, Fiscal Assistance, Insular Affairs, Interior
description: Federal fiscal assistance payments to U.S. territories (American Samoa, Guam, USVI, CNMI) for general government operations, capital improvements, and technical assistance to territorial governments.
beneficiaries: Governments and residents of U.S. territories including American Samoa, Guam, U.S. Virgin Islands, and the Northern Mariana Islands

name: Procurement, Construction, and Improvements, U.S. Customs and Border Protection, Homeland Security
description: Funds CBP's acquisition of technology, equipment, and construction projects including border surveillance systems, non-intrusive inspection equipment, and facilities at ports of entry and along U.S. borders.
beneficiaries: CBP officers and agents, border communities, ports of entry, the border security mission

name: Overseas Humanitarian, Disaster, and Civic Aid, Defense
description: Funds DoD humanitarian assistance activities abroad including disaster relief, humanitarian mine action, foreign disaster response, and civic assistance projects conducted by U.S. military forces.
beneficiaries: Foreign civilian populations affected by disasters and humanitarian crises, partner nation militaries, U.S. combatant commands

name: United States Trustee System Fund, Justice
description: Fee-funded program within DOJ that oversees the administration of bankruptcy cases, monitors the conduct of bankruptcy parties, and combats bankruptcy fraud in federal courts across 48 states.
beneficiaries: Bankruptcy courts, creditors and debtors in bankruptcy proceedings, the integrity of the federal bankruptcy system

name: Community Development Financial Institutions Fund, Program Account, Treasury
description: Provides grants, loans, tax credit allocations (New Markets Tax Credit), and technical assistance to certified Community Development Financial Institutions serving low-income and underserved communities.
beneficiaries: CDFIs (community development banks, credit unions, loan funds, venture capital funds), low-income communities, underserved rural and urban areas

name: Major Research Equipment and Facilities Construction, National Science Foundation
description: Funds the construction and acquisition of major research facilities and equipment that provide unique capabilities for the U.S. scientific community, such as large telescopes, research vessels, and particle detectors.
beneficiaries: University researchers, the U.S. scientific community, NSF-funded large facility projects

name: Vaccine Injury Compensation Trust Fund, Treasury Managed, Health and Human Services
description: Trust fund financed by a per-dose excise tax on covered vaccines, paying compensation to individuals injured by childhood vaccines through the no-fault National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program.
beneficiaries: Individuals injured by covered vaccines, families of vaccine-injured children, vaccine manufacturers (liability protection)

name: Operations and Support, Office of the Inspector General, Homeland Security
description: Funds the DHS OIG's audits, inspections, and investigations of DHS programs and operations to promote efficiency, effectiveness, and accountability across the Department's components.
beneficiaries: DHS programs and taxpayers (accountability), Congress (oversight), DHS employees (whistleblower protections)

name: National Agricultural Statistics Service, Agriculture
description: Funds NASS's collection, analysis, and publication of official U.S. agricultural statistics including crop production, livestock inventories, prices, labor, and the Census of Agriculture.
beneficiaries: Farmers, ranchers, agribusinesses, commodity markets, agricultural policymakers, researchers

name: Gulf Coast Ecosystem Restoration Council
description: Independent federal entity established by the RESTORE Act to coordinate and fund ecosystem restoration in the Gulf Coast region using civil penalties from the Deepwater Horizon oil spill settlement.
beneficiaries: Gulf Coast states (TX, LA, MS, AL, FL), Gulf of Mexico marine ecosystems, coastal communities, fishing and tourism industries

name: Records Center Revolving Fund, National Archives and Records Administration
