description: Revolving fund financing NARA's Federal Records Centers, which store, service, and eventually dispose of non-current federal records on a fee-for-service basis for federal agencies.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies storing inactive records, NARA records center staff, government information management

name: Chronic Disease Prevention and Health Promotion, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Health and Human Services
description: Funds CDC programs addressing chronic diseases including heart disease, cancer, diabetes, obesity, tobacco use, and promoting healthy behaviors through surveillance, epidemiology, and community-based prevention programs.
beneficiaries: Americans at risk for chronic diseases, state and local health departments, community health organizations

name: Defense Production Act, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds DOE's use of Defense Production Act Title III authorities to strengthen domestic supply chains for energy-related critical materials, rare earth elements, and components essential to national defense and clean energy.
beneficiaries: Domestic manufacturers of critical energy materials, national defense supply chains, clean energy industry

name: Salaries and Expenses, Federal Payment to the District of Columbia Courts
description: Federal payment funding the operations of the D.C. court system including the D.C. Superior Court and D.C. Court of Appeals, covering judicial salaries, court personnel, and facility operations.
beneficiaries: D.C. judiciary, litigants and defendants in D.C. courts, District of Columbia residents relying on the court system

name: Research, Engineering and Development, Airport and Airway Trust Fund, Federal Aviation Administration, Transportation
description: Funds FAA's research, engineering, and development activities focused on improving aviation safety, air traffic management, airport technology, environmental sustainability, and unmanned aircraft systems integration.
beneficiaries: The aviation industry, air travelers, air traffic controllers, airport operators, aviation safety researchers

name: Environmental Health, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Health and Human Services
description: Funds CDC's National Center for Environmental Health and the Agency for Toxic Substances and Disease Registry (ATSDR), addressing environmental exposures (lead, asbestos, chemicals) and their health effects.
beneficiaries: Communities exposed to environmental hazards, children at risk of lead poisoning, populations near hazardous waste sites

name: Salaries and Expenses, Office of Terrorism and Financial Intelligence, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Funds TFI's mission to combat terrorist financing, money laundering, WMD proliferation finance, and other national security threats through sanctions, intelligence, and enforcement under OFAC, FinCEN, and related offices.
beneficiaries: National security community, the global financial system, populations in sanctioned regimes, law enforcement partners

name: Health Workforce, Health Resources and Services Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Funds HRSA programs to strengthen the health workforce through the National Health Service Corps, nursing education, graduate medical education, area health education centers, and health professions training grants.
beneficiaries: Health professional students (loan repayment/scholarships), underserved communities gaining health providers, teaching hospitals, nursing programs

name: Construction, Major Projects, Departmental Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds major construction projects (over $20 million) for VA medical facilities, national cemeteries, and other VA infrastructure including new hospitals, major renovations, and seismic corrections.
beneficiaries: Veterans needing modernized health care facilities, VA medical centers undergoing major renovation, national cemetery system

name: Ocean Energy Management, Bureau of Ocean Energy Management, Interior
description: Funds BOEM's management of energy and mineral resources on the Outer Continental Shelf, including oil and gas lease sales, renewable (offshore wind) energy leasing, resource evaluation, and environmental assessments.
beneficiaries: Offshore energy developers, coastal states receiving revenue sharing, the federal treasury (lease revenues), marine environment

name: Assistance for Farmers and Ranchers Account, Farm Service Agency, Agriculture
description: Emergency and supplemental funding for direct payments and market facilitation to farmers and ranchers affected by trade disruptions, natural disasters, and commodity price downturns.
beneficiaries: Farmers and ranchers affected by trade retaliation, natural disasters, or market disruptions

name: Salaries and Expenses, Departmental Management, Commerce
description: Funds the Department of Commerce's central management offices including the Secretary's office, general counsel, legislative affairs, public affairs, and departmental administrative services.
beneficiaries: Commerce Department leadership and staff, the Department's bureaus receiving management support

name: Substance Abuse Prevention, Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Funds SAMHSA programs for substance abuse prevention, early intervention, community-based prevention strategies, and the Center for Substance Abuse Prevention's grants and technical assistance.
beneficiaries: Communities and youth at risk for substance abuse, state substance abuse prevention agencies, community-based prevention organizations

name: Peacekeeping Operations, Funds Appropriated to the President
description: Funds U.S. voluntary contributions to international peacekeeping activities managed by the State Department, including multilateral peacekeeping forces, conflict resolution, and security sector reform in conflict zones.
beneficiaries: Populations in conflict-affected countries, international peacekeeping forces, U.S. foreign policy objectives for global stability

name: Assistance for Europe, Eurasia, and Central Asia, Funds Appropriated to the President, US Agency for International Development
description: Funds U.S. bilateral assistance to countries of the former Soviet Union and Central/Eastern Europe for democracy promotion, economic reform, rule of law, civil society development, and security cooperation.
beneficiaries: Citizens of post-Soviet and Central/Eastern European states, democratic institutions, civil society organizations in the region

name: Procurement, Construction, and Improvements, Coast Guard, Homeland Security
description: Funds the Coast Guard's acquisition of new cutters, aircraft, boats, shore facilities, and information technology systems to modernize the service's capital assets.
beneficiaries: Coast Guard personnel operating modernized assets, maritime safety and security, search and rescue operations

name: Immunization and Respiratory Diseases, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Health and Human Services
description: Funds CDC's National Center for Immunization and Respiratory Diseases, supporting the Vaccines for Children program, influenza surveillance and pandemic preparedness, and immunization infrastructure grants to states.
beneficiaries: Children receiving recommended vaccinations, state immunization programs, populations at risk for influenza and respiratory diseases

name: Administration Expenses, Export-Import Bank of the United States
description: Funds the administrative operations of the Export-Import Bank, the official export credit agency of the United States, which provides loans, guarantees, and insurance to support U.S. export transactions.
beneficiaries: U.S. exporters (especially small businesses), Ex-Im Bank staff, foreign buyers of U.S. goods and services

name: Research and Development, Science and Technology Directorate, Homeland Security
description: Funds DHS S&T's research and development of technologies to address homeland security threats including chemical/biological/radiological/nuclear detection, cybersecurity, border security, and first responder capabilities.
beneficiaries: DHS operational components, first responders, homeland security technology developers, critical infrastructure operators

name: Contribution to the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development, Treasury
description: U.S. capital contributions and subscription payments to the World Bank (IBRD), supporting its lending operations for economic development and poverty reduction in middle-income and creditworthy developing countries.
beneficiaries: Developing countries receiving World Bank development loans, global economic development, U.S. influence in multilateral finance

name: Native American Programs, Public and Indian Housing, Housing and Urban Development
description: Funds the Indian Housing Block Grant program under NAHASDA and other housing assistance for Native American and Alaska Native communities, including new construction, rehabilitation, and housing-related infrastructure.
beneficiaries: Federally recognized Indian tribes and tribally designated housing entities, Native American and Alaska Native families

name: NIH Innovation, CURES Act, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Mandatory funding authorized by the 21st Century Cures Act for three NIH innovation initiatives: the All of Us precision medicine cohort, the BRAIN Initiative, and the Cancer Moonshot.
beneficiaries: Biomedical researchers, patients benefiting from precision medicine and cancer research advances, neuroscience community

name: Democracy Fund, State
description: Funds State Department programs promoting democratic governance, human rights, free media, rule of law, and civil society development in countries transitioning to or consolidating democracy.
beneficiaries: Pro-democracy organizations abroad, human rights defenders, independent media, citizens in authoritarian and transitioning states

name: Operation and Research, Liquidation of Contract Authorization, Limitation on Obligations, Highway Trust Fund, Transportation
description: Liquidating appropriation that pays the obligations incurred under the Federal-Aid Highway Program's contract authority from the Highway Trust Fund for highway construction, maintenance, and safety projects.
beneficiaries: State departments of transportation, highway users, construction contractors, communities served by federal-aid highways

name: Colorado River Basins Power Marketing Fund, Western Area Power Administration, Energy
description: Revolving fund for the Western Area Power Administration to market and transmit hydroelectric power generated at federal dams in the Colorado River Basin, including Hoover Dam and Glen Canyon Dam.
beneficiaries: Public utilities and cooperatives purchasing federal hydropower in western states, electricity ratepayers in the Colorado River Basin

name: Commissary Stores Surcharge Program, Defense
description: Funds derived from the 5% surcharge on commissary store sales, used to cover the costs of building, maintaining, and renovating Defense Commissary Agency retail store facilities on military installations.
beneficiaries: Active-duty military members, retirees, and their families who shop at military commissaries

name: Contract Support Costs, Bureau of Indian Affairs and Bureau of Indian Education, Interior
description: Funds the administrative overhead costs incurred by Indian tribes and tribal organizations when they assume responsibility for operating federal programs under the Indian Self-Determination Act (P.L. 93-638 contracts).
beneficiaries: Federally recognized Indian tribes operating self-determination contracts/compacts, tribal administrative operations

name: Expenses and Refunds, Inspection and Grading of Farm Products, Agricultural Marketing Service, Agriculture
description: Fee-funded account for USDA's voluntary grading, inspection, and certification services for agricultural commodities (grain, cotton, dairy, fruits, vegetables) based on official quality standards.
beneficiaries: Agricultural producers, commodity handlers and processors, exporters, consumers benefiting from quality standards

name: Credit for Previously-Owned Clean Vehicles, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds the IRS's administration of the used clean vehicle tax credit created by the Inflation Reduction Act, providing up to $4,000 for qualifying previously-owned electric and plug-in hybrid vehicles.
beneficiaries: Buyers of qualifying used electric vehicles, low-to-moderate income households, used EV dealers

name: National Service Life Insurance Fund, Veterans Affairs
description: Trust fund holding premiums and paying benefits for the National Service Life Insurance program, which provided low-cost life insurance to World War II and Korean War-era veterans and service members.
beneficiaries: WWII and Korean War-era veterans and their beneficiaries holding NSLI policies

name: Operations and Training, Maritime Administration, Transportation
description: Funds MARAD's administration of maritime programs including the U.S. Merchant Marine Academy, mariner training, port and intermodal operations, ship disposal, and sealift readiness.
beneficiaries: U.S. Merchant Marine Academy midshipmen, merchant mariners, the U.S. maritime industry, national defense sealift capability

name: Pipeline Safety, Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration, Transportation
description: Funds PHMSA's pipeline safety program including inspection of natural gas and hazardous liquid pipelines, enforcement, state pipeline safety grants, research, and emergency response.
beneficiaries: Communities near pipelines, pipeline operators, state pipeline safety offices, the 2.7 million miles of U.S. pipeline infrastructure

name: Construction of Research Facilities, National Institute of Standards and Technology, Commerce
description: Funds construction, renovation, and maintenance of NIST's research laboratories and facilities in Gaithersburg, MD and Boulder, CO to support measurement science and standards research.
beneficiaries: NIST researchers, U.S. industry relying on measurement standards, the broader scientific and engineering community

name: Housing Trust Fund, Community Planning and Development, Housing and Urban Development
description: Provides formula grants to states for the production, preservation, rehabilitation, and operation of rental housing for extremely low-income and very low-income households, funded by assessments on Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.
beneficiaries: Extremely low-income and very low-income renter households, state housing agencies, affordable housing developers

name: Strategic Petroleum Reserve, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds the operation, maintenance, and management of the Strategic Petroleum Reserve, the world's largest government-owned emergency crude oil stockpile stored in underground salt caverns along the Gulf Coast.
beneficiaries: The U.S. economy and energy security, consumers during oil supply disruptions, petroleum markets

name: Public Safety Officers Benefits, Office of Justice Programs, Justice
description: Provides death and disability benefits to public safety officers (or their survivors) killed or permanently disabled in the line of duty, and education benefits for families of fallen officers.
beneficiaries: Families of fallen law enforcement officers, firefighters, and first responders; officers permanently disabled in the line of duty

name: Payments to Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund, Health Care Fraud and Abuse, Health and Human Services
description: Transfers to the Medicare Hospital Insurance Trust Fund from amounts recovered through the Health Care Fraud and Abuse Control Program, which combats fraud, waste, and abuse in Medicare and Medicaid.
beneficiaries: Medicare trust fund solvency, Medicare beneficiaries, taxpayers funding the health care system

name: Corporate Capital Account, United States International Development Finance Corporation
description: Capital account of the DFC (successor to OPIC), providing equity investments, loans, loan guarantees, and political risk insurance to support private-sector investment in developing countries aligned with U.S. foreign policy.
beneficiaries: Private investors in developing countries, developing country populations, U.S. foreign policy and development objectives

name: Operations and Support, Science and Technology Directorate, Homeland Security
description: Funds the operational activities and management of DHS S&T Directorate, including laboratory operations (national biodefense, chemical security), testing and evaluation centers, and workforce costs.
beneficiaries: DHS operational components receiving S&T support, homeland security research labs, first responder technology programs

name: Investigations, Corps of Engineers-Civil
description: Funds the Army Corps of Engineers' feasibility studies, planning investigations, and engineering assessments for potential new water resources projects including flood control, navigation, and ecosystem restoration.
beneficiaries: Communities seeking federal water resources projects, Corps planners and engineers, Congress (project authorization decisions)

name: Salaries and Expenses, Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Funds TIGTA's independent oversight of the Internal Revenue Service through audits, investigations, and inspections to promote integrity and efficiency in federal tax administration.
beneficiaries: Taxpayers (IRS accountability), IRS employees (integrity investigations), Congress (oversight of IRS)

name: Grants and Administration, National Endowment for the Humanities, National Foundation on the Arts and Humanities
description: Funds NEH grants supporting research, education, preservation, and public programming in the humanities, including history, literature, philosophy, languages, and cultural heritage.
beneficiaries: Humanities scholars and researchers, museums, libraries, universities, state humanities councils, the general public

name: Education Construction, Bureau of Indian Affairs and Bureau of Indian Education, Interior
description: Funds the construction, replacement, and renovation of Bureau of Indian Education school facilities on or near Indian reservations, addressing severely deteriorated school infrastructure.
beneficiaries: Native American students in BIE-funded schools, tribal communities with aging school facilities

name: Expenses, Presidio Trust
description: Funds the Presidio Trust, a federal corporation managing the Presidio of San Francisco (a former Army post within Golden Gate National Recreation Area) as a self-sustaining national park site through leasing and programming.
beneficiaries: Visitors to the Presidio, tenants and lessees, the San Francisco community, the National Park System

name: Office of Medicare Hearings and Appeals, Office of the Secretary, Health and Human Services
description: Funds the administrative law judges and staff who adjudicate Medicare and Medicaid appeals at the third level (after initial determination and reconsideration), reducing the backlog of disputed claims.
beneficiaries: Medicare beneficiaries and providers appealing denied claims, Medicaid appellants, the Medicare appeals system

name: Reimbursements to Federal Reserve Banks, Bureau of the Fiscal Service, Treasury
description: Reimburses Federal Reserve Banks for the costs of acting as fiscal agents of the United States, including processing Treasury securities, government checks, electronic fund transfers, and currency operations.
beneficiaries: Federal Reserve Banks performing fiscal agent duties, Treasury operations, holders of government securities

name: Salaries and Expenses, General Administration, Justice
description: Funds the general management and administrative offices of the Department of Justice, including the Attorney General's office, Deputy AG, Associate AG, and central administrative support functions.
beneficiaries: DOJ leadership and management staff, the department's litigating divisions and components

name: Grants and Administration, National Endowment for the Arts
description: Funds NEA grants supporting the creation, presentation, and dissemination of the arts in America, including visual arts, music, theater, dance, literature, media arts, and arts education programs.
beneficiaries: Artists, arts organizations, state arts agencies, community arts programs, schools with arts education, the American public

name: Ferry Service for Rural Communities, Federal Transit Administration, Transportation
description: Provides formula and competitive grants to support essential ferry services in rural areas where ferries provide critical transportation links for communities without bridge or road alternatives.
beneficiaries: Rural communities dependent on ferry service, ferry operators in rural areas, island and remote communities

name: Birth Defects, Developmental Disabilities, Disabilities and Health, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Health and Human Services
description: Funds CDC's National Center on Birth Defects and Developmental Disabilities, conducting surveillance, research, and prevention of birth defects, fetal alcohol spectrum disorders, developmental disabilities, and blood disorders.
beneficiaries: Children and families affected by birth defects and developmental disabilities, state health departments, disability advocacy organizations

name: Historic Preservation Fund, National Park Service
description: Funded by Outer Continental Shelf mineral receipts, providing matching grants to State Historic Preservation Officers, Tribal Historic Preservation Officers, and the Save America's Treasures program for historic preservation activities.
beneficiaries: State and tribal historic preservation offices, owners of National Register-listed properties, historic sites and landmarks

name: Operations and Administration, Bureau of Industry and Security, Commerce
description: Funds the Bureau of Industry and Security's administration of U.S. export controls on dual-use goods and technologies, enforcement against illegal exports, and industry compliance programs to protect national security.
beneficiaries: U.S. exporters of controlled technologies, national security (preventing WMD proliferation), defense industrial base

name: Reforestation Trust Fund, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Trust fund financed by tariffs on imported wood products, funding reforestation and timber stand improvement on National Forest System lands that have been harvested or damaged by fire, insects, or disease.
beneficiaries: National Forest lands needing reforestation, timber-dependent communities, forest ecosystems

name: Self-Sufficiency Programs, Public and Indian Housing, Housing and Urban Development
description: Funds programs helping public housing residents and Housing Choice Voucher recipients achieve economic self-sufficiency through case management, job training, education, financial literacy, and supportive services.
beneficiaries: Public housing residents and Section 8 voucher holders seeking economic independence, housing authority self-sufficiency coordinators

name: Salaries and Expenses, Financial Crimes Enforcement Network, Treasury
description: Funds FinCEN's mission to safeguard the financial system from illicit use by collecting, analyzing, and disseminating financial intelligence from Bank Secrecy Act reports (SARs, CTRs) and administering AML/CFT regulations.
beneficiaries: Law enforcement agencies using financial intelligence, financial institutions subject to BSA, the integrity of the U.S. financial system

name: Immigration User Fee Account, BICE, Homeland Security
description: Fee-funded account from immigration inspection user fees charged to air and sea passengers, funding the immigration inspection services provided by CBP at ports of entry.
beneficiaries: International travelers entering the U.S., CBP immigration inspectors at airports and seaports

name: Safe Streets and Roads for All, Office of the Secretary, Transportation
description: Competitive grant program created by the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law funding local and regional efforts to develop and implement comprehensive safety action plans to prevent roadway deaths and serious injuries.
beneficiaries: Metropolitan planning organizations, local and tribal governments, Vision Zero communities, pedestrians and cyclists

name: National Institute of Nursing Research, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Funds research on clinical and community-based nursing practices, including symptom science, self-management of chronic conditions, palliative and end-of-life care, and health promotion and disease prevention.
beneficiaries: Nursing science researchers, patients benefiting from evidence-based nursing practices, the nursing profession

name: Natural Gas Distribution Infrastructure Safety and Modernization Grant Program, Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration, Transportation
description: Bipartisan Infrastructure Law grant program funding the repair, rehabilitation, and replacement of aging natural gas distribution pipelines, especially legacy cast iron and bare steel pipes that pose safety risks.
beneficiaries: Municipal and community-owned gas utilities, communities with aging gas infrastructure, pipeline safety

name: Rural Economic Development Grants, Rural Business-Cooperative Service, Agriculture
description: Provides zero-interest loans to rural electric cooperatives and telephone utilities, which re-lend the funds to local businesses and communities for job creation and economic development in rural areas.
beneficiaries: Rural businesses, rural electric and telephone cooperatives, rural communities seeking economic development

name: Salaries and Expenses, Employee Benefits Security Administration, Labor
description: Funds EBSA's enforcement and regulatory oversight of employee benefit plans under ERISA, including pension plans, 401(k)s, and employer-sponsored health insurance covering approximately 150 million Americans.
beneficiaries: Workers and retirees in employer-sponsored retirement and health plans, plan fiduciaries, plan participants

name: Program Administration, Employment and Training Administration, Labor
description: Funds ETA's administration and oversight of federal employment and training programs including workforce development grants, Job Corps, apprenticeship, unemployment insurance, and Trade Adjustment Assistance.
beneficiaries: ETA staff administering workforce programs, state workforce agencies, job seekers and workers receiving training services

name: Capital Improvement and Maintenance, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Funds the maintenance, repair, and improvement of Forest Service infrastructure including roads, bridges, trails, recreation facilities, administrative buildings, and dams on National Forest System lands.
beneficiaries: National Forest visitors, Forest Service employees, rural communities dependent on forest roads, recreation users

name: Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Contribution, Reserve Personnel, Air Force
description: The Air Force Reserve's annual accrual payment to the DoD Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund, pre-funding future TRICARE for Life benefits for Air Force Reserve retirees eligible for Medicare.
beneficiaries: Medicare-eligible Air Force Reserve retirees and their dependents

name: Housing for the Elderly, Housing Programs, Housing and Urban Development
description: Funds the Section 202 Supportive Housing for the Elderly program, providing capital advances and project-based rental assistance to develop and operate affordable housing with supportive services for very low-income elderly.
beneficiaries: Very low-income elderly households (age 62+), nonprofit sponsors of elderly housing, elderly residents needing supportive services

name: Contribution to the African Development Fund, Treasury
description: U.S. contributions to the African Development Fund, the concessional lending window of the African Development Bank Group, providing low-interest loans and grants to the poorest African countries.
beneficiaries: Low-income African nations receiving concessional development financing, U.S. influence in African development policy

name: Salaries and Expenses, Office of the Secretary, Transportation
description: Funds the Office of the Secretary of Transportation's leadership, policy development, budget formulation, congressional affairs, and oversight of DOT modal administrations.
beneficiaries: DOT Secretary's office and immediate staff, transportation policy process, DOT modal administrations

name: Federal Citizen Services Fund, General Services Administration
description: Funds GSA's citizen-facing digital services including USA.gov, the Federal Citizen Information Center, and government-wide shared digital platforms that help the public access federal information and services.
beneficiaries: Members of the public accessing federal government information, federal agencies using shared digital platforms

name: Tribal Trust Fund, Bureau of Trust Funds Administration, Interior
description: Holds and manages funds held in trust by the federal government for individual Indian beneficiaries and Indian tribes, including income from natural resources on Indian trust lands.
beneficiaries: Individual Indian trust fund account holders, federally recognized Indian tribes with trust fund accounts

name: Indian Education, Office of Elementary and Secondary Education, Education
description: Funds formula grants and discretionary programs supporting the education of American Indian and Alaska Native students in public schools and BIE schools, including culturally relevant supplemental instruction.
beneficiaries: American Indian and Alaska Native students in public and BIE schools, tribal education departments, LEAs serving Native students

name: Emergency Conservation Program, Farm Service Agency, Agriculture
description: Provides cost-share assistance to farmers and ranchers for restoring farmland damaged by natural disasters, including debris removal, grading, fencing, water conservation measures, and restoring livestock water facilities.
beneficiaries: Farmers and ranchers whose land has been damaged by floods, hurricanes, drought, or other natural disasters

name: Service-Disabled Veterans Insurance Fund, Veterans Affairs
description: Trust fund providing life insurance coverage (Service-Disabled Veterans Insurance / RH Insurance) to veterans with service-connected disabilities who may be unable to obtain private insurance.
beneficiaries: Veterans with service-connected disabilities and their beneficiaries

name: Energy Assistance Payments, Rural Business-Cooperative Service, Agriculture
description: Provides grants and payments to rural communities and utilities to address energy costs, support energy efficiency improvements, and assist rural residents with energy expenses.
beneficiaries: Rural electric cooperatives, rural communities with high energy costs, low-income rural households

name: Buildings and Facilities, Federal Prison System, Justice
description: Funds the construction, modernization, and repair of federal correctional institutions, including new facility construction, major renovations, and site acquisitions for the Bureau of Prisons.
beneficiaries: Federal prison staff and inmates, BOP facility infrastructure, communities hosting federal prisons

name: Payment to States for the Child Care and Development Block Grant, Administration for Children and Families, Health and Human Services
description: Discretionary funding for the Child Care and Development Block Grant, providing subsidies to help low-income working families afford child care and improving the quality of child care settings.
beneficiaries: Low-income working families needing child care assistance, child care providers, states administering CCDBG programs

name: Afghanistan Security Forces Fund, Army
description: Funded the training, equipping, and sustaining of the Afghan National Defense and Security Forces during the U.S. military mission in Afghanistan (winding down after 2021 withdrawal).
beneficiaries: Afghan National Army and National Police (historical), U.S. and coalition forces (during active operations)

name: Essential Air Service and Rural Airport Improvement Fund, Office of the Secretary, Transportation
description: Subsidizes commercial air service to small and rural communities that would otherwise lose scheduled airline service, ensuring basic transportation connectivity under the Essential Air Service program.
beneficiaries: Small and rural communities at risk of losing air service, regional airlines providing EAS routes, rural air travelers

name: Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Contribution, Reserve Personnel, Navy
description: The Navy Reserve's annual accrual payment to the DoD Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund, pre-funding future TRICARE for Life benefits for Navy Reserve retirees eligible for Medicare.
beneficiaries: Medicare-eligible Navy Reserve retirees and their dependents

name: Student and Exchange Visitor Program, Border and Transportation Security, Homeland Security
description: Fee-funded program administering the SEVIS (Student and Exchange Visitor Information System) database that tracks international students and exchange visitors on F, M, and J visas in the United States.
beneficiaries: International students and exchange visitors, U.S. educational institutions certified to enroll foreign students, DHS immigration enforcement

name: Debt Collection Fund, Bureau of the Fiscal Service, Treasury
description: Revolving fund used by the Bureau of the Fiscal Service to collect delinquent non-tax debts owed to the federal government through the Treasury Offset Program and cross-servicing referrals.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies recovering delinquent debts, taxpayers (reducing losses from uncollected debts)

name: Payment to National Service Trust, Corporation for National and Community Service
description: Funds the National Service Trust that provides Segal AmeriCorps Education Awards to AmeriCorps members upon completion of their national service term, used for higher education costs or student loan repayment.
beneficiaries: AmeriCorps members completing service terms, educational institutions where awards are applied

name: Nutrition Programs Administration, Food and Nutrition Service, Agriculture
description: Funds the administrative operations of the Food and Nutrition Service, which oversees SNAP, WIC, school meals, and other federal nutrition assistance programs serving tens of millions of Americans.
beneficiaries: FNS staff and operations, the administration and oversight of federal nutrition assistance programs

name: Departmental Operations, Office of the Secretary, Interior
description: Funds the Department of the Interior's central office operations including the Secretary's office, policy analysis, budget, communications, and management functions overseeing DOI's bureaus.
beneficiaries: DOI Secretary's office and senior staff, departmental bureaus receiving central management support

name: Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health-NIH, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Funds ARPA-H, a new agency within NIH modeled after DARPA, to drive transformative biomedical and health research breakthroughs by funding high-risk, high-reward projects to accelerate cures and health innovations.
beneficiaries: Patients with serious diseases, biomedical researchers pursuing breakthrough therapies, the U.S. health innovation ecosystem

name: National Center for Complementary and Integrative Health, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Funds scientific research on complementary and integrative health approaches such as natural products, mind-body practices, and their safety, efficacy, and mechanisms of action.
beneficiaries: Researchers in complementary medicine, patients using integrative health approaches, health care providers

name: Integrated Activities, National Institute of Food and Agriculture, Agriculture
description: Funds NIFA competitive grants that integrate agricultural research, education, and extension activities, including the Agriculture and Food Research Initiative and water quality and pest management programs.
beneficiaries: Land-grant universities, agricultural researchers, extension agents, farmers adopting research-based practices

name: Non-Proliferation, Anti-Terrorism, Demining and Related Programs, International Security Assistance, State
description: Funds State Department programs to prevent WMD proliferation, counter terrorism, clear landmines and unexploded ordnance, and support small arms/light weapons destruction in post-conflict countries.
beneficiaries: Populations in mine-affected and post-conflict countries, non-proliferation verification regimes, counter-terrorism partners

name: National Parks and Public Land Legacy Restoration Fund, Department-Wide Programs, Interior
description: Established by the Great American Outdoors Act, providing mandatory funding to address the deferred maintenance backlog at national parks, wildlife refuges, BLM lands, BIE schools, and other DOI facilities.
beneficiaries: National parks and public lands with deferred maintenance, park visitors, BIE students in aging schools

name: Office of Natural Resources Revenue, Department-Wide Programs, Interior
description: Funds ONRR's collection, verification, and disbursement of federal and Indian energy and mineral revenue from oil, gas, coal, and renewable energy production on federal and Indian lands.
beneficiaries: Federal treasury, states receiving mineral revenue sharing, Indian tribes and individual Indian mineral owners

name: Construction, Bureau of Indian Affairs
description: Funds BIA construction projects including roads, bridges, dams, irrigation systems, and administrative facilities on Indian reservations serving federally recognized tribes.
beneficiaries: Indian reservation communities, tribal governments, BIA road and infrastructure users

name: Gallaudet University, Special Institutions for Persons with Disabilities, Education
description: Direct federal appropriation to Gallaudet University, the world's only university specifically designed for deaf and hard-of-hearing students, supporting its educational programs, research, and campus operations in Washington, D.C.
beneficiaries: Deaf and hard-of-hearing students, Gallaudet faculty and staff, the deaf community nationally and internationally

name: Consumer Product Safety Commission
description: Independent federal agency protecting the public from unreasonable risks of injury and death from consumer products through safety standards, recalls, research, and enforcement.
beneficiaries: American consumers, particularly children (toy and nursery product safety), manufacturers seeking compliance guidance

name: Salaries and Expenses, Consumer Product Safety Commission
description: Funds the CPSC's operating costs including compliance officers, laboratory testing, hazard identification, recall coordination, import surveillance, and public information activities.
beneficiaries: CPSC staff, American consumers protected by product safety standards and recalls

name: Salaries and Expenses, Alcohol and Tobacco Tax and Trade Bureau, Treasury
description: Funds TTB's regulation of the alcohol and tobacco industries, including collection of excise taxes, permitting, labeling and advertising review, and trade practice enforcement.
beneficiaries: Federal revenue (excise tax collection), alcohol and tobacco industry participants, consumers (product integrity)

name: National Parks and Public Land Legacy Restoration Fund, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Great American Outdoors Act mandatory funding addressing the deferred maintenance backlog on National Forest System lands including recreation facilities, roads, bridges, trails, and administrative facilities.
beneficiaries: National Forest visitors, Forest Service staff, rural communities dependent on forest infrastructure

name: Office of Inspector General, General Administration, Justice
description: Funds the DOJ OIG's independent audits, investigations, inspections, and reviews of DOJ programs and personnel, including the FBI, BOP, DEA, ATF, and U.S. Marshals Service.
beneficiaries: DOJ programs (accountability), Congress (oversight), DOJ employees (whistleblower protections), the public

name: Salaries and Expenses, Office of Workers' Compensation Programs, Labor
description: Funds OWCP's administration of workers' compensation programs including the Federal Employees' Compensation Act (FECA), Longshore and Harbor Workers' Act, Black Lung Benefits Act, and Energy Employees Program.
beneficiaries: Injured federal employees, coal miners with black lung, longshore workers, energy workers exposed to radiation or toxic substances

name: Working Capital Fund, National Institute of Standards and Technology, Commerce
description: Revolving fund enabling NIST to perform measurements, calibrations, standards testing, and research on a reimbursable basis for other government agencies, private industry, and international partners.
beneficiaries: Government agencies and private sector clients receiving NIST calibration and measurement services

name: Office of Inspector General, Management and Administration, Housing and Urban Development
description: Funds the HUD OIG's independent oversight of HUD programs through audits, investigations, and evaluations to detect and prevent fraud, waste, and abuse in housing and community development programs.
beneficiaries: HUD program beneficiaries, taxpayers, Congress (oversight), HUD employees

name: Recreation Fee Demonstration Program, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Allows the Forest Service to collect and retain recreation fees at designated sites, using the revenue for facility maintenance, visitor services, resource protection, and recreation improvements on National Forest lands.
beneficiaries: National Forest recreation visitors, Forest Service recreation sites, local communities near popular forest recreation areas

name: Operations and Support, Countering Weapons of Mass Destruction Office, Homeland Security
description: Funds the DHS CWMD Office's coordination of efforts to detect, prevent, and respond to chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear threats to the homeland.
beneficiaries: The American public (CBRN threat protection), DHS components, first responders, public health agencies

name: Department of Defense, Education Benefits Fund
description: Accrual fund for DoD contributions to the Post-9/11 GI Bill and other military education benefit programs, pre-funding the estimated future education costs for service members.
beneficiaries: Active-duty service members, veterans, and their dependents using military education benefits

name: State Grants and Demonstration, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, Health and Human Services
description: Funds CMS grants to states for Medicaid program administration, insurance marketplace operations, and demonstration projects testing innovative approaches to health care delivery and financing.
beneficiaries: State Medicaid agencies, health insurance marketplace operations, populations served by Medicaid demonstration waivers

name: Public Transportation Emergency Relief Program, Federal Transit Administration, Transportation
description: Provides emergency funding to transit agencies for the repair, replacement, and restoration of public transportation systems damaged by natural disasters and other emergencies.
beneficiaries: Transit agencies whose systems are damaged by disasters, transit riders in disaster-affected areas

name: State Maritime Academy Operations, Maritime Administration, Transportation
description: Supports the six state maritime academies (California, Great Lakes, Maine, Massachusetts, New York, Texas) that train future merchant marine officers, providing funding for training ships, fuel, and student stipends.
beneficiaries: Students at state maritime academies, the U.S. merchant marine, maritime industry employers

name: Salaries and Expenses, the White House, Executive Office of the President
description: Funds the compensation, travel, and operating expenses of the White House Office staff who directly support the President, including senior advisors, policy councils, and White House operations.
beneficiaries: The President, White House senior staff and support personnel, the executive policymaking process

name: Emergency Connectivity Fund for Educational Connections and Devices, Federal Communications Commission
description: American Rescue Plan program providing FCC-administered funding for schools and libraries to purchase laptops, tablets, Wi-Fi hotspots, and broadband connections for students and library patrons lacking connectivity.
beneficiaries: K-12 students and teachers without adequate home internet, libraries, schools in low-income areas

name: Global Environment Facility, Treasury
description: U.S. contributions to the Global Environment Facility, a multilateral trust fund financing projects in developing countries on biodiversity, climate change, international waters, land degradation, and chemicals/waste.
beneficiaries: Developing countries implementing environmental projects, global biodiversity and climate change mitigation, U.S. environmental diplomacy

name: State Supplemental Fees, Social Security Administration
description: Funds SSA's administration of federally administered state supplementary payments made on behalf of states that supplement the federal Supplemental Security Income (SSI) benefit for aged, blind, and disabled individuals.
beneficiaries: SSI recipients in states choosing federal administration of state supplements, SSA staff processing supplementary payments

name: Rural Energy For America Program, Rural Business-Cooperative Service, Agriculture
description: Provides grants and loan guarantees to rural small businesses and agricultural producers for energy efficiency improvements and renewable energy systems installations under the Farm Bill.
beneficiaries: Rural small businesses, agricultural producers, rural communities benefiting from clean energy and reduced energy costs

name: Washington Metropolitan Area Transit Authority, Federal Transit Administration, Transportation
description: Dedicated federal funding for the Washington Metropolitan Area Transit Authority (WMATA/Metro) capital and preventive maintenance needs, subject to matching requirements from D.C., Maryland, and Virginia.
beneficiaries: WMATA/Metro riders in the D.C. metropolitan area, the Metro transit system, federal employees commuting via Metro

name: Salaries and Expenses, Office of Management and Budget, Executive Office of the President
description: Funds OMB's operations including federal budget preparation, management oversight, regulatory review (OIRA), legislative clearance, and information policy for the President and executive agencies.
beneficiaries: The President, executive branch agencies (budget and management guidance), Congress (budget submissions), the regulatory process

name: Forest Service Operations, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Funds the Forest Service's general administrative operations and management of the 193-million-acre National Forest System including leadership, planning, financial management, and workforce support.
beneficiaries: Forest Service employees, National Forest System administration, the public using national forests

name: Public Wireless Supply Chain Innovation Fund, National Telecommunications and Information Administration, Commerce
description: Funds grants for research, development, and deployment of open and interoperable wireless network technologies (Open RAN) to strengthen the domestic 5G supply chain and reduce dependence on foreign vendors.
beneficiaries: U.S. telecommunications equipment developers, wireless carriers, 5G supply chain security, Open RAN ecosystem

name: Federal Assistance, Countering Weapons of Mass Destruction Office, Homeland Security
description: Funds grants to state and local governments for equipment, training, and exercises to prepare for and respond to chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, and explosives (CBRNE) incidents.
beneficiaries: State and local first responders, emergency management agencies, communities preparing for WMD threats

name: Salaries and Expenses, National Telecommunications and Information Administration, Commerce
description: Funds NTIA's operations as the President's principal advisor on telecommunications policy, managing federal spectrum, broadband programs, and internet policy.
beneficiaries: Federal spectrum users, broadband expansion programs, the telecommunications policy process

name: Construction, National Park Service, Interior
description: Funds construction, major rehabilitation, and facility improvement projects across the National Park System, including roads, bridges, buildings, water and wastewater systems, and visitor facilities.
beneficiaries: National Park visitors, NPS infrastructure, park communities

name: Office for Civil Rights, Departmental Management, Education
description: Funds the Office for Civil Rights' enforcement of federal civil rights laws in education, including Title VI (race), Title IX (sex), Section 504 (disability), and ADA compliance in federally funded educational institutions.
beneficiaries: Students protected from discrimination in K-12 and postsecondary education, educational institutions, complaint filers

name: Administrative Expenses, Energy Employees Occupational Illness Compensation Fund, Office of Workers' Compensation Programs, Labor
description: Funds the Labor Department's administration of the Energy Employees Occupational Illness Compensation Program, providing benefits to DOE workers and contractors who became ill from radiation, beryllium, or silica exposure.
beneficiaries: Former DOE workers and contractors (or their survivors) with occupational illnesses from nuclear weapons production

name: Salaries and Expenses, National Security Division, Justice
description: Funds DOJ's National Security Division, which prosecutes espionage, terrorism, cyber-enabled national security threats, and enforces FISA, export control, and foreign agent registration laws.
beneficiaries: National security, counter-terrorism and counter-espionage efforts, FISA Court operations

name: Claims for Contract Disputes (Indefinite), Bureau of the Fiscal Service, Treasury
description: Permanent indefinite appropriation to pay judgments and settlements arising from Contract Disputes Act cases adjudicated by the Civilian Board of Contract Appeals or the Court of Federal Claims.
beneficiaries: Government contractors awarded judgments in contract disputes, federal agencies resolving contract claims

name: National Transportation Safety Board
description: Independent federal agency investigating civil transportation accidents in aviation, surface (rail, highway, pipeline), and marine transportation to determine probable cause and issue safety recommendations.
beneficiaries: The traveling public, transportation operators, the safety of U.S. transportation systems

name: Salaries and Expenses, National Transportation Safety Board
description: Funds NTSB's operational costs including accident investigators, laboratory staff, hearing examiners, and administrative support for conducting transportation accident investigations.
beneficiaries: NTSB investigators and staff, transportation safety through investigations and recommendations

name: Home Investment Partnerships Program, Community Planning and Development, Housing and Urban Development
description: Formula grants (HOME) to state and local governments for affordable housing activities including construction, rehabilitation, tenant-based rental assistance, and homebuyer assistance for low-income households.
beneficiaries: Low-income renters and homebuyers, state and local affordable housing programs, community housing development organizations

name: Yard Fund, USCG, Homeland Security
description: Revolving fund for the Coast Guard Yard in Baltimore, Maryland, the service's sole shipyard, providing depot-level maintenance, repair, and renovation of Coast Guard cutters and boats.
beneficiaries: Coast Guard fleet maintenance, CG Yard workforce, operational readiness of Coast Guard vessels

name: Ready Reserve Force, Maritime Administration, Transportation
description: Funds the maintenance and readiness of a fleet of government-owned cargo ships in reduced operating status, available for rapid activation to support DoD sealift requirements during national emergencies.
beneficiaries: National defense sealift capability, U.S. Transportation Command, military logistics during contingencies

name: Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics Engagement, National Aeronautics and Space Administration
description: Funds NASA's STEM education and engagement programs including internships, fellowships, competitions, educator professional development, and outreach to inspire the next generation in STEM fields.
beneficiaries: Students (K-graduate), educators, universities, underrepresented communities in STEM, the future STEM workforce

name: Veterans Insurance and Indemnities, Veterans Benefits Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds death gratuities, burial awards, and insurance indemnities for veterans' beneficiaries not covered by specific VA insurance trust funds, including Servicemembers' Group Life Insurance supplemental payments.
beneficiaries: Surviving spouses, dependents, and beneficiaries of deceased veterans and service members

name: Mutual Mortgage Insurance Program Account, Federal Housing Administration, Housing and Urban Development
description: The FHA's primary mortgage insurance fund, backing single-family home mortgage guarantees for borrowers (often first-time and low-to-moderate income) who might not qualify for conventional loans.
beneficiaries: First-time homebuyers, low-to-moderate income borrowers, FHA-approved lenders, the housing market

name: Counter-ISIS Train and Equip Fund, Army
description: Funded the training, equipping, and sustaining of vetted Syrian opposition forces and Iraqi security forces to counter the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria.
beneficiaries: Iraqi Security Forces, vetted Syrian partners, civilian populations liberated from ISIS control

name: Energy Information Administration, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds the independent statistical agency within DOE that collects, analyzes, and disseminates energy information (production, consumption, prices, forecasts) to support energy policymaking and public understanding.
beneficiaries: Energy policymakers, industry analysts, researchers, the public (energy data at eia.gov), financial markets

name: Buildings and Facilities, Agricultural Research Service, Agriculture
description: Funds the construction, renovation, and repair of USDA Agricultural Research Service laboratories and research facilities across the country, supporting the nation's agricultural research infrastructure.
beneficiaries: ARS scientists and researchers, the agricultural research enterprise, farmers and consumers benefiting from agricultural innovations

name: Office of the Inspector General, Social Security Administration
description: Funds the SSA OIG's investigations, audits, and evaluations of Social Security programs to detect and prevent fraud, waste, and abuse in retirement, disability, and SSI programs.
beneficiaries: Social Security beneficiaries, SSI recipients, taxpayers, Congress (oversight of SSA)

name: Salaries and Expenses, Corporation for National and Community Service
description: Funds the administrative operations of the Corporation for National and Community Service (now AmeriCorps), which manages AmeriCorps, Senior Corps, and the Social Innovation Fund.
beneficiaries: AmeriCorps members, AmeriCorps VISTA and Senior Corps volunteers, communities served by national service programs

name: Assistance to Territories, Insular Affairs, Interior
description: Provides grants and technical assistance to U.S. territories for capital improvements, operations, maintenance of critical infrastructure, and capacity building in territorial government operations.
beneficiaries: U.S. territorial governments (American Samoa, Guam, USVI, CNMI, Marshall Islands, Palau, FSM), island communities

name: Financial Research Fund, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Funds the Office of Financial Research (OFR), established by the Dodd-Frank Act to collect financial data, develop analytical tools, and assess threats to the financial stability of the United States.
beneficiaries: Financial Stability Oversight Council, financial regulators, the stability of the U.S. financial system

name: Boat Safety, USCG, Homeland Security
description: Transfers from the Sport Fish Restoration and Boating Trust Fund to the Coast Guard for recreational boating safety programs, including state grant assistance, safety education, and enforcement.
beneficiaries: Recreational boaters, state boating safety programs, the Coast Guard's boating safety mission

name: Other Federal Drug Control Programs, Executive Office of the President
description: Funds anti-drug programs managed by the Office of National Drug Control Policy, including the Drug-Free Communities Support Program, anti-drug media campaigns, and drug policy coordination.
beneficiaries: Communities implementing drug prevention programs, youth at risk for substance abuse, the national drug control strategy

name: Veterans Special Life Insurance Fund, Trust Revolving Fund, Veterans Affairs
description: Trust fund providing life insurance for Korean War-era veterans with service-connected disabilities under the Veterans Special Life Insurance (VSLI/RS) program.
beneficiaries: Korean War-era veterans with service-connected disabilities holding VSLI policies and their beneficiaries

name: Medical and Prosthetic Research, Veterans Health Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds VA's intramural biomedical, clinical, health services, and rehabilitation research programs focused on conditions prevalent among veterans, including PTSD, traumatic brain injury, prosthetics, and chronic diseases.
beneficiaries: Veterans benefiting from VA research advances, VA physician-researchers, the broader medical research community

name: Office of the Inspector General, State
description: Funds the State Department OIG's oversight of State Department and U.S. Agency for Global Media programs through inspections of embassies, audits, and investigations of fraud, waste, and misconduct.
beneficiaries: State Department programs (accountability), diplomatic posts, taxpayers, Congress (foreign affairs oversight)

name: Salaries and Expenses, Economic and Statistical Analysis, Commerce
description: Funds the Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA), which produces key national economic statistics including GDP, personal income, trade balances, and regional economic accounts for the United States.
beneficiaries: Economic policymakers, Federal Reserve, businesses, investors, researchers relying on national economic data

name: Construction, International Boundary and Water Commission, United States and Mexico, State
description: Funds the U.S. section of the International Boundary and Water Commission's construction projects for flood control, water sanitation, and boundary demarcation infrastructure along the U.S.-Mexico border.
beneficiaries: Border communities along the U.S.-Mexico border, Rio Grande and Colorado River water users, binational environmental protection

name: Payments to States, National Forests Fund, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Distributes 25% of National Forest gross receipts (timber, grazing, minerals) to states for the benefit of public schools and roads in counties containing National Forest lands.
beneficiaries: Counties with National Forest lands, rural public schools and road systems in forest counties

name: Procurement, Construction, and Improvements, Federal Emergency Management Agency, Homeland Security
description: Funds FEMA's capital investments including the Integrated Public Alert and Warning System (IPAWS), emergency communications, facility construction, and IT infrastructure modernization.
beneficiaries: The public (emergency alerts), FEMA operational capabilities, emergency communications systems

name: Transition Initiatives, Funds Appropriated to the President, US Agency for International Development
description: Funds USAID's Office of Transition Initiatives, providing fast, flexible, short-term assistance to countries undergoing political transitions, emerging from conflict, or facing complex crises.
beneficiaries: Populations in politically transitioning or post-conflict countries, civil society and democratic institutions in crisis states

name: Selis-Qlispe Ksanka Settlement Trust Fund, Bureau of Indian Affairs and Bureau of Indian Education, Interior
description: Trust fund established by the CSKT Water Compact to settle the water rights claims of the Confederated Salish and Kootenai Tribes (Selis-Qlispe-Ksanka) of the Flathead Reservation in Montana.
beneficiaries: The Confederated Salish and Kootenai Tribes of the Flathead Reservation, Montana water users

name: International Trade Commission
description: Independent quasi-judicial federal agency that investigates trade complaints (antidumping, countervailing duties, intellectual property/Section 337), assesses trade impacts, and advises on tariff and trade policy.
beneficiaries: U.S. industries seeking trade remedies, importers and exporters, Congress and the President (trade policy advice)

name: Salaries and Expenses, International Trade Commission
description: Funds the ITC's personnel, investigations, economic analysis, and administrative operations, including trade injury investigations, Harmonized Tariff Schedule maintenance, and intellectual property enforcement.
beneficiaries: ITC commissioners and staff, U.S. industries involved in trade remedy cases

name: National Cancer Institute, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Funds NCI's cancer research portfolio including basic, clinical, translational, and population science research, the Cancer Moonshot, NCI-designated cancer centers, and clinical trials to reduce cancer incidence and mortality.
beneficiaries: Cancer patients and survivors, cancer researchers, NCI-designated cancer centers, oncology clinical trials

name: Housing for Persons with Disabilities, Housing Programs, Housing and Urban Development
description: Funds the Section 811 Supportive Housing for Persons with Disabilities program, providing capital advances and project-based rental assistance for affordable housing with supportive services for very low-income adults with disabilities.
beneficiaries: Very low-income adults with physical, developmental, or mental disabilities; nonprofit disability housing sponsors

name: Farm Credit Insurance Fund, Capital Corporation Investment Fund, Farm Credit Administration
description: Managed by the Farm Credit System Insurance Corporation, this fund insures the timely payment of principal and interest on Farm Credit System debt obligations issued to investors in agricultural credit markets.
beneficiaries: Farm Credit System banks and associations, investors in Farm Credit securities, farmers and ranchers accessing credit

name: Construction, Minor Projects, Departmental Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds minor construction projects (under $20 million) at VA medical facilities and national cemeteries, including renovations, upgrades, and infrastructure improvements.
beneficiaries: Veterans served by VA facilities undergoing minor construction improvements, VA health care and cemetery infrastructure

name: Salaries and Expenses, Office of the Solicitor, Interior
description: Funds the Office of the Solicitor, the principal legal advisor to the Secretary of the Interior, providing legal counsel to all DOI bureaus on natural resources, Indian affairs, and public lands law.
beneficiaries: DOI bureaus and offices receiving legal counsel, the administration of public lands and Indian trust responsibilities

name: Construction and Environmental Compliance and Restoration, National Aeronautics and Space Administration
description: Funds NASA's construction of new facilities, renovation of existing buildings, environmental compliance, and environmental restoration/remediation activities at NASA centers across the country.
beneficiaries: NASA centers, NASA workforce, communities near NASA facilities (environmental remediation)

name: Child Enrollment Contingency Fund, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, Health and Human Services
description: Provides additional CHIP funding to states that experience shortfalls in their federal CHIP allotments due to higher-than-expected children's health insurance enrollment.
beneficiaries: States with CHIP enrollment exceeding projections, children who would lose coverage without contingency funding

name: Oregon and California Grant Lands, Bureau of Land Management
description: Funds BLM's management of the revested Oregon and California Railroad Grant Lands (O&C Lands) in western Oregon, managed for sustained-yield timber production with revenue sharing to O&C counties.
beneficiaries: O&C counties in western Oregon (revenue payments), timber industry, forest ecosystems on O&C lands

name: Land Acquisition, United States Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Funds the purchase of lands and interests in lands for addition to the National Wildlife Refuge System, primarily through the Land and Water Conservation Fund, to protect critical habitats and wildlife corridors.
beneficiaries: Wildlife species and their habitats, the National Wildlife Refuge System, hunters, anglers, and wildlife watchers

name: Motor Carrier Safety Grants, Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration, Transportation
description: Funds the Motor Carrier Safety Assistance Program (MCSAP) grants to states for commercial motor vehicle safety inspections, compliance reviews, traffic enforcement, and hazardous materials transportation enforcement.
beneficiaries: State commercial vehicle safety programs, commercial truck and bus operators, highway users, the public

name: Health Care Systems, Health Resources and Services Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Funds HRSA programs supporting health care delivery systems including community health centers, organ transplantation (UNOS), poison control centers, telehealth, and the 340B drug pricing program administration.
beneficiaries: Community health center patients, organ transplant recipients, 340B-eligible safety net providers, poison control callers

name: Outer Continental Shelf Revenues, LWCF Share from Certain Leases, National Park Service
description: Transfers a portion of OCS oil and gas revenues to the Land and Water Conservation Fund for federal land acquisition and state-side grants for outdoor recreation and conservation.
beneficiaries: Federal land management agencies acquiring land, state outdoor recreation programs, the public enjoying protected lands

name: Office of the Inspector General, Agriculture
description: Funds the USDA OIG's independent oversight of USDA programs including SNAP, crop insurance, food safety, farm programs, and rural development through audits, investigations, and inspections.
beneficiaries: USDA programs (accountability), taxpayers, SNAP integrity, agricultural program participants

name: Management of Lands and Resources, Bureau of Land Management, Interior
description: Funds BLM's core land management activities on 245 million acres of public lands, including rangeland management, forestry, wild horses and burros, cultural resources, wildlife habitat, and recreation management.
beneficiaries: Public land users (ranchers, recreationists, miners), wildlife, wild horses and burros, BLM field staff

name: Energy Projects, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds DOE energy project activities including uranium enrichment decontamination and decommissioning, power marketing administration oversight, and specific energy infrastructure projects.
beneficiaries: Communities near former uranium enrichment sites, power marketing administration customers, energy infrastructure

name: Salaries and Expenses, Office of Administration, Executive
description: Funds the Office of Administration within the Executive Office of the President, providing financial management, IT, human resources, library, and facilities management services to EOP components.
beneficiaries: Executive Office of the President staff, White House operations, EOP administrative functions

name: Disaster Assistance Direct Loan Program Account, Federal Emergency Management Agency, Homeland Security
description: Provides direct federal loans to state and local governments that have suffered a major disaster and need to meet their cost-share requirements for FEMA public assistance grants when they cannot obtain credit elsewhere.
beneficiaries: State and local governments unable to meet disaster cost-sharing requirements, disaster-affected communities

name: Acquisition, Construction, and Improvements, Coast Guard, Homeland Security
description: Funds the Coast Guard's acquisition of major assets including cutters, aircraft, boats, and shore facilities as well as IT and C4ISR system improvements for the service's multiple missions.
beneficiaries: Coast Guard personnel, maritime safety and security operations, search and rescue capabilities

name: Federal Permitting Improvement Steering Council
description: Interagency council established by the FAST Act to improve the timeliness and transparency of federal environmental review and permitting for large, complex infrastructure projects.
beneficiaries: Infrastructure project sponsors, federal permitting agencies, communities awaiting infrastructure development

name: Environmental Review Improvement Fund, Federal Permitting Improvement Steering Council
description: Funds the operations of the Federal Permitting Improvement Steering Council and its Permitting Dashboard, facilitating coordinated environmental reviews and permitting schedules for covered infrastructure projects.
beneficiaries: Developers of large infrastructure projects, federal agencies coordinating environmental reviews, the federal permitting process

name: National Service Trust, Corporation for National and Community Service
description: The trust fund from which Segal AmeriCorps Education Awards are disbursed to members who complete terms of national service through AmeriCorps programs.
beneficiaries: AmeriCorps members earning education awards, colleges and universities receiving award payments, student loan servicers

name: Operation and Maintenance, Southwestern Power Administration, Power Marketing Administrations, Energy
description: Funds SWPA's operation and maintenance of transmission facilities marketing hydroelectric power from Army Corps of Engineers dams in the six-state southwestern region (AR, KS, LA, MO, OK, TX).
beneficiaries: Municipal utilities and cooperatives purchasing federal hydropower in the southwest, SWPA transmission system

name: Peacekeeping Operations, International Security Assistance, State
description: State Department account for assessed U.S. contributions to United Nations peacekeeping operations worldwide and voluntary contributions to regional peacekeeping activities.
beneficiaries: Populations in conflict zones with UN peacekeeping missions, U.S. diplomatic engagement in multilateral security

name: Periodic Censuses and Programs, Bureau of the Census, Commerce
description: Funds the decennial census, economic census, Census of Governments, American Community Survey, and other periodic census programs conducted by the U.S. Census Bureau.
beneficiaries: Congressional apportionment, federal fund allocation to states and localities, businesses, researchers, and all data users

name: Regulation and Technology, Office of Surface Mining Reclamation and Enforcement
description: Funds OSMRE's regulatory programs under the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act, including oversight of state regulatory programs, technical assistance, and technology transfer for coal mining reclamation.
beneficiaries: Communities near coal mining operations, state mining regulatory programs, the coal industry (compliance), mined land restoration

name: Payments to States, Northern Spotted Owl Guarantee, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Guaranteed minimum payments to Pacific Northwest counties that experienced reduced timber receipts due to northern spotted owl protections under the Endangered Species Act, compensating for lost revenue sharing.
beneficiaries: Pacific Northwest counties (Oregon, Washington) that lost timber revenue sharing, rural schools and roads in those counties

name: Salaries and Expenses, Office of Inspector General, Transportation
description: Funds the DOT OIG's audits, investigations, and inspections of transportation programs and contracts to promote safety, efficiency, effectiveness, and accountability across all DOT modal administrations.
beneficiaries: DOT programs (accountability), Congress (transportation oversight), transportation safety

name: United States Trade and Development Agency
description: Independent agency funding feasibility studies, technical assistance, and pilot projects to help U.S. companies compete for infrastructure and industrial projects in developing and middle-income countries.
beneficiaries: U.S. exporters of goods and services, developing countries planning infrastructure projects, U.S. trade competitiveness

name: Trade and Development Agency, Funds Appropriated to the President
description: Appropriation account for the U.S. Trade and Development Agency, funding project preparation activities that connect U.S. private sector capabilities with development priorities in emerging economies.
beneficiaries: U.S. companies seeking overseas project opportunities, developing countries with infrastructure needs

name: Cooperative Endangered Species Conservation Fund, United States Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Provides grants to states and territories for species and habitat conservation on non-federal lands, including habitat conservation plans, safe harbor agreements, and candidate species recovery.
beneficiaries: Endangered and threatened species, state fish and wildlife agencies, private landowners participating in conservation agreements

name: Working Capital Fund, Geological Survey
description: Revolving fund enabling the USGS to provide reimbursable services including laboratory analyses, equipment, publications, and technical support to other federal agencies and external customers.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies and other customers purchasing USGS analytical, laboratory, and technical services

name: Colorado River Dam Fund, Boulder Canyon Project, Bureau of Reclamation
description: Revolving fund associated with the Boulder Canyon Project (Hoover Dam), receiving revenues from power sales and repaying the federal investment in dam construction and operations.
beneficiaries: Southwestern states receiving Hoover Dam hydropower and water, power purchasers, Colorado River water users

name: Federal Trust Programs, Bureau of Trust Funds Administration, Interior
description: Funds the Bureau of Trust Funds Administration's (formerly OST's) fiduciary management of individual Indian and tribal trust fund accounts, including accounting, investment, and beneficiary services.
beneficiaries: Individual Indian beneficiaries and tribal trust fund holders, the federal Indian trust responsibility

name: Special Compliance Personnel Program, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds additional IRS compliance personnel and activities focused on high-income individual and corporate tax enforcement, complex partnerships, and international tax compliance to close the tax gap.
beneficiaries: Federal revenue collection, compliant taxpayers (fairness), the tax administration system

name: Salaries and Expenses, Cemeterial Expenses, Army
description: Funds the operation of Arlington National Cemetery and the Soldiers' and Airmen's Home National Cemetery, including interment services, grounds maintenance, and cemetery administration.
beneficiaries: Families of service members and veterans interred at Arlington National Cemetery, cemetery visitors, the nation's honor to fallen service members

name: Payments in Lieu of Coal Fee Receipts, Office of Surface Mining Reclamation and Enforcement
description: Mandatory Treasury payments replacing expiring coal excise tax fee receipts, funding continued reclamation of abandoned coal mine lands and payments to states, tribes, and the United Mine Workers combined benefit fund.
beneficiaries: Communities affected by abandoned coal mines, states and tribes with abandoned mine land problems, UMWA retirees

name: Grants-in-Aid for Airports, Federal Aviation Administration, Transportation
description: Funds the Airport Improvement Program (AIP), providing grants for the planning and development of public-use airports including runway construction, safety improvements, noise mitigation, and airport capacity projects.
beneficiaries: Public-use airports (commercial and general aviation), state aviation agencies, airport sponsors, air travelers

name: Buildings and Facilities, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Funds the construction, renovation, and repair of NIH's research facilities on its main campus in Bethesda, MD and other NIH-owned research locations, maintaining the physical infrastructure for biomedical research.
beneficiaries: NIH intramural researchers, the NIH campus and facilities, the biomedical research enterprise

name: Limitation on Administration, Railroad Retirement Board
description: Limits the administrative expenses of the Railroad Retirement Board, which administers retirement, survivor, disability, unemployment, and sickness benefits for railroad workers and their families.
beneficiaries: RRB employees administering benefits, railroad workers and retirees receiving benefits

name: Airport Infrastructure Grants, Federal Aviation Administration
description: Provides grants for airport terminal development, runway reconstruction, and multimodal connections, established under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA) as a supplement to the traditional AIP program.
beneficiaries: Commercial and general aviation airports, state and local airport authorities, passengers

name: Watershed and Flood Prevention Operations, Natural Resources Conservation Service
description: Supports planning and construction of small upstream flood-prevention structures, watershed protection projects, and water-management improvements under the Watershed Protection and Flood Prevention Act (PL-566).
beneficiaries: Rural communities, farmers, local watershed districts, downstream flood-prone areas

name: Payments in Lieu of Taxes, Departmental Management (Interior)
