description: Compensates local governments for the presence of tax-exempt federal lands within their jurisdictions, since federal land cannot be taxed by counties. Authorized by the Payments in Lieu of Taxes Act.
beneficiaries: County and local governments in states with significant federal land holdings (especially western states)

name: State Grants and Demonstration, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
description: Provides formula and competitive grants to states for health insurance program innovations, Medicaid demonstrations, and initiatives to improve health care delivery systems and expand coverage.
beneficiaries: State Medicaid agencies, low-income beneficiaries, health care providers participating in demonstrations

name: Grants-in-Aid for Airports, Federal Aviation Administration
description: The Airport Improvement Program (AIP) providing formula and discretionary grants funded from the Airport and Airway Trust Fund for runway safety improvements, capacity enhancements, noise mitigation, and airport planning.
beneficiaries: Public-use airports, airport sponsors (state/local governments), airline passengers, surrounding communities

name: National Institute on Alcohol Abuse and Alcoholism, NIH
description: Conducts and supports biomedical and behavioral research on the causes, consequences, prevention, and treatment of alcohol use disorder and alcohol-related problems including fetal alcohol spectrum disorders.
beneficiaries: Researchers, clinicians treating alcohol use disorders, individuals and families affected by alcohol misuse

name: Payment to the Foreign Service Retirement and Disability Fund, State
description: Mandatory appropriation covering the unfunded liability of the Foreign Service Retirement and Disability System, ensuring pension obligations for retired Foreign Service officers and survivors are met.
beneficiaries: Retired and disabled Foreign Service officers, surviving dependents

name: Contingency Fund for State Welfare Programs, Administration for Children and Families
description: Provides supplemental TANF (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families) funding to states experiencing economic downturns or increased caseloads, authorized by the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act.
beneficiaries: States with rising welfare caseloads, low-income families with children

name: Salaries and Expenses, Bureau of the Fiscal Service, Treasury
description: Funds operations of the Bureau of the Fiscal Service (formed from merger of Financial Management Service and Bureau of Public Debt), which manages federal payments, collections, debt issuance, and government-wide accounting.
beneficiaries: All federal payment recipients (Social Security, tax refunds, vendor payments), Treasury bond investors, federal agencies

name: U.S. Coronavirus Payments, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Administered Economic Impact Payments (stimulus checks) to eligible individuals and families under the CARES Act, Consolidated Appropriations Act 2021, and American Rescue Plan Act during the COVID-19 pandemic.
beneficiaries: Eligible U.S. individuals and families below income thresholds

name: Nonrecurring Expense Fund, Office of the Secretary, HHS
description: A no-year fund that captures unobligated balances from expired HHS discretionary accounts to finance one-time information technology, facilities, and infrastructure improvement projects across the department.
beneficiaries: HHS operating divisions, IT modernization projects, facilities requiring upgrades

name: Operations, Research and Facilities, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration
description: NOAA's primary operating account funding weather forecasting (National Weather Service), ocean and atmospheric research, fisheries management, coastal zone management, satellite operations, and charting services.
beneficiaries: General public (weather forecasts), fisheries, coastal communities, aviation/maritime industries, climate researchers

name: Safe Schools and Citizenship Education, Education
description: Funded programs promoting school safety, drug-free schools, character education, and civic learning, including the Safe and Drug-Free Schools and Communities Act grants and related initiatives.
beneficiaries: K-12 students, school districts, teachers, school safety personnel

name: Diversion Control Fee Account, Justice
description: A fee-funded account within the Drug Enforcement Administration financing the registration and regulatory oversight of manufacturers, distributors, dispensers, and researchers handling controlled substances.
beneficiaries: DEA regulatory operations, legitimate pharmaceutical supply chain, public safety

name: Energy Community Revitalization Program, Department-Wide Programs, Interior
description: Funds reclamation and remediation of abandoned mine lands and orphaned oil and gas wells on federal, state, tribal, and private lands under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act, revitalizing energy-impacted communities.
beneficiaries: Former coal and fossil fuel communities, state/tribal reclamation programs, local workers

name: Public Broadcasting Fund, Corporation for Public Broadcasting
description: Provides federal appropriations to the Corporation for Public Broadcasting, which distributes funds to local public television and radio stations for programming, operations, and interconnection services.
beneficiaries: Local PBS and NPR member stations, public media audiences, rural and underserved communities

name: Water Infrastructure Finance and Innovation Program Account, EPA
description: The WIFIA program provides long-term, low-cost supplemental credit (loans and guarantees) for nationally and regionally significant water and wastewater infrastructure projects, leveraging private and non-federal investment.
beneficiaries: Municipalities, water utilities, ratepayers, communities needing water/wastewater infrastructure upgrades

name: Administrative Services Franchise Fund, FAA
description: A revolving fund enabling FAA to provide shared administrative services (accounting, IT, human resources, procurement) to FAA lines of business and other federal customers on a fee-for-service basis.
beneficiaries: FAA organizational units, other federal agencies using FAA shared services

name: Business Loans Program Account, Small Business Administration
description: Provides the subsidy costs for SBA's 7(a) general business loan guarantees and 504 certified development company debentures, enabling small businesses to access affordable credit for start-up, expansion, and equipment.
beneficiaries: Small businesses, entrepreneurs, participating lenders, local economies

name: Payment to the Legal Services Corporation
description: Federal appropriation to the Legal Services Corporation, an independent nonprofit that funds civil legal aid programs nationwide, providing free legal assistance to low-income Americans in matters such as housing, family law, and public benefits.
beneficiaries: Low-income individuals and families needing civil legal representation

name: Payment to Issuer of Qualified School Construction Bonds, IRS, Treasury
description: Provides direct federal subsidy payments to issuers of Qualified School Construction Bonds (QSCBs) authorized under the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act, supporting school construction and renovation financing.
beneficiaries: School districts, state and local governments issuing QSCBs, students attending improved schools

name: First Responder Network Authority, NTIA, Commerce
description: Operates the Nationwide Public Safety Broadband Network (FirstNet), a dedicated interoperable wireless broadband network for first responders, established by the Middle Class Tax Relief and Job Creation Act of 2012.
beneficiaries: Police, fire, EMS, and other first responders; state and local public safety agencies

name: Promoting Safe and Stable Families, Administration for Children and Families
description: Provides formula grants to states for family preservation, family reunification, adoption promotion, and support services to prevent child abuse and unnecessary foster care placements, authorized under Title IV-B, subpart 2 of the Social Security Act.
beneficiaries: At-risk families, children in or near the child welfare system, state child welfare agencies

name: National Institute on Minority Health and Health Disparities, NIH
description: Leads scientific research to understand and address health disparities affecting racial/ethnic minorities, rural populations, and other underserved groups, funding studies on social determinants of health and intervention development.
beneficiaries: Racial/ethnic minority populations, rural communities, health disparities researchers, medically underserved groups

name: Interagency Crime and Drug Enforcement, Justice
description: Funds the Organized Crime Drug Enforcement Task Forces (OCDETF), which coordinate multi-agency investigations and prosecutions targeting major drug trafficking organizations and money laundering networks.
beneficiaries: Federal law enforcement agencies (DEA, FBI, ATF, IRS-CI), U.S. Attorneys, communities affected by drug trafficking

name: Facilities and Equipment, Federal Aviation Administration
description: Funds procurement, installation, and modernization of FAA air traffic control systems, navigation aids, weather systems, and communication equipment from general fund appropriations (distinct from the Trust Fund-sourced F&E).
beneficiaries: National Airspace System users, air traffic controllers, flying public

name: General and Special Risk Program Account, Federal Housing Administration, HUD
description: Supports FHA's mortgage insurance programs for higher-risk segments including hospitals, nursing homes, multifamily rental housing, and manufactured housing, recording subsidy costs and insurance claim payments.
beneficiaries: Hospitals, nursing facilities, multifamily housing developers, manufactured home buyers, underserved mortgage borrowers

name: National Institute on Deafness and Other Communication Disorders, NIH
description: Conducts and supports research on hearing, balance, taste, smell, voice, speech, and language disorders, funding studies on cochlear implants, hearing loss prevention, and communication rehabilitation.
beneficiaries: Individuals with hearing loss, speech/language disorders, balance disorders; audiologists, speech-language pathologists, researchers

name: Working Capital Fund, Office of the Secretary, Transportation
description: A revolving fund providing shared administrative services (IT, human resources, facilities, financial management) to DOT operating administrations on a reimbursable basis.
beneficiaries: DOT operating administrations (FHWA, FTA, FAA, etc.), improving departmental efficiency

name: Office of the Inspector General, Defense
description: Funds the DoD IG's independent audits, investigations, and evaluations of defense programs and operations to detect fraud, waste, abuse, and mismanagement across the Department of Defense.
beneficiaries: Taxpayers, Congress (oversight), DoD management, whistleblowers

name: Salaries and Expenses, Federal Energy Regulatory Commission
description: Funds FERC's regulatory activities overseeing interstate electricity transmission, natural gas pipelines, hydropower licensing, and energy market oversight. Fully offset by fees and annual charges on regulated entities.
beneficiaries: Energy consumers, regulated utilities, interstate energy markets

name: Sport Fish Restoration Account, U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Distributes excise tax revenues from fishing tackle, motorboat fuel, and import duties on fishing equipment to state fish and wildlife agencies for sport fish habitat restoration, aquatic education, and boating access under the Dingell-Johnson/Wallop-Breaux Act.
beneficiaries: Recreational anglers, state fish and wildlife agencies, aquatic ecosystems

name: Salaries and Expenses, Federal Communications Commission
description: Funds FCC personnel and operations regulating interstate and international communications by radio, television, wire, satellite, and cable, including spectrum management, broadband policy, and media regulation.
beneficiaries: Telecommunications consumers, broadcasters, wireless carriers, internet users

name: Commodity Assistance Program, Food and Nutrition Service, Agriculture
description: Funds commodity procurement and distribution programs including The Emergency Food Assistance Program (TEFAP), the Commodity Supplemental Food Program (CSFP) for low-income seniors, and commodity purchases for schools and food banks.
beneficiaries: Low-income families, elderly persons, food banks, schools, tribal organizations

name: Research, Evaluation, and Statistics, Office of Justice Programs, Justice
description: Supports the Bureau of Justice Statistics (BJS) and the National Institute of Justice (NIJ) in conducting criminal justice research, program evaluations, data collection (e.g., National Crime Victimization Survey), and technology development.
beneficiaries: Criminal justice policymakers, law enforcement agencies, researchers, crime victims, state/local justice systems

name: National Institute of Dental and Craniofacial Research, NIH
description: Supports research on dental, oral, and craniofacial diseases and conditions including tooth decay, periodontal disease, oral cancer, cleft palate, TMJ disorders, and salivary gland dysfunction.
beneficiaries: Dental patients, oral health researchers, dentists and oral surgeons, individuals with craniofacial conditions

name: Lead Hazard Reduction, Office of Lead Hazard Control and Healthy Homes, HUD
description: Provides grants to state and local governments for lead-based paint hazard identification and remediation in privately owned low-income housing, protecting children from lead poisoning.
beneficiaries: Children in low-income housing, property owners, local health departments, lead abatement workers

name: Maternal, Infant, and Early Childhood Home Visiting Programs, HRSA
description: Funds evidence-based home visiting programs (e.g., Nurse-Family Partnership, Parents as Teachers) for at-risk pregnant women and families with young children, providing support in health, parenting, child development, and self-sufficiency.
beneficiaries: At-risk pregnant women, new parents, infants and toddlers, tribal communities

name: Operations, Preparedness, and Emergency Response, ASPR, HHS
description: Funds the Administration for Strategic Preparedness and Response (formerly BARDA and PHEMCE components) for medical countermeasure development, Strategic National Stockpile management, emergency medical response, and public health preparedness.
beneficiaries: General public during health emergencies, hospitals, state/local health departments, biodefense infrastructure

name: Operations and Administration, International Trade Administration, Commerce
description: Funds ITA's mission of promoting U.S. exports, enforcing trade laws (antidumping/countervailing duty investigations), and ensuring compliance with trade agreements, operating through domestic offices and overseas posts.
beneficiaries: U.S. exporters, domestic industries facing unfair trade competition, U.S. workers in trade-affected industries

name: National Library of Medicine, NIH
description: Operates the world's largest biomedical library, maintaining databases including PubMed/MEDLINE, MedlinePlus, ClinicalTrials.gov, and the National Center for Biotechnology Information (NCBI/GenBank), and funding biomedical informatics research.
beneficiaries: Biomedical researchers, clinicians, public health professionals, patients seeking health information, bioinformatics community

name: Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy (ARPA-E), Energy
description: Funds high-risk, high-reward energy technology research and development projects that are too early-stage for private investment, aiming for transformational breakthroughs in energy generation, storage, and efficiency.
beneficiaries: Energy researchers, start-up companies, national laboratories, clean energy sector, U.S. energy consumers

name: Motor Carrier Safety Grants, Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration
description: Provides formula grants to states for commercial motor vehicle safety inspections, compliance reviews, traffic enforcement, data collection, and driver/vehicle safety programs under the Motor Carrier Safety Assistance Program (MCSAP).
beneficiaries: State motor carrier enforcement agencies, commercial truck and bus drivers, motoring public

name: Office of the Inspector General, Office of the Secretary, HHS
description: Funds independent oversight of HHS programs through audits, investigations, evaluations, and fraud enforcement, with particular focus on Medicare/Medicaid fraud, waste, and abuse (the single largest area of federal health care fraud).
beneficiaries: Taxpayers, Medicare/Medicaid beneficiaries, HHS program integrity, whistleblowers

name: Federal Salaries and Expenses, National Nuclear Security Administration, Energy
description: Covers NNSA's administrative personnel costs, corporate management, infrastructure, and information technology supporting the nuclear weapons stockpile, naval reactors, and nonproliferation programs.
beneficiaries: NNSA workforce, nuclear weapons complex operations, national security mission

name: Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Contribution, Reserve Personnel, Army
description: Annual DoD accrual payment to the Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund (MERHCF) covering future TRICARE-for-Life benefits for Army Reserve personnel who will become Medicare-eligible retirees.
beneficiaries: Army Reserve retirees eligible for Medicare, TRICARE-for-Life beneficiaries

name: North Atlantic Treaty Organization Security Investment Program, Defense
description: U.S. contribution to NATO's common-funded infrastructure program financing construction of military facilities, communications systems, and logistics infrastructure across the Alliance for collective defense.
beneficiaries: NATO alliance, U.S. and allied military forces, European security infrastructure

name: Salaries and Expenses, Departmental Management, Labor
description: Funds the Office of the Secretary of Labor and departmental management offices providing policy direction, legal services, administration, congressional affairs, and public affairs for the Department of Labor.
beneficiaries: DOL operating agencies, Secretary of Labor's office, labor policy development

name: Procurement, Construction, and Improvements, Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, Homeland Security
description: Finances CISA's acquisition of cybersecurity tools, protective security equipment, infrastructure assessments, and facility improvements for protecting federal networks and critical infrastructure.
beneficiaries: Federal civilian agencies, critical infrastructure operators, state/local governments, private sector cybersecurity

name: Community Development Financial Institutions Fund Program Account, Treasury
description: Provides grants, equity investments, and technical assistance to certified CDFIs (community banks, credit unions, loan funds, venture capital funds) serving economically distressed communities with affordable financial products.
beneficiaries: Low-income communities, minority and underbanked populations, CDFIs, small businesses in underserved areas

name: National Cemetery Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Operates and maintains 155+ national cemeteries, providing burial and memorial benefits to eligible veterans and family members, including headstones, markers, and Presidential Memorial Certificates.
beneficiaries: Deceased veterans and eligible family members, surviving families, visitors to national cemeteries

name: Veterans Housing Benefit Program Fund, Veterans Benefits Administration
description: Finances the VA Home Loan Guaranty program, which guarantees a portion of mortgage loans made by private lenders to eligible veterans, active-duty service members, and surviving spouses, enabling home purchases with no down payment.
beneficiaries: Veterans, active-duty military, eligible surviving spouses, participating mortgage lenders

name: Rural Community Facilities Program Account, Rural Housing Service, Agriculture
description: Provides direct loans, guaranteed loans, and grants to develop essential community facilities (hospitals, fire stations, schools, libraries, public buildings) in rural areas and towns under 20,000 population.
beneficiaries: Rural communities, small towns, rural hospitals, fire departments, rural schools

name: Operations and Support, Federal Law Enforcement Training Centers, Homeland Security
description: Funds FLETC's operations training federal law enforcement officers from 90+ partner agencies at facilities in Glynco GA, Artesia NM, Charleston SC, and Cheltenham MD, covering basic and advanced training programs.
beneficiaries: Federal law enforcement agencies, federal law enforcement recruits and officers

name: Working Capital Fund, Environmental Protection Agency
description: A revolving fund providing centralized administrative services (IT, financial management, human resources, facilities) to EPA program offices on a fee-for-service basis.
beneficiaries: EPA program offices, operational efficiency of environmental protection mission

name: Information Technology Fund, Management and Administration, HUD
description: Consolidates HUD's information technology investments funding enterprise systems, cybersecurity, data infrastructure, and IT modernization across all HUD program offices.
beneficiaries: HUD program offices, grantees interfacing with HUD systems, applicants for HUD programs

name: National Parks and Public Land Legacy Restoration Fund, Interior
description: Created by the Great American Outdoors Act, providing up to $1.9B annually (over 5 years) from energy development revenues to address the deferred maintenance backlog in national parks, forests, wildlife refuges, and other federal lands.
beneficiaries: National park visitors, NPS/USFWS/BLM facilities, gateway communities, recreation users

name: International Narcotics Control and Law Enforcement, State
description: Funds U.S. assistance to foreign countries for counter-narcotics programs, law enforcement capacity building, judicial reform, anti-corruption efforts, and rule-of-law programs worldwide.
beneficiaries: Partner country law enforcement and judicial institutions, countries affected by drug trafficking, U.S. counter-narcotics objectives

name: Agency Operations and Award Management, National Science Foundation
description: Funds NSF's internal operations including salaries, IT systems, facilities, and the merit review process that evaluates and manages approximately 50,000 competitive grant proposals annually across all fields of science and engineering.
beneficiaries: NSF staff, scientific community (grant applicants), merit review panelists

name: Support for U.S. Relocation to Guam Activities, Defense
description: Funds military construction and infrastructure improvements on Guam associated with the realignment of U.S. Marine Corps forces from Okinawa, Japan to Guam under the U.S.-Japan Alliance agreement.
beneficiaries: U.S. Marine Corps, Guam community and economy, U.S.-Japan security alliance

name: Peace Corps, Funds Appropriated to the President
description: Funds the Peace Corps' operations deploying American volunteers to developing countries for two-year service assignments in education, health, agriculture, environment, youth development, and community economic development.
beneficiaries: Developing country communities, Peace Corps volunteers, U.S. public diplomacy and cross-cultural understanding

name: Elective Payment for Energy Property and Electricity Produced from Certain Renewable Resources, IRS, Treasury
description: Provides direct-pay tax credit payments (elective pay) to tax-exempt entities (state/local governments, nonprofits, tribal entities) for qualifying clean energy investments and production under the Inflation Reduction Act.
beneficiaries: Tax-exempt entities investing in renewable energy, municipalities, tribal governments, rural electric cooperatives

name: Aging Infrastructure Account, Bureau of Reclamation, Interior
description: Addresses the maintenance backlog at Bureau of Reclamation facilities (dams, canals, power plants) in the 17 western states, funded under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.
beneficiaries: Western water users, irrigators, hydropower consumers, communities downstream of aging dams

name: Assistance for Europe, Eurasia, and Central Asia, Funds Appropriated to the President, USAID
description: Provides economic and democratic development assistance to countries of the former Soviet Union and Central/Eastern Europe, supporting governance, civil society, market reforms, energy security, and rule of law under the FREEDOM Support and SEED Acts.
beneficiaries: Countries in Eastern Europe, Eurasia, and Central Asia undergoing democratic and market transitions; NGOs, civil society

name: Equal Employment Opportunity Commission
description: Funds the EEOC's enforcement of federal civil rights laws prohibiting workplace discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, and genetic information through charge processing, litigation, mediation, and outreach.
beneficiaries: Workers facing employment discrimination, employers seeking compliance guidance, federal sector employees

name: Consolidated Rail Infrastructure and Safety Improvements Grants, Federal Railroad Administration
description: Competitive grant program (CRISI) funding rail infrastructure projects including track rehabilitation, grade crossing improvements, positive train control implementation, short-line railroad upgrades, and rail safety technology.
beneficiaries: Freight and passenger railroads, short-line railroads, Amtrak, commuter rail, communities along rail corridors

name: Rural Energy for America Program, Rural Business-Cooperative Service, Agriculture
description: Provides grants and loan guarantees to agricultural producers and rural small businesses for renewable energy systems (solar, wind, biomass) and energy efficiency improvements under the Farm Bill.
beneficiaries: Rural small businesses, agricultural producers, rural electric cooperatives, rural economies

name: Federal Trade Commission
description: Funds FTC operations protecting consumers from unfair or deceptive business practices and promoting competition through antitrust enforcement, merger reviews, consumer protection rulemaking, and public education.
beneficiaries: Consumers, competitive markets, businesses facing anticompetitive practices, privacy protection

name: Housing Opportunities for Persons with AIDS (HOPWA), Community Planning and Development, HUD
description: Provides formula and competitive grants to states and localities for housing assistance and supportive services for low-income persons living with HIV/AIDS and their families, preventing homelessness.
beneficiaries: Low-income individuals and families living with HIV/AIDS, state and local housing agencies, community-based organizations

name: Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health (ARPA-H), NIH, HHS
description: Supports high-risk, high-reward biomedical and health research to accelerate breakthroughs in disease prevention, detection, and treatment, modeled on DARPA and ARPA-E for transformative health solutions.
beneficiaries: Patients with hard-to-treat diseases, biomedical researchers, health innovation ecosystem

name: National Institute of Biomedical Imaging and Bioengineering, NIH
description: Funds research at the intersection of engineering and life sciences, developing new biomedical imaging technologies (MRI, ultrasound, optical), biosensors, drug delivery systems, tissue engineering, and computational modeling for health applications.
beneficiaries: Biomedical engineers, imaging researchers, clinicians using diagnostic technologies, patients benefiting from medical devices

name: Program Administration, Departmental Management, Education
description: Funds the Department of Education's central administrative functions including the Office of the Secretary, Office for Civil Rights, budget office, communications, and departmental management supporting oversight of federal education programs.
beneficiaries: Education Department staff, schools and institutions subject to civil rights enforcement, education program management

name: Operating Expenses, National Archives and Records Administration
description: Funds NARA's operations preserving and providing access to federal government records, presidential libraries, the National Historical Publications and Records Commission, and the Federal Register.
beneficiaries: Researchers, genealogists, general public, federal agencies (records management), historians

name: CDC-Wide Activities and Program Support, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
description: Funds cross-cutting CDC activities including public health infrastructure, laboratory capacity, data modernization, workforce development, global health security, and administrative support spanning multiple CDC centers and programs.
beneficiaries: Public health system nationally, state/local/tribal health departments, global health security

name: Motor Carrier Safety Operations and Programs, FMCSA, Transportation
description: Funds FMCSA's federal operations including commercial driver's license standards, compliance investigations, safety ratings, hazardous materials transportation oversight, and the national motor carrier registration system.
beneficiaries: Commercial motor vehicle operators, trucking industry, motoring public, highway safety

name: Industrial Technology Services, National Institute of Standards and Technology, Commerce
description: Funds the Manufacturing Extension Partnership (MEP) program and the Manufacturing USA network helping small and medium manufacturers adopt advanced technologies, improve processes, and develop workforce capabilities.
beneficiaries: Small and medium U.S. manufacturers, manufacturing workforce, advanced manufacturing innovation institutes

name: Contribution to the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (World Bank), Treasury
description: U.S. capital contributions and subscriptions to the World Bank's IBRD, which provides loans and technical assistance to middle-income and creditworthy low-income countries for development projects.
beneficiaries: Developing countries receiving World Bank loans, global economic development, U.S. influence in international development finance

name: International Organizations and Programs, Funds Appropriated to the President, State
description: Funds U.S. voluntary contributions to international organizations (UN agencies, international bodies) for programs in health, environment, humanitarian response, science, and other multilateral initiatives.
beneficiaries: International organizations (UNICEF, UNDP, UNEP, etc.), populations served by multilateral programs

name: Community Service Employment for Older Americans, ETA, Labor
description: Funds the Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP) providing subsidized part-time community service positions and job training to low-income persons aged 55+, transitioning them to unsubsidized employment.
beneficiaries: Low-income older Americans (55+), community organizations receiving senior workers, local labor markets

name: Health Surveillance and Program Support, SAMHSA, HHS
description: Funds SAMHSA's data collection, surveillance systems (National Survey on Drug Use and Health), program evaluation, workforce development, and administrative support for behavioral health programs.
beneficiaries: Behavioral health policymakers, substance abuse and mental health treatment providers, public health data users

name: Operation of Indian Education Programs, Bureau of Indian Affairs and Bureau of Indian Education, Interior
description: Funds the Bureau of Indian Education's operation of approximately 183 elementary and secondary schools and dormitories on or near Indian reservations serving Native American students, plus tribal colleges support.
beneficiaries: Native American students in BIE-funded schools, tribal communities, tribal colleges

name: Commissary Funds, Federal Prisons, Justice
description: A revolving fund operating commissary stores in federal prisons where inmates can purchase approved personal items (hygiene products, food, stationery) using funds from their prison accounts, with profits supporting inmate welfare programs.
beneficiaries: Federal prison inmates, Bureau of Prisons inmate welfare programs

name: Land Acquisition and State Assistance, National Park Service
description: Funds acquisition of private inholdings within authorized national park boundaries and provides matching grants to states for outdoor recreation land acquisition and development through the Land and Water Conservation Fund.
beneficiaries: National parks (boundary completion), state outdoor recreation agencies, public recreation users

name: Surveys, Investigations, and Research, U.S. Geological Survey, Interior
description: The USGS's primary account funding earth science research, geologic and topographic mapping, water resources monitoring, natural hazards assessment (earthquakes, volcanoes, landslides), and mineral resources evaluation.
beneficiaries: Emergency managers, water resource planners, geoscientists, land-use planners, mining industry, general public

name: National Guard and Reserve Equipment Account, Defense
description: Procures high-priority equipment for National Guard and Reserve component units to address equipment shortages and modernization needs not fully met through active component procurement programs.
beneficiaries: National Guard and Reserve units across all services, domestic emergency response capability

name: Canteen Service Revolving Fund, Veterans Affairs
description: Operates the Veterans Canteen Service, running retail stores, food courts, and vending operations at VA medical centers, with revenues reinvested into services and amenities for veteran patients.
beneficiaries: Veteran patients at VA medical facilities, VA medical center staff, visitors

name: Safety, Security and Mission Services, NASA
description: Funds NASA's institutional capabilities including center operations, environmental compliance, security, IT infrastructure, agency management, and engineering/safety oversight supporting all NASA missions.
beneficiaries: NASA centers and workforce, space mission safety, NASA institutional infrastructure

name: Payment to the NIH Innovation Account, CURES Act, NIH, HHS
description: Mandatory funding authorized by the 21st Century Cures Act for specific NIH research priorities including the All of Us precision medicine cohort, the BRAIN Initiative, cancer moonshot, and regenerative medicine.
beneficiaries: Precision medicine researchers, neuroscience community, cancer patients, regenerative medicine field

name: Capital Investment Fund, State
description: Funds major information technology modernization and infrastructure improvements across the State Department, including cybersecurity upgrades, consular systems, and enterprise IT platforms.
beneficiaries: State Department operations, embassy/consulate IT infrastructure, passport/visa processing systems

name: Presidential Election Campaign Fund, Treasury
description: Distributes public funds to eligible presidential candidates and national party conventions financed by the $3 voluntary taxpayer check-off on federal income tax returns, created by the Revenue Act of 1971.
beneficiaries: Eligible presidential candidates accepting public financing, national party convention committees

name: Presidio Trust
description: Funds the Presidio Trust, a federal corporation managing the Presidio of San Francisco (former Army post in Golden Gate National Recreation Area), operating it as a financially self-sustaining national park site with leased buildings, trails, and cultural programs.
beneficiaries: Presidio visitors, tenants, San Francisco Bay Area community, national park heritage

name: Healthcare Research and Quality, Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality, HHS
description: Funds AHRQ's research on health care quality, patient safety, outcomes, effectiveness, and cost, developing evidence-based tools (clinical guidelines, patient safety indicators) for improving health care delivery.
beneficiaries: Health care providers, patients, health systems, quality improvement organizations, policymakers

name: Family Housing Operation and Maintenance, Navy and Marine Corps
description: Funds operation, maintenance, and utilities for government-owned Navy and Marine Corps family housing units in the U.S. and overseas, including management of privatized military housing oversight.
beneficiaries: Navy and Marine Corps service members and families in military housing

name: Internal Revenue, Collections for Puerto Rico, Treasury
description: Covers the return of federal excise taxes (primarily on rum) collected on articles produced in Puerto Rico and shipped to the mainland, as required by Section 7652 of the Internal Revenue Code.
beneficiaries: Government of Puerto Rico (receiving returned excise tax revenues)

name: National Flood Insurance Reserve Fund, Homeland Security
description: Established by the Biggert-Waters Act as a reserve fund within the National Flood Insurance Program to build up a financial reserve from policyholder premiums and surcharges to cover future catastrophic flood losses.
beneficiaries: NFIP policyholders, flood-prone communities, FEMA flood insurance program solvency

name: Salaries and Expenses, Mine Safety and Health Administration
description: Funds MSHA's enforcement of the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act through mine inspections, safety standards development, training, and investigation of mine accidents in coal and metal/nonmetal mines.
beneficiaries: Mine workers, mine operators, mining communities, mine safety inspectors

name: Non-Defense Environmental Cleanup, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds remediation and decommissioning of contaminated sites from former civilian nuclear energy and research activities, including the West Valley Demonstration Project and various former nuclear facilities.
beneficiaries: Communities near contaminated DOE civilian sites, environmental restoration, site workers

name: Payments for Tribal Leases, Indian Health Service, HHS
description: Funds IHS lease payments for tribally owned or leased facilities used to deliver health care services to American Indians and Alaska Natives, enabling tribes to construct and maintain health care facilities.
beneficiaries: American Indian and Alaska Native communities, tribal health care facilities, tribal governments

name: Expenses, Isotope Production and Distribution Program Fund, Energy
description: A revolving fund operating DOE's isotope production and distribution program, producing radioactive and stable isotopes for medical diagnostics, cancer treatment, industrial applications, and research.
beneficiaries: Medical facilities (PET scans, cancer therapy), industrial users, nuclear physics researchers

name: Operations and Support, Office of the Secretary and Executive Management, DHS
description: Funds the DHS Secretary's office and senior leadership, providing executive direction, policy development, public affairs, legislative affairs, and management oversight for the Department of Homeland Security.
beneficiaries: DHS component agencies, homeland security policy coordination, departmental leadership

name: Family Housing Operation and Maintenance, Air Force
description: Funds operation, maintenance, utilities, and leasing for government-owned Air Force family housing in the U.S. and overseas, including oversight of privatized housing partnerships.
beneficiaries: Air Force service members and families in military housing

name: Procurement, Construction, and Improvements, Management Directorate, DHS
description: Finances DHS headquarters capital investments including facilities consolidation (St. Elizabeths campus), IT infrastructure, and departmental shared services construction and improvements.
beneficiaries: DHS workforce, headquarters consolidation, departmental IT modernization

name: Mental Health, Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration, HHS
description: Funds the Community Mental Health Services Block Grant and other mental health programs including suicide prevention, children's mental health, Project AWARE, and crisis care services for individuals with serious mental illness.
beneficiaries: Individuals with serious mental illness, state mental health authorities, community mental health centers, youth

name: Appalachian Regional Commission
description: An independent federal-state partnership providing economic development assistance to 423 counties across 13 states in the Appalachian region through grants for infrastructure, education, workforce, and community development.
beneficiaries: Distressed communities in Appalachia (from New York to Mississippi), local development districts, state Appalachian programs

name: Defense Production Act Medical Supplies Enhancement
description: Invoked under the Defense Production Act during COVID-19 to fund domestic production of critical medical supplies including N95 masks, ventilators, swabs, reagents, and pharmaceutical ingredients.
beneficiaries: U.S. health care system, domestic medical supply manufacturers, pandemic preparedness

name: Payment to Air Carriers, Office of the Secretary, Transportation
description: Subsidizes Essential Air Service (EAS) program payments to airlines serving small and remote communities that would otherwise lose scheduled air service due to insufficient passenger demand.
beneficiaries: Small and rural communities with EAS-subsidized routes, air carriers providing essential air service, rural travelers

name: Farm Production and Conservation Business Center, Agriculture
description: Centralized administrative support for USDA's Farm Production and Conservation mission area, providing shared IT, financial management, human resources, and customer service to FSA, NRCS, and RMA.
beneficiaries: Farm Service Agency, NRCS, Risk Management Agency operations and efficiency, farmers/ranchers served by these agencies

name: Enduring Welcome Administration and Support, State
description: Funds State Department operations supporting the resettlement and processing of Afghan and other special immigrant populations following the U.S. withdrawal from Afghanistan, covering SIV processing and reception services.
beneficiaries: Afghan Special Immigrant Visa holders, Afghan refugees, resettlement processing operations

name: Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Contribution, National Guard Personnel, Air Force
description: Annual accrual payment to the Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund for Air National Guard personnel, pre-funding future TRICARE-for-Life benefits for members who will become Medicare-eligible retirees.
beneficiaries: Air National Guard retirees becoming Medicare-eligible, TRICARE-for-Life beneficiaries

name: Operations and Support, Intelligence, Analysis, and Operations Coordination, DHS
description: Funds the DHS Office of Intelligence and Analysis (I&A), which collects, analyzes, and shares intelligence with state, local, tribal, and private-sector partners through fusion centers to address homeland security threats.
beneficiaries: State and local law enforcement, fusion centers, critical infrastructure owners, homeland security intelligence community

name: Customs and Border Protection, Transfer and Expenses of Operation, Puerto Rico
description: Funds CBP operations in Puerto Rico including customs inspections, border security, and trade enforcement at Puerto Rican ports of entry, with costs transferred from collections.
beneficiaries: Puerto Rican trade and travel, CBP operations in Puerto Rico, customs enforcement

name: Fees and Expenses of Witnesses, Justice
description: Funds witness fees, mileage, and subsistence for witnesses appearing in federal court cases on behalf of the United States, as required by law, including expert witness fees and witness protection relocation costs.
beneficiaries: Federal court witnesses, U.S. Attorneys' offices, federal judicial proceedings

name: Rural Health, Health Resources and Services Administration, HHS
description: Funds grants and programs supporting health care access in rural areas, including Rural Health Clinics, telehealth networks, small rural hospital improvement, rural emergency medical services, and the State Offices of Rural Health.
beneficiaries: Rural hospitals, rural health clinics, rural residents, telehealth programs, rural EMS services

name: Fisheries Disaster Assistance, NOAA, Commerce
description: Provides financial assistance to fishing communities, fishermen, and related businesses affected by commercial fishery failures or disasters declared by the Secretary of Commerce, covering lost income and industry recovery.
beneficiaries: Commercial fishermen, fishing communities, fishery-dependent businesses, aquaculture operations affected by disasters

name: National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health, CDC, HHS
description: Conducts research and makes recommendations for preventing work-related injury, illness, and death, covering mining safety, respiratory disease, musculoskeletal disorders, and workplace chemical exposures.
beneficiaries: Workers across all industries, employers implementing safety measures, occupational health researchers

name: Fossil Energy and Carbon Management, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds R&D on carbon capture, utilization, and storage (CCUS), hydrogen production, critical minerals extraction, methane reduction, and technologies to reduce carbon emissions from fossil energy use.
beneficiaries: Energy industry, communities near fossil fuel facilities, carbon management researchers, climate mitigation efforts

name: Ryan White HIV/AIDS Program, HRSA, HHS
description: The largest federal program specifically for people living with HIV/AIDS, providing grants to cities, states, and community organizations for medical care, medications (AIDS Drug Assistance Program), support services, and early intervention.
beneficiaries: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS (especially uninsured/underinsured), community health centers, state ADAP programs

name: Recreation Enhancement Fee Program, National Park Service
description: Retains and uses recreation fees collected at NPS sites (entrance fees, camping fees, special use permits) for facility maintenance, visitor services, resource protection, and fee collection costs under the Federal Lands Recreation Enhancement Act.
beneficiaries: National park visitors, park facilities and maintenance, visitor experience improvements

name: District of Columbia Courts
description: Federal appropriation funding the operations of the D.C. court system (Superior Court and Court of Appeals), which unlike other local court systems receives federal funding because D.C. is a federal district.
beneficiaries: D.C. residents using the court system, judges, court staff, litigants in D.C. courts

name: Gulf Coast Restoration Trust Fund, Bureau of the Fiscal Service, Treasury
description: Receives 80% of Clean Water Act civil and criminal penalties from the Deepwater Horizon oil spill, distributing funds to Gulf Coast states and federal agencies for ecosystem restoration, economic recovery, and research under the RESTORE Act.
beneficiaries: Gulf Coast states (TX, LA, MS, AL, FL), Gulf ecosystem restoration, affected communities and industries

name: State's Share from Certain Gulf of Mexico Leases, Office of Natural Resources Revenue, Interior
description: Distributes revenue sharing payments to Gulf Coast producing states (TX, LA, MS, AL) from oil and gas leases in specified areas of the Gulf of Mexico Outer Continental Shelf under the Gulf of Mexico Energy Security Act (GOMESA).
beneficiaries: Texas, Louisiana, Mississippi, Alabama state/local governments, coastal restoration and conservation

name: Forest and Rangeland Research, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Funds the Forest Service's research branch operating a network of forest and range experiment stations and research labs, conducting science on forest health, fire behavior, watershed hydrology, wildlife habitat, wood utilization, and climate adaptation.
beneficiaries: Forest managers, wildfire scientists, timber industry, watershed managers, forest ecology researchers

name: Cybersecurity, Energy Security, and Emergency Response, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds DOE's CESER office addressing cybersecurity threats to the energy sector, energy supply disruptions, and emergency response coordination for natural disasters and physical/cyber attacks on energy infrastructure.
beneficiaries: Electric utilities, oil/gas infrastructure, energy grid cybersecurity, communities vulnerable to energy disruptions

name: Current Surveys and Programs, Bureau of the Census, Commerce
description: Funds the Census Bureau's ongoing demographic, economic, and housing surveys (Current Population Survey, American Community Survey, economic censuses, foreign trade statistics) conducted between decennial censuses.
beneficiaries: Federal/state/local governments (data for policy), businesses (market data), researchers, congressional apportionment data users

name: Housing for Persons with Disabilities, Housing Programs, HUD
description: The Section 811 program providing capital advances and project rental assistance to nonprofit developers for supportive housing for very low-income adults with disabilities, integrating housing with voluntary services.
beneficiaries: Very low-income adults with disabilities, nonprofit housing developers, disability service organizations

name: Veterans Employment and Training, Departmental Management, Labor
description: Funds the Veterans' Employment and Training Service (VETS) administering programs to help veterans transition to civilian employment, including Jobs for Veterans State Grants, the Homeless Veterans Reintegration Program, and USERRA enforcement.
beneficiaries: Veterans seeking employment, transitioning service members, homeless veterans, employers hiring veterans

name: Working Capital Fund, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: A revolving fund providing centralized services (IT, fleet management, financial systems, geospatial data) to Forest Service program areas on a reimbursable basis, improving operational efficiency.
beneficiaries: Forest Service operational units, National Forest System management efficiency

name: Building Maintenance Fund, Defense
description: Funds maintenance, repair, and minor construction for Pentagon reservation buildings and other DoD headquarters facilities in the National Capital Region.
beneficiaries: Pentagon building operations, DoD headquarters staff, facility maintenance workers

name: Child Nutrition Programs, Food and Nutrition Service, Agriculture
description: Funds the National School Lunch Program, School Breakfast Program, Child and Adult Care Food Program, Summer Food Service Program, and Special Milk Program, providing meals to children in schools and care institutions.
beneficiaries: School-age children (especially low-income), schools, child care centers, summer meal program sites

name: Choice Neighborhoods Initiative, Public and Indian Housing Programs, HUD
description: Competitive grants for comprehensive neighborhood transformation linking severely distressed public housing redevelopment with investments in surrounding neighborhoods covering housing, education, safety, and economic opportunity.
beneficiaries: Residents of distressed public housing, surrounding neighborhoods, local governments, community development organizations

name: Construction and Environmental Compliance and Restoration, NASA
description: Funds construction of new NASA facilities, renovation of existing infrastructure (test stands, launch pads, laboratories), environmental remediation of contaminated sites, and demolition of obsolete structures across NASA centers.
beneficiaries: NASA centers, space mission infrastructure, environmental restoration at NASA sites, local communities near NASA centers

name: Natural Resource Damage Assessment and Restoration Fund, Office of the Secretary (Interior)
description: Funds Interior's assessment of natural resource damages from oil spills, hazardous substance releases, and other environmental contamination, and conducts restoration of injured resources using recovered damages.
beneficiaries: Injured natural resources (fish, wildlife, habitat), communities affected by contamination, restoration ecology

name: Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing Loan Program, Energy
description: Provides direct loans to automobile manufacturers and component suppliers for retooling, expanding, or establishing manufacturing facilities producing advanced technology vehicles and components that improve fuel economy.
beneficiaries: Auto manufacturers, electric vehicle companies, auto workers, advanced vehicle component suppliers

name: Working Capital Fund, Bureau of Land Management
description: A revolving fund providing centralized services (IT, business operations, cadastral survey) to BLM state and field offices on a reimbursable basis for managing 245 million acres of public lands.
beneficiaries: BLM field operations, public land management efficiency, ranchers/miners/recreationists on BLM lands

name: Children and Families Services Programs, Administration for Children and Families, HHS
description: Funds a range of ACF programs supporting vulnerable children and families including Community Services Block Grants, runaway and homeless youth programs, child abuse prevention, family violence prevention, and Native American social services.
beneficiaries: Low-income families, at-risk youth, runaway/homeless youth, domestic violence survivors, tribal communities

name: Capital Magnet Fund, Community Development Financial Institutions, Treasury
description: Provides competitive grants to CDFIs and nonprofit housing organizations to attract private capital for affordable housing and community development, leveraging each federal dollar with at least $10 in private investment.
beneficiaries: Low-income families needing affordable housing, CDFIs, nonprofit housing developers, underserved communities

name: Working Capital Fund, Departmental Management, Commerce
description: A revolving fund providing shared administrative services (HR, IT, procurement, financial management, facilities) to all Commerce Department bureaus on a reimbursable basis.
beneficiaries: Commerce Department bureaus (Census, NOAA, NIST, ITA, etc.), departmental administrative efficiency

name: Buildings and Facilities, National Institutes of Health, HHS
description: Funds construction, renovation, and major repair of research laboratories and support facilities on the NIH campus in Bethesda, MD and other NIH locations, maintaining world-class biomedical research infrastructure.
beneficiaries: NIH intramural researchers, biomedical research infrastructure, NIH campus operations

name: Payments to the Patient-Centered Outcomes Research Trust Fund
description: Mandatory appropriations to the PCORI Trust Fund, which finances the Patient-Centered Outcomes Research Institute conducting comparative clinical effectiveness research to help patients and clinicians make better-informed health care decisions.
beneficiaries: Patients, clinicians, health care decision-makers, clinical effectiveness researchers

name: Working Capital Fund, National Aeronautics and Space Administration
description: A revolving fund providing shared services across NASA centers including IT services, scientific and engineering workstation support, and administrative services on a reimbursable basis.
beneficiaries: NASA centers and missions, agency-wide IT and administrative operations

name: National Endowment for Democracy, State
description: Federally funded, privately operated grant-making foundation supporting democratic institutions, free media, human rights organizations, and civil society worldwide through its core institutes (NDI, IRI, CIPE, Solidarity Center).
beneficiaries: Pro-democracy organizations worldwide, civil society groups, independent media, labor organizations, human rights defenders

name: Marketing Services, Agricultural Marketing Service, Agriculture
description: Funds AMS operations including commodity grading and inspection, organic certification oversight, farmers' market promotion, specialty crop block grants, country-of-origin labeling, and fair trade practice enforcement under PACA.
beneficiaries: Farmers, food processors, consumers (food quality assurance), organic producers, specialty crop growers

name: Maritime Security Program, Maritime Administration, Transportation
description: Provides annual stipend payments to U.S.-flag commercial vessel operators to maintain a fleet of militarily useful merchant ships in international trade, ensuring sealift capacity and a trained mariner workforce for national defense.
beneficiaries: U.S.-flag maritime operators, U.S. merchant mariners, military sealift readiness, defense logistics

name: Formerly Utilized Sites Remedial Action Program (FUSRAP), Corps of Engineers, Civil
description: Cleans up sites contaminated by activities of the Manhattan Project and early Atomic Energy Commission operations, remediating radioactive contamination from former uranium/thorium processing and research facilities.
beneficiaries: Communities near contaminated Manhattan Project-era sites, environmental restoration, public health protection

name: Operation and Maintenance, Marine Corps Reserve
description: Funds day-to-day operations, training, equipment maintenance, and base support for Marine Corps Reserve units, ensuring reserve force readiness for mobilization and integration with active component operations.
beneficiaries: Marine Corps Reserve units and personnel, reserve training centers, combat readiness

name: Federal-Aid Highways (Liquidation of Contract Authorization), FHWA
description: Duplicate entry for the cash disbursement mechanism that pays states for work completed on federal-aid highway projects, converting authorized contract authority into actual Treasury payments.
beneficiaries: State DOTs, highway construction projects, Highway Trust Fund cash management

name: Counter-ISIS Train and Equip Fund
description: Duplicate listing for the fund that equipped and trained partner forces fighting ISIS in Iraq and Syria.
beneficiaries: Iraqi and Syrian partner forces, anti-ISIS coalition

name: Food for Peace Title II
description: Duplicate listing for international food aid grants.
beneficiaries: Food-insecure populations in developing countries

name: State Grants and Demonstration, CMS (batch 6 entry)
description: Duplicate listing for CMS grants to states for health care delivery innovation and Medicaid demonstration projects.
beneficiaries: State Medicaid programs, low-income health care beneficiaries

name: Entrepreneurial Development Programs, SBA
description: Duplicate listing for SBA counseling and training programs.
beneficiaries: Small businesses, entrepreneurs

name: National Endowment for Democracy
description: Duplicate listing for the democracy-promotion grant-making foundation.
beneficiaries: Democratic institutions worldwide

name: Marketing Services, AMS
description: Duplicate listing for agricultural marketing and grading services.
beneficiaries: Farmers, food supply chain

name: Maritime Security Program
description: Duplicate listing for U.S.-flag merchant fleet stipends.
beneficiaries: U.S. maritime industry, defense sealift

name: FUSRAP, Corps of Engineers
description: Duplicate listing for Manhattan Project site remediation.
beneficiaries: Communities near legacy nuclear contamination sites

name: Watershed and Flood Prevention Operations, Natural Resources Conservation Service, Agriculture
description: Supports planning and construction of upstream flood-prevention dams, watershed protection projects, and water management improvements under PL-566 (Watershed Protection and Flood Prevention Act).
beneficiaries: Rural communities, farmers, local watershed districts, flood-prone downstream populations

name: Payments in Lieu of Taxes, Departmental Management, Interior
description: Compensates county and local governments for the loss of tax revenue due to tax-exempt federal lands within their jurisdictions under the PILT Act.
beneficiaries: County and local governments in states with substantial federal land holdings, particularly in the West

name: State Grants and Demonstration, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, HHS
description: Provides formula and competitive grants to states for Medicaid demonstrations, health insurance innovations, and health care delivery improvement projects.
beneficiaries: State Medicaid agencies, low-income beneficiaries, health care providers in demonstration projects

name: National Institute on Alcohol Abuse and Alcoholism, NIH, HHS
description: Conducts and supports biomedical and behavioral research on the causes, consequences, prevention, and treatment of alcohol use disorder, fetal alcohol spectrum disorders, and alcohol-related organ damage.
beneficiaries: Researchers, clinicians treating alcohol use disorders, individuals and families affected by alcohol misuse

name: Contingency Fund for State Welfare Programs, Administration for Children and Families, HHS
description: Provides supplemental TANF funding to states experiencing economic downturns or caseload increases, authorized by the 1996 welfare reform law (Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act).
beneficiaries: States facing rising welfare caseloads, low-income families with children receiving TANF

name: Extension Activities, National Institute of Food and Agriculture, Agriculture
description: Supports the Cooperative Extension System, delivering research-based education through land-grant universities to farmers, ranchers, communities, and youth (4-H) on agriculture, nutrition, natural resources, and community development.
beneficiaries: Farmers, rural communities, 4-H youth, land-grant universities, consumers

name: Public Broadcasting Fund, Corporation for Public Broadcasting, Treasury
description: Federal appropriation to the Corporation for Public Broadcasting distributing funds to local public television and radio stations for programming, operations, interconnection, and the Ready To Learn educational media initiative.
beneficiaries: Local PBS and NPR member stations, public media audiences, rural/underserved communities

name: Administrative Services Franchise Fund, FAA, Transportation
description: Revolving fund enabling FAA to deliver shared administrative services (accounting, HR, IT, procurement) to FAA lines of business and external federal customers on a fee-for-service basis.
beneficiaries: FAA organizational units, other federal agencies purchasing FAA shared services

name: Promoting Safe and Stable Families, Administration for Children and Families, HHS
description: Formula grants to states for family preservation, family reunification, adoption promotion, and support services preventing child abuse and unnecessary foster care placements under Title IV-B, subpart 2 of the Social Security Act.
beneficiaries: At-risk families, children in or near the child welfare system, state child welfare agencies, adoptive families

name: National Institute on Minority Health and Health Disparities, NIH, HHS
description: Leads and supports research to understand and eliminate health disparities affecting racial/ethnic minorities, rural populations, sexual/gender minorities, and socioeconomically disadvantaged groups, studying social determinants of health.
beneficiaries: Health disparities researchers, minority populations, rural communities, medically underserved groups

name: Interagency Crime and Drug Enforcement, Interagency Law Enforcement, Justice
description: Funds the Organized Crime Drug Enforcement Task Forces (OCDETF) coordinating investigations and prosecutions by DEA, FBI, ATF, IRS-CI, and U.S. Attorneys targeting major drug trafficking organizations and money laundering.
beneficiaries: Federal law enforcement agencies, communities affected by drug trafficking, U.S. Attorneys

name: Facilities and Equipment, Federal Aviation Administration, Transportation
description: Procurement, installation, and sustainment of FAA air traffic control systems, navigation equipment, weather systems, and communications infrastructure from general fund appropriations (separate from Trust Fund F&E).
beneficiaries: National Airspace System, air traffic controllers, airlines, general aviation, flying public

name: National Institute on Deafness and Other Communication Disorders, NIH, HHS
description: Funds research on hearing, balance, taste, smell, voice, speech, and language disorders, supporting studies on cochlear implants, hearing loss prevention, auditory processing, and communication rehabilitation.
beneficiaries: People with hearing/speech/language/balance disorders, audiologists, speech-language pathologists, researchers

name: Salaries and Expenses, Federal Energy Regulatory Commission, Energy
description: Funds FERC's regulation of interstate electricity transmission, wholesale power markets, natural gas pipelines, LNG terminals, and hydropower licensing. Costs are fully offset by fees on regulated entities.
beneficiaries: Energy consumers, electric utilities, natural gas pipeline operators, hydropower licensees, energy market participants

name: Research and Education Activities, National Institute of Food and Agriculture, Agriculture
description: Funds competitive research grants (Agriculture and Food Research Initiative - AFRI), capacity grants to land-grant universities, and higher education programs in agriculture, food science, natural resources, and bioenergy.
beneficiaries: Agricultural researchers, land-grant universities, graduate students, farmers benefiting from applied research

name: National Institute of Dental and Craniofacial Research, NIH, HHS
description: Supports research on dental caries, periodontal disease, oral cancer, cleft lip/palate, TMJ disorders, salivary gland dysfunction, and craniofacial development and regeneration.
beneficiaries: Dental patients, oral health researchers, dentists, oral surgeons, individuals with craniofacial conditions

name: Maternal, Infant, and Early Childhood Home Visiting Programs, HRSA, HHS
description: Funds evidence-based home visiting programs (Nurse-Family Partnership, Parents as Teachers, Healthy Families America) for at-risk pregnant women and parents with young children, supporting health, parenting, and child development.
beneficiaries: At-risk pregnant women, new parents, infants and toddlers, tribal communities, home visiting workforce

name: National Library of Medicine, NIH, HHS
description: Operates the world's largest biomedical library and major databases: PubMed/MEDLINE, ClinicalTrials.gov, MedlinePlus, GenBank (NCBI), and funds biomedical informatics and data science research.
beneficiaries: Biomedical researchers, clinicians, public health professionals, patients, bioinformatics community, global health information access

name: Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy (ARPA-E), Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds high-risk, high-reward research in transformative energy technologies too early for private investment: advanced batteries, grid-scale storage, fusion, carbon removal, sustainable fuels, and novel generation concepts.
beneficiaries: Energy researchers, startups, national laboratories, clean energy innovation, long-term U.S. energy competitiveness

name: Consolidated Rail Infrastructure and Safety Improvements Grants, Federal Railroad Administration, Transportation
description: CRISI competitive grants for rail projects: track rehabilitation, grade crossing safety, positive train control, short-line upgrades, rail safety technology, and passenger rail improvements.
beneficiaries: Freight railroads, short-line/regional railroads, Amtrak, commuter rail, communities along rail corridors

name: National Institute of Biomedical Imaging and Bioengineering, NIH, HHS
description: Funds research integrating engineering with life sciences: advanced imaging technologies (MRI, optical, ultrasound), biosensors, point-of-care diagnostics, tissue engineering, drug delivery, and computational modeling for medicine.
beneficiaries: Biomedical engineers, medical device developers, clinicians using advanced diagnostics, patients benefiting from imaging/device innovations

name: CDC-Wide Activities and Program Support, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, HHS
description: Cross-cutting CDC account for public health infrastructure, laboratory science, data modernization, IT systems, workforce development, global health security, buildings/facilities, and administrative support across all CDC centers.
beneficiaries: National public health infrastructure, state/local/tribal health departments, global health security, CDC operations

name: Community Service Employment for Older Americans, Employment and Training Administration, Labor
description: Funds the Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP): subsidized part-time community service jobs and training for low-income persons 55+ to transition to unsubsidized employment, administered through national and state grantees.
beneficiaries: Low-income Americans 55+, host community organizations receiving senior workers, aging workforce integration

name: National Cemetery Administration, Veterans Health Administration, Veterans Affairs (second entry)
description: Duplicate entry for the account operating national cemeteries providing burial benefits to eligible veterans and family members.
beneficiaries: Eligible veterans and dependents, surviving families

name: Procurement, Acquisition and Construction, NOAA, Commerce
description: Finances NOAA capital assets: weather satellites (GOES-R, JPSS), research/survey vessels, hurricane hunter aircraft, high-performance computing, and facility construction for weather, ocean, and fisheries missions.
beneficiaries: Weather forecasting, satellite data users, maritime community, fisheries enforcement, climate observation

name: Operation of Indian Education Programs, BIA/BIE, Interior
description: Funds the Bureau of Indian Education's operation of ~183 schools and dormitories serving approximately 46,000 Native American students on or near reservations, plus tribally controlled postsecondary schools support.
beneficiaries: Native American students in BIE schools, tribal education systems, tribal communities

name: Operation of Indian Programs, Bureau of Indian Affairs, Interior
description: BIA's main operating account funding federal trust responsibilities to 574 federally recognized tribes: tribal governance support, natural resource management, law enforcement, social services, real estate services, and economic development.
beneficiaries: Federally recognized tribes, tribal members on reservations, tribal courts, Indian country law enforcement

name: Customs and Border Protection, Transfer and Expenses of Operation, Puerto Rico, Homeland Security
description: CBP operations in Puerto Rico covering customs inspection, trade enforcement, and agricultural quarantine at Puerto Rican ports of entry, historically funded through transferred collections.
beneficiaries: Puerto Rico trade and travel, CBP enforcement operations in Puerto Rico, agricultural protection

name: Federal-Aid Highways, Liquidation of Contract Authorization, FHWA, Transportation
description: Provides cash (liquidating appropriations) from the Highway Trust Fund to reimburse states for expenditures on authorized Federal-Aid Highway projects, converting contract authority into Treasury disbursements.
beneficiaries: State DOTs receiving federal highway reimbursements, highway construction, motoring public

name: Natural Resource Damage Assessment and Restoration Fund, Office of the Secretary, Interior
description: Funds Interior's trustee role assessing natural resource injuries from oil spills and hazardous substance releases, and restoring injured resources using damages recovered from responsible parties under CERCLA and OPA.
beneficiaries: Injured natural resources (fish, wildlife, habitats), affected communities, restoration ecology

name: Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing Loan Program, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Direct loans to auto manufacturers and suppliers for retooling facilities to produce advanced technology vehicles (EVs, hybrids, fuel-efficient vehicles) and components, authorized by EISA 2007 and expanded by IRA 2022.
beneficiaries: Auto manufacturers, EV/advanced vehicle companies, auto workers, advanced vehicle component suppliers

name: Capital Magnet Fund, CDFI, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Competitive awards to CDFIs and qualified nonprofits leveraging private investment (minimum 10:1 ratio) for affordable housing development/preservation and related community development activities in underserved areas.
beneficiaries: Low-income families needing affordable housing, CDFIs, nonprofit housing developers, underserved communities

name: Working Capital Fund, NASA
description: Revolving fund providing shared IT services, scientific/engineering workstations, telecommunications, and administrative support across NASA centers on a reimbursable basis.
beneficiaries: NASA centers, mission support operations, agency-wide IT infrastructure

name: Contract Support Costs, BIA/BIE, Interior
description: Reimburses federally recognized tribes for administrative overhead costs (indirect costs) of operating federal programs assumed under Indian Self-Determination Act (PL 93-638) contracts and compacts, as mandated by statute and court rulings.
beneficiaries: Self-determination tribes, tribal administrative capacity, programs transferred from BIA to tribal operation

name: Salaries and Expenses, Federal Payment to the D.C. Courts
description: Federal appropriation covering judicial salaries, court security, technology, and operating expenses of the District of Columbia court system (Superior Court and Court of Appeals).
beneficiaries: D.C. judges, court staff, D.C. justice system, litigants and residents of the District of Columbia

name: Homeless Assistance Grants, Community Planning and Development, HUD (batch 6 entry)
description: Additional year/sub-account entry for HUD's homelessness programs including Continuum of Care, Emergency Solutions Grants, and related assistance.
beneficiaries: People experiencing homelessness, shelters, permanent supportive housing

name: Natural Resource Damage Assessment and Restoration Fund, Office of the Secretary (batch 6 entry)
description: Additional entry for Interior's assessment and restoration of natural resources injured by contamination events.
beneficiaries: Injured ecosystems, affected communities

name: Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing Loan Program, Energy (batch 6 entry)
description: Additional year entry for ATVM loans to auto manufacturers retooling for advanced vehicles.
beneficiaries: Auto manufacturers, EV sector, advanced vehicle workers

name: Working Capital Fund, Bureau of Land Management (batch 6 entry)
description: Additional entry for BLM's revolving fund providing centralized services.
beneficiaries: BLM field operations, public land management

name: Children and Families Services Programs, ACF, HHS (batch 6 entry)
description: Additional entry for ACF discretionary programs serving vulnerable children and families.
beneficiaries: Low-income families, at-risk youth, domestic violence survivors

name: Salaries and Expenses, Foreign Agricultural Service, Agriculture (batch 6 entry)
description: Additional entry for FAS overseas operations and agricultural trade promotion.
beneficiaries: U.S. agricultural trade promotion, farmers, exporters

name: Management Fund, NIH (batch 6 entry)
description: Additional entry for NIH shared services revolving fund.
beneficiaries: NIH Institutes and Centers, administrative operations

name: Capital Magnet Fund, CDFI, Treasury (batch 6 entry)
description: Additional entry for the affordable housing leveraged investment program.
beneficiaries: Affordable housing, CDFIs, low-income communities

name: Working Capital Fund, Departmental Management, Commerce (batch 6 entry)
description: Additional entry for Commerce Department shared administrative services.
beneficiaries: Commerce Department bureaus

name: Nuclear Energy, Energy Programs, Energy (batch 6 entry)
description: Additional entry for nuclear energy research and development.
beneficiaries: Nuclear energy industry, clean energy

name: Buildings and Facilities, NIH, HHS (batch 6 entry)
description: Additional entry for NIH campus construction and renovation.
beneficiaries: NIH research facilities, intramural science

name: National Sea-Based Deterrence Fund, Navy (batch 6 entry)
description: Additional entry for Columbia-class submarine procurement.
beneficiaries: U.S. nuclear deterrence, submarine force

name: Payments to the Patient-Centered Outcomes Research Trust Fund (batch 6 entry)
description: Additional entry for PCORI Trust Fund mandatory payments.
beneficiaries: Clinical effectiveness research, patients, health system evidence

name: Working Capital Fund, NASA (batch 6 entry)
description: Additional entry for NASA shared IT and administrative services.
beneficiaries: NASA centers and missions

name: Contract Support Costs, BIA/BIE, Interior (batch 6 entry)
description: Additional entry for tribal self-determination contract overhead reimbursement.
beneficiaries: Self-governance tribes, tribal program administration

name: Emergency Rental Assistance, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Provides emergency rental assistance to households unable to pay rent or utilities due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Funds are distributed through state, local, and tribal governments.
beneficiaries: Low-income renters and landlords facing pandemic-related financial hardship

name: Emergency Watershed Protection Program, Natural Resources Conservation Programs, Agriculture
description: Provides funding to safeguard lives and property from floods, drought, and the effects of erosion caused by natural disasters. Helps communities recover from watershed emergencies.
beneficiaries: Rural landowners, farmers, and communities affected by natural disasters impacting watersheds

name: Emerging and Zoonotic Infectious Diseases, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Health and Human Services
description: Supports surveillance, prevention, and control of infectious diseases that emerge from animal-to-human transmission or other novel pathways. Funds laboratory capacity and outbreak response.
beneficiaries: General public, public health agencies, and healthcare providers

name: Energy Assistance Payments, Rural Business - Cooperative Service, Agriculture
description: Provides payments to rural energy providers and cooperatives to help reduce energy costs for rural communities. Supports energy efficiency improvements in rural areas.
