beneficiaries: Rural electric cooperatives, rural businesses, and rural households

name: Energy Employees Occupational Illness Compensation Program, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Health and Human Services
description: Provides lump-sum compensation and medical benefits to current and former employees of the Department of Energy and its contractors who became ill due to exposure to radiation, beryllium, or silica. Administered under the Energy Employees Occupational Illness Compensation Program Act.
beneficiaries: Former DOE employees, contractors, and uranium workers (or their survivors) with occupational illnesses

name: Energy Security and Infrastructure Modernization Fund
description: Finances modernization of U.S. energy infrastructure to enhance reliability, resilience, and security against physical and cyber threats. Created under the Inflation Reduction Act and related energy legislation.
beneficiaries: Energy utilities, grid operators, and communities dependent on energy infrastructure

name: Energy Security, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Administers energy-related tax credits and incentives under the Inflation Reduction Act, including clean energy production and investment tax credits. Ensures compliance with energy tax provisions.
beneficiaries: Clean energy producers, businesses investing in renewable energy, and consumers receiving energy tax credits

name: Enhanced Inspectional Services, U.S. Customs and Border Protection, Homeland Security
description: Funds overtime and enhanced customs inspection services at ports of entry requested by private sector stakeholders. Costs are reimbursed by the requesting parties.
beneficiaries: International trade entities, airports, seaports, and border communities requiring additional customs processing capacity

name: Environmental Compliance and Restoration, Coast Guard, Homeland Security
description: Funds the investigation, assessment, and remediation of environmental contamination at current and former Coast Guard facilities. Ensures compliance with federal and state environmental laws.
beneficiaries: Communities near contaminated Coast Guard sites and the environment

name: Environmental Dispute Resolution Fund, Morris K. Udall and Stewart L. Udall Foundation
description: Provides funding for the U.S. Institute for Environmental Conflict Resolution to assist in resolving federal environmental disputes through mediation and collaboration. Helps avoid costly litigation.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies, state and local governments, tribes, and private parties involved in environmental disputes

name: Environmental Improvement and Restoration Fund, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, Commerce
description: Receives and distributes funds from Outer Continental Shelf oil and gas leasing revenues for coastal and marine environmental improvement. Supports habitat restoration and conservation efforts.
beneficiaries: Coastal communities, marine ecosystems, and fisheries

name: Environmental Restoration, Army
description: Funds the cleanup of contamination from past military activities at Army installations, formerly used defense sites, and Base Realignment and Closure locations. Addresses hazardous waste and unexploded ordnance.
beneficiaries: Communities near current and former Army installations, and the environment

name: Environmental Reviews, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Conducts environmental impact assessments and compliance reviews for energy projects under the National Environmental Policy Act. Ensures DOE activities meet environmental requirements.
beneficiaries: Communities affected by energy projects, environmental stakeholders, and project developers

name: Eunice Kennedy Shriver National Institute of Child Health and Human Development, National Institute of Health, Health and Human Resources
description: Conducts and supports research on fertility, pregnancy, childhood development, and rehabilitation science. Studies birth defects, intellectual and developmental disabilities, and demographic and behavioral processes.
beneficiaries: Children, pregnant women, families, and individuals with developmental disabilities

name: Executive Office for Immigration Review, General Administration, Justice
description: Houses the immigration court system that adjudicates immigration cases including removal proceedings and asylum claims. Includes the Board of Immigration Appeals.
beneficiaries: Immigrants, asylum seekers, and respondents in immigration court proceedings

name: Executive Offices, Management and Administration, Housing and Urban Development
description: Funds the executive leadership and management offices of the Department of Housing and Urban Development. Supports the Secretary and senior officials in policy direction and oversight.
beneficiaries: HUD leadership and staff, and indirectly all HUD program beneficiaries

name: Expenses and Refunds, Inspection of Farm Products, Food Safety and Inspection Service, Agriculture
description: Funds the inspection of meat, poultry, and egg products to ensure they are safe, wholesome, and properly labeled. Covers related expenses and refunds.
beneficiaries: Consumers, meatpackers, poultry processors, and the food industry

name: Expenses of Transportation Audit Contracts and Contract Administration, General Services Administration
description: Funds auditing of freight and passenger transportation bills paid by the federal government to ensure carriers are paid correctly. Recovers overcharges and deductions.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies and taxpayers through cost recovery on transportation expenditures

name: Expenses, Brush Disposal, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Covers the costs of disposing of brush and other debris from timber sales and forest management activities on national forests. Reduces wildfire fuel loads.
beneficiaries: Communities near national forests and forest ecosystems

name: Expenses, Customer Protection, Commodity Futures Trading Commission
description: Funds the Commodity Futures Trading Commission's activities to protect customers in derivatives markets from fraud, manipulation, and abusive practices. Supports market oversight and enforcement.
beneficiaries: Investors, traders, and participants in futures and derivatives markets

name: Expenses, Denali Commission
description: Funds the operating expenses of the Denali Commission, an independent federal agency designed to provide critical utilities, infrastructure, and economic support throughout Alaska. Focuses on rural Alaska communities.
beneficiaries: Remote and rural communities in Alaska

name: Expenses, Disposal of Surplus Real and Related Personal Property, General Services Administration
description: Covers costs associated with the disposal of surplus federal real property and related personal property. Includes appraisals, environmental assessments, and transfer activities.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies disposing of surplus property and communities receiving transferred properties

name: Expenses, Government-Wide Policy, General Services Administration
description: Funds GSA's development and implementation of government-wide policies for federal property management, travel, fleet, and technology. Promotes efficiency across the federal government.
beneficiaries: All federal agencies and taxpayers through improved government operations

name: Expenses, James Madison Memorial Fellowship Foundation
description: Covers operating expenses of the foundation that awards fellowships to secondary school teachers to pursue graduate study of the U.S. Constitution. Strengthens constitutional education.
beneficiaries: Secondary school teachers of American history and government, and their students

name: Expenses, Office of the Chief Financial Officer, Agriculture
description: Funds the operations of USDA's Office of the Chief Financial Officer, providing financial management, accounting, and budgetary services. Ensures sound financial stewardship of department resources.
beneficiaries: USDA programs and offices, and taxpayers through improved financial management

name: Expenses, Office of the Chief Information Officer, Agriculture
description: Supports information technology management and cybersecurity for the Department of Agriculture. Provides IT services, policy guidance, and technology modernization across USDA.
beneficiaries: USDA agencies, employees, and stakeholders who depend on department IT systems

name: Expenses, Road Maintenance Deposits, Bureau of Land Management
description: Manages deposits from timber purchasers and other users for maintenance and repair of roads on Bureau of Land Management lands. Ensures road infrastructure supports resource management.
beneficiaries: Timber purchasers, recreational users, and communities with access needs on BLM lands

name: Expenses, Rural Housing Assistance Grants, Rural Housing Service, Agriculture
description: Provides grants for very low-income rural homeowners to repair and rehabilitate their dwellings, and for rental assistance in rural areas. Helps preserve safe rural housing stock.
beneficiaries: Very low-income rural homeowners, elderly rural residents, and rural renters

name: FDA Innovation, CURES Act, Food and Drug Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Provides dedicated funding under the 21st Century Cures Act for FDA to advance regulatory science, modernize clinical trials, and accelerate medical product development. Supports innovation in drug and device review.
beneficiaries: Patients, medical product developers, and the healthcare industry

name: FDA Working Capital Fund, Food and Drug Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Operates as a revolving fund to provide shared administrative services to FDA centers and offices on a cost-reimbursable basis. Covers IT, facilities, financial, and human resource services.
beneficiaries: FDA organizational components and indirectly the public through efficient FDA operations

name: FHA-General and Special Risk Insurance Fund Liquidating Account, Housing and Urban Development
description: Manages the liquidation of FHA mortgage insurance commitments made before fiscal year 1992 under the General and Special Risk programs. Handles claims, property disposition, and loan servicing.
beneficiaries: Mortgage lenders, borrowers with FHA-insured loans, and the federal government's financial position

name: Facilities Management and Construction, National Telecommunications and Information Administration, Commerce
description: Funds construction, maintenance, and management of telecommunications research facilities operated by NTIA. Supports the nation's spectrum management infrastructure.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies and telecommunications stakeholders dependent on spectrum management

name: Facilities and Equipment, Airport and Airway Trust Fund, Federal Aviation Administration, Transportation
description: Funds modernization and improvement of the national airspace system, including air traffic control facilities and equipment, funded through the Airport and Airway Trust Fund. Critical for aviation safety and capacity.
beneficiaries: Airlines, pilots, air travelers, and air traffic controllers

name: Fair Housing Activities, Fair Housing and Equal Opportunity, Housing and Urban Development
description: Funds enforcement of fair housing laws and programs to prevent discrimination in housing based on race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, or familial status. Supports investigations and education.
beneficiaries: Individuals and families subjected to housing discrimination

name: Falcon and Amistad Operating and Maintenance Fund, Energy
description: Covers the operating and maintenance costs of the Falcon and Amistad dams and hydroelectric power plants on the Rio Grande, jointly operated with Mexico. Revenue is generated from power sales.
beneficiaries: Electricity consumers in the Texas border region and communities dependent on Rio Grande water management

name: Family Housing Operation and Maintenance, Defense-Wide
description: Provides for the operation and maintenance of military family housing serving defense agencies that are not service-specific. Covers shared housing infrastructure costs.
beneficiaries: Military families of defense agency personnel living in government-provided housing

name: Farm Production and Conservation Business Center, Farm Production and Conservation Programs, Agriculture
description: Provides centralized business and administrative services (IT, financial management, human resources) to USDA's farm production and conservation mission area agencies. Improves operational efficiency.
beneficiaries: USDA farm program agencies (FSA, NRCS, RMA) and the farmers they serve

name: Farm Storage Facility Loans Program Accounts, Farm Service Agency, Agriculture
description: Provides low-interest loans to farmers and ranchers to build or upgrade on-farm storage and handling facilities for eligible commodities. Helps producers manage harvest timing and marketing.
beneficiaries: Farmers and ranchers needing commodity storage and handling infrastructure

name: Federal Aid to Wildlife Restoration, United States Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Distributes excise tax revenues from firearms, ammunition, and archery equipment to states for wildlife restoration, conservation, and hunter education. Known as the Pittman-Robertson Act program.
beneficiaries: State fish and wildlife agencies, hunters, and wildlife populations

name: Federal Assistance, U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services, Homeland Security
description: Funds grants to states and communities for citizenship and integration services, including English language training and civics instruction for immigrants. Supports lawful immigration processing.
beneficiaries: Immigrants pursuing citizenship and communities providing integration services

name: Federal Election Commission
description: Administers and enforces federal campaign finance laws, including disclosure of campaign contributions and expenditures, public funding of presidential elections, and contribution limits. Independent regulatory agency.
beneficiaries: Voters, political candidates, parties, and political action committees

name: Federal Financial Institutions Examination Council
description: Prescribes uniform principles, standards, and report forms for the federal examination of financial institutions. Coordinates interagency supervisory activities among banking regulators.
beneficiaries: Financial institutions, banking regulators, and depositors

name: Federal Housing Administration Fund, Housing and Urban Development
description: Operates the FHA mortgage insurance programs that enable homeownership by insuring lenders against losses on mortgage loans. The largest government mortgage insurance program.
beneficiaries: Homebuyers (especially first-time and low-to-moderate income), mortgage lenders, and the housing market

name: Federal Interest Liabilities to the States, Bureau of the Fiscal Service, Treasury
description: Pays interest to states when the federal government is late in transferring grant funds or other payments owed to states. Ensures timely federal financial obligations to states are met.
beneficiaries: State governments awaiting federal fund transfers

name: Federal Labor Relations Authority
description: Administers the labor-management relations program for federal employees, resolving disputes and ensuring compliance with collective bargaining rights under the Civil Service Reform Act.
beneficiaries: Federal employees, federal employee unions, and federal agency management

name: Federal Land Disposal Account, Bureau of Land Management
description: Receives proceeds from the sale of public lands identified for disposal under the Federal Land Transaction Facilitation Act. Funds are used for acquisition of environmentally sensitive lands.
beneficiaries: Conservation interests, land trusts, and communities near acquired conservation lands

name: Federal Maritime Commission
description: Regulates the U.S. international ocean transportation system for the benefit of exporters, importers, and the consumer. Ensures competitive and reliable ocean shipping services.
beneficiaries: U.S. exporters, importers, ocean carriers, and consumers of imported goods

name: Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service
description: Provides mediation and conflict resolution services to prevent and resolve labor-management disputes in the private sector, public sector, and international arena. Independent agency promoting industrial peace.
beneficiaries: Employers, labor unions, and workers involved in labor disputes

name: Federal Mine Safety and Health Review Commission
description: Provides adjudicative review of mine safety and health enforcement actions initiated by the Mine Safety and Health Administration. Independent quasi-judicial agency deciding contested cases.
beneficiaries: Mine operators, miners, and mining industry stakeholders

name: Federal Payment for Emergency Planning and Security Costs in the District of Columbia, Treasury
description: Provides federal payments to the District of Columbia to cover extraordinary costs of emergency planning and security related to the District's role as the nation's capital. Covers events of national significance.
beneficiaries: District of Columbia government and residents, and visitors to the national capital

name: Federal Payment for Judicial Commissions, Treasury
description: Provides federal funding to support the operations of judicial commissions in the District of Columbia, including the Commission on Judicial Disabilities and Tenure. Ensures judicial accountability.
beneficiaries: The D.C. court system, litigants, and D.C. residents

name: Federal Payment for Resident Tuition Support, Treasury
description: Funds the DC Tuition Assistance Grant Program (DC TAG), which helps District of Columbia residents attend public universities in any state at in-state tuition rates. Expands higher education access.
beneficiaries: District of Columbia residents attending out-of-state public colleges and universities

name: Federal Payment for School Improvement, Treasury
description: Provides federal funding to improve educational outcomes in District of Columbia public and charter schools. Supports academic programs and school reform initiatives.
beneficiaries: D.C. public school and charter school students, teachers, and families

name: Federal Payment for Testing and Treatment of HIV/AIDS, Treasury
description: Funds HIV/AIDS testing, treatment, and prevention programs in the District of Columbia. Addresses the disproportionately high HIV/AIDS rates in the District.
beneficiaries: D.C. residents at risk of or living with HIV/AIDS

name: Federal Payment for Water and Sewer Services, Treasury
description: Reimburses the District of Columbia for water and sewer services provided to federal properties in the District. Covers costs that would otherwise be borne by D.C. ratepayers.
beneficiaries: D.C. Water and Sewer Authority and D.C. ratepayers

name: Federal Payment for the District of Columbia National Guard, Treasury
description: Provides a federal contribution to the District of Columbia National Guard to support its unique responsibilities as the guard force for the nation's capital. Funds personnel and operational costs.
beneficiaries: D.C. National Guard members and the security of the national capital region

name: Federal Payment to Morris K. Udall Scholarship and Excellence in National Environmental Policy Foundation
description: Provides the federal contribution to the Udall Foundation supporting scholarships for students pursuing environmental and Native American health careers. Also funds environmental conflict resolution.
beneficiaries: College students in environmental studies and Native American health fields

name: Federal Payment to the Criminal Justice Coordinating Council, Treasury
description: Funds the D.C. Criminal Justice Coordinating Council to coordinate criminal justice policy and information sharing among D.C. agencies and federal law enforcement. Improves public safety coordination.
beneficiaries: D.C. criminal justice agencies, law enforcement, and D.C. residents

name: Federal Payment to the District of Columbia Judicial Survivors and Annuity Fund, Treasury
description: Provides the federal contribution to the retirement annuity fund for survivors of deceased D.C. judges. Ensures continued financial support for judicial families.
beneficiaries: Surviving spouses and dependents of deceased District of Columbia judges

name: Federal Payment to the District of Columbia Water and Sewer Authority, Treasury
description: Provides direct federal capital contributions to DC Water for major infrastructure projects, including combined sewer overflow remediation. Supports clean water compliance.
beneficiaries: DC Water and Sewer Authority and D.C. area residents and waterways

name: Federal Payment to the Federal District of Columbia Pension Fund, Treasury
description: Covers the federal government's share of pension obligations for D.C. police, firefighters, teachers, and judges hired before 1987. Assumed by the federal government under the 1997 Revitalization Act.
beneficiaries: Retired D.C. police officers, firefighters, teachers, and judges hired before 1987

name: Federal Payments to the Railroad Retirement Accounts, Railroad Retirement Board
description: Transfers federal funds to the Railroad Retirement Trust Fund to cover the portion of railroad retirement benefits equivalent to Social Security. Ensures railroad retirees receive comparable benefits.
beneficiaries: Retired railroad workers and their families

name: Federal Reserve Bank Reimbursement Fund, Bureau of the Fiscal Service, Treasury
description: Reimburses Federal Reserve Banks for services performed as fiscal agents of the United States, including processing government checks, savings bonds, and Treasury securities.
beneficiaries: Federal Reserve Banks performing fiscal agency services and Treasury operations

name: Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund-Treasury Managed, Health and Human Services
description: Manages the trust fund financing Medicare Part B (physician and outpatient services) and Part D (prescription drug coverage). Funded by beneficiary premiums and general revenues.
beneficiaries: Medicare beneficiaries receiving Part B and Part D benefits

name: Federal Tax Lien Revolving Fund, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Provides a revolving fund for the IRS to redeem real property to which the United States has a tax lien, when the property is sold to third parties. Protects federal tax lien interests.
beneficiaries: The federal government (tax revenue recovery) and property owners subject to federal tax liens

name: Federal Unemployment Benefits and Allowances, Employment and Training Administration, Labor
description: Provides trade adjustment assistance and extended unemployment benefits to workers adversely affected by foreign trade, including retraining allowances and relocation assistance under the Trade Act.
beneficiaries: Workers who have lost jobs due to foreign trade competition

name: Federal Unemployment Compensation Program, Employment and Training Administration, Labor
description: Provides unemployment compensation to federal civilian employees and ex-service members under the Unemployment Compensation for Federal Employees program. Mirrors state unemployment insurance.
beneficiaries: Unemployed former federal civilian workers and military veterans

name: Financial Agent Services, Bureau of the Fiscal Service, Treasury
description: Reimburses financial institutions designated as financial agents of the federal government for services performed, including processing government payments, collections, and managing federal accounts.
beneficiaries: Financial institutions serving as fiscal agents and the efficient operation of federal financial services

name: Financial Assistance Oversight and Technical Assistance, Federal Railroad Administration, Transportation
description: Provides oversight of railroad financial assistance programs and technical assistance to railroads and states for rail safety and infrastructure improvements. Supports effective use of federal rail investments.
beneficiaries: Railroad operators, states receiving federal rail grants, and rail passengers

name: Financial Management Capital, Office of the Secretary, Transportation
description: Funds modernization of financial management systems and shared services across the Department of Transportation. Ensures accurate financial reporting and compliance.
beneficiaries: DOT agencies and the public through improved financial accountability

name: First Responder Network Authority, National Telecommunications and Information Administration, Commerce
description: Operates FirstNet, the nationwide broadband network dedicated to public safety communications, created by the Middle Class Tax Relief and Job Creation Act. Ensures first responders have reliable communications.
beneficiaries: Police, fire, EMS, and other first responders nationwide

name: Fisheries Disaster Assistance, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, Commerce
description: Provides financial assistance to fishing communities and fishermen affected by commercial fishery failures or disasters. Helps communities recover from catastrophic fishery losses.
beneficiaries: Commercial fishermen, fishing communities, and related businesses affected by fishery disasters

name: Fisheries Enforcement Asset Forfeiture Fund, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, Commerce
description: Receives proceeds from the forfeiture of property seized in connection with violations of fisheries laws. Funds are used to support fisheries enforcement operations.
beneficiaries: NOAA fisheries enforcement operations and sustainable fisheries management

name: Fisheries Finance Program Account, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, Commerce
description: Provides direct loans for the acquisition, construction, or reconstruction of fishing vessels and shoreside facilities, as well as for fisheries refinancing. Supports the fishing industry's capital needs.
beneficiaries: Commercial fishing vessel owners, aquaculture operators, and fishing shoreside facility operators

name: Fishermen's Contingency Fund, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, Commerce
description: Compensates commercial fishermen for damage to or loss of fishing gear and vessels caused by oil and gas exploration and development on the Outer Continental Shelf.
beneficiaries: Commercial fishermen whose gear or vessels are damaged by offshore energy activities

name: Flexible Benefits Plan Reserve, Office of Personnel Management
description: Maintains the reserve fund for the Federal Flexible Benefits Plan (FEHB-related flexible spending accounts) that allows federal employees to set aside pre-tax dollars for health and dependent care expenses.
beneficiaries: Federal employees participating in flexible spending account programs

name: Food Supply Chain and Agriculture Pandemic Response Program Account, Office of the Secretary, Agriculture
description: Provided pandemic-related assistance to maintain the food supply chain, support agricultural producers, and distribute food to those in need during COVID-19 disruptions.
beneficiaries: Farmers, ranchers, food processors, distributors, and food-insecure households

name: Food and Nutrition, Development Assistance, Economic Assistance, Funds Appropriated to the President
description: Funds international food and nutrition programs as part of U.S. development assistance to promote food security in developing countries. Supports agricultural development and nutrition initiatives abroad.
beneficiaries: Populations in developing countries facing food insecurity and malnutrition

name: Foreign Military Financing Program, Funds, Appropriated to the President
description: Provides grants and loans to foreign governments for the acquisition of U.S. defense equipment, services, and training. A primary tool of U.S. security assistance policy.
beneficiaries: Allied and partner nation militaries purchasing U.S. defense articles and services

name: Foreign National Employees Separation Liability Fund, Agency for International Development
description: Funds the accrued separation pay liability for foreign national employees of USAID upon their departure from employment. Ensures orderly payment of severance obligations abroad.
beneficiaries: Foreign national employees of USAID who are separated from service

name: Foreign National Employees Separation Pay Account, Defense
description: Covers separation pay obligations for foreign national employees of the Department of Defense who are terminated from their positions at overseas installations.
beneficiaries: Foreign national employees of DOD at overseas military installations

name: Foreign Service National Defined Contributions Retirement Fund, State
description: Manages a defined contribution retirement plan for foreign service national employees (locally employed staff) of the Department of State at overseas posts.
beneficiaries: Locally employed staff at U.S. embassies and consulates worldwide

name: Foreign Service National Separation Liability Trust Fund, Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service, Agriculture
description: Funds the accrued separation pay liability for foreign national employees of APHIS working at overseas posts, typically on agricultural inspection and quarantine activities.
beneficiaries: Foreign national employees of APHIS who are separated from service

name: Foreign Service National Separation Liability Trust Fund, Foreign Agricultural Service, Agriculture
description: Covers separation pay liabilities for foreign national employees of the Foreign Agricultural Service at U.S. agricultural trade offices and embassies abroad.
beneficiaries: Locally employed FAS staff at U.S. agricultural offices overseas

name: Foreign Service National Separation Liability Trust Fund, State
description: Funds separation pay obligations for locally employed staff of the Department of State at embassies and consulates worldwide. Provides for severance costs when local employees are separated.
beneficiaries: Locally employed staff at U.S. diplomatic missions globally

name: Foreign Service National Separation Liability Trust Fund, United States Agency for Global Media
description: Covers accrued separation pay liabilities for foreign national employees of the U.S. Agency for Global Media (formerly Broadcasting Board of Governors) at overseas broadcast facilities.
beneficiaries: Foreign national employees of USAGM-supported broadcast operations abroad

name: Foreign Service Retirement and Disability Fund, State
description: Provides retirement, disability, and survivor benefits to retired Foreign Service officers and their families. Operates similarly to other federal employee retirement systems.
beneficiaries: Retired Foreign Service officers, their surviving spouses, and dependents

name: Forest Ecosystems Health and Recovery, Bureau of Land Management
description: Funds forest health treatments and ecosystem restoration activities on BLM-managed public lands, including hazardous fuels reduction and insect and disease management.
beneficiaries: Public lands, communities near fire-prone BLM forests, and forest ecosystems

name: Formerly Utilized Sites Remedial Action Program, Corps of Engineers, Civil
description: Cleans up sites contaminated with radioactive waste from early nuclear weapons development activities of the Manhattan Project and the Atomic Energy Commission. Known as FUSRAP.
beneficiaries: Communities near formerly utilized nuclear sites and the environment

name: Free Clinics Malpractice Claims Fund, Health Resources and Service Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Provides medical malpractice coverage for free clinics and their health care professionals, enabling them to provide care to uninsured patients without the burden of malpractice insurance costs.
beneficiaries: Free clinic operators, volunteer health professionals, and uninsured patients

name: Funds for Strengthening Markets, Income, and Supply (Section 32), Agriculture
description: Provides permanent appropriations from customs receipts to encourage the domestic consumption of agricultural commodities through purchases, diversion programs, and export subsidies. One of the oldest farm support mechanisms.
beneficiaries: Agricultural producers, food assistance program recipients, and the farm economy

name: GSE Mortgage-Backed Securities Purchase Program Account, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Established during the 2008 financial crisis to purchase mortgage-backed securities guaranteed by Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, and Ginnie Mae to stabilize mortgage markets. Now in wind-down.
beneficiaries: Mortgage borrowers, the housing market, and financial market stability

name: General Departmental Management, Departmental Management, Health and Human Services
description: Supports the Secretary of HHS and senior officials in providing department-wide leadership, policy development, and management oversight of all HHS programs and agencies.
beneficiaries: HHS leadership and the public served by HHS health and human service programs

name: General Fund Payment To National Surface Transportation and Innovative Finance Bureau Highway Trust Fund Account, Upward Reestimates, Office of Secretary, Transportation
description: Covers upward reestimates of credit program costs for surface transportation loans and grants administered through the Highway Trust Fund. Ensures accurate credit subsidy accounting.
beneficiaries: State and local transportation agencies receiving federal highway and transit funding

name: General Fund Payment to the Trade Enforcement Trust Fund, Office of the U.S. Trade Representative, Executive Office of the President
description: Transfers general fund resources to support enhanced trade enforcement activities, including monitoring and enforcement of U.S. trade agreements and trade preference programs.
beneficiaries: U.S. industries, workers, and exporters affected by unfair trade practices

name: General Fund Payment, Armed Forces Retirement Home Trust Fund, Armed Forces Retirement Home
description: Provides the federal contribution to the Armed Forces Retirement Home Trust Fund, which operates retirement communities for eligible enlisted military veterans in Washington, D.C. and Gulfport, Mississippi.
beneficiaries: Retired and disabled enlisted military veterans residing at Armed Forces Retirement Homes

name: General Gift Fund, USCG, Homeland Security
description: Receives and expends gifts, bequests, and donations made to the U.S. Coast Guard. Funds may support Coast Guard missions, morale, welfare, and community relations activities.
beneficiaries: Coast Guard personnel, operations, and community programs

name: General Post Fund, National Homes, Veterans Affairs
description: Manages funds received from veterans and other sources at VA medical centers and domiciliary facilities for the welfare and comfort of veteran patients and residents. Supports recreation and amenities.
beneficiaries: Veteran patients and residents at VA medical centers and domiciliaries

name: General and Special Risk Program Account, Federal Housing Administration, Housing and Urban Development
description: Insures mortgages for hospitals, nursing homes, multifamily housing, and other specialized properties under FHA's General and Special Risk programs. Supports credit-subsidized lending.
beneficiaries: Hospitals, nursing homes, multifamily housing developers, and their communities

name: Geothermal Lease Revenues, Payment to Counties, Office of Natural Resources Revenue, Interior
description: Distributes a share of federal geothermal energy leasing revenues to counties where geothermal resources are located. Compensates local governments for impacts of energy development.
beneficiaries: County governments in areas with federal geothermal energy development

name: Gifts and Bequests, Agriculture
description: Receives and administers gifts, bequests, and donations made to the Department of Agriculture for purposes consistent with USDA's mission. Funds are used as specified by donors.
beneficiaries: USDA programs and the agricultural community as designated by donors

name: Gifts and Bequests, Departmental Management, Commerce
description: Accepts and utilizes monetary and in-kind gifts and bequests donated to the Department of Commerce in support of its programs and missions.
beneficiaries: Commerce Department programs as designated by donors

name: Gifts and Bequests, Maritime Administration, Transportation
description: Receives gifts and bequests for the benefit of the Maritime Administration's programs supporting the U.S. merchant marine and maritime industry.
beneficiaries: The U.S. merchant marine, maritime industry, and maritime training programs

name: Gifts and Bequests, Treasury
description: Accepts monetary gifts and bequests to the Department of the Treasury for purposes specified by donors or for the general benefit of Treasury operations.
beneficiaries: Treasury programs and operations as directed by donors

name: Gifts and Contributions, Corporation for National and Community Service
description: Receives gifts and contributions from individuals and organizations to support AmeriCorps and other national service programs. Supplements appropriated funds for community service.
beneficiaries: National service programs, AmeriCorps members, and communities they serve

name: Gifts and Contributions, Peace Corps
description: Accepts gifts and contributions to support Peace Corps volunteer activities and programs in developing countries. Supplements appropriated funds for international development and service.
beneficiaries: Peace Corps volunteers and communities they serve in developing nations

name: Gifts and Donations, African Development Foundation
description: Receives gifts and donations to support the African Development Foundation's mission of providing grants to community-based organizations in Africa for grassroots economic development.
beneficiaries: African community organizations and grassroots development initiatives

name: Gifts and Donations, Agency for International Development
description: Accepts gifts and donations to supplement USAID's international development and humanitarian assistance programs. Supports partnership between private donors and U.S. foreign assistance.
beneficiaries: International development programs and populations in developing countries

name: Gifts and Donations, Bureau of Indian Affairs
description: Receives gifts and donations for the benefit of programs serving American Indian and Alaska Native communities through the Bureau of Indian Affairs.
beneficiaries: American Indian and Alaska Native communities and BIA programs

name: Gifts and Donations, Bureau of Indian Affairs and Bureau of Indian, Interior
description: Accepts gifts and donations for programs supporting American Indian and Alaska Native education, tribal governance, and natural resource management under BIA and BIE.
beneficiaries: American Indian and Alaska Native tribal members and communities

name: Gifts and Donations, Centers for Disease Control, Health and Human Services
description: Receives gifts and donations from private entities to support CDC's public health mission including disease prevention, health promotion, and emergency preparedness activities.
beneficiaries: CDC research and public health programs and the general public

name: Gifts and Donations, Commission for the Preservation of America's Heritage Abroad
description: Accepts gifts and donations to support the Commission's mission of preserving cemeteries, monuments, and historic buildings in Eastern and Central Europe associated with the American heritage.
beneficiaries: Preservation of American cultural heritage sites in Eastern and Central Europe

name: Gifts and Donations, Denali Commission
description: Receives gifts and donations to supplement the Denali Commission's work providing infrastructure and economic development in remote Alaska communities.
beneficiaries: Rural Alaska communities and the Denali Commission's infrastructure programs

name: Gifts and Donations, Department Management, Homeland Security
description: Accepts gifts and donations for use by the Department of Homeland Security in carrying out its missions of border security, disaster response, and counterterrorism.
beneficiaries: DHS programs and the public safety mission of the department

name: Gifts and Donations, Energy
description: Receives gifts and donations to support the Department of Energy's missions in energy research, nuclear security, and environmental cleanup.
beneficiaries: DOE programs in energy, science, and national security

name: Gifts and Donations, Institute of Museum and Library Services
description: Accepts gifts and donations to support IMLS programs that strengthen museums and libraries, fund research, and expand access to cultural and educational resources nationwide.
beneficiaries: Museums, libraries, and the communities they serve across the United States

name: Gifts and Donations, National Endowment for the Arts
description: Receives gifts and donations from the private sector to supplement NEA grant-making activities supporting arts education, creativity, and public access to the arts.
beneficiaries: Artists, arts organizations, and communities across the country

name: Gifts and Donations, National Endowment for the Humanities
description: Accepts gifts and donations to augment NEH's support for research, education, and public programs in the humanities including history, philosophy, and literature.
beneficiaries: Scholars, educators, cultural institutions, and the general public interested in the humanities

name: Gifts and Donations, the White House Conference on Drug Abuse and Control, Executive Office of the President
description: Receives private gifts and donations to support activities related to White House conferences and initiatives addressing drug abuse and drug control policy.
beneficiaries: Anti-drug abuse programs and drug policy initiatives

name: Gifts, Crime Victims Fund, Office of Justice Programs, Justice
description: Accepts gifts and donations to supplement the Crime Victims Fund, which supports victim assistance and compensation programs through fines and penalties collected from federal offenders.
beneficiaries: Victims of crime and victim services organizations

name: Gifts, Donations and Contributions, Inter-American Foundation
description: Receives gifts, donations, and contributions to support the Inter-American Foundation's grassroots development programs in Latin America and the Caribbean.
beneficiaries: Community organizations and grassroots development in Latin America and the Caribbean

name: Global Agriculture and Food Security Program, Treasury
description: Provides the U.S. contribution to the multilateral Global Agriculture and Food Security Program trust fund, which finances agricultural development and food security in low-income countries.
beneficiaries: Smallholder farmers and food-insecure populations in developing countries

name: Global HIV/AIDS Initiative, State
description: Funds the President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief (PEPFAR) activities managed by the State Department, supporting HIV/AIDS prevention, treatment, and care programs worldwide.
beneficiaries: People living with and at risk of HIV/AIDS in partner countries, primarily in sub-Saharan Africa

name: Global Security Contingency Fund, State
description: Provides flexible funding for the Departments of State and Defense to respond quickly to emerging security challenges, including training and equipping foreign security forces. Supports security sector assistance.
beneficiaries: Foreign security forces and partner nations facing urgent security threats

name: Government Payment for Annuitants, Employee Life Insurance Benefits, Office of Personnel Management
description: Covers the government's share of life insurance premiums for retired federal employees and their survivors under the Federal Employees Group Life Insurance program.
beneficiaries: Retired federal employees and survivors receiving government life insurance benefits

name: Grants for Construction of State Extended Care Facilities, Departmental Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Provides matching grants to states for the construction or renovation of state veterans nursing homes, domiciliary facilities, and adult day health care centers.
beneficiaries: Veterans in state-operated extended care facilities and state veterans affairs departments

name: Grants for Construction of State Veterans Cemeteries, Departmental Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Provides grants to states for the establishment, expansion, and improvement of state veterans cemeteries. Ensures dignified burial options for veterans closer to their families.
beneficiaries: Veterans, their families, and states operating veterans cemeteries

name: Grants-In-Aid for Airports, Liquidation of Contract Authorization, Airport and Airway Trust Fund, Federal Aviation Administration, Transportation
description: Liquidates contract authority obligations for airport improvement grants from the Airport and Airway Trust Fund. Provides cash disbursements for previously authorized airport construction projects.
beneficiaries: Airports receiving federal improvement grants and the traveling public

name: Grassroots Source Water Protection Program, Farm Service Agency, Agriculture
description: Provides grants to grassroots organizations working to protect source water areas from contamination in rural communities. Supports local, voluntary, community-driven conservation efforts.
beneficiaries: Rural communities, local water utilities, and grassroots conservation organizations

name: Great Lakes St. Lawrence Seaway Development Corporation Fund, Transportation
description: Operates and maintains the U.S. portion of the St. Lawrence Seaway, a deep-draft waterway connecting the Great Lakes to the Atlantic Ocean. Supports commercial shipping and navigation.
beneficiaries: Commercial shipping interests, Great Lakes port communities, and international trade

name: Green and Resilient Retrofit Program for Multifamily Housing, Housing Programs, Housing and Urban Development
description: Provides grants and loans to retrofit HUD-assisted multifamily housing for improved energy efficiency, climate resilience, and indoor air quality. Created under the Inflation Reduction Act.
beneficiaries: Residents of HUD-assisted multifamily housing and property owners

name: Grid Deployment, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds deployment of electric grid infrastructure including transmission lines, smart grid technologies, and grid resilience improvements under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.
beneficiaries: Electric utilities, grid operators, and electricity consumers

name: Guam World War II Claims Fund, Treasury
description: Provides compensation to residents of Guam who suffered death, personal injury, forced labor, or internment during the Japanese occupation in World War II. Addresses long-standing claims.
beneficiaries: Guam residents and their survivors who suffered during WWII Japanese occupation

name: Guaranteed Student Loans, Liquidating Account, Education
description: Manages the liquidation of pre-1992 Federal Family Education Loan Program guaranteed student loan commitments. Handles defaulted loan collections and lender claims.
beneficiaries: The federal government through recovery of defaulted student loan funds

name: Guarantees of Mortgage-Backed Securities Fund, Government National Mortgage Association, Housing and Urban Development
description: Supports Ginnie Mae's guarantee of timely payment of principal and interest on mortgage-backed securities backed by federally insured or guaranteed loans. Ensures liquidity in the mortgage market.
beneficiaries: Mortgage investors, lenders, and homebuyers benefiting from liquid mortgage markets

name: Guarantees of Mortgage-Backed Securities Loan Guarantees, Program Account, Government National Mortgage Association, Housing and Urban Development
description: Accounts for the credit subsidy costs of Ginnie Mae's mortgage-backed securities guarantee program under FCRA. Supports ongoing government guarantees of MBS pools.
beneficiaries: Mortgage lenders issuing Ginnie Mae securities and homebuyers accessing affordable mortgages

name: Gulf Coast Ecosystem Restoration Science, Observation, Monitoring and Technology, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, Commerce
description: Funds NOAA's scientific research, monitoring, and technology development to support Gulf Coast ecosystem restoration after the Deepwater Horizon oil spill. Informs restoration planning.
beneficiaries: Gulf Coast ecosystems, fishing communities, and restoration decision-makers

name: H-1B and L Fraud Prevention and Detection Account, Citizenship and Immigration Services, Homeland Security
description: Receives fraud prevention fees from H-1B and L visa petitions to fund anti-fraud activities and compliance reviews by USCIS. Supports investigations of visa fraud.
beneficiaries: USCIS fraud detection programs and the integrity of the H-1B/L visa system

name: H-1B and L Fraud Prevention and Detection Account, State
description: Funds the State Department's share of H-1B and L visa fraud prevention and detection activities at consular posts abroad. Supports pre-screening and compliance monitoring.
beneficiaries: U.S. consular operations and the integrity of the employment visa system

name: H-1B and L Fraud Protection and Detection Account, Wage and Hour Division, Labor
description: Funds Department of Labor enforcement activities to detect and prevent fraud and abuse in the H-1B and L visa programs, including wage and workplace violations investigations.
beneficiaries: U.S. and foreign workers protected from H-1B/L visa fraud and wage abuse

name: HHS Accrual Contribution to the Uniformed Services Retiree Health Care Fund, Office of the Assistant Secretary for Health, Health and Human Services
description: Covers HHS's accrual contribution for the future health care costs of uniformed service members (Commissioned Corps) currently serving within HHS. Required by the National Defense Authorization Act.
beneficiaries: U.S. Public Health Service Commissioned Corps members and future retirees

name: HHS Service and Supply Fund, Program Support Center, Health and Human Services
description: Operates as a revolving fund providing shared administrative services to HHS agencies including financial management, human resources, IT, and procurement on a fee-for-service basis.
beneficiaries: HHS agencies using shared services and the public through efficient HHS operations

name: HIRE Vets Medallion Award Fund, Departmental Management, Labor
description: Funds the HIRE Vets Medallion Program which recognizes employers who recruit, employ, and retain veterans through a tiered award system. Encourages veteran hiring in the private sector.
beneficiaries: Military veterans seeking employment and employers committed to hiring veterans

name: HIV/AIDS Working Capital Fund, Agency for International Development
description: Operates as a revolving fund to provide efficient management and procurement of commodities and services for PEPFAR-related HIV/AIDS programs administered by USAID.
beneficiaries: HIV/AIDS program recipients in developing countries supported by PEPFAR

name: HIV/AIDS, Viral Hepatitis, Sexually Transmitted Diseases, and Tuberculosis Prevention, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Health and Human Services
description: Supports CDC's domestic prevention programs for HIV/AIDS, viral hepatitis, STDs, and tuberculosis through surveillance, testing, partner services, and public health interventions.
beneficiaries: Individuals at risk of or living with HIV, hepatitis, STDs, or TB in the United States

name: Harbor Maintenance Fee Collection, USCS, Homeland Security
description: Collects the harbor maintenance fee (ad valorem tax) on commercial cargo imported through U.S. ports to fund the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund for port and harbor dredging and maintenance.
beneficiaries: U.S. ports, the shipping industry, and domestic and international trade

name: Hardwood Technology Transfer and Applied Research Fund, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Funds research and technology transfer focused on hardwood forest management and utilization in the eastern United States. Supports sustainable hardwood industry practices.
beneficiaries: Hardwood timber industry, forest landowners, and forest researchers

name: Harry S Truman Scholarship Foundation
description: Awards merit-based scholarships to college students committed to careers in public service. Provides up to $30,000 for graduate study and leadership development opportunities.
beneficiaries: Outstanding college juniors pursuing careers in government or public service

name: Harry S. Truman Memorial Scholarship Trust Fund, Harry S Truman Scholarship Foundation
description: Manages the trust fund whose investment income finances the Truman Scholarship program. The endowment was established by Congress to perpetually fund scholarships for future public servants.
beneficiaries: Truman Scholars and aspiring public servants

name: Hazardous Materials Management, Agriculture
description: Funds the identification, assessment, and cleanup of hazardous materials contamination at USDA facilities and on USDA-managed lands. Ensures compliance with environmental regulations.
beneficiaries: USDA facilities, neighboring communities, and the environment

name: Hazardous Materials Safety, Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration, Transportation
description: Regulates the safe transportation of hazardous materials by all modes of transport (highway, rail, air, and water) and funds safety research, training, and emergency response planning.
beneficiaries: Communities near hazardous materials transportation routes, shippers, carriers, and first responders

name: Hazardous Waste Electronic Manifest System Fund, Environmental Protection Agency
description: Operates the national electronic manifest system (e-Manifest) for tracking hazardous waste shipments from generation to disposal. Funded by user fees from waste handlers.
beneficiaries: Hazardous waste generators, transporters, treatment facilities, and EPA enforcement

name: Health Centers Malpractice Claims, Health Resources and Services Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Provides medical malpractice coverage under the Federal Tort Claims Act for community health centers and their practitioners, reducing insurance costs and enabling care in underserved areas.
beneficiaries: Federally qualified health center practitioners and the patients they serve

name: Health Education Assistance Loans Liquidating Account, Education
description: Manages the liquidation of pre-1992 Health Education Assistance Loan commitments, including collections on defaulted loans to students in health professions programs.
beneficiaries: The federal government through recovery of defaulted health education loan funds

name: Health Education Assistance Loans Program Account, Education
description: Accounts for post-1991 credit reform costs of the Health Education Assistance Loan program providing federally insured loans to graduate students in health professions schools.
beneficiaries: Graduate students in medical, dental, veterinary, and other health professions programs

name: Health Resources and Services, Health Resources and Services Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Funds HRSA programs that improve access to health care for underserved populations, including maternal and child health, organ transplantation, and health workforce development.
beneficiaries: Underserved and vulnerable populations, maternal and child health, and health care providers

name: Health Surveillance and Program Support, Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Supports SAMHSA's data collection, surveillance, and program management activities for behavioral health including the National Survey on Drug Use and Health. Informs substance abuse and mental health policy.
beneficiaries: Behavioral health policymakers, researchers, and populations affected by substance abuse and mental illness

name: Healthcare Research and Quality, Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality, Health and Human Services
description: Funds research to improve the quality, safety, efficiency, and effectiveness of health care for all Americans. Supports evidence-based clinical practice guidelines and patient safety initiatives.
beneficiaries: Health care providers, patients, health systems, and policymakers

name: Healthy Foods Financing Initiative, Rural Business-Cooperative Service, Agriculture
description: Provides financial assistance to increase access to healthy, affordable food in underserved urban and rural communities, commonly called food deserts. Supports grocery stores and food retail projects.
beneficiaries: Residents of food deserts and food retail businesses in underserved communities

name: Helium Fund, Bureau of Land Management
description: Manages the federal helium reserve and related operations including storage, processing, and sale of crude helium. Transitioning toward privatization of federal helium assets.
beneficiaries: Scientific research institutions, medical facilities, defense and aerospace industries dependent on helium

name: Hermit's Peak/Calf Canyon Fire Assistance Account, Federal Emergency Management Agency, Homeland
description: Provides claims payments and assistance to individuals, businesses, and communities affected by the Hermit's Peak/Calf Canyon Fire in New Mexico, which was caused by federal prescribed burn operations.
beneficiaries: Individuals, tribes, businesses, and local governments in New Mexico affected by the 2022 fire

name: High Energy Cost Grants, Rural Utilities Service, Agriculture
description: Provides grants to reduce the high cost of energy in rural communities where energy costs significantly exceed the national average. Supports energy generation, transmission, and efficiency projects.
beneficiaries: Rural communities with exceptionally high energy costs, particularly in Alaska and remote areas

name: Higher Education Facilities Loans and Insurance, Education
description: Managed the now-inactive program of direct loans and loan insurance for construction, renovation, and improvement of academic facilities at institutions of higher education.
beneficiaries: Colleges and universities (program is in liquidation/inactive status)

name: Highway Traffic Safety Grants, Liquidation of Contract Authorization, Trust Fund, National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, Transportation
description: Liquidates obligations for highway traffic safety grants from the Highway Trust Fund to states for programs reducing traffic crashes, deaths, and injuries. Covers behavioral safety programs.
beneficiaries: State highway safety offices, drivers, and the traveling public

name: Highway Trust Fund - Treasury Managed, Transportation
description: Manages the investment and cash flow of the Highway Trust Fund, which finances federal highway and transit programs through motor fuel taxes and other transportation-related fees.
beneficiaries: State and local transportation agencies, highway users, and transit riders

name: Historically Black College and University Capital Financing Program Account, Education
description: Provides low-cost capital financing to Historically Black Colleges and Universities for construction, renovation, and infrastructure improvements through federally guaranteed loans.
beneficiaries: HBCUs and their students through improved campus facilities and infrastructure

name: Home Investment Partnerships Program, Community and Planning Development, Housing and Urban Development
description: Provides formula grants to states and localities for a wide range of affordable housing activities including construction, rehabilitation, and rental assistance for low-income households. Known as HOME.
beneficiaries: Low-income families, homebuyers, and renters in participating jurisdictions

name: Home Ownership Preservation Entity Fund Program Account, Housing and Urban Development
description: Created during the housing crisis to support entities working to prevent foreclosures and preserve homeownership through counseling and loan modification assistance.
beneficiaries: Homeowners facing foreclosure and housing counseling organizations

name: Homeowner Assistance Fund, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Provides financial assistance to homeowners who experienced financial hardship due to the COVID-19 pandemic, helping with mortgage payments, property taxes, utilities, and insurance. Created by the American Rescue Plan Act.
beneficiaries: Homeowners at risk of mortgage default or foreclosure due to COVID-19 hardship

name: Homeowners Assistance Fund - Recovery Act, Defense
description: Expanded the Homeowners Assistance Program under the Recovery Act to help military and civilian DOD personnel who suffered home value losses due to BRAC closures or the economic downturn.
beneficiaries: Military members and DOD civilians who experienced home value loss near military installations

name: Homeowners Assistance Fund, Defense
description: Assists DOD military and civilian employees who are unable to sell their homes due to base realignment and closure actions or other military relocations that reduce local property values.
beneficiaries: Military service members and DOD civilians affected by base closures or realignments

name: Host Country Resident Contractors Separation Liability Fund, Peace Corps
description: Funds accrued separation pay liabilities for host country resident contractors employed by the Peace Corps at overseas posts. Ensures proper payment upon contract termination.
beneficiaries: Host country contractors working for the Peace Corps at overseas posts

name: Host Nation Support for U.S. Relocation Activities, Defense
description: Receives and manages contributions from host nations (primarily Japan) supporting the relocation and realignment of U.S. military forces, particularly in the Pacific region.
beneficiaries: U.S. military forces undergoing relocation and host nation communities

name: Housing Certificate Fund, Public and Indian Housing, Housing and Urban Development
description: Manages legacy Section 8 housing certificate and voucher program funding for project-based and tenant-based rental assistance contracts. Being consolidated into the Housing Choice Voucher program.
beneficiaries: Low-income tenants receiving Section 8 rental assistance and participating landlords

name: Housing Counseling Assistance, Housing Programs, Housing and Urban Development
description: Provides grants to HUD-approved housing counseling agencies to deliver homeownership counseling, rental counseling, and foreclosure prevention services to consumers.
beneficiaries: Homebuyers, homeowners, renters, and individuals facing housing challenges

name: Housing Opportunities for Persons with AIDS, Community Planning and Development, Housing and Urban Development
description: Provides housing assistance and supportive services for low-income persons living with HIV/AIDS and their families through grants to states, cities, and nonprofit organizations. Known as HOPWA.
beneficiaries: Low-income individuals and families living with HIV/AIDS

name: Housing and Other Credit Guaranty Programs Liquidating Account, United States International Development Finance Corporation
description: Manages the liquidation of legacy housing and credit guarantee commitments made by the former Overseas Private Investment Corporation in developing countries.
beneficiaries: The federal government through recovery of overseas housing and credit guarantee obligations

name: Housing for the Elderly or Handicapped Fund, Housing and Urban Development
description: Manages the legacy loan portfolio for elderly and handicapped housing projects financed under the Section 202 program prior to 1990 reforms. In liquidation mode.
beneficiaries: Elderly and disabled residents of legacy Section 202 housing projects

name: Housing, Program Office Salaries and Expenses, Housing and Urban Development
description: Funds the salaries and administrative expenses of HUD's Office of Housing, which oversees FHA mortgage insurance, multifamily housing programs, and manufactured housing standards.
beneficiaries: HUD Housing Office staff and beneficiaries of HUD housing programs

name: Human Space Flight, National Aeronautics and Space Administration
description: Funds NASA's human spaceflight programs including the International Space Station, commercial crew transportation, and deep space exploration initiatives such as Artemis.
beneficiaries: Astronauts, the aerospace industry, scientific researchers, and the general public

name: IT Modernization and Working Capital, Office of Personnel Management
description: Funds the modernization of OPM's legacy information technology systems and provides working capital for IT operations supporting federal human resources management.
beneficiaries: OPM, federal agencies, and federal employees and retirees who depend on OPM systems

name: IT Modernization, Departmental Management, Labor
description: Funds the modernization of the Department of Labor's information technology infrastructure to improve service delivery, cybersecurity, and operational efficiency across DOL agencies.
beneficiaries: DOL agencies, workers, employers, and beneficiaries of DOL programs

name: Immigration Enforcement Account, Border and Transportation Security, Homeland Security
description: Receives proceeds from fines, penalties, and forfeitures related to immigration enforcement activities and uses them to fund additional enforcement operations.
beneficiaries: Immigration enforcement operations and border security programs

name: Immigration Services, H-1 B Funded, Homeland Security
description: Uses H-1B visa fee revenues to fund USCIS adjudication services and grants for education, training, and scholarship programs to develop the domestic workforce in STEM fields.
beneficiaries: USCIS operations, and U.S. workers and students in STEM education and training programs

name: Independent Counsel, Justice
description: Provided funding for independent counsels appointed to investigate and prosecute high-ranking government officials. The Independent Counsel statute expired in 1999 and this account is largely inactive.
beneficiaries: The justice system and government accountability (account largely inactive)

name: Indian Arts and Crafts Fund, Departmental Management
description: Supports the Indian Arts and Crafts Board in promoting and protecting the economic development of American Indian and Alaska Native arts and crafts. Combats counterfeit Indian-made products.
beneficiaries: American Indian and Alaska Native artists and craftspeople

name: Indian Direct Loan Program Account, Bureau of Indian Affairs, Interior
description: Provides direct loans from the Bureau of Indian Affairs for economic development, housing, and other purposes on Indian reservations where private credit is not available.
beneficiaries: American Indian tribes, tribal organizations, and individual Indians seeking development financing

name: Indian Energy Policy and Programs, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Supports energy development and efficiency on tribal lands through technical assistance, grants, and loan guarantees. Helps tribes develop conventional and renewable energy resources.
beneficiaries: American Indian tribes and Alaska Native communities developing energy resources

name: Indian Guaranteed Loan Program Account, Bureau of Indian Affairs and Bureau of Indian Education, Interior
description: Guarantees loans made by private lenders to Indian individuals, tribes, and organizations for business and economic development on or near reservations. Encourages private lending.
beneficiaries: Indian entrepreneurs, tribal businesses, and economic development on reservations

name: Indian Health Service Gift Fund, Health and Human Services
description: Receives gifts and donations for the benefit of the Indian Health Service and the American Indian and Alaska Native populations it serves. Supplements appropriated health care funding.
beneficiaries: American Indian and Alaska Native communities served by IHS health facilities

name: Indian Housing Loan Guarantee Fund Program Account, Public and Indian Housing, Housing and Urban Development
description: Guarantees private sector mortgage loans to American Indian and Alaska Native families and tribes for home purchase, construction, and rehabilitation in Indian areas. Known as Section 184.
beneficiaries: American Indian and Alaska Native families seeking homeownership on or near tribal lands

name: Indian Land Consolidation, Bureau of Indian Affairs, Interior
description: Funds the purchase of fractionated land interests from willing sellers to consolidate Indian land ownership and reduce the administrative burden of managing highly fractionated allotments.
beneficiaries: American Indian landowners, tribes, and the federal trust land management system

name: Indian Land and Water Claim Settlements and Miscellaneous Payments to Indians, Bureau of Indian Affairs and Bureau of Indian Education, Interior
description: Provides funding for congressionally authorized settlements of Indian land and water claims and miscellaneous payments to tribes. Each settlement addresses specific tribal claims against the federal government.
beneficiaries: American Indian tribes with congressionally settled land and water claims

name: Infectious Diseases Rapid Response Reserve Fund, Centers for Disease Control, Health and Human Services
description: Provides a rapid-response reserve fund for CDC to respond to infectious disease outbreaks before supplemental appropriations can be enacted. Allows swift deployment of resources for emerging threats.
beneficiaries: The general public and global health security during infectious disease emergencies

name: Informant Payments, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds payments to individuals who provide information to the IRS about tax law violations, including the IRS Whistleblower Program for reporting large-scale tax evasion.
beneficiaries: Tax whistleblowers and the federal government through improved tax compliance and revenue collection

name: Information Technology Fund, Management and Administration, Housing and Urban Development
description: Provides centralized IT investment funding for HUD to modernize and maintain information systems supporting housing programs, financial management, and department operations.
beneficiaries: HUD programs, staff, and the public accessing HUD services and information

name: Information Technology Oversight and Reform, Funds Appropriated to the President, Executive Office of the President
description: Funds the U.S. Digital Service and government-wide IT oversight activities to improve the performance and security of federal technology projects. Established after HealthCare.gov challenges.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies modernizing IT systems and the public using government digital services

name: Information Technology System Modernization and Working Capital Fund, Small Business Administration
description: Provides funding for SBA to modernize legacy IT systems that support small business lending, disaster assistance, and entrepreneurial development programs.
beneficiaries: SBA operations, small business loan applicants, and disaster assistance recipients

name: Injury Prevention and Control, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Health and Human Services
description: Funds CDC programs to prevent injuries and violence, including research and programs on traumatic brain injury, opioid overdose prevention, adverse childhood experiences, and firearm injury prevention.
beneficiaries: The general public, particularly populations at risk of injury and violence

name: Inland Oil Spill Programs, Environmental Protection Agency
description: Funds EPA's preparedness, prevention, and response activities for oil spills in inland waters. Supports the Oil Pollution Act implementation and coordination with state and local responders.
beneficiaries: Communities and ecosystems near inland waterways, and environmental emergency responders

name: Inland Waterways Trust Fund - Treasury Managed, Corps of Engineers - Civil
description: Manages the Inland Waterways Trust Fund, financed by a fuel tax on commercial barge traffic, to share the cost of construction and major rehabilitation of inland waterway navigation infrastructure.
beneficiaries: Commercial barge operators, shippers using inland waterways, and the freight transportation system

name: Inspection and Weighing Services, Grain Inspection, Packers and Stockyards Administration, Agriculture
description: Provides official inspection and weighing services for grain and other commodities to facilitate domestic and international trade. Ensures accuracy and integrity of grain grading.
beneficiaries: Grain producers, exporters, buyers, and the agricultural trading system

name: Inspector General Assets Forfeiture, Justice
description: Receives and manages proceeds from assets forfeited in connection with investigations conducted by the DOJ Office of Inspector General. Funds support OIG investigative operations.
beneficiaries: DOJ Inspector General operations and government accountability

name: Inspector General, Export-Import Bank of the United States
description: Funds the independent Inspector General office that conducts audits, investigations, and inspections of Export-Import Bank programs to detect waste, fraud, and abuse.
beneficiaries: Taxpayers and the integrity of Export-Import Bank programs

name: Inspector General, United States International Development Finance Corporation
description: Funds the Inspector General office providing independent oversight of the Development Finance Corporation (successor to OPIC) through audits, evaluations, and investigations.
beneficiaries: Taxpayers and the integrity of U.S. international development finance activities

name: Inspectors General Council Fund, Council of the Inspectors General on Integrity and Efficiency
description: Funds the operations of the Council of Inspectors General on Integrity and Efficiency (CIGIE), which coordinates and enhances the work of federal inspectors general across the government.
beneficiaries: Federal inspectors general, government accountability, and taxpayers

name: Intellectual Property Enforcement Coordinator, Executive Office of the President
description: Coordinates the development and implementation of the Joint Strategic Plan for intellectual property enforcement across federal agencies. Combats counterfeiting and piracy domestically and internationally.
beneficiaries: U.S. intellectual property holders, innovation industries, and consumers

name: Intelligence and Vetting, Transportation Security Administration, Homeland Security
description: Funds TSA's intelligence analysis, threat assessment, and security vetting programs including PreCheck and other trusted traveler screening. Identifies threats to transportation security.
beneficiaries: Air travelers, transportation operators, and national security

name: Inter-American Foundation
description: Provides grants to grassroots and community-based organizations in Latin America and the Caribbean for economic development, democratic participation, and social inclusion projects.
beneficiaries: Grassroots organizations and communities in Latin America and the Caribbean

name: Interagency America the Beautiful Pass Revenues, Corps of Engineers-Civil
description: Manages the Corps of Engineers' share of revenues from America the Beautiful interagency annual passes sold for access to federal recreation areas. Funds recreation site improvements.
beneficiaries: Visitors to Corps of Engineers recreation areas and facilities

name: Interest on Uninvested Funds (Indefinite), Bureau of the Fiscal Service, Treasury
description: Pays interest on federal funds held by financial institutions that have not yet been invested or disbursed. Covers the government's obligations for use of uninvested balances.
beneficiaries: The federal government's financial management and fiscal agents holding federal funds

name: Intermediary Relending Program Fund Account, Rural Business-Cooperative Service, Agriculture
description: Provides low-interest direct loans to intermediary lenders (nonprofits, tribes, cooperatives) who re-lend to small businesses and community development projects in rural areas.
beneficiaries: Small rural businesses, tribal enterprises, and rural community development organizations

name: Internal Revenue Service, Miscellaneous Retained Fees, Treasury
description: Retains fees collected by the IRS for user fees, photocopying charges, and other miscellaneous services. Offsets IRS operational costs.
beneficiaries: IRS operations and taxpayers through offset of appropriation needs

name: International Affairs Technical Assistance, Office of Technical Assistance, Treasury
description: Provides expert technical assistance to developing and transitioning countries in areas of public financial management, revenue policy, banking regulation, and government debt management.
beneficiaries: Finance ministries and central banks in developing countries

name: International Broadcasting Operations, United States Agency for Global Media
description: Funds the operations of U.S. international broadcasting entities including Voice of America, Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty, and Radio Free Asia. Promotes press freedom and democratic values.
beneficiaries: International audiences, particularly in countries with restricted press freedom

name: International Center, Washington, D. C., State
description: Manages the International Center complex in Washington, D.C., which houses international organizations, diplomatic offices, and related facilities on a campus near the National Cathedral.
beneficiaries: International organizations and diplomatic entities located at the International Center

name: International Disaster Assistance, Funds Appropriated to the President, US Agency for International Development
description: Provides emergency humanitarian assistance in response to natural disasters and complex emergencies worldwide, including food, shelter, medical care, and logistical support.
beneficiaries: Populations affected by international disasters and humanitarian crises

name: International Fisheries Commissions, State
description: Funds U.S. contributions to and participation in international fisheries commissions that manage shared fish stocks and marine resources across national boundaries.
beneficiaries: U.S. commercial and recreational fishing industries, and international marine conservation

name: International Litigation Fund, State
