description: Provides funding for the State Department to pay expenses associated with international litigation and arbitration in which the United States is a party before international tribunals.
beneficiaries: U.S. legal interests in international disputes and tribunals

name: Iraq Relief and Reconstruction Fund, Army
description: Provided funding for relief and reconstruction activities in Iraq following the 2003 invasion, including infrastructure, governance, security, and economic development projects. Now largely closed.
beneficiaries: The Iraqi population and reconstruction efforts (account largely completed)

name: Iraq and Afghanistan Service Grants Program, Office of Federal Student AID, Education
description: Provides Pell Grant-equivalent awards to students whose parent or guardian died as a result of military service in Iraq or Afghanistan after September 11, 2001.
beneficiaries: Children of military service members killed in Iraq or Afghanistan

name: James Madison Memorial Fellowship Foundation
description: Awards fellowships to secondary school teachers pursuing graduate study of the U.S. Constitution and its history. Strengthens the teaching of constitutional principles in American schools.
beneficiaries: Secondary school teachers of American history, government, and social studies

name: Japan-United States Friendship Commission
description: Promotes mutual understanding and cooperation between Japan and the United States through support for Japanese studies, public affairs, and cultural exchange programs.
beneficiaries: U.S. and Japanese scholars, artists, students, and cultural exchange participants

name: Japan-United States Friendship Trust Fund, Japan-United States Friendship Commission
description: Manages the trust fund established from repayment of U.S. postwar assistance to Japan, whose income finances the Friendship Commission's cultural and educational exchange programs.
beneficiaries: The Japan-United States Friendship Commission and its grant recipients

name: John E. Fogarty International Center, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Supports and facilitates global health research conducted by U.S. and international investigators, trains the next generation of global health scientists, and builds research capacity in developing countries.
beneficiaries: Global health researchers, scientists in developing countries, and populations affected by global diseases

name: Joint Department of Defense - Department of Veterans Affairs Medical Facility Demonstration Fund, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds joint medical facilities shared between DOD and VA to improve health care delivery and reduce costs for both active-duty military personnel and veterans. Promotes interagency collaboration.
beneficiaries: Active-duty service members and veterans receiving care at joint DOD-VA medical facilities

name: Joint Improvised Explosive Device Defeat Fund, Army
description: Funded urgent efforts to counter improvised explosive devices (IEDs) in Iraq and Afghanistan through technology development, training, and force protection measures. Now largely completed.
beneficiaries: U.S. military personnel in combat zones threatened by IEDs (account largely completed)

name: Joint Improvised-Threat Defeat Fund, Defense
description: Successor to the JIED Defeat Fund, providing resources to counter improvised threats including explosive devices and other asymmetric weapons used against U.S. forces worldwide.
beneficiaries: U.S. military forces facing improvised threats in operational environments

name: Judgements, Court of Claims (Indefinite), Bureau of the Fiscal Service, Treasury
description: Provides indefinite appropriations to pay judgments and settlements awarded by the U.S. Court of Federal Claims against the federal government. Mandatory spending account.
beneficiaries: Plaintiffs who win judgments against the United States in the Court of Federal Claims

name: Judgements, United States Courts (Indefinite), Bureau of the Fiscal Service, Treasury
description: Provides indefinite appropriations to pay judgments, awards, and compromise settlements rendered against the United States by federal district courts and courts of appeals.
beneficiaries: Plaintiffs who win judgments against the United States in federal courts

name: Justice Gift Fund, Justice
description: Receives and administers gifts, bequests, and donations made to the Department of Justice for purposes consistent with DOJ's law enforcement and justice mission.
beneficiaries: DOJ programs as designated by donors and the justice system

name: Justice Information Sharing Technology, General Administration, Justice
description: Funds information technology systems that enable sharing of justice and public safety information among federal, state, local, and tribal agencies. Supports interoperability and data exchange.
beneficiaries: Law enforcement and justice agencies at all levels of government

name: Justice Prisoner and Alien Transportation System Fund, United States Marshals Service, Justice
description: Operates the Justice Prisoner and Alien Transportation System (JPATS), which transports federal prisoners and immigration detainees between judicial districts, correctional institutions, and deportation flights.
beneficiaries: The federal justice system, Bureau of Prisons, ICE, and the courts

name: Lahontan Valley and Pyramid Lake Fish and Wildlife Fund, United States Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Funds fish and wildlife conservation, habitat restoration, and water management activities in the Lahontan Valley and Pyramid Lake areas of Nevada under the Truckee-Carson-Pyramid Lake Water Rights Settlement Act.
beneficiaries: Fish and wildlife populations, the Pyramid Lake Paiute Tribe, and the Nevada environment

name: Land Acquisition, Bureau of Land Management
description: Funds the acquisition of lands, waters, and interests therein for the Bureau of Land Management from the Land and Water Conservation Fund to protect significant natural, cultural, and recreational resources.
beneficiaries: The public and conservation of nationally significant landscapes on BLM lands

name: Land Acquisition, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Funds the acquisition of private lands within or adjacent to National Forests for conservation and public recreation purposes. Authorized under the Land and Water Conservation Fund Act.
beneficiaries: General public, conservation efforts, rural communities near National Forests

name: Land Between the Lakes Management Fund, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Supports recreation, environmental education, and natural resource management at the Land Between the Lakes National Recreation Area in Kentucky and Tennessee. Replaced TVA management under the LBL Protection Act of 1998.
beneficiaries: Visitors and communities near the Land Between the Lakes recreation area

name: Land Between the Lakes Trust Fund, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Receives receipts from commercial activities at Land Between the Lakes and makes them available for resource management. Complements the management fund for operational flexibility.
beneficiaries: Local communities, recreationists, and environmental education participants in the LBL region

name: Land and Resources Management Trust Fund, Bureau of Land Management
description: Holds receipts from land use authorizations on BLM-managed lands, funding resource management activities. Established under the Federal Land Policy and Management Act.
beneficiaries: Public land users, ranchers, miners, recreationists, and local communities near BLM lands

name: Landowner Incentive Program, U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Provided grants to states, territories, and tribes to protect and restore habitats on private lands for at-risk species. Funded voluntary conservation partnerships with private landowners.
beneficiaries: Private landowners, at-risk and endangered species, state wildlife agencies

name: Leaking Underground Storage Tank Trust Fund, Environmental Protection Agency
description: Finances cleanup of petroleum releases from underground storage tanks and related prevention activities. Funded by a per-gallon tax on motor fuels under SARA amendments.
beneficiaries: Communities affected by groundwater contamination from leaking underground storage tanks

name: Lease of Department of Defense Real Property, Defense
description: Receives proceeds from the lease of underutilized DoD real property to non-federal entities, with receipts applied to maintenance and repair. Supports efficient use of military installations.
beneficiaries: DoD installations, military readiness, and local communities benefiting from leased properties

name: Licensee Programs, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Manages special use permits and licenses for activities on National Forest System lands such as ski areas, resorts, and utility corridors. Fees collected support permit administration.
beneficiaries: Commercial permittees operating on National Forest lands, recreationists, and utility companies

name: Limitation on the Office of the Inspector General, Railroad Retirement Board
description: Provides for the Inspector General's office within the Railroad Retirement Board, conducting audits and investigations. Funded from the railroad retirement trust funds subject to appropriation limitations.
beneficiaries: Railroad workers and taxpayers through fraud prevention and program integrity

name: Limited Access System Administration Fund, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, Commerce
description: Manages cost-recovery fees from participants in limited-access privilege programs for federal fisheries. Funds administration of individual fishing quota and catch share programs under the Magnuson-Stevens Act.
beneficiaries: Commercial fisheries participants in catch-share programs, sustainable fisheries management

name: Lincoln County Land Act, Bureau of Land Management
description: Authorized the disposal of certain public lands in Lincoln County, Nevada, with proceeds funding conservation, parks, and water infrastructure. Enacted to support community growth while protecting natural resources.
beneficiaries: Lincoln County, Nevada residents, conservation efforts, and local water systems

name: Low Income Home Energy Assistance, Administration for Children and Families, Health and Human Services
description: Block grants to states, tribes, and territories to help low-income households meet home energy costs, including heating and cooling. One of the primary federal safety-net programs for energy assistance.
beneficiaries: Low-income households, elderly, disabled, and families with young children facing high energy costs

name: Lower Brule Sioux Tribe Terrestrial Wildlife Habitat Restoration Trust Fund, Bureau of the Fiscal Service, Treasury
description: Compensates the Lower Brule Sioux Tribe for wildlife habitat lost due to dam construction on the Missouri River. Interest earnings fund habitat restoration and related purposes.
beneficiaries: Lower Brule Sioux Tribe members and wildlife habitat along the Missouri River

name: Lower Colorado River Basin Development Fund, Bureau of Reclamation
description: Finances the construction, operation, and maintenance of water resource projects in the Lower Colorado River Basin, including the Central Arizona Project. Authorized by the Colorado River Basin Project Act.
beneficiaries: Water users, irrigators, municipalities, and tribes in Arizona and the Lower Colorado Basin

name: MENA Loan Guarantee Program Account, Funds Appropriated to the President, US Agency for International Development
description: Provides sovereign loan guarantees to countries in the Middle East and North Africa to support economic stabilization and democratic transitions. Reduces borrowing costs for qualifying MENA nations.
beneficiaries: Citizens of MENA countries undergoing democratic transitions, particularly Tunisia and Jordan

name: MNP Rental Fee Account, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Collects and distributes rental fees from communications site uses and other special uses on National Forest System lands under the Middle Class Tax Relief and Job Creation Act. Shares receipts with state and local governments.
beneficiaries: State and local governments, Forest Service administrative operations

name: Maintenance and Operation of Dams and Other Improvements of Navigable Waters, Corps of Engineers - Civil
description: Funds the maintenance and operation of dams, locks, and other navigation structures on the nation's waterways managed by the Army Corps of Engineers. Ensures safe and reliable inland navigation.
beneficiaries: Commercial shippers, inland waterway users, and communities dependent on flood control

name: Management of National Forest Lands for Subsistence Uses, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Supports management of National Forest lands in Alaska to provide subsistence uses by rural residents, as mandated by the Alaska National Interest Lands Conservation Act. Ensures continued traditional food harvesting access.
beneficiaries: Rural Alaska residents who depend on subsistence hunting, fishing, and gathering on National Forest lands

name: Manufactured Housing Fees Trust Fund, Housing Programs, Housing and Urban Development
description: Collects fees from manufactured housing producers to fund HUD's administration of federal manufactured housing construction and safety standards. Authorized by the Manufactured Housing Improvement Act.
beneficiaries: Manufactured housing residents through improved safety standards, and manufacturers through uniform federal regulation

name: Marine Mammal Commission
description: An independent federal agency that provides science-based oversight and recommendations on domestic and international policies for the protection and conservation of marine mammals. Established by the Marine Mammal Protection Act.
beneficiaries: Marine mammal populations, marine ecosystems, and the scientific and conservation communities

name: Maritime Guaranteed Loan, (Title XI) Program Account, Maritime Administration, Transportation
description: Provides federal guarantees of private-sector debt financing for the construction or reconstruction of vessels in U.S. shipyards and modernization of shipyard facilities. Promotes the U.S. merchant marine and domestic shipbuilding.
beneficiaries: U.S. shipyards, ship owners, and the domestic maritime industry

name: Maternal and Child Health, Health Resources and Services Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Title V block grants to states for maternal and child health services, including prenatal care, well-child visits, and services for children with special health care needs. One of the oldest federal public health programs.
beneficiaries: Pregnant women, mothers, infants, children, and children with special health care needs, especially in underserved areas

name: Maternal, Infant, and Early Childhood Home Visiting Programs, Health Resources and Services Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Funds evidence-based home visiting programs for at-risk pregnant women and families with young children, providing parenting support, health screening, and referrals. Authorized by the Affordable Care Act.
beneficiaries: At-risk pregnant women, new parents, infants, and young children in disadvantaged communities

name: Medicare Health Information Technology Incentive Payments - Recovery Act, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, Health and Human Services
description: Provided incentive payments to eligible hospitals and professionals under Medicare for meaningful use of certified electronic health record technology. Part of the HITECH Act enacted under the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act.
beneficiaries: Medicare providers adopting electronic health records, and Medicare beneficiaries through improved care coordination

name: Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Contribution, Reserve Personnel, Marine Corps
description: Marine Corps Reserve's accrual-based contribution to fund future Medicare-eligible retiree health benefits. Part of the DoD-wide accrual funding system established in FY 2003.
beneficiaries: Medicare-eligible Marine Corps Reserve retirees and their dependents

name: Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Contribution, Space Force, Air Force
description: U.S. Space Force's contribution to the DoD Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund for future retiree health liabilities. Established when the Space Force was created as a new military branch.
beneficiaries: Medicare-eligible Space Force retirees and their dependents

name: Merit Systems Protection Board
description: An independent quasi-judicial agency that adjudicates appeals of federal employee adverse actions and conducts studies of the federal merit systems. Established by the Civil Service Reform Act.
beneficiaries: Federal employees seeking protection from prohibited personnel practices and due process in adverse actions

name: Middle Mile Deployment, National Telecommunications and Information Administration, Commerce
description: Funds construction and improvement of middle mile broadband infrastructure connecting local networks to the internet backbone. Authorized by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.
beneficiaries: Underserved and unserved communities, internet service providers, and rural areas lacking broadband backbone connectivity

name: Migratory Bird Conservation Account, United States Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Receives proceeds from the sale of federal duck stamps and import duties on arms and ammunition to fund acquisition of migratory bird habitat. Authorized by the Migratory Bird Conservation Act.
beneficiaries: Migratory bird populations, waterfowl hunters, birdwatchers, and the National Wildlife Refuge System

name: Military Construction, Air Force Reserve
description: Funds planning, design, and construction of facilities for Air Force Reserve units, including training centers, maintenance hangars, and operational support buildings. Part of the annual military construction appropriation.
beneficiaries: Air Force Reserve units and personnel requiring modern training and operational facilities

name: Military Construction, Navy Reserve
description: Funds the design and construction of facilities for Navy Reserve activities, including reserve centers, training facilities, and operational spaces. Supports reserve readiness and force structure.
beneficiaries: Navy Reserve units and personnel requiring updated facilities for training and operations

name: Military Personnel, Space Force, Air Force
description: Funds pay, allowances, bonuses, and other compensation for active-duty Space Force military personnel. Established with the creation of the United States Space Force.
beneficiaries: Active-duty Space Force service members and their families

name: Minority Business Development, Minority Business Development Agency, Commerce
description: Supports the growth and competitiveness of minority-owned businesses through business consulting, access to capital, and market development. MBDA was made permanent by the MBDA Act of 2021.
beneficiaries: Minority-owned businesses and entrepreneurs seeking capital, contracts, and technical assistance

name: Miscellaneous Contributed Funds, Agricultural Research Service, Agriculture
description: Receives and expends contributions from domestic and foreign sources to support cooperative agricultural research conducted by ARS. Enables public-private research partnerships.
beneficiaries: Agricultural researchers, farmers, and the food system through collaborative research

name: Miscellaneous Contributed Funds, Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service, Agriculture
description: Receives contributed funds from states, foreign governments, and private entities to support cooperative programs protecting animal and plant health. Enables cost-sharing for pest and disease control.
beneficiaries: Agricultural producers, trading partners, and the public through enhanced animal and plant health protection

name: Miscellaneous Contributed Funds, National Agricultural Statistics Service, Agriculture
description: Accepts contributions from cooperating entities to fund statistical surveys and data collection on agricultural production, economics, and demographics. Supplements appropriated funding for NASS surveys.
beneficiaries: Agricultural stakeholders, policymakers, and researchers relying on accurate agricultural statistics

name: Miscellaneous Highway Project, Federal Highway Administration, Transportation
description: Covers miscellaneous highway-related projects and activities that do not fit under other specific FHWA program accounts. Typically funded through the Highway Trust Fund.
beneficiaries: State and local transportation agencies and the traveling public

name: Mississippi River and Tributaries, Corps of Engineers, Civil
description: Funds flood control, navigation, and environmental restoration along the Mississippi River and its tributaries following the Great Mississippi Flood of 1927. One of the largest civil works programs of the Corps.
beneficiaries: Communities along the Mississippi River and tributaries, commercial navigation interests, and flood-prone populations

name: Morris K. Udall and Stewart L. Udall Foundation
description: An independent federal foundation providing scholarships, fellowships, and funding for programs in environmental policy, conflict resolution, and Native American education. Honors the Udall brothers' public service legacy.
beneficiaries: Students in environmental and Native American health fields, communities engaged in environmental conflict resolution

name: Morris K. Udall and Stewart L. Udall Trust Fund, Morris K. Udall and Stewart L. Udall Foundation
description: The trust fund providing the financial basis for the Udall Foundation's scholarship and fellowship programs, invested in Treasury securities. Earnings finance the Foundation's activities.
beneficiaries: Scholarship and fellowship recipients in environmental studies and Native American health care

name: Motor Carrier Safety Operations and Programs, Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration, Transportation
description: Funds FMCSA's federal motor carrier safety activities including rulemaking, enforcement, research, technology, and outreach to reduce crashes involving large trucks and buses. The agency's primary operating account.
beneficiaries: The traveling public, commercial motor vehicle operators, and motor carriers through improved highway safety

name: Multi-Family Housing Revitalization Program Account, Rural Housing Service, Agriculture
description: Provides competitive grants and loans to preserve and revitalize existing USDA-financed rural multifamily rental housing for low-income tenants. Addresses deteriorating rural housing stock.
beneficiaries: Low-income rural tenants in aging USDA-financed multifamily housing developments

name: Multinational Species Conservation Fund, United States Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Provides grants for conservation of highly endangered species across international borders, including African and Asian elephants, rhinoceroses, tigers, great apes, and marine turtles. Leverages matching funds from partners.
beneficiaries: Endangered species worldwide, conservation organizations, and range-country communities

name: Mutual and Self-Help Housing Grants, Rural Housing Service, Agriculture
description: Provides grants to nonprofit organizations and public bodies to assist groups of low-income families in building their own homes through mutual self-help (sweat equity) construction methods. Reduces housing costs significantly.
beneficiaries: Low-income rural families willing to provide labor in constructing their own homes

name: Mutually Beneficial Activities, Defense
description: Funds activities and programs undertaken cooperatively with allies and partner nations that benefit both parties, including combined exercises and capacity-building. Supports defense cooperation objectives.
beneficiaries: U.S. military forces, allied and partner nation militaries, and international security partnerships

name: NOAA Accrual Contribution to the Uniformed Services Retiree Health, Commerce
description: NOAA Commissioned Corps' contribution to the DoD Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund for the uniformed services of NOAA. Prefunds future retiree health benefits on an accrual basis.
beneficiaries: Medicare-eligible NOAA Commissioned Corps retirees and their dependents

name: NTIS Revolving Fund, National Technical Information Service, Commerce
description: A self-supporting revolving fund through which NTIS collects, indexes, and disseminates scientific, technical, and engineering information from federal agencies to the public. Operates on a cost-recovery basis.
beneficiaries: Researchers, businesses, and the public seeking access to government-funded scientific and technical reports

name: Natick Land Conveyance, Army
description: Authorizes and funds the conveyance of excess Army land at the Natick Soldier Systems Center in Massachusetts for community redevelopment. Part of military installation realignment activities.
beneficiaries: The Town of Natick, Massachusetts and surrounding communities benefiting from redeveloped former military land

name: National Archives Gift Fund, National Archives and Records Administration
description: Receives and expends monetary gifts and bequests donated to the National Archives for specific purposes such as exhibits, educational programs, and preservation of historical records.
beneficiaries: The public through enhanced access to and preservation of national historical records and exhibits

name: National Archives Trust Fund, National Archives and Records Administration
description: Receives revenues from the sale of reproductions of historical documents, publications, and museum shop merchandise at NARA facilities. A revolving trust fund supporting NARA's public programs.
beneficiaries: NARA operations and the public through funded preservation and educational activities

name: National Capital Arts and Cultural Affairs, Commission of Fine Arts
description: Provides grants to nonprofit performing arts organizations, museums, and cultural institutions in the Washington, D.C. metropolitan area. Supports the national capital's role as a center of arts and culture.
beneficiaries: Arts and cultural organizations in Washington D.C. and the public attending performances and exhibitions

name: National Capital Planning Commission
description: The central planning agency for the federal government in the National Capital Region, reviewing plans and proposals affecting federal land and buildings in the Washington D.C. area. Prepares the Federal Capital Improvements Program.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies, D.C. residents, and visitors through coherent planning of the national capital

name: National Cemetery Administration Facilities Operation Fund, Veterans Affairs
description: Receives reimbursements and other collections to support the operation and maintenance of national cemeteries administered by VA. Complements direct appropriations for cemetery upkeep.
beneficiaries: Veterans and their families through properly maintained national cemeteries

name: National Cemetery Administration, Veterans Health Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds the operation and maintenance of national cemeteries that provide burial benefits for eligible veterans and their dependents. Manages over 155 national cemeteries.
beneficiaries: Eligible veterans, their spouses, and dependents seeking burial in a national cemetery

name: National Center for Advancing Translational Sciences, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Catalyzes the generation of innovative methods and technologies to accelerate the translation of scientific discoveries into new diagnostics and therapeutics. Focuses on improving the translational research pipeline.
beneficiaries: Biomedical researchers, clinical trial networks, rare disease patients, and the public through faster drug development

name: National Center for Complementary and Integrative Health, National Institute of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Conducts and supports research on complementary and integrative health approaches such as natural products, mind-body practices, and their integration with conventional medicine. Formerly NCCAM.
beneficiaries: Patients and health providers seeking evidence-based information on complementary health approaches

name: National Culvert Removal, Replacement, and Restoration Grant Program, Office of the Secretary, Transportation
description: Competitive grants for replacing, removing, or repairing culverts or weirs that would improve passage for anadromous fish, flood control, and community safety. Authorized by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.
beneficiaries: Anadromous fish populations, communities at flood risk, and state/local transportation agencies

name: National Defense Sealift Fund, Navy
description: Funds the construction, conversion, and maintenance of strategic sealift vessels needed to deploy and sustain military forces overseas. Consolidated sealift programs under a single account.
beneficiaries: U.S. military forces requiring strategic sealift capacity for force projection and sustainment

name: National Energy Technology Laboratory Research and Development, Energy
description: Funds research and development at DOE's National Energy Technology Laboratory on fossil energy, carbon management, and advanced energy systems. Operates as one of DOE's government-owned, government-operated labs.
beneficiaries: Energy industry, researchers, and the public through cleaner fossil energy technologies and carbon capture advancement

name: National Eye Institute, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Conducts and supports research on the causes, prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of diseases and disorders of the eye, visual system, and brain areas responsible for processing visual information.
beneficiaries: Patients with eye diseases such as glaucoma, macular degeneration, diabetic retinopathy, and vision researchers

name: National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Funds research on heart, blood vessel, lung, and blood diseases, as well as sleep disorders and transfusion medicine. Administers major clinical trials and population studies.
beneficiaries: Patients with cardiovascular, pulmonary, and hematologic diseases, and the broader biomedical research community

name: National Historical Publications and Records Commission, National Archives and Records Administration
description: Provides grants to state and local governments, nonprofits, and academic institutions for preserving, publishing, and providing access to America's documentary heritage. The grant-making arm of NARA.
beneficiaries: Archives, historical societies, libraries, universities, and researchers preserving and accessing historical records

name: National Human Genome Research Institute, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Led the Human Genome Project and now funds research on the structure, function, and role of genomics in health and disease, including ethical, legal, and social implications. Advances genomic medicine.
beneficiaries: Patients with genetic disorders, genomics researchers, and the public through precision medicine advances

name: National Indian Gaming Commission, Gaming Activity Fees
description: Funds the regulatory operations of the NIGC through fees assessed on Indian gaming operations. The Commission oversees and regulates Indian gaming under the Indian Gaming Regulatory Act.
beneficiaries: Indian tribes operating gaming facilities, and the public through regulation ensuring fair and honest gaming

name: National Institute For Occupational Safety and Health, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Health and Human Services
description: Conducts research and makes recommendations to prevent workplace injuries, illnesses, and deaths. Investigates hazardous working conditions and develops guidance on occupational safety.
beneficiaries: Workers in all industries, employers, and occupational health professionals

name: National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Conducts and supports research on infectious, immunologic, and allergic diseases including HIV/AIDS, emerging infectious diseases, biodefense, and pandemic preparedness. One of the largest NIH institutes.
beneficiaries: Patients with infectious and immune-mediated diseases, global health security, and vaccine/therapeutic development

name: National Institute of Arthritis and Musculoskeletal and Skin Diseases, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Supports research into the causes, treatment, and prevention of arthritis, musculoskeletal, and skin diseases. Funds basic, translational, and clinical research across these disciplines.
beneficiaries: Patients with arthritis, osteoporosis, skin diseases, and musculoskeletal disorders, and related researchers

name: National Institute of Biomedical Imaging and Bioengineering, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Funds research and development of new biomedical imaging and bioengineering technologies, including point-of-care diagnostics, medical devices, and computational modeling. Bridges engineering and medicine.
beneficiaries: Biomedical engineers, clinicians, and patients benefiting from advanced imaging and diagnostic technologies

name: National Institute of Dental and Craniofacial Research, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Funds research on oral, dental, and craniofacial diseases and conditions, including dental caries, periodontal disease, and craniofacial birth defects. Promotes oral health through science.
beneficiaries: Dental patients, oral health professionals, and researchers in dental and craniofacial sciences

name: National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Conducts and supports research on how environmental exposures affect human health, including toxicology, epidemiology, and climate-related health effects. Also houses the National Toxicology Program.
beneficiaries: Communities exposed to environmental hazards, environmental health researchers, and public health policymakers

name: National Institute of General Medical Sciences, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Supports basic biomedical research not targeted to specific diseases, including cell biology, genetics, pharmacology, and computational biology. Also funds research training and institutional development grants.
beneficiaries: Basic science researchers, trainees, and the broader biomedical enterprise through foundational discoveries

name: National Institute on Aging, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Leads the federal effort on research into aging processes, age-related diseases including Alzheimer's disease, and the special needs of older populations. Funds both biological and behavioral aging research.
beneficiaries: Older adults, Alzheimer's disease patients and caregivers, and aging researchers

name: National Institute on Alcohol Abuse and Alcoholism, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Conducts and supports research on the causes, consequences, prevention, and treatment of alcohol use disorder and alcohol-related problems. Translates research into clinical practice and public health policy.
beneficiaries: Individuals with alcohol use disorders, their families, clinicians, and alcohol researchers

name: National Institute on Deafness and Other Communication Disorders, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Supports research on hearing, balance, taste, smell, voice, speech, and language disorders. Funds studies on both normal and disordered communication processes.
beneficiaries: Individuals with hearing loss, speech disorders, and other communication disabilities, and related researchers

name: National Institute on Minority Health and Health Disparities, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Leads scientific research to improve minority health and reduce health disparities among racial and ethnic minorities and underserved populations. Elevated from a center to an institute in 2010.
beneficiaries: Racial and ethnic minority communities, underserved populations, and health disparities researchers

name: National Institutes of Health Conditional Gift Fund, Health and Human Services
description: Receives and expends monetary gifts donated to NIH with conditions specified by the donor, such as supporting a particular disease or research area. Supplements appropriated research funding.
beneficiaries: NIH researchers and patients in disease areas targeted by conditional donors

name: National Institutes of Health Unconditional Gift Fund, Health and Human Services
description: Receives monetary gifts donated to NIH without specific conditions, available for any research purpose the NIH Director determines. Provides flexible supplementary research funding.
beneficiaries: NIH research programs broadly and the public through additional biomedical research capacity

name: National Library of Medicine, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: The world's largest biomedical library, maintaining databases including PubMed/MEDLINE and providing access to biomedical literature and health information. Supports biomedical informatics research.
beneficiaries: Health professionals, researchers, students, and the public accessing biomedical literature and health information

name: National Mediation Board
description: An independent federal agency that facilitates labor-management relations in the railroad and airline industries through mediation, voluntary arbitration, and representation election procedures. Operates under the Railway Labor Act.
beneficiaries: Railroad and airline employees, unions, and carriers in labor disputes

name: National Railroad Retirement Investment Trust, Railroad Retirement Board
description: An independent trust that manages and invests railroad retirement system assets in a diversified portfolio of stocks, bonds, and other securities. Created to improve investment returns beyond Treasury securities.
beneficiaries: Railroad retirees and their families through improved funding of railroad retirement benefits

name: National Recreation and Preservation, National Park Service, Interior
description: Funds NPS programs supporting recreation and historic preservation beyond park boundaries, including heritage areas, wild and scenic rivers, national trails, and technical assistance to communities.
beneficiaries: Communities with heritage areas, trail users, river recreationists, and historic preservation efforts nationwide

name: National Science Center, Army
description: Supported the National Science Center for Communications and Electronics at Fort Gordon, Georgia, providing STEM education to students. Operations have since been consolidated with other Army education programs.
beneficiaries: Students and educators in STEM fields, particularly in communications and electronics

name: National Space Council
description: Advises and assists the President on national space policy and strategy, coordinating civil, commercial, and national security space activities across the federal government. Re-established in 2017 after a period of dormancy.
beneficiaries: The space policy community, federal space agencies, the commercial space industry, and national security

name: National Surface Transportation Finance Bureau, Highway Trust Fund Account, Transportation
description: Supports innovative financing mechanisms for surface transportation projects, including TIFIA credit assistance, from the Highway Trust Fund. Merged into the Build America Bureau.
beneficiaries: State and local transportation agencies and private entities financing large infrastructure projects

name: National Surface Transportation and Innovative Finance Bureau, Office of the Secretary, Transportation
description: Administers DOT credit programs including TIFIA and RRIF to support surface transportation infrastructure through loans, loan guarantees, and lines of credit. Now known as the Build America Bureau.
beneficiaries: State and local governments, transit agencies, and private developers of major transportation infrastructure

name: National Technical Institute for the Deaf, Special Institutions for Persons with Disabilities, Department of Education
description: Provides postsecondary technical and professional education for deaf and hard-of-hearing students at Rochester Institute of Technology. One of the federally chartered institutions for persons with disabilities.
beneficiaries: Deaf and hard-of-hearing students seeking technical and professional degrees

name: National Wildlife Refuge Fund, United States Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Makes payments to counties in lieu of taxes for lands within the National Wildlife Refuge System, based on revenues generated or fair market value of the land. Known as refuge revenue sharing.
beneficiaries: Counties and local governments hosting National Wildlife Refuges that cannot collect property taxes on federal land

name: Native American Institutions Endowment Fund, Agriculture
description: An endowment fund for Native American-serving higher education institutions, with interest earnings supporting capacity building in teaching, research, and extension at 1994 Land-Grant Tribal Colleges.
beneficiaries: Tribal colleges and universities (1994 Land-Grant institutions) and their Native American students

name: Native American Veteran Housing Loan Program Account, Veterans Benefits Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Provides direct home loans to Native American veterans living on federal trust lands where conventional and VA-guaranteed mortgages are generally unavailable. Addresses unique challenges of trust land lending.
beneficiaries: Native American veterans seeking homeownership on tribal trust lands

name: Native Hawaiian Housing Block Grant, Public and Indian Housing, Housing and Urban Development
description: Provides block grants to the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands for affordable housing activities for Native Hawaiian families on Hawaiian home lands. Modeled after the Indian Housing Block Grant.
beneficiaries: Native Hawaiian families eligible for housing on Hawaiian home lands

name: Native Hawaiian Housing Loan Guarantee Fund Program Account, Public and Indian Housing, Housing and Urban Development
description: Guarantees private loans for Native Hawaiian families to purchase, construct, or rehabilitate housing on Hawaiian home lands. Section 184A loan guarantees under NAHASDA.
beneficiaries: Native Hawaiian families seeking mortgage financing for homes on Hawaiian home lands

name: Natural Resource Damage Assessment Fund, Natural Resource Damage Assessment and Restoration, Interior
description: Funds Interior's assessment of natural resource damages caused by oil spills and hazardous substance releases, and supports restoration planning. Authorized under CERCLA and the Oil Pollution Act.
beneficiaries: Natural resources injured by contamination, affected communities, and the public through restoration of damaged ecosystems

name: Navajo Utah Settlement Trust Fund, Bureau of Indian Affairs, Interior
description: Holds funds for the benefit of the Navajo Nation for water development and other purposes related to the Navajo Utah Water Rights Settlement. Supports resolution of Navajo water claims in Utah.
beneficiaries: Navajo Nation members in Utah gaining access to reliable water supply through settlement implementation

name: Naval Petroleum Reserve Numbered 2 Lease Revenue Account
description: Receives revenues from oil and gas leases on Naval Petroleum Reserve No. 2 (Buena Vista Hills, California) after transfer from government operation. Receipts deposited to the U.S. Treasury.
beneficiaries: The U.S. Treasury and taxpayers through revenue from petroleum production on former naval reserves

name: Naval Petroleum and Oil Shale Reserves, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Manages the remaining DOE-administered naval petroleum and oil shale reserves, including environmental remediation of formerly produced reserves. Most reserves have been sold or transferred.
beneficiaries: National energy security and local communities through environmental cleanup of former petroleum reserves

name: Neighborhood Access and Environmental Programs, Federal Highway Administration, Transportation
description: Funds grants to improve walkability, safety, and affordable transportation access in disadvantaged communities impacted by highway construction, and to address environmental harm from transportation facilities. Authorized by the IIJA.
beneficiaries: Disadvantaged communities affected by highway infrastructure, pedestrians, cyclists, and environmental justice populations

name: Neotropical Migratory Bird Conservation, U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Provides competitive matching grants for conservation of neotropical migratory birds in the United States, Latin America, and the Caribbean. Funds habitat protection, research, and education.
beneficiaries: Neotropical migratory bird populations, international conservation partners, and bird enthusiasts

name: Network Construction Fund, Telecommunications and Information Administration, Commerce
description: Supports the Public Telecommunications Facilities Program for planning and construction of public broadcasting infrastructure. Funding has largely transitioned to broadband deployment programs.
beneficiaries: Public broadcasting stations, educational institutions, and communities served by public telecommunications

name: No Surprises Act Implementation Fund, Office of the Secretary, Health & Human Services
description: Funds HHS implementation and administration of the No Surprises Act, which protects patients from surprise medical bills for out-of-network emergency services and certain other situations. Includes the independent dispute resolution process.
beneficiaries: Patients receiving emergency or certain out-of-network health care services who are protected from surprise billing

name: Nonrecurring Expenses Fund, Departmental Management, Commerce
description: Allows the Department of Commerce to transfer unobligated balances from expired discretionary accounts to fund IT modernization and facility improvements. A capital investment fund for the department.
beneficiaries: Commerce Department operations through modernized IT systems and improved facilities

name: Nonrecurring Expenses Fund, Management Directorate, Departmental Management, Intelligence, Situational Awareness, and Oversight, Homeland Security
description: Permits DHS to transfer unobligated balances from expired discretionary accounts to fund capital acquisition and IT modernization projects across the department.
beneficiaries: DHS operations and cybersecurity through modernized technology infrastructure

name: Nonrecurring Expenses Fund, Office of the Chief Financial Officer, Agriculture
description: Allows USDA to transfer unobligated balances from expired discretionary accounts to fund IT modernization, cybersecurity, and facility improvements across the department.
beneficiaries: USDA operations, farmers, and the public through modernized agricultural information systems

name: North American Wetlands Conservation Fund, United States Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Provides matching grants to conserve, restore, and enhance wetland ecosystems and associated upland habitats for migratory birds in North America under the North American Wetlands Conservation Act.
beneficiaries: Migratory birds, wetland ecosystems, hunters, birdwatchers, and conservation partners in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico

name: North Atlantic Treaty Organization, Security Investment Program, Defense
description: Funds the U.S. share of NATO's common-funded military construction and infrastructure program for allied defense installations. Supports collective defense through shared infrastructure investments.
beneficiaries: NATO member nations, U.S. military forces in Europe, and transatlantic security

name: North Pacific Fishery Observer Fund, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, Commerce
description: Collects fees from the North Pacific fishing industry to fund fishery observer programs that monitor catch, bycatch, and compliance with fishery management plans. Ensures sustainable fisheries management.
beneficiaries: North Pacific fisheries, commercial fishing industry through sustainable management, and marine ecosystems

name: Northeast Home Heating Oil Reserve, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Maintains a government-owned reserve of heating oil in the northeastern United States to provide a short-term emergency supply during severe winter supply disruptions. Typically holds one to two million barrels.
beneficiaries: Northeastern U.S. households dependent on heating oil during severe winter supply emergencies

name: Northern Border Regional Commission
description: A federal-state partnership providing economic development grants to distressed and economically disadvantaged counties in northern border states (Maine, New Hampshire, Vermont, and New York). Modeled after the Appalachian Regional Commission.
beneficiaries: Economically distressed communities in northern New England and northern New York

name: Nuclear Waste Disposal, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds DOE's activities related to the disposal of high-level radioactive waste and spent nuclear fuel in a permanent geologic repository. Originally designated for the Yucca Mountain project.
beneficiaries: Communities near nuclear waste storage sites, nuclear utilities, and the public through safe permanent waste disposal

name: Nuclear Waste Technical Review Board
description: An independent federal agency that evaluates the technical and scientific validity of DOE's activities related to the management and disposal of high-level radioactive waste and spent nuclear fuel.
beneficiaries: The public and policymakers through independent scientific review of nuclear waste disposal programs

name: OPM Building Delegation Fund, Office of Personnel Management
description: A revolving fund covering the costs of operating and maintaining the OPM headquarters building, funded through charges to OPM accounts occupying the space. Manages facility services.
beneficiaries: OPM employees and operations through maintained headquarters facilities

name: Occupational Safety and Health Review Commission
description: An independent federal agency that adjudicates contested citations and penalties issued by OSHA for workplace safety and health violations. Provides an independent judicial forum for employers and employees.
beneficiaries: Employers contesting OSHA citations and workers seeking enforcement of workplace safety standards

name: Office for Civil Rights, Departmental Management, Health and Human Services
description: Enforces federal civil rights, conscience, and religious freedom laws in HHS-funded programs, including HIPAA privacy and security rules. Investigates complaints and provides technical assistance.
beneficiaries: Patients and individuals protected from discrimination and privacy violations in health care and human services programs

name: Office of Advocacy, Small Business Administration
description: Serves as an independent voice for small business within the federal government, reviewing proposed regulations for their impact on small entities and researching small business conditions. Required by the Regulatory Flexibility Act.
beneficiaries: Small businesses and entrepreneurs through reduced regulatory burden and policy advocacy

name: Office of Assistant Secretary of the Army for Civil Works, Corps of Engineers, Civil
description: Funds the policy oversight and management of the Army Corps of Engineers' civil works program by the Assistant Secretary of the Army. Provides civilian leadership for water resources projects.
beneficiaries: Communities served by Corps civil works projects through effective program oversight and policy direction

name: Office of Biometric Identity Management, National Protection and Programs Directorate, Homeland Security
description: Provides biometric identity services to DHS and its partners for immigration, border management, credentialing, and national security through the Automated Biometric Identification System (IDENT/HART).
beneficiaries: DHS components, law enforcement agencies, and national security through identity verification and fraud prevention

name: Office of Budget and Program Analysis, Executive Operations, Agriculture
description: Provides centralized budget formulation, legislative analysis, and regulatory review functions for the Secretary of Agriculture and all USDA agencies. Coordinates USDA's annual budget submissions.
beneficiaries: USDA agencies, the Secretary, and the public through effective budget management and resource allocation

name: Office of Civil Rights, Agriculture
description: Ensures compliance with civil rights laws and USDA nondiscrimination policies in programs, activities, and employment across the department. Investigates discrimination complaints filed by USDA employees and program participants.
beneficiaries: USDA employees, program applicants, and participants protected from discrimination

name: Office of Civil Rights, Office of the Secretary, Transportation
description: Ensures nondiscrimination in DOT programs and activities, enforces Title VI, ADA, and environmental justice requirements, and processes discrimination complaints. Leads DOT's Disadvantaged Business Enterprise program.
beneficiaries: Transportation system users, minority and disadvantaged business owners, and DOT employees and applicants

name: Office of Codex Alimentarius, Office of the Secretary, Agriculture
description: Coordinates U.S. participation in the international Codex Alimentarius Commission, which develops harmonized food safety and quality standards for international trade. Ensures U.S. interests in global food standards.
beneficiaries: U.S. food exporters, consumers, and the global food trade through harmonized safety standards

name: Office of Environmental Quality Management Fund, Council on Environmental Quality and Office of Environmental Quality, Executive Office of the President
description: Supports the Council on Environmental Quality in advising the President on environmental policy and overseeing federal agency implementation of NEPA. Receives reimbursements for interagency services.
beneficiaries: The President, federal agencies implementing NEPA, and the public through sound environmental policy

name: Office of Ethics, Agriculture
description: Provides ethics guidance, training, and financial disclosure administration for USDA employees across all agencies. Ensures compliance with government ethics laws and regulations.
beneficiaries: USDA employees and the public through ethical government service and conflict-of-interest prevention

name: Office of Financial Stability, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Administered the Troubled Asset Relief Program (TARP) created by the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act during the 2008 financial crisis. Winding down remaining investments and obligations.
beneficiaries: Financial system stability, troubled financial institutions during the crisis, and taxpayers through recovered investments

name: Office of Government Ethics
description: An independent agency that leads the executive branch ethics program, setting policies for financial disclosure, standards of conduct, and conflict-of-interest prevention for all executive branch employees.
beneficiaries: The public through ethical government, and executive branch employees receiving ethics guidance

name: Office of Hearings and Appeals Division, Executive Operations, Agriculture
description: Conducts administrative hearings and reviews for various USDA programs, providing an independent forum for adjudication of disputes involving program participants and agency actions.
beneficiaries: USDA program participants and regulated entities seeking fair adjudication of disputes

name: Office of Inspector General - Recovery Act, Departmental Management, Commerce
description: Provided additional oversight funding to the Commerce OIG for auditing and investigating the use of ARRA stimulus funds across Commerce Department programs. A temporary Recovery Act account.
beneficiaries: Taxpayers through oversight ensuring proper use of Recovery Act funds in Commerce programs

name: Office of Inspector General, Corporation for National and Community Service
description: Conducts audits, investigations, and evaluations of CNCS programs including AmeriCorps and Senior Corps to prevent fraud, waste, and abuse. Independent oversight of national service programs.
beneficiaries: AmeriCorps members, grantees, and taxpayers through program integrity and accountability

name: Office of Inspector General, Environmental Protection Agency
description: Provides independent oversight of EPA programs through audits, evaluations, and criminal investigations to improve efficiency and prevent fraud. Covers environmental cleanup, grants, and regulatory programs.
beneficiaries: The environment, public health, and taxpayers through effective oversight of EPA spending and programs

name: Office of Inspector General, Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
description: Conducts audits and investigations of FDIC programs and operations to promote economy, efficiency, and effectiveness in deposit insurance and banking supervision activities.
beneficiaries: Bank depositors, the Deposit Insurance Fund, and the financial system through FDIC program integrity

name: Office of Inspector General, General Services Administration
description: Provides oversight of GSA programs including federal procurement, real property management, and technology services through audits, investigations, and inspections.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies using GSA services and taxpayers through fraud prevention in government procurement and property management

name: Office of Inspector General, National Aeronautics and Space Administration
description: Provides independent oversight of NASA programs and operations through audits, investigations, and reviews, with focus on contract management, safety, and major project performance.
beneficiaries: NASA mission success, contractor accountability, and taxpayers through oversight of space program spending

name: Office of Inspector General, National Archives and Records Administration
description: Conducts audits and investigations of NARA programs and operations to promote efficiency and prevent fraud, waste, and abuse in federal records management and archival activities.
beneficiaries: NARA operations and the public through accountability in preservation and management of federal records

name: Office of Inspector General, National Science Foundation
description: Provides oversight of NSF programs through audits and investigations, with particular focus on research grant integrity, scientific misconduct, and financial management of research awards.
beneficiaries: Scientific research community, NSF grantees, and taxpayers through integrity in federal research funding

name: Office of Inspector General, Small Business Administration
description: Conducts audits, investigations, and reviews of SBA programs including disaster loans, small business lending, and contracting programs to detect and prevent fraud.
beneficiaries: Small business owners, SBA borrowers, and taxpayers through accountability in small business assistance programs

name: Office of Job Corps - Recovery Act, Departmental Management, Labor
description: Provided additional ARRA funding for Job Corps construction, rehabilitation, and acquisition of centers to improve residential vocational training facilities for disadvantaged youth.
beneficiaries: Disadvantaged youth in Job Corps programs through improved training facilities funded by Recovery Act stimulus

name: Office of Navajo and Hopi Indian Relocation
description: Implements the relocation of Navajo and Hopi families affected by the Navajo-Hopi Land Settlement Act, providing replacement housing and relocation assistance. Winding down operations as relocations near completion.
beneficiaries: Navajo and Hopi families requiring relocation due to the partitioned lands dispute in Arizona

name: Office of Safety, Security, and Protection, Executive Operations, Agriculture
description: Provides security, law enforcement, and personnel protection services for the USDA headquarters complex, senior officials, and critical infrastructure. Manages USDA's continuity of operations.
beneficiaries: USDA employees, visitors, and operations through physical security and protection services

name: Office of Science and Technology Policy, Executive Office of the President
description: Advises the President on science, technology, and innovation policy, coordinates federal R&D investments, and leads interagency science and technology initiatives. Headed by the President's Science Advisor.
beneficiaries: The President, federal science agencies, the research community, and the public through science-informed policy

name: Office of Special Counsel
description: An independent federal investigative and prosecutorial agency that protects whistleblowers, investigates prohibited personnel practices, and enforces the Hatch Act's restrictions on political activity by federal employees.
beneficiaries: Federal employees and whistleblowers protected from retaliation and prohibited personnel practices

name: Office of the Assistant Secretary for Civil Rights, Agriculture
description: Provides leadership, direction, and oversight to ensure USDA compliance with civil rights laws and policies in program delivery and employment. Leads department-wide civil rights strategic planning.
beneficiaries: USDA program participants, especially historically underserved communities, and USDA employees protected from discrimination

name: Office of the Assistant Secretary for Natural Resources and Environment, Agriculture
description: Provides policy direction and oversight for the Forest Service and Natural Resources Conservation Service within USDA. Coordinates natural resource and environmental policy for the department.
beneficiaries: National Forest and natural resource programs through coordinated policy leadership

name: Office of the Chief Economist, Executive Operations, Agriculture
description: Provides economic intelligence, analysis, and forecasts to the Secretary of Agriculture on agricultural markets, trade, climate, and food security. Houses the World Agricultural Outlook Board and related analytical units.
beneficiaries: USDA leadership, agricultural markets, and policymakers through expert economic analysis and crop forecasts

name: Office of the Chief Information Officer, Departmental Management, Intelligence, Situational Awareness, and Oversight, Homeland Security
description: Manages DHS enterprise IT strategy, cybersecurity, data management, and IT investment oversight across all DHS components. Leads departmental digital transformation initiatives.
beneficiaries: DHS components and operations through unified IT management, and the public through improved cybersecurity

name: Office of the General Counsel, Agriculture
description: Provides legal advice, counsel, and representation to the Secretary of Agriculture and all USDA agencies on the full range of legal issues including rulemaking, litigation, and program administration.
beneficiaries: USDA agencies and programs through legal support ensuring lawful and effective program implementation

name: Office of the Inspector General, Departmental Management, Commerce
description: Conducts audits and investigations of Commerce Department programs including Census, NOAA, NIST, and economic development programs to promote efficiency and prevent fraud.
beneficiaries: Commerce Department programs and taxpayers through effective oversight of trade, science, and data programs

name: Office of the Inspector General, Departmental Management, Education
description: Provides oversight of Department of Education programs through audits and investigations, with focus on student financial aid fraud, grant management, and data integrity.
beneficiaries: Students receiving federal financial aid and taxpayers through accountability in education spending

name: Office of the Inspector General, Departmental Management, Labor
description: Conducts criminal investigations and audits of DOL programs including unemployment insurance, workers' compensation, job training, and labor racketeering. Combats fraud in workforce programs.
beneficiaries: Workers, unemployment insurance beneficiaries, and taxpayers through fraud prevention in labor programs

name: Office of the Inspector General, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Provides oversight of DOE programs including nuclear security, energy research, environmental cleanup, and loan guarantee programs through audits, inspections, and investigations.
beneficiaries: National security, energy program integrity, and taxpayers through oversight of DOE's diverse missions

name: Office of the Inspector General, Nuclear Regulatory Commission
description: Conducts audits and investigations of NRC programs and operations to promote safety, efficiency, and integrity in nuclear regulatory activities.
beneficiaries: Public safety, nuclear industry regulation, and NRC operations through independent oversight

name: Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology - Recovery Act, Office of the Secretary, Health and Human Services
description: Provided ARRA funding to ONC for health IT adoption, including standards development, state health information exchanges, and workforce training for electronic health record implementation.
beneficiaries: Health care providers, patients, and the health system through accelerated adoption of health information technology

name: Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology, Departmental Management, Health and Human Services
description: Coordinates nationwide efforts to implement and use health information technology and electronic health records to improve health care quality, safety, and efficiency. Develops interoperability standards.
beneficiaries: Health care providers, patients, and the health system through interoperable electronic health information exchange

name: Office of the National Science Board, National Science Foundation
description: Supports the National Science Board, the governing body of NSF that sets policies, identifies national science priorities, and serves as an advisory body to the President and Congress on science and engineering.
beneficiaries: The scientific community, NSF, and policymakers through strategic guidance on national science priorities

name: Office of the Secretary Gift Fund, Health and Human Services
description: Receives unconditional monetary gifts and donations to the Office of the Secretary of HHS for use in support of departmental activities at the Secretary's discretion.
beneficiaries: HHS programs and operations supplemented by charitable donations

name: Office of the Under Secretary for Farm and Foreign Agricultural Services, Agriculture
description: Provides leadership and coordination for USDA agencies dealing with farm programs, foreign agricultural trade, and commodity credit. Oversees FSA, RMA, and FAS operations.
beneficiaries: American farmers, agricultural exporters, and farm program participants through coordinated farm policy leadership

name: Office of the Under Secretary for Food Safety, Agriculture
description: Provides policy direction and oversight for the Food Safety and Inspection Service (FSIS), which inspects meat, poultry, and processed egg products. Leads USDA's food safety mission.
beneficiaries: Consumers through safe food supply, and the meat and poultry industry through effective inspection programs

name: Office of the Under Secretary for Food, Nutrition and Consumer Services, Agriculture
description: Oversees the Food and Nutrition Service and the Center for Nutrition Policy and Promotion, coordinating USDA's nutrition assistance programs including SNAP, WIC, and school meals.
beneficiaries: Millions of Americans receiving federal nutrition assistance and communities served by feeding programs

name: Office of the Under Secretary for Management, Departmental Management and Operations, Homeland Security
description: Provides management services to DHS including financial management, human capital, procurement, and facilities. Oversees the department's administrative operations and business processes.
beneficiaries: DHS components through effective management services and administrative support

name: Office of the Under Secretary for Marketing and Regulatory Programs, Cooperative State Research, Education, and Extension Service, Agriculture
description: Coordinates USDA's marketing, animal and plant health, and regulatory programs through oversight of AMS, APHIS, and the Grain Inspection, Packers and Stockyards Administration.
beneficiaries: Agricultural producers, consumers, and trading partners through coordinated marketing and regulatory oversight

name: Office of the Under Secretary for Research, Education and Economics, Agriculture
description: Coordinates USDA's research, education, statistics, and extension activities through oversight of ARS, NIFA, ERS, and NASS. Leads the department's scientific enterprise.
beneficiaries: Agricultural researchers, land-grant universities, farmers, and the public through coordinated agricultural science

name: Office of the Under Secretary for Rural Development, Agriculture
description: Provides leadership for USDA's rural development mission through oversight of the Rural Housing Service, Rural Utilities Service, and Rural Business-Cooperative Service.
beneficiaries: Rural communities, businesses, utilities, and homeowners through coordinated rural development programs

name: Office of the Under Secretary for Trade and Foreign Agricultural Affairs, Office of the Secretary, Agriculture
description: Coordinates USDA's trade policy and foreign agricultural affairs activities, established by the 2014 Farm Bill to elevate trade within USDA's organizational structure.
beneficiaries: U.S. agricultural exporters and the farm economy through strategic trade policy leadership

name: Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund-Treasury Managed, Homeland Security
description: The Treasury-managed portion of the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund, financed by a per-barrel tax on petroleum, covering federal oil spill response, natural resource damage assessments, and claim payments.
beneficiaries: Coastal communities, marine ecosystems, and claimants affected by oil spills

name: Oil Spill Recovery, USCG, Homeland Security
description: Receives reimbursements and cost recoveries from responsible parties for Coast Guard oil spill response activities. Funds are returned to the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund.
beneficiaries: The Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund and taxpayers through recovered spill response costs

name: Oil Spill Research, Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement, Interior
description: Funds research to improve oil spill prevention, detection, response, and cleanup technologies for offshore oil and gas operations on the Outer Continental Shelf.
beneficiaries: Offshore energy industry, marine environments, and coastal communities through improved spill response capabilities

name: Operating Account, Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
description: The FDIC's primary operating account funding supervision and insurance of banks and thrift institutions, consumer protection, receivership management, and resolution of failed banks. Funded by bank assessments, not appropriations.
beneficiaries: Bank depositors, the banking system, and financial stability through deposit insurance and bank supervision

name: Operating Expenses of the United States Agency for International Development, Office of Inspector General, Funds Appropriated to the President
description: Funds the USAID OIG's audit and investigative oversight of U.S. foreign assistance programs worldwide, including development, humanitarian, and democracy assistance.
beneficiaries: Foreign aid recipients, USAID programs, and taxpayers through oversight of international development spending

name: Operating Expenses, General Services Administration
description: Funds GSA's management and operation of government-owned buildings, real property disposal, and administrative services for federal agencies. One of GSA's largest accounts.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies occupying GSA-managed buildings and the public through efficient government facilities management

name: Operating Expenses, Official Residence of the Vice President, Executive Office of the President
description: Funds the operation and maintenance of the Vice President's official residence at Number One Observatory Circle on the grounds of the U.S. Naval Observatory in Washington, D.C.
beneficiaries: The Vice President and official functions held at the residence

name: Operation and Maintenance of Quarters, Bureau of Indian Affairs
description: Funds the maintenance and operation of government-owned housing provided to BIA employees stationed at remote locations on or near Indian reservations where private housing is unavailable.
beneficiaries: BIA employees in remote duty stations requiring government quarters on or near reservations

name: Operation and Maintenance of Quarters, Bureau of Indian Education, Interior
description: Funds maintenance and operation of government quarters for Bureau of Indian Education staff at remote school locations on Indian reservations where adequate private housing is not available.
beneficiaries: BIE teachers and staff serving at remote schools on Indian reservations

name: Operation and Maintenance of Quarters, Bureau of Land Management
description: Funds the operation and maintenance of government-owned housing for BLM employees at remote field locations where private housing is insufficient or unavailable.
beneficiaries: BLM employees stationed in remote locations on public lands

name: Operation and Maintenance of Quarters, Bureau of Reclamation, Interior
description: Funds the operation and maintenance of government-owned housing for Bureau of Reclamation employees at remote dam sites and water project locations.
beneficiaries: Bureau of Reclamation employees stationed at remote water project sites

name: Operation and Maintenance of Quarters, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Funds the operation and maintenance of government-owned housing for Forest Service employees stationed at remote locations within the National Forest System where private housing is not available.
beneficiaries: Forest Service rangers and staff at remote duty stations in National Forests

name: Operation and Maintenance of Quarters, Geological Survey
description: Funds maintenance of government quarters for USGS employees at remote field stations, observatories, and research sites where private housing options are limited.
beneficiaries: USGS scientists and staff at remote research and monitoring stations

name: Operation and Maintenance of Quarters, Indian Health Services, Health and Human Services
description: Funds operation and maintenance of government quarters for IHS health professionals stationed at remote clinics and hospitals on Indian reservations where private housing is scarce.
beneficiaries: IHS physicians, nurses, and health professionals serving in remote tribal health facilities

name: Operation and Maintenance of Quarters, National Park Service
description: Funds the maintenance of government-owned housing for NPS employees at national park sites, many of which are in remote locations where private housing is unavailable or impractical.
beneficiaries: Park rangers and NPS staff stationed at remote national park units

name: Operation and Maintenance of Quarters, United States Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Funds the operation and maintenance of government quarters for FWS employees at remote wildlife refuges and field stations where private housing is not available.
beneficiaries: FWS employees at remote National Wildlife Refuges and fish hatcheries

name: Operation and Maintenance, Corps of Engineers-Civil
description: The Army Corps of Engineers' largest civil works account, funding the operation, maintenance, and care of existing navigation, flood control, hydropower, recreation, and environmental infrastructure nationwide.
beneficiaries: Commercial navigation, flood-prone communities, hydropower consumers, and recreation users at Corps projects

name: Operation and Maintenance, Indian Irrigation Systems, Bureau of Indian Affairs
description: Funds the operation, maintenance, and rehabilitation of federally owned irrigation systems on Indian reservations, providing water for agricultural use by tribal members. Supported by user fees and appropriations.
beneficiaries: Native American farmers and ranchers on reservations with federally operated irrigation systems

name: Operation and Maintenance, Southeastern Power Administration, Power Marketing Administrations, Energy
description: Funds the operation and maintenance of transmission systems used by SEPA to market and deliver hydroelectric power generated at Army Corps of Engineers dams in the southeastern United States.
beneficiaries: Electric utilities and consumers in the southeastern U.S. receiving low-cost federal hydroelectric power

name: Operational Expenses, Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration, Transportation
description: Funds PHMSA's oversight of the safe transportation of hazardous materials by all modes and the safety of the nation's pipeline infrastructure through inspections, enforcement, and standards development.
beneficiaries: Communities near pipelines and hazardous materials routes, pipeline operators, and hazmat shippers through safety regulation

name: Operational Support, Office of the Secretary, Transportation
description: Funds core operational support activities for the Office of the Secretary of Transportation, including IT, facilities, and administrative services shared across DOT headquarters.
beneficiaries: DOT leadership and operations through centralized administrative support services

name: Operations and Maintenance, Great Lakes St. Lawrence Seaway Development Corporation, Transportation
description: Funds the operation and maintenance of the U.S. portion of the St. Lawrence Seaway, including locks, channels, and related infrastructure for commercial navigation between the Great Lakes and the Atlantic Ocean.
beneficiaries: Commercial shippers, Great Lakes ports and communities, and the agricultural and industrial economy of the Great Lakes region
