name: Operations and Support, Federal Emergency Management Agency, Homeland Security
description: FEMA's primary operating account funding disaster preparedness, response, and recovery coordination, the National Flood Insurance Program administration, and continuity of government operations.
beneficiaries: Communities affected by disasters, state and local emergency managers, and NFIP policyholders

name: Operations and Support, Intelligence, Analysis, and Operations Coordination, Departmental Management, Intelligence, Situational Awareness, and Oversight, Homeland Security
description: Funds DHS's intelligence analysis and operations coordination activities, providing threat assessments and situational awareness to federal, state, and local partners.
beneficiaries: Federal, state, and local law enforcement and homeland security stakeholders through intelligence sharing and threat analysis

name: Operations and Support, Office of the Secretary and Executive Management, Departmental Management, Intelligence, Situational Awareness, and Oversight, Homeland Security
description: Funds the Office of the Secretary of Homeland Security and executive management functions including policy development, public affairs, legislative affairs, and departmental leadership.
beneficiaries: DHS leadership, department operations, and national security through executive management and policy coordination

name: Operations, Preparedness, and Emergency Response, Administration for Strategic Preparedness and Response, Health and Human Services
description: Funds ASPR's medical countermeasure development, Strategic National Stockpile, Hospital Preparedness Program, and emergency response activities for public health and medical emergencies.
beneficiaries: The public during health emergencies, hospitals preparing for mass casualty events, and the health security enterprise

name: Outreach for Socially Disadvantaged Farmers, Office of the Secretary, Agriculture
description: Funds outreach, education, and technical assistance to socially disadvantaged and veteran farmers and ranchers to improve their participation in USDA programs. Section 2501 program.
beneficiaries: Minority, socially disadvantaged, and veteran farmers and ranchers seeking equitable access to USDA programs

name: Overseas Humanitarian, Disaster, and Civic AID, Defense
description: Funds DoD humanitarian assistance, disaster relief, and civic action programs in foreign countries conducted in conjunction with military operations or exercises. Includes humanitarian mine action and disaster preparedness.
beneficiaries: Populations in developing countries affected by natural disasters, landmines, and inadequate infrastructure

name: Pacific Coastal Salmon Recovery, National Oceanic and Atmospheric, Commerce
description: Provides grants to states and tribes in the Pacific Coast region for salmon conservation, habitat restoration, and recovery of Pacific salmon and steelhead populations listed under the Endangered Species Act.
beneficiaries: Pacific salmon and steelhead populations, Pacific Coast tribes, fishing communities, and aquatic ecosystems

name: Panama Canal Commission Compensation Fund, Labor
description: Provides workers' compensation benefits for employees and former employees of the Panama Canal Commission and its predecessor agencies for work-related injuries and occupational diseases.
beneficiaries: Former Panama Canal Commission employees and their survivors receiving federal workers' compensation benefits

name: Pandemic Response Accountability Committee, Council of the Inspectors General on Integrity and Efficiency
description: Provides oversight of federal pandemic relief spending through the coordinated efforts of federal Inspectors General, investigating fraud and ensuring accountability of CARES Act and related pandemic funds.
beneficiaries: Taxpayers and pandemic relief recipients through fraud prevention and accountability in trillions of dollars of pandemic spending

name: Park Buildings Lease and Maintenance Fund, National Park Service
description: A revolving fund receiving proceeds from the leasing of underutilized historic buildings in national parks to private entities, with receipts used for repair and maintenance of leased buildings and other park structures.
beneficiaries: National park visitors through restored historic buildings and lessees operating businesses in park settings

name: Park Concessions Franchise Fees, National Park Service
description: Receives franchise fees from commercial concession operations in national parks (lodging, food service, recreation) and makes them available for park visitor facility improvements.
beneficiaries: National park visitors through improved visitor facilities funded by concession franchise fees

name: Patient Protection and Affordable Care - Program Management, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid, Health and Human Services
description: Funds CMS implementation and administration of the Affordable Care Act, including operation of HealthCare.gov, marketplace oversight, and insurance market reforms.
beneficiaries: Individuals and families obtaining health insurance through the ACA marketplace and benefiting from insurance market reforms

name: Patients' Benefit Fund, Health Resources and Services Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Holds funds derived from the labor of patients at HRSA-supported facilities for their personal benefit and welfare, a legacy account from the former Public Health Service hospital system.
beneficiaries: Patients at federally supported health facilities

name: Patients' Benefit Fund, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Manages personal funds held on behalf of patients at the NIH Clinical Center who participate in clinical research protocols, for their personal use during treatment.
beneficiaries: Patients participating in clinical trials at the NIH Clinical Center in Bethesda, Maryland

name: Payment Where Certain Tax Credits Exceeds Liability for Corporate Tax, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds refundable payments to corporations when certain business tax credits exceed their federal income tax liability, allowing the excess to be received as a direct payment.
beneficiaries: Corporations with qualifying tax credits exceeding their tax liability

name: Payment Where Health Coverage Tax Credit Exceeds Liability for Tax, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds refundable payments of the Health Coverage Tax Credit for trade-affected workers and PBGC pension recipients whose health insurance premium credit exceeds their tax liability. Authorized by the TAA Reform Act.
beneficiaries: Workers displaced by trade and PBGC pension recipients paying health insurance premiums

name: Payment Where Small Business Health Insurance Tax Credit Exceeds Liability for Tax, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds refundable payments to small businesses and tax-exempt organizations whose ACA small business health insurance tax credit exceeds their tax liability.
beneficiaries: Small employers with low-wage workers who provide health insurance coverage

name: Payment for Tax Losses on Land Acquired for Grand Teton National Park, National Park Service
description: Reimburses Teton County, Wyoming for property tax revenue lost due to federal acquisition of private lands for Grand Teton National Park, as required by the 1950 act establishing the park's current boundaries.
beneficiaries: Teton County, Wyoming government and residents compensated for lost tax base from federal land acquisition

name: Payment of Claims (Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund), Homeland Security
description: Pays claims for removal costs and damages resulting from oil spills from the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund when the responsible party does not pay or cannot be identified. Administered by the Coast Guard.
beneficiaries: Individuals, businesses, and government entities damaged by oil spills where responsible parties have not compensated them

name: Payment to Expenses and Refunds, Inspection and Grading of Farm Products, Agricultural Marketing Service, Agriculture
description: Processes refunds and adjustments related to fees collected for the voluntary inspection and grading of agricultural commodities by USDA's Agricultural Marketing Service.
beneficiaries: Agricultural producers and handlers who have overpaid inspection and grading fees

name: Payment to Issuer of New Clean Renewable Energy Bonds, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds direct payments to issuers of New Clean Renewable Energy Bonds in lieu of traditional tax credits, subsidizing renewable energy projects by governmental entities, cooperatives, and public power providers.
beneficiaries: Public power utilities, electric cooperatives, and governmental bodies financing renewable energy projects

name: Payment to Issuer of Qualified Energy Conservation Bonds, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Provides direct subsidy payments to issuers of Qualified Energy Conservation Bonds for energy conservation projects including energy efficiency capital expenditures, green community programs, and renewable energy.
beneficiaries: State, local, and tribal governments financing energy conservation and efficiency projects

name: Payment to Issuer of Qualified School Construction Bonds, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds direct payments to issuers of Qualified School Construction Bonds used for the construction, rehabilitation, or repair of public school facilities in high-poverty areas.
beneficiaries: School districts in high-poverty areas building or renovating school facilities

name: Payment to Issuer of Qualified Zone Academy Bonds, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Provides direct subsidy payments to issuers of Qualified Zone Academy Bonds that finance renovations, equipment, teacher training, and course development for schools in empowerment zones or with high-poverty enrollment.
beneficiaries: Schools in empowerment zones and high-poverty areas improving facilities and educational programs

name: Payment to Minnesota (Cook, Lake, and Saint Louis Counties) from the National Forests Fund, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Provides special revenue-sharing payments to three Minnesota counties from National Forest receipts in lieu of standard 25% payments, due to unique circumstances of the Superior National Forest.
beneficiaries: Cook, Lake, and St. Louis Counties in Minnesota hosting Superior National Forest lands

name: Payment to Oklahoma (Royalties), Office of Natural Resources Revenue, Interior
description: Distributes mineral royalty payments to the State of Oklahoma from production on former Indian lands within the state, pursuant to historical agreements regarding Oklahoma statehood.
beneficiaries: The State of Oklahoma through mineral royalty revenue from federal and Indian lands

name: Payment to States Under Federal Power Act, Energy
description: Distributes a portion of receipts from federal hydroelectric power licenses to states in which the licensed projects are located, as required by the Federal Power Act. Compensates states for use of water resources.
beneficiaries: States hosting federal hydroelectric power projects receiving revenue-sharing payments

name: Payment to the American Institute in Taiwan, State
description: Funds the American Institute in Taiwan, a nonprofit corporation that conducts the unofficial relationship between the United States and Taiwan, performing consular and other functions following derecognition.
beneficiaries: U.S. citizens and businesses in Taiwan, Taiwanese visa applicants, and U.S.-Taiwan relations

name: Payment to the Asia Foundation, State
description: Provides an annual appropriation to The Asia Foundation, a private nonprofit organization that supports governance, law, economic development, women's empowerment, and international cooperation programs across Asia.
beneficiaries: Communities in Asian countries benefiting from governance, development, and civil society programs

name: Payment to the FDA Innovation Account, CURES Act, Food and Drug Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Provides mandatory funding authorized by the 21st Century Cures Act for FDA to advance regulatory science, modernize clinical trials, and accelerate medical product development review processes.
beneficiaries: Patients awaiting new drugs and medical devices, and the pharmaceutical and device industries through expedited FDA processes

name: Payment to the Legal Services Corporation, Legal Services Corporation
description: Provides the annual federal appropriation to the Legal Services Corporation, which makes grants to independent nonprofit legal aid programs providing civil legal assistance to low-income Americans.
beneficiaries: Low-income Americans needing legal representation in civil matters such as housing, family law, and public benefits

name: Payment to the NIH Innovation Account, CURES Act, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Mandatory funding from the 21st Century Cures Act for NIH's high-priority research initiatives including the All of Us precision medicine cohort, BRAIN Initiative, and Cancer Moonshot programs.
beneficiaries: Biomedical research community and patients through transformative research in precision medicine, neuroscience, and oncology

name: Payment to the Radiation Exposure Compensation Trust Fund, Justice
description: Funds compassionate lump-sum payments to individuals who contracted certain diseases from exposure to radiation during atmospheric nuclear testing or uranium mining for the U.S. nuclear weapons program.
beneficiaries: Downwinders, onsite participants in nuclear testing, and uranium miners and millers exposed to radiation

name: Payment to the Resolution Funding Corporation, Bureau of the Fiscal Service, Treasury
description: Services the interest payments on bonds issued by the Resolution Funding Corporation to fund the savings and loan crisis resolution in the late 1980s and early 1990s. Bonds mature through 2030.
beneficiaries: Bondholders of Resolution Funding Corporation obligations and financial system stability

name: Payments for Credits Against Health Care Contributions, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, Health and Human Services
description: Funded the ACA's early retiree reinsurance program and related health care contribution credits to reduce health insurance costs before marketplace exchanges were operational.
beneficiaries: Early retirees and employers providing health coverage through reduced health care contribution costs

name: Payments for Credits Against Social Security Contributions, Social Security Administration
description: Reimbursed the Social Security trust funds for revenue forgone due to the temporary payroll tax reduction enacted in the Tax Relief, Unemployment Insurance Reauthorization, and Job Creation Act of 2010.
beneficiaries: The Social Security trust funds held harmless from the temporary 2% payroll tax cut, and workers who received the tax relief

name: Payments for Tribal Leases, Bureau of Indian Affairs, Interior
description: Funds BIA payments for facility leases from Indian tribes under Section 105(l) of the Indian Self-Determination Act, allowing tribes to lease their own facilities to the federal government for program delivery.
beneficiaries: Indian tribes leasing facilities to BIA for delivery of self-determination programs and services

name: Payments for Tribal Leases, Indian Health Service, Health and Human Services
description: Funds IHS payments to Indian tribes for leasing tribally owned health facilities under Section 105(l) of the Indian Self-Determination Act, supporting tribal self-governance in health care delivery.
beneficiaries: Indian tribes operating health care facilities under self-determination contracts and compacts with IHS

name: Payments in Lieu of Taxes, Departmental Management
description: Compensates local governments for non-taxable federal lands within their jurisdictions under the Payments in Lieu of Taxes Act.
beneficiaries: County and local governments containing federal lands

name: Payments to Alaska from Oil and Gas Leases, National Petroleum Reserve, Office of Natural Resources Revenue, Interior
description: Distributes a share of oil and gas lease revenues from the National Petroleum Reserve in Alaska to the state.
beneficiaries: State of Alaska and its communities

name: Payments to Coos and Douglas Counties, Oregon, from Receipts, Coos Bay Wagon Road Grant Lands, Bureau of Land Management
description: Shares receipts from the Coos Bay Wagon Road Grant Lands with the two Oregon counties where these lands are located.
beneficiaries: Coos and Douglas Counties, Oregon

name: Payments to Counties, National Grasslands, Bureau of Land Management
description: Distributes revenue from national grasslands administered by BLM to the counties in which those grasslands are situated.
beneficiaries: Counties containing BLM-managed national grasslands

name: Payments to Counties, National Grasslands, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Shares receipts from national grasslands managed by the Forest Service with local county governments.
beneficiaries: Counties containing Forest Service national grasslands

name: Payments to Counties, Oregon and California Grant Lands, Bureau of Land Management
description: Distributes revenues from the Oregon and California Railroad grant lands to 18 Oregon counties under the O&C Act.
beneficiaries: Eighteen Oregon counties with O&C grant lands

name: Payments to Local Units, Klamath Reclamation Area, Bureau of Reclamation
description: Provides payments to local government units in the Klamath Reclamation Area as compensation for tax-exempt federal lands.
beneficiaries: Local governments in the Klamath Reclamation Area, Oregon and California

name: Payments to State and County from Clark County, Nevada Land Sales, Bureau of Land Management
description: Distributes proceeds from the sale of federal land in Clark County, Nevada, to the state and county for conservation and infrastructure.
beneficiaries: State of Nevada and Clark County

name: Payments to States (Proceeds of Sales), Bureau of Land Management
description: Distributes to states a percentage of proceeds from the sale of federal public domain lands within their borders.
beneficiaries: States where federal land sales occur

name: Payments to States and Possessions, Agricultural Marketing Service, Agriculture
description: Provides funds to states and territories for cooperative agricultural marketing inspection and grading services.
beneficiaries: State departments of agriculture and U.S. territories

name: Payments to States for Child Support Enforcement and Family Support Programs, Administration for Children and Families, Health and Human Services
description: Funds state programs that locate absent parents, establish paternity, and enforce child support obligations. Also supports related family support activities.
beneficiaries: Custodial parents, children owed support, and state child support agencies

name: Payments to States from Grazing Receipts, Etc., Public Lands Outside Grazing Districts, Bureau of Land Management
description: Shares grazing fee receipts from public lands outside organized grazing districts with the states where those lands are located.
beneficiaries: States with BLM grazing lands outside grazing districts

name: Payments to States from Grazing Receipts, Etc., Public Lands within Grazing Districts, Bureau of Land Management
description: Distributes a share of grazing receipts collected within organized grazing districts on BLM-managed lands to affected states.
beneficiaries: States with BLM lands within grazing districts

name: Payments to States from Grazing Receipts, Etc., Public Lands within Grazing Districts, Miscellaneous, Bureau of Land Management
description: Distributes miscellaneous grazing-related receipts from within BLM grazing districts to states for range improvement and county services.
beneficiaries: States and counties with BLM grazing district lands

name: Payments to States, Flood Control Act of 1954, Corps of Engineers - Civil
description: Distributes to states a share of revenues from Corps of Engineers flood control lands and reservoir areas.
beneficiaries: States containing Corps of Engineers flood control projects

name: Payments to States, Flood Control Act of 1954, Office of Natural Resources Revenue, Interior
description: Distributes Interior-collected revenues from flood control project lands to the states as required by the Flood Control Act of 1954.
beneficiaries: States with flood control reservoir lands managed by Interior

name: Payments to States, National Forest Fund, Office of Natural Resources Revenue, Interior
description: Transfers to states a portion of national forest receipts collected by Interior's revenue office for roads and schools.
beneficiaries: States and counties containing national forest lands

name: Payments to the Unemployment Trust Fund, Employment and Training Administration, Labor
description: Transfers federal funds into the Unemployment Trust Fund to cover administrative costs and extended unemployment benefits.
beneficiaries: Unemployed workers and state unemployment insurance systems

name: Payments, Federal Payment for Defender Services in District of Columbia Courts
description: Provides federal funding for public defender and court-appointed counsel services in the District of Columbia court system.
beneficiaries: Indigent defendants in D.C. courts

name: Perishable Agricultural Commodities Act Fund, Agricultural Marketing Service, Agriculture
description: Finances the enforcement of fair trading practices in the fruit and vegetable industry through licensing, inspection, and dispute resolution.
beneficiaries: Produce growers, shippers, and dealers

name: Permit Processing Fund, Bureau of Land Management
description: Collects and uses fees from oil and gas permit applications to fund timely processing of drilling permits on federal lands.
beneficiaries: Oil and gas operators seeking permits and BLM field offices

name: Pershing Hall Revolving Fund, Veterans Affairs
description: Manages revenues from the Pershing Hall property in Paris, France, a memorial to General Pershing and the American Expeditionary Forces.
beneficiaries: Veterans organizations and the maintenance of Pershing Hall

name: Pesticide Registration Fund, Environmental Protection Agency
description: Collects fees from pesticide registrants to accelerate EPA review and registration of new pesticide products and uses.
beneficiaries: Pesticide manufacturers, farmers, and the public through timely safety reviews

name: Pima Agriculture Cotton Trust Fund, Farm Service Agency, Agriculture
description: Provides payments to Pima cotton growers, importers of Pima cotton fabric, and yarn spinners to support the domestic Pima cotton industry.
beneficiaries: Pima cotton growers and domestic Pima cotton textile manufacturers

name: Policy and Administration, Bureau of Reclamation, Interior
description: Supports executive direction, policy development, and administrative management for the Bureau of Reclamation's water resource programs.
beneficiaries: Bureau of Reclamation and western water users

name: Post-Vietnam Era, Veterans Education Account, Veterans Affairs
description: Provides educational benefits to veterans who entered military service after the Vietnam era and contributed to the program through payroll deductions.
beneficiaries: Post-Vietnam era veterans pursuing education or training

name: Postal Service Health Benefits Program Implementation Fund, Office of Personnel Management
description: Funds the establishment of a separate health benefits program for Postal Service employees and retirees as required by the Postal Service Reform Act.
beneficiaries: U.S. Postal Service employees and retirees

name: Power Systems, Indian Irrigation Projects, Bureau of Indian Affairs
description: Operates and maintains electrical power systems that serve Indian irrigation projects on tribal reservations.
beneficiaries: Native American tribal communities with irrigation projects

name: Practice and Registration Fees Fund, United States Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims
description: Collects attorney registration and practice fees to support bar admission and attorney discipline functions of the court.
beneficiaries: Veterans seeking legal representation and attorneys practicing before the court

name: Pre-Election Presidential Transition, General Services Administration
description: Funds transition planning activities for eligible presidential candidates before the general election, including office space, equipment, and communications.
beneficiaries: Major party presidential candidates and their transition teams

name: Primary Health Care Access, Health Resources and Services Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Supports community health centers and other primary care programs serving medically underserved populations regardless of ability to pay.
beneficiaries: Low-income, uninsured, and underserved patients in community health centers

name: Prison Industries Fund, Justice
description: Operates Federal Prison Industries (UNICOR), a government corporation employing federal inmates to manufacture products and provide services.
beneficiaries: Federal inmates gaining job skills and federal agency customers

name: Privacy and Civil Liberties Oversight Board
description: Funds an independent executive branch board that reviews counterterrorism policies to ensure they adequately protect privacy and civil liberties.
beneficiaries: U.S. citizens and residents whose civil liberties may be affected by counterterrorism programs

name: Private Collection Agent Program, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Contracts with private debt collection agencies to collect certain overdue federal tax debts that the IRS is not actively pursuing.
beneficiaries: Federal government through increased tax revenue collection

name: Proceeds from Sales, Water Resource Development Projects, United States Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Collects and distributes proceeds from the sale of products or property at Fish and Wildlife Service water resource development project sites.
beneficiaries: Fish and wildlife conservation programs

name: Procurement, Construction, and Improvements, Countering Weapons of Mass Destruction Office, Homeland Security
description: Funds acquisition of detection, prevention, and response equipment for countering chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear threats.
beneficiaries: First responders and homeland security personnel countering WMD threats

name: Procurement, Construction, and Improvements, Federal Law Enforcement Training Centers, Homeland Security
description: Funds construction, renovation, and equipment acquisition for federal law enforcement training facilities.
beneficiaries: Federal law enforcement agencies using FLETC training facilities

name: Procurement, Construction, and Improvements, Management Directorate, Departmental Management, Intelligence, Situational Awareness, and Oversight, Homeland Security
description: Funds facility and technology improvements for DHS headquarters management, intelligence, and oversight functions.
beneficiaries: DHS management and intelligence operations

name: Procurement, Construction, and Improvements, Office of the Secretary and Executive Management, Departmental Management, Intelligence, Situational Awareness, and Oversight, Homeland Security
description: Provides capital investment funding for the DHS Secretary's office and executive management operations.
beneficiaries: DHS executive leadership and departmental oversight functions

name: Procurement, Construction, and Improvements, Science and Technology Directorate, Homeland Security
description: Funds acquisition of laboratory equipment, facilities, and research infrastructure for DHS science and technology programs.
beneficiaries: DHS research programs and homeland security technology development

name: Procurement, Construction, and Improvements, Transportation Security Administration, Homeland Security
description: Funds procurement of passenger and baggage screening equipment, facility modifications, and technology improvements for TSA.
beneficiaries: Air travelers and transportation security operations

beneficiaries: SNCF Réseau and French railway infrastructure modernization

name: Conduite et pilotage des politiques économiques et financières
description: Funds the strategic management and coordination of French economic and financial policies within the Ministry of Economy and Finance.
beneficiaries: French Ministry of Economy and Finance operations

name: Conseil économique, social et environnemental
description: Funds the French Economic, Social, and Environmental Council, a constitutional advisory body representing organized civil society on policy matters.
beneficiaries: French government and civil society through policy consultation

name: Contribution à l'équipement des collectivités territoriales pour l'amélioration des transports en commun, de la sécurité et de la circulation routières
description: Provides French state contributions to local authorities for improving public transit, road safety, and traffic management infrastructure.
beneficiaries: French local authorities and public transit users

name: Contrôle et modernisation de la politique de la circulation et du stationnement routiers
description: Funds road traffic enforcement, automated speed camera systems, and modernization of parking and traffic management policies in France.
beneficiaries: French road users and road safety

name: Développement agricole et rural
description: Funds French agricultural and rural development through applied research, innovation, and technical support for farming modernization.
beneficiaries: French farmers and rural communities

name: Développement et transfert en agriculture
description: Supports technology development and transfer programs in French agriculture to improve productivity and sustainability.
beneficiaries: French agricultural sector and farming innovation

name: Expertise, information géographique et météorologie
description: Funds French geographic information services (IGN) and meteorological services (Météo-France) for mapping, weather forecasting, and climate monitoring.
beneficiaries: French public and government through geographic and weather data services

name: Financement des aides aux collectivités pour l'électrification rurale
description: Funds grants to French local authorities for rural electrification projects, extending and modernizing electrical networks in underserved rural areas.
beneficiaries: French rural communities lacking adequate electrical infrastructure

name: Financement structurel des écosystèmes d'innovation
description: Provides structural financing for French innovation ecosystems including technology transfer, startup incubators, and research-industry partnerships.
beneficiaries: French startups, innovation hubs, and research institutions

name: Fonds d'accélération de la transition écologique dans les territoires
description: Accelerates ecological transition in French territories through grants for local environmental projects including energy renovation, biodiversity, and circular economy initiatives.
beneficiaries: French local authorities pursuing ecological transition projects

name: Fonds de solidarité pour le développement
description: Finances France's international development solidarity initiatives, funded partly by the tax on airline tickets, for global health and climate programs.
beneficiaries: Developing countries receiving French international solidarity aid

name: Fonds de soutien relatif aux prêts et contrats financiers structurés à risque
description: Provides financial support to French local authorities burdened by toxic structured loans contracted before the 2008 financial crisis.
beneficiaries: French local authorities holding risky structured financial products

name: France Médias Monde
description: Funds France's international broadcasting group including France 24 television, Radio France Internationale, and Monte Carlo Doualiya.
beneficiaries: Global audiences seeking French-language international news and media

name: Indemnisation des victimes des persécutions antisémites et des actes de barbarie pendant la seconde guerre mondiale
description: Provides compensation to victims of antisemitic persecution and acts of barbarism during World War II committed by or with the complicity of the French state.
beneficiaries: Survivors of WWII antisemitic persecution and their families

name: Intégration et accès à la nationalité française
description: Funds integration programs for immigrants and refugees in France including language training, civic education, and pathways to French nationality.
beneficiaries: Immigrants and refugees integrating into French society

name: Opérations de maîtrise de la demande d'électricité, de production d'électricité par des énergies renouvelables ou de production de proximité dans les zones non interconnectées
description: Funds energy demand management, renewable electricity production, and local generation in French non-interconnected territories (overseas departments and Corsica).
beneficiaries: French overseas territories and Corsica for energy independence

name: Opérations immobilières et entretien des bâtiments de l'État
description: Funds acquisition, construction, renovation, and maintenance of French state buildings and real property.
beneficiaries: French government operations through maintained state buildings

name: Paysages, eau et biodiversité
description: Funds French landscape protection, water resource management, and biodiversity conservation including natural area protection and species preservation.
beneficiaries: French ecosystems, water resources, and biodiversity

name: Performance et résilience des bâtiments de l'État et de ses opérateurs
description: Funds energy performance improvements and climate resilience upgrades for French state buildings and government agency facilities.
beneficiaries: French government through energy-efficient and resilient state buildings

name: Plan de relance
description: Funded France's national economic recovery plan (France Relance) following the COVID-19 pandemic, covering green transition, competitiveness, and social cohesion.
beneficiaries: French economy, businesses, and workers during post-pandemic recovery

name: Prévention, sécurité sanitaire et offre de soins
description: Funds public health prevention, health security preparedness, and health care provision programs in France.
beneficiaries: French population through preventive health and health care access

name: Prêts du Trésor à des États étrangers en vue de faciliter la vente de biens et de services concourant au développement du commerce extérieur de la France
description: Provides French Treasury loans to foreign states to facilitate the purchase of French goods and services, promoting French export commerce.
beneficiaries: French exporters and recipient foreign governments

name: Prêts et avances pour le logement des agents de l'État
description: Provides loans and advances to French government employees for housing, helping civil servants secure accommodation.
beneficiaries: French civil servants seeking housing assistance

name: Prêts et avances à des services de l'État
description: Provides Treasury loans and advances to French government services for temporary financing needs and cash management.
beneficiaries: French government services requiring temporary financing

name: Prêts pour le développement économique et social
description: Provides French Treasury loans for economic and social development projects both domestically and in partner countries.
beneficiaries: Economic and social development initiatives

name: Prêts à FranceAgriMer au titre des préfinancements de fonds européens
description: Provides Treasury loans to FranceAgriMer for pre-financing European agricultural fund payments before EU reimbursement.
beneficiaries: FranceAgriMer and French farmers awaiting EU agricultural payments

name: Prêts à des États étrangers pour consolidation de dettes envers la France
description: Provides loans to foreign states to consolidate and restructure their debts owed to France through Paris Club agreements.
beneficiaries: Debtor nations restructuring obligations to France

name: Prêts à l'Agence française de développement en vue de favoriser le développement économique et social dans des États étrangers
description: Provides Treasury loans to the French Development Agency (AFD) for economic and social development programs in developing countries.
beneficiaries: Developing countries receiving AFD development assistance

name: Prêts à la société concessionnaire de la liaison express entre Paris et l'aéroport Paris-Charles de Gaulle
description: Provides loans to the concessionaire building the CDG Express, a direct rail link between Paris Gare de l'Est and Charles de Gaulle Airport.
beneficiaries: Air travelers and the Paris-CDG airport rail connection project

name: Recherche appliquée et innovation en agriculture
description: Funds applied agricultural research and innovation programs in France to improve farming practices, food quality, and agricultural competitiveness.
beneficiaries: French agricultural research institutions and farmers

name: Recherche et enseignement supérieur en matière économique et industrielle
description: Funds higher education and research in economic and industrial fields through French grandes écoles, universities, and research institutions.
beneficiaries: French students, researchers, and industrial innovation

name: Renforcement des fonds propres de l'Agence française de développement
description: Strengthens the equity capital of the French Development Agency to expand its capacity for development lending and investment.
beneficiaries: AFD's development mission and beneficiary countries

name: Restitution des « biens mal acquis »
description: Manages the restitution of illicitly acquired assets confiscated in France from foreign leaders, returning proceeds to the populations of origin.
beneficiaries: Populations of countries whose leaders' ill-gotten assets were confiscated in France

name: Reversement à la sécurité sociale des recettes de la Facilité pour la Relance et la Résilience (FRR) européenne au titre du volet « Ségur investissement » du plan national de relance et de résilience (PNRR)
description: Transfers European Recovery and Resilience Facility funds to the French social security system for the Ségur health investment component of the national recovery plan.
beneficiaries: French social security and health care infrastructure

name: Régimes de retraite des mines, de la SEITA, et divers
description: Funds pension obligations for former employees of French mines, the SEITA (state tobacco monopoly), and other legacy public enterprises.
beneficiaries: Retirees of French mining, SEITA, and other legacy state enterprises

name: Régimes de retraite et de sécurité sociale des marins
description: Funds the special retirement and social security system for French merchant mariners and fishermen (ENIM).
beneficiaries: French seafarers, fishermen, and their families

name: Soutien des progrès de l'enseignement et de la recherche
description: Supports progress in French education and research through institutional grants and pedagogical innovation programs.
beneficiaries: French educational institutions and researchers

name: Structures et dispositifs de sécurité routière
description: Funds road safety infrastructure, equipment, and enforcement systems in France including signage, barriers, and safety assessments.
beneficiaries: French road users through improved road safety infrastructure

name: Transports aériens, surveillance et certification
description: Funds French civil aviation oversight including air transport regulation, aircraft surveillance, and airworthiness certification by the DGAC.
beneficiaries: Air travelers, airlines, and aviation safety in France

name: Édition et diffusion
description: Funds official French government publishing and dissemination services including the Journal Officiel and public information distribution.
beneficiaries: French citizens and public access to official government publications

name: 400 Years of African-American History Commission
description: Established by the 400 Years of African American History Commission Act to plan and coordinate the 400th anniversary commemoration of the arrival of Africans in the English colonies in 1619. The commission developed programs, projects, and activities to acknowledge the impact of slavery and African-American contributions to the United States.
beneficiaries: General public, African-American communities, educational institutions, cultural organizations

name: 9-11 Response and Biometric Exit Account, U.S. Customs and Border Protection, Homeland Security
description: Funds the development and implementation of a biometric entry-exit tracking system at U.S. ports of entry, as mandated by post-9/11 security legislation. Supports border security technology to verify the departure of foreign nationals from the United States.
beneficiaries: U.S. Customs and Border Protection, general public through enhanced national security

name: APEC Business Travel Card Account, Customs and Border Protection, Homeland Security
description: Finances the issuance and management of APEC Business Travel Cards, which allow pre-cleared business travelers expedited entry into APEC member economies. Funded through user fees collected from applicants.
beneficiaries: U.S. business travelers engaged in trade with APEC member countries

name: Abandoned Mine Reclamation Fund, Office of Surface Mining Reclamation and Enforcement
description: Created by the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 to reclaim and restore land and water resources adversely affected by past coal mining operations. Funded by a fee assessed on current coal production.
beneficiaries: Communities near abandoned coal mines, state and tribal reclamation programs, environmental restoration efforts

name: Access Board
description: An independent federal agency that promotes equality for people with disabilities through the development of accessibility guidelines and standards for the built environment, transportation, communication, and information technology. Originally established as the Architectural and Transportation Barriers Compliance Board.
beneficiaries: People with disabilities, architects, designers, federal agencies, and the general public

name: Acquisition Workforce Training Fund, General Services Administration
description: Provides resources for the recruitment, hiring, and training of the federal acquisition workforce to improve the government's ability to manage contracts and procurement. Funded by a portion of fees collected from federal contracts.
beneficiaries: Federal acquisition professionals, government agencies seeking improved procurement capabilities

name: Acquisition of Lands for National Forests, Special Acts, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Supports the purchase of privately held lands within or adjacent to National Forest boundaries under the authority of the Weeks Act and subsequent legislation. Protects watersheds, wildlife habitat, and public recreation resources.
beneficiaries: General public, recreational users, conservation interests, rural communities near national forests

name: Acquisition of Lands to Complete Land Exchanges, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Funds the equalization payments needed to complete land exchanges between the Forest Service and private or state landowners. Facilitates the consolidation of federal land ownership within national forest boundaries.
beneficiaries: Forest Service, private landowners engaged in exchanges, conservation interests

name: Administration of Rights-of-Way and Other Land Uses Fund, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Covers costs associated with processing and administering permits, easements, and rights-of-way across National Forest System lands. Funded by fees paid by applicants seeking to use forest land for roads, pipelines, power lines, and similar infrastructure.
beneficiaries: Utility companies, infrastructure developers, state and local governments requiring access across federal forest lands

name: Administrative Conference of the U.S.
description: An independent federal agency that studies the efficiency, adequacy, and fairness of administrative procedures used by federal agencies and recommends improvements. Issues recommendations to improve government operations and regulatory processes.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies, regulated industries, citizens interacting with federal administrative processes

name: Administrative Expenses, Children's Health Insurance Program, Social Security Administration
description: Funds the administrative costs associated with operating the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP), which provides health coverage to eligible children in families with incomes too high to qualify for Medicaid. Covers enrollment, outreach, and program management costs.
beneficiaries: Low-income children and families, state health agencies administering CHIP

name: Administrative Expenses, Federal Transit Administration, Transportation
description: Covers the salaries, expenses, and operational costs of the Federal Transit Administration in carrying out federal transit programs. Supports oversight of grants, technical assistance, and research for public transportation systems.
beneficiaries: Public transit agencies, commuters, and communities served by public transportation

name: Administrative Services Franchise Fund, Federal Aviation Administration, Transportation
description: A revolving fund that provides common administrative services to the Federal Aviation Administration on a fee-for-service basis. Covers shared services such as accounting, human resources, and information technology.
beneficiaries: FAA offices and programs receiving centralized administrative support

name: Administrative Support Offices, Management and Administration, Housing and Urban Development
description: Funds the internal management, administrative support, and overhead functions of the Department of Housing and Urban Development. Supports financial management, human resources, IT, procurement, and other departmental services.
beneficiaries: HUD program offices and their beneficiaries through more efficient program delivery

name: Administrative and Operating Expenses, Office of Risk Management, Agriculture
description: Covers the administrative costs of managing risk management programs under the Federal Crop Insurance Corporation and the Risk Management Agency. Supports the delivery of crop insurance products to American farmers.
beneficiaries: American farmers and ranchers participating in federal crop insurance programs

name: Advanced Manufacturing Investment Credit, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Provides a tax credit for investments in semiconductor manufacturing and related advanced manufacturing facilities in the United States, established by the CHIPS and Science Act. Incentivizes domestic production of semiconductors and related equipment.
beneficiaries: Semiconductor manufacturers, advanced manufacturing companies investing in U.S. production facilities

name: Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds high-risk, high-reward research and development of transformational energy technologies that are too early-stage for private sector investment. Modeled after DARPA to accelerate breakthroughs in clean energy.
beneficiaries: Energy researchers, technology startups, universities, national laboratories, and ultimately energy consumers

name: Advances from State Cooperating Agencies, Foreign Governments, and Other Federal Agencies, Federal Highway Administration, Transportation
description: Receives and manages advance payments from state agencies, foreign governments, and other federal agencies for highway-related cooperative work performed by the Federal Highway Administration. Enables collaborative infrastructure projects.
beneficiaries: State transportation departments, cooperating federal agencies, foreign governments with bilateral agreements

name: Advisory Council on Historic Preservation
description: An independent federal agency that promotes the preservation, enhancement, and productive use of the nation's historic resources. Advises the President and Congress on historic preservation matters and reviews federal projects affecting historic properties under Section 106 of the National Historic Preservation Act.
beneficiaries: Historic property owners, state and tribal historic preservation offices, communities with historic resources

name: Aeronautics, National Aeronautics and Space Administration
description: Funds NASA's aeronautics research programs to develop technologies that improve aircraft safety, efficiency, noise reduction, and environmental performance. Supports fundamental aeronautics research, integrated aviation systems, and advanced air vehicle programs.
beneficiaries: Aviation industry, commercial airlines, aircraft manufacturers, passengers, and the general public

name: African Development Foundation
description: An independent federal agency that invests in grassroots African enterprises and community-based organizations to promote economic development and self-sufficiency. Provides grants and technical assistance to small and medium enterprises across Sub-Saharan Africa.
beneficiaries: African entrepreneurs, small enterprises, community-based organizations in Sub-Saharan Africa

name: Agricultural Credit Insurance Fund Program Account, Farm Service Agency, Agriculture
description: Provides direct and guaranteed loans to farmers and ranchers who cannot obtain commercial credit at reasonable terms. Supports farm ownership loans, operating loans, and emergency loans to agricultural producers.
beneficiaries: Beginning farmers, socially disadvantaged farmers, family farmers unable to obtain commercial credit

name: Agricultural Credit Insurance Fund, Liquidating Account, Consolidated Farm Service Agency, Agriculture
description: Manages the portfolio of agricultural loans made before credit reform legislation (pre-1992), collecting repayments and managing defaults on legacy farm loans. Winds down the older portfolio of direct farm credit obligations.
beneficiaries: Farm borrowers with pre-1992 agricultural loans

name: Agricultural Disaster Relief Trust Fund, Farm Service Agency, Agriculture
description: Provides assistance to agricultural producers affected by natural disasters through supplemental revenue, livestock, and tree assistance programs. Funded by customs duties and established by the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008.
beneficiaries: Farmers and ranchers suffering crop, livestock, or tree losses from natural disasters

name: Agriculture Buildings and Facilities, Agriculture
description: Funds the construction, repair, improvement, and maintenance of buildings and facilities used by the Department of Agriculture. Covers capital projects at USDA research centers, offices, and laboratories.
beneficiaries: USDA employees, agricultural researchers, program delivery operations

name: Agriculture Wool Apparel Manufacturers Trust Fund, Farm Service Agency, Agriculture
description: Provides grants to domestic manufacturers of wool fabric, yarn, and apparel to offset the cost of purchasing domestically produced wool. Established to support the U.S. wool industry and domestic apparel manufacturing.
beneficiaries: Domestic wool apparel manufacturers, U.S. wool producers, textile industry workers

name: Air Carrier Worker Support, Departmental Offices, Department of Treasury
description: Provided payroll support payments to air carriers and contractors to protect the wages and benefits of airline workers during the COVID-19 pandemic. Established under the CARES Act and subsequent pandemic relief legislation.
beneficiaries: Airline employees, air carrier companies, airline contractors

name: Air and Marine Operations, U.S. Customs and Border Protection, Homeland Security
description: Funds CBP's air and marine interdiction and law enforcement operations to detect, interdict, and prevent the unlawful entry of people, narcotics, and other contraband. Operates aircraft and marine vessels along U.S. borders and coastal waters.
beneficiaries: General public through enhanced border security, law enforcement agencies

name: Airport Checkpoint Screening Fund, Transportation Security Administration, Homeland Security
description: Receives security fees collected from airline passengers to fund aviation security screening operations at airports. Supports TSA checkpoint screening personnel and equipment.
beneficiaries: Air travelers, airports, airlines, general public through aviation security

name: Airport Terminal Program, Federal Aviation Administration, Transportation
description: Provides competitive grants for airport terminal development projects including construction, renovation, and modernization of airport terminal facilities. Established through the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
beneficiaries: Airports, air travelers, airport workers, surrounding communities

name: Airport and Airway Trust Fund - Treasury Managed, Transportation
description: The primary funding mechanism for the federal aviation system, financed by aviation excise taxes on airline tickets, cargo, and aviation fuel. Supports FAA operations, airport improvement grants, and air traffic control modernization.
beneficiaries: Airports, airlines, air travelers, general aviation community, air traffic controllers

name: Alaska Resupply Program, Bureau of Indian Affairs
description: Funds the delivery of essential goods, fuel, and supplies to remote Alaska Native villages that are inaccessible by road. Ensures year-round resupply to communities dependent on barge, aircraft, or other non-road transportation.
beneficiaries: Alaska Native villages and communities in remote areas without road access

name: All Stations Accessibility Program, Federal Transit Administration, Transportation
description: Provides competitive grants for legacy transit agencies to upgrade older rail stations to meet ADA accessibility standards. Established by the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law to make public transit accessible to people with disabilities.
beneficiaries: Transit riders with disabilities, older adults, transit agencies with legacy rail stations

name: Allowances and Office Staff for Former Presidents, General Services Administration
description: Funds pensions, office space, staff, travel, and other allowances for former Presidents of the United States as authorized by the Former Presidents Act. Also provides funding for the transition of outgoing presidents.
beneficiaries: Former Presidents of the United States and their office staff

name: American Battle Monuments Commission
description: An independent federal agency that designs, constructs, maintains, and operates permanent American military cemeteries and memorials on foreign soil. Manages 26 overseas military cemeteries and 32 federal memorials, monuments, and markers.
beneficiaries: Families of fallen service members, veterans, the American public, foreign visitors

name: American Printing House for the Blind, Special Institutions for Persons with Disabilities, Education
description: Provides annual federal appropriations to the American Printing House for the Blind to produce and distribute educational materials in accessible formats for students who are blind or visually impaired. Supplies braille, large print, and audio materials to schools nationwide.
beneficiaries: Students who are blind or visually impaired, schools serving visually impaired students

name: American Sections, International Commissions, State
description: Funds the U.S. participation in international boundary and fisheries commissions, including the International Boundary Commission (U.S.-Canada) and various international fisheries commissions. Supports diplomatic and technical cooperation on shared natural resources.
beneficiaries: U.S.-Canada border communities, commercial fishing industries, international cooperation efforts

name: Appalachian Regional Development Programs, Appalachian Regional Commission
description: Funds specific economic and community development programs administered by the Appalachian Regional Commission, including highway construction, area development, and local development district assistance. Targets the most economically distressed areas within the Appalachian region.
beneficiaries: Appalachian communities, local development districts, state and local governments in the region

name: Armed Forces Retirement Home
description: An independent federal agency that provides residences and related services for certain retired and former members of the U.S. Armed Forces. Operates the Armed Forces Retirement Home facilities in Washington, D.C. and Gulfport, Mississippi.
beneficiaries: Eligible retired and former enlisted military members and warrant officers

name: Armed Forces Retirement Home Trust Fund, Armed Forces Retirement Home
description: The trust fund that finances operations of the Armed Forces Retirement Home, funded by monthly payroll deductions from enlisted members, fines, forfeitures, and investment income. Supports residential care, medical services, and facility maintenance.
beneficiaries: Residents of Armed Forces Retirement Home facilities, eligible military retirees

name: Assessment Funds, Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, Treasury
description: Collects assessments from national banks and federal savings associations to fund the operations of the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency. Entirely self-funded through bank examinations and assessments without congressional appropriations.
beneficiaries: National banks, federal savings associations, banking customers through regulatory oversight

name: Asset Proceeds and Space Management Fund, General Services Administration
description: Receives proceeds from the sale or exchange of surplus federal real property and uses them for acquisition, improvement, and management of federal space. Supports the efficient management of the federal real property portfolio.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies occupying government-owned or leased space

name: Assets Forfeiture Fund, Justice
description: Receives proceeds from the forfeiture of assets seized by federal law enforcement agencies in connection with criminal activity. Funds are used to support law enforcement operations, pay for asset management and disposal costs, and provide restitution to crime victims.
beneficiaries: Federal law enforcement agencies, crime victims receiving restitution, state and local law enforcement through equitable sharing

name: Assistance for the Independent States of the Former Soviet Union, Funds Appropriated to the President, United States Agency for International Development
description: Provides economic and democratic development assistance to the newly independent states following the dissolution of the Soviet Union under the FREEDOM Support Act. Supports market reforms, democratic governance, and civil society development.
beneficiaries: Citizens and institutions in former Soviet states undergoing democratic and economic transitions

name: Assistance to Shipyards, Maritime Administration, Transportation
description: Provides federal assistance for the modernization and improvement of U.S. shipyard facilities to maintain the domestic shipbuilding industrial base. Supports capital improvements and equipment upgrades at small and medium-sized shipyards.
beneficiaries: U.S. shipyards, shipyard workers, the domestic maritime industry

name: Assisted Housing Inspections and Risk Assessments, Public and Indian Housing, Housing & Urban Development
description: Funds inspections and risk assessments of HUD-assisted multifamily housing and public housing properties to ensure compliance with physical condition standards. Supports housing quality and safety for federally assisted tenants.
beneficiaries: Residents of HUD-assisted housing, public housing tenants, housing authorities

name: Aviation Insurance Revolving Fund, Transportation
description: Provides aviation insurance and reinsurance when commercial insurance is not available on reasonable terms for U.S. air carriers and aircraft. Activated during national emergencies or when the commercial insurance market cannot provide adequate coverage.
beneficiaries: U.S. air carriers, aircraft operators, aviation industry during emergencies or market disruptions

name: Aviation User Fees, Federal Aviation Administration, Transportation
description: Collects fees from operators of aircraft that are neither combatant nor combatant-support aircraft for air traffic control and related services provided by the FAA. Applies primarily to fees charged to foreign-registered aircraft.
beneficiaries: FAA operations, the national airspace system, general aviation safety

name: Barry Goldwater Scholarship and Excellence In Education Foundation
description: An independent federal agency that awards scholarships to outstanding college sophomores and juniors who intend to pursue careers in mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering. Honors Senator Barry Goldwater's commitment to science education.
beneficiaries: Undergraduate STEM students, future scientists, engineers, and mathematicians

name: Biomass Research and Development, National Institute of Food and Agriculture, Agriculture
description: Supports research and development of technologies for converting biomass into bioenergy, biofuels, and bioproducts. Coordinates federal biomass R&D efforts across agencies to advance the bioeconomy.
beneficiaries: Bioenergy researchers, agricultural producers, rural communities, energy consumers

name: Biorefinery Assistance Program Account, Rural Business - Cooperative Service, Agriculture
description: Provides loan guarantees for the development, construction, and retrofitting of commercial-scale biorefineries that produce advanced biofuels. Supports the development of domestic renewable fuel production capacity.
beneficiaries: Biorefinery developers, advanced biofuel producers, rural communities, energy consumers

name: Black Lung Disability Trust Fund - Treasury Managed, Labor
description: Provides compensation and medical benefits to coal miners totally disabled by pneumoconiosis (black lung disease) and their survivors when no responsible mine operator can be identified. Funded by an excise tax on coal.
beneficiaries: Disabled coal miners with black lung disease, surviving dependents of deceased miners

name: Blackfeet Water Settlement Implementation Fund, Bureau of Reclamation, Interior
description: Funds the implementation of the Blackfeet Water Rights Settlement Act, which resolved water rights claims of the Blackfeet Tribe in Montana. Supports the construction and rehabilitation of water infrastructure on the Blackfeet Reservation.
beneficiaries: Blackfeet Tribe, Blackfeet Reservation residents, Montana water users

name: Border Security Fencing, Infrastructure, and Technology, U.S. Customs and Border Protection, Homeland Security
description: Funds the construction and maintenance of border fencing, roads, lighting, and surveillance technology along U.S. land borders. Supports tactical infrastructure and technology deployments to secure the border.
beneficiaries: Border communities, Customs and Border Protection agents, general public through enhanced border security

name: Breach Bond/Detention Fund, Border and Transportation Security, Homeland Security
description: Collects and manages cash bonds and breach bond penalties from immigration cases where aliens fail to comply with conditions of release. Funds are used to support immigration detention and removal operations.
beneficiaries: Immigration enforcement operations, ICE detention programs

name: Broadband Connectivity Fund, National Telecommunications and Information Administration, Commerce
description: Provides funding to expand broadband internet access and adoption, particularly in underserved and unserved areas of the United States. Established under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.
beneficiaries: Underserved rural and urban communities lacking broadband access, internet service providers, digital inclusion programs

name: Broadcasting Capital Improvements, Broadcasting Board of Governors
description: Funded capital improvements and maintenance of broadcasting infrastructure used by U.S. international broadcasting entities including Voice of America and Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty. Supported transmission facilities, studios, and broadcast equipment.
beneficiaries: U.S. international broadcasting operations, foreign audiences of U.S. government-funded media

name: Buildings and Facilities, Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service, Agriculture
description: Funds the construction, renovation, and maintenance of laboratory and office facilities used by APHIS for animal and plant health inspection, veterinary diagnostics, and pest detection. Supports critical biosecurity infrastructure.
beneficiaries: APHIS employees, U.S. agricultural producers protected from pests and diseases

name: Buildings and Facilities, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Health and Human Services
description: Funds construction, renovation, and maintenance of CDC laboratories, research facilities, and office buildings. Supports infrastructure critical to public health surveillance, disease investigation, and emergency response.
beneficiaries: CDC researchers, the public health workforce, the general public through improved disease surveillance infrastructure

name: Buildings and Facilities, Environmental Protection Agency
description: Funds the construction, repair, and improvement of EPA laboratory and office facilities used for environmental research and regulatory operations. Supports the physical infrastructure necessary for environmental protection activities.
beneficiaries: EPA employees, environmental researchers, communities benefiting from environmental protection

name: Buildings and Facilities, Food and Drug Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Funds the construction, renovation, and improvement of FDA laboratories, offices, and testing facilities used for ensuring the safety of food, drugs, medical devices, and other regulated products. Supports modernization of FDA's physical infrastructure.
beneficiaries: FDA staff, consumers protected by FDA regulatory activities, regulated industries

name: Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection Fund, Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection
description: The primary funding account for the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, receiving transfers from the Federal Reserve System rather than congressional appropriations. Supports the bureau's consumer financial protection enforcement, regulation, and education activities.
beneficiaries: Consumers of financial products and services, particularly those vulnerable to unfair or abusive practices

name: Bureau of Reclamation Loans Program Account, Bureau of Reclamation, Interior
description: Provides direct and guaranteed loans for water infrastructure projects in the western United States, including dam safety, water recycling, and rural water supply systems. Supports Reclamation's mission of water resource management.
beneficiaries: Western U.S. water districts, municipalities, irrigators, rural water systems

name: Business Assistance Trust Fund, Small Business Administration
description: A trust fund within the Small Business Administration that provides assistance to small businesses, including advisory and counseling services. Supports small business development centers and entrepreneurial development programs.
beneficiaries: Small business owners, entrepreneurs, small business development centers

name: Business Loan and Investment Fund, Liquidating Account, Small Business Administration
description: Manages the portfolio of SBA business loans made before the Federal Credit Reform Act of 1990, collecting repayments and handling defaults on legacy loans. Winds down the pre-reform direct loan portfolio.
beneficiaries: Small businesses with pre-1992 SBA loans

name: Business Loans Program Account - Recovery Act, Small Business Administration
description: Provided enhanced small business lending under the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009, including increased SBA loan guarantees and reduced fees during the economic recovery period. A temporary stimulus-era expansion of SBA lending programs.
beneficiaries: Small businesses needing credit during the 2008-2009 economic downturn

name: Business Systems Modernization, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds the modernization and upgrade of the IRS's information technology systems and business processes to improve tax administration and taxpayer services. Supports large-scale IT projects and system replacements.
beneficiaries: Taxpayers through improved services, IRS operations through modernized systems

name: CDC Working Capital Fund, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Health and Human Services
description: A revolving fund that provides common administrative and support services to CDC programs on a fee-for-service basis. Covers centralized services such as information technology, financial management, and procurement.
beneficiaries: CDC programs and centers receiving shared administrative services

name: Cable Security Fleet, Maritime Administration, Transportation
description: Provides stipend payments to maintain a fleet of commercially viable, privately owned U.S.-flagged cable laying and maintenance vessels to ensure national security and critical undersea cable infrastructure capabilities. Modeled on the Maritime Security Program.
beneficiaries: U.S.-flagged cable ship operators, national security interests, undersea cable infrastructure

name: California Bay-Delta Restoration, Bureau of Reclamation, Interior
description: Funds federal participation in the CALFED Bay-Delta Program to restore the ecological health of the San Francisco Bay/Sacramento-San Joaquin River Delta while improving water supply reliability. Supports ecosystem restoration, water quality, and water use efficiency projects.
beneficiaries: California water users, Bay-Delta ecosystem, agricultural and urban water districts, endangered species

name: Capital Magnet Fund, Community Development Financial Institutions, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Provides competitively awarded grants to Community Development Financial Institutions and nonprofit housing organizations to attract private capital for affordable housing and community development. Funded through allocations from the GSEs (Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac).
beneficiaries: Low-income families needing affordable housing, CDFIs, nonprofit housing developers, underserved communities

name: Carbon Dioxide Transportation Infrastructure Finance and Innovation Program Account, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Provides loans, loan guarantees, and other financial assistance for the development of carbon dioxide transportation infrastructure including pipelines for carbon capture and storage. Established under the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
beneficiaries: Carbon capture and storage project developers, CO2 pipeline operators, communities benefiting from emissions reduction

name: Carson City Special Account, Bureau of Land Management, Interior
description: Receives proceeds from the sale of public lands in the Carson City, Nevada district under the Southern Nevada Public Land Management Act. Funds are used for capital improvements, conservation initiatives, and land acquisition in Nevada.
beneficiaries: Carson City and surrounding Nevada communities, conservation programs, public lands users

name: Census Working Capital Fund, Bureau of the Census, Commerce
description: A revolving fund that provides reimbursable statistical services and surveys to other federal agencies and entities on a cost-recovery basis. Allows the Census Bureau to conduct specialized surveys beyond its core demographic programs.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies needing statistical data, policymakers, researchers relying on Census-produced data

name: Centennial Challenge, National Park Service, Interior
description: A matching grant program that leverages private donations with federal funds to support signature projects and programs in the National Park System. Launched in conjunction with the National Park Service's centennial in 2016.
beneficiaries: National parks, park visitors, philanthropic partners, conservation organizations

name: Center for Middle Eastern-Western Dialogue Trust Fund, State
description: A trust fund that supports dialogue and exchange programs between the United States and Middle Eastern countries to promote mutual understanding. Funds educational and cultural exchange activities managed by the State Department.
beneficiaries: U.S. and Middle Eastern scholars, students, cultural organizations, diplomatic understanding

name: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Innovation, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid, Health and Human Services
description: Tests innovative payment and service delivery models to reduce health care costs while preserving or enhancing quality of care for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries. Established by the Affordable Care Act with authority to scale successful models nationwide.
beneficiaries: Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries, healthcare providers, the health care system through cost savings

name: Central Hazardous Materials Fund, Department-Wide Programs, Interior
description: Funds the assessment, cleanup, and remediation of hazardous materials contamination on Department of Interior lands. Addresses environmental contamination from legacy mining, industrial, and government activities.
beneficiaries: Communities near contaminated Interior lands, environmental restoration, public health protection

name: Central Utah Project Completion Account, Central Utah Project, Interior
description: Funds the completion of the Central Utah Project, a large-scale water development project that diverts water from the Colorado River basin to the Bonneville basin for municipal, industrial, and agricultural use. Authorized by the Central Utah Project Completion Act.
beneficiaries: Utah water users, municipalities, irrigators, environmental mitigation programs

name: Central Valley Project Restoration Fund, Bureau of Reclamation
description: Funds habitat restoration, fish and wildlife protection, and environmental mitigation activities associated with California's Central Valley Project. Financed by charges on CVP water and power users to offset environmental impacts of the project.
beneficiaries: Fish and wildlife populations in the Central Valley, environmental restoration, CVP water users

name: Cheyenne River Sioux Tribe Terrestrial Wildlife Habitat Restoration Trust Fund, Bureau of the Fiscal Service, Treasury
description: A trust fund established to compensate the Cheyenne River Sioux Tribe for terrestrial wildlife habitat losses caused by the construction of the Oahe Dam on the Missouri River. Funds wildlife habitat restoration on the reservation.
beneficiaries: Cheyenne River Sioux Tribe, wildlife habitat restoration on the reservation

name: Child Care Entitlement to States, Administration for Children and Families, Health and Human Services
description: Provides mandatory federal funding to states, territories, and tribes for child care subsidies for low-income families, particularly those transitioning from welfare to work. Established under the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act.
beneficiaries: Low-income working families, families transitioning off welfare, children receiving subsidized child care

name: Child Survival and Health Programs Fund, United States Agency for International Development
description: Funds international health programs focused on reducing child mortality, combating infectious diseases, and improving maternal health in developing countries. Supports USAID's global health initiatives including immunization, nutrition, and disease prevention.
beneficiaries: Children and mothers in developing countries, global health organizations, USAID partner countries

name: Child and Dependent Care Tax Credit, Internal Revenue Service, Department of the Treasury
description: Provides a tax credit to working families for expenses incurred for the care of qualifying children under age 13 or dependents who are physically or mentally incapable of self-care. Offsets a portion of child care costs to support workforce participation.
beneficiaries: Working parents and families with child care expenses, caregivers of disabled dependents

name: Children's Research and Technical Assistance, Administration for Children and Families, Health and Human Services
description: Funds research, evaluation, and technical assistance on programs serving children and families, including child welfare, Head Start, child care, and family support services. Supports evidence-based policy development for children's programs.
beneficiaries: Children and families served by ACF programs, researchers, program administrators, policymakers

name: Civilian Board of Contract Appeals, General Services Administration
description: An independent tribunal that hears and decides contract disputes between government contractors and civilian federal agencies under the Contract Disputes Act. Consolidated the various agency boards of contract appeals into one entity.
beneficiaries: Government contractors, civilian federal agencies involved in contract disputes

name: Claims and Treaty Obligations, Bureau of Indian Affairs (Indefinite)
description: Funds payments owed to Indian tribes and individuals pursuant to treaties, court judgments, and settlement agreements with the federal government. Covers obligations arising from historical agreements and legal claims.
beneficiaries: Indian tribes and individual tribal members with treaty rights and legal claims against the federal government

name: Claims for Damages (Indefinite), Bureau of the Fiscal Service, Treasury
description: Provides indefinite budget authority to pay claims against the federal government for damages caused by government activities, including tort claims and property damage. A permanent appropriation for settling civil claims.
beneficiaries: Individuals and entities with valid claims for damages caused by federal government actions

name: Coastal Impact Assistance, Fish and Wildlife Service, Interior
description: Provides financial assistance to oil and gas producing coastal states and their political subdivisions to mitigate the impacts of offshore energy development. Funded from federal offshore oil and gas revenues under the Energy Policy Act of 2005.
beneficiaries: Coastal states with offshore energy production, coastal communities, conservation programs

name: Coastal Wetlands Restoration Trust Fund, Corps of Engineers - Civil
