description: Funds the restoration and protection of coastal wetlands in Louisiana and other coastal states through the Coastal Wetlands Planning, Protection and Restoration Act. Financed by a portion of Sport Fish Restoration revenues.
beneficiaries: Coastal communities, Louisiana wetlands ecosystems, fisheries, storm protection beneficiaries

name: College Housing Loans, Education
description: A legacy program that provided low-interest federal loans to institutions of higher education for the construction of student housing and related facilities. No new loans have been made for decades; the account manages the remaining portfolio.
beneficiaries: Colleges and universities with outstanding federal housing loans, students in campus housing

name: College Housing and Academic Facilities Loans Program, Education
description: A legacy program that provided federal loans for the construction of academic facilities, libraries, and student housing at colleges and universities under the Higher Education Facilities Act. Manages the remaining loan portfolio from historical lending.
beneficiaries: Higher education institutions with outstanding federal facility loans

name: Colorado River Basins Power Marketing Fund, Western Area Power Administration, Power Marketing Administration, Energy
description: Manages the generation, transmission, and marketing of hydroelectric power from Colorado River dams including Hoover, Glen Canyon, and others. Revenues from power sales repay project construction costs and fund operations.
beneficiaries: Electric utilities and consumers in the western U.S., preference customers (municipalities, cooperatives)

name: Commission for the Preservation of America's Heritage Abroad
description: An independent federal agency that identifies and reports on cemeteries, monuments, and historic buildings in Eastern and Central Europe associated with the American heritage, particularly those of U.S. citizens' ancestral communities. Works with foreign governments to ensure preservation.
beneficiaries: Americans with ancestral heritage in Eastern and Central Europe, cultural preservation efforts

name: Commission of Fine Arts
description: An independent federal agency that advises the President, Congress, and federal agencies on matters of architecture, sculpture, painting, and other fine arts as they relate to the appearance of Washington, D.C. Reviews designs of federal buildings, monuments, coins, and medals.
beneficiaries: Washington D.C. residents, visitors, federal agencies, the public interest in the national capital's aesthetic character

name: Commission on Civil Rights
description: An independent, bipartisan federal agency that investigates complaints of discrimination, studies federal civil rights policy, and issues reports and recommendations to the President and Congress. Monitors federal civil rights enforcement.
beneficiaries: Individuals subject to discrimination, civil rights advocacy, policymakers

name: Committee for Purchase from People Who Are Blind or Severely Disabled
description: Oversees the AbilityOne Program, which directs federal agencies to purchase designated products and services from nonprofit agencies employing people who are blind or have significant disabilities. The largest source of employment for people who are blind or severely disabled in the United States.
beneficiaries: People who are blind or severely disabled, nonprofit agencies employing disabled workers

name: Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States Fund, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Collects filing fees from parties submitting transactions for national security review by CFIUS, established under the Foreign Investment Risk Review Modernization Act (FIRRMA). Funds CFIUS operations including review and investigation of foreign investments.
beneficiaries: National security interests, federal agencies participating in CFIUS reviews

name: Commodity Credit Corporation Direct Loans, Liquidating Account, Agriculture
description: Manages the legacy portfolio of direct loans made by the Commodity Credit Corporation prior to credit reform, including commodity price support loans and farm storage facility loans. Collects repayments on pre-1992 loans.
beneficiaries: Farmers with outstanding pre-reform CCC loans

name: Commodity Credit Corporation Export Loans Program Account, Foreign Agricultural Service, Agriculture
description: Provides export credit guarantees to promote U.S. agricultural exports by reducing financial risk for U.S. exporters and foreign buyers. Supports programs like the GSM-102 Export Credit Guarantee Program.
beneficiaries: U.S. agricultural exporters, foreign buyers of U.S. farm products, the U.S. agricultural sector

name: Commodity Futures Trading Commission Customer Protection Fund, Commodity Futures Trading Commission
description: Receives monetary sanctions and penalties collected by the CFTC from enforcement actions and uses them to fund whistleblower awards and customer education initiatives. Established by the Dodd-Frank Act.
beneficiaries: Whistleblowers reporting commodities fraud, market participants, investors through customer education

name: Community Development Financial Institutions Fund Program, Emergency Support, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Provided emergency financial support to Community Development Financial Institutions during the COVID-19 pandemic to maintain lending capacity in underserved communities. A temporary emergency supplement to the regular CDFI Fund program.
beneficiaries: CDFIs, low-income and minority communities served by CDFIs during the pandemic

name: Community Development Revolving Loan Fund, National Credit Union
description: Provides loans and technical assistance to low-income designated credit unions to increase the availability of financial services to underserved communities. Helps credit unions build capacity to serve low-income members.
beneficiaries: Low-income credit unions, underserved communities lacking access to financial services

name: Community Oriented Policing Services, Office of Justice Programs, Justice
description: Provides grants to state, local, and tribal law enforcement agencies to advance community policing strategies through hiring of officers, training, and technology. Known as the COPS program, established under the Violent Crime Control and Law Enforcement Act.
beneficiaries: State, local, and tribal law enforcement agencies, communities through improved public safety

name: Community Planning and Development, Program Office Salaries and Expenses, Housing and Urban Development
description: Funds the salaries and operational expenses of HUD's Office of Community Planning and Development, which administers community development and housing programs including CDBG, HOME, and homeless assistance. Supports program oversight and technical assistance.
beneficiaries: Communities receiving CPD grants, homeless populations, low-income residents

name: Compact of Free Association, Insular Affairs, Interior
description: Provides economic assistance and federal services to the Freely Associated States (Marshall Islands, Federated States of Micronesia, and Palau) under Compacts of Free Association. Supports education, health, infrastructure, and governance in these Pacific island nations.
beneficiaries: Citizens of the Marshall Islands, Federated States of Micronesia, and Palau

name: Compensation of the President, Executive Office of the President
description: Provides for the salary and compensation of the President of the United States as established by Article II of the Constitution. The current annual salary is $400,000, set by Congress in 2001.
beneficiaries: The President of the United States

name: Complex Crises Fund, Funds Appropriated to the President, US Agency for International Development
description: Provides flexible, rapid-response funding for emerging or unforeseen complex crises abroad, including political transitions, conflict situations, and humanitarian emergencies. Allows USAID to respond quickly without waiting for new appropriations.
beneficiaries: Populations in countries experiencing complex crises, conflict, or political transitions

name: Concessioner Improvement Accounts, National Park Service
description: Receives a portion of franchise fees paid by concession operators in national parks and uses funds for infrastructure improvements at concession facilities. Established by the National Parks Omnibus Management Act.
beneficiaries: National park visitors, park concession facility users, concession operators

name: Concessions Fees and Volunteer Services, Agricultural Research Service, Agriculture
description: Collects fees from concession activities on Agricultural Research Service properties and supports volunteer service programs at ARS research facilities. Funds minor improvements and visitor services at ARS sites.
beneficiaries: ARS research facilities, volunteers, visitors to agricultural research sites

name: Conditional Gift Fund, General, State
description: Receives conditional gifts and donations made to the Department of State for specific purposes designated by the donors. Allows the State Department to accept and expend private donations with conditions on their use.
beneficiaries: State Department programs and activities designated by donors

name: Conditional Gift Fund, Health Resources and Services Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Receives conditional gifts and donations to HRSA for health-related purposes specified by donors. Allows the agency to accept private funding for public health programs, research, and services.
beneficiaries: HRSA programs, underserved communities benefiting from donor-designated health initiatives

name: Connecting Minority Communities Fund, National Telecommunications and Information Administration, Commerce
description: Provides grants to Historically Black Colleges and Universities, Tribal Colleges and Universities, and Minority-Serving Institutions to purchase broadband internet access and equipment. Addresses the digital divide at minority-serving educational institutions.
beneficiaries: HBCUs, Tribal Colleges, Minority-Serving Institutions, students and faculty at these institutions

name: Construction, Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives, Justice
description: Funds the construction and major renovation of ATF laboratory facilities, offices, and other infrastructure. Supports ATF's law enforcement mission through modernized physical facilities.
beneficiaries: ATF personnel, law enforcement operations, public safety

name: Construction, Cemeterial Expenses, Army
description: Funds the construction, maintenance, and operation of Arlington National Cemetery and the Soldiers' and Airmen's Home National Cemetery. Supports burial operations, grave markers, and cemetery infrastructure.
beneficiaries: Families of deceased veterans and military personnel, visitors to national cemeteries

name: Construction, Drug Enforcement Administration, Justice
description: Funds construction and major renovation projects for DEA laboratories, offices, and operational facilities. Supports the physical infrastructure for federal drug law enforcement operations.
beneficiaries: DEA personnel, drug enforcement operations, public safety

name: Construction, Federal Bureau of Investigation, Justice
description: Funds construction, acquisition, and major renovation of FBI facilities including field offices, laboratories, and the training academy at Quantico. Supports the physical infrastructure for federal law enforcement and counterintelligence.
beneficiaries: FBI personnel, federal law enforcement operations, national security

name: Construction, Rehabilitation, Operation, and Maintenance, Western Area Power Administration, Power Marketing Administration, Energy
description: Funds the construction, rehabilitation, operation, and maintenance of high-voltage transmission systems operated by the Western Area Power Administration across 15 western states. Delivers hydroelectric power from federal dams to wholesale customers.
beneficiaries: Electric utilities, municipalities, cooperatives, and tribal entities purchasing federal hydropower in the western U.S.

name: Construction, United States Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Funds construction and major maintenance projects at national wildlife refuges, fish hatcheries, and other Fish and Wildlife Service facilities. Supports visitor centers, habitat restoration infrastructure, and operational facilities.
beneficiaries: Wildlife refuge visitors, fish and wildlife conservation, local communities near FWS facilities

name: Construction, United States Marshals Service, Justice
description: Funds construction and major renovation of U.S. Marshals Service facilities including courthouses, prisoner transport infrastructure, and witness security program facilities. Supports the operational needs of the nation's oldest federal law enforcement agency.
beneficiaries: U.S. Marshals Service personnel, the federal judicial system, protected witnesses

name: Consumer Financial Civil Penalty Fund, Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection
description: Receives civil penalties collected by the CFPB from enforcement actions against financial institutions for consumer protection violations. Funds are used to compensate victims of those violations or, if victims cannot be located, for consumer education and financial literacy.
beneficiaries: Victims of consumer financial protection violations, financial literacy programs, consumers

name: Consumer Operated and Oriented Plan Program Account, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, Health and Human Services
description: Provided loans to establish consumer-governed, nonprofit health insurance cooperatives (CO-OPs) under the Affordable Care Act. Most CO-OPs have since closed; the account manages remaining loan recoveries.
beneficiaries: Health insurance consumers in markets served by CO-OP plans (program largely wound down)

name: Contingency Fund for State Welfare Programs, Administration for Children and Families, Health and Human Services
description: Provides additional TANF (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families) funding to states experiencing economic downturns or increased welfare caseloads. Acts as a countercyclical buffer for state welfare programs during recessions.
beneficiaries: States with increased welfare needs, low-income families receiving TANF benefits

name: Continued Dumping and Subsidy Offset, United States Customs Service, Treasury
description: Distributed antidumping and countervailing duties collected on unfairly traded imports directly to affected domestic producers under the Byrd Amendment. The program was repealed in 2006 but the account managed remaining distributions.
beneficiaries: Domestic manufacturers harmed by dumped or subsidized imports (program repealed 2006)

name: Contract Support Costs, Indian Health Service, Health and Human Services
description: Reimburses Indian tribes and tribal organizations for administrative and overhead costs associated with operating healthcare programs under Indian Self-Determination Act contracts with the Indian Health Service.
beneficiaries: Indian tribes and tribal health organizations operating IHS programs under self-determination contracts

name: Contributed Funds, Geological Survey, Interior
description: Receives contributions from state, local, and other federal agencies and non-federal entities for cooperative scientific research and investigations conducted by the U.S. Geological Survey. Enables collaborative geological, hydrological, and biological research.
beneficiaries: Contributing agencies and organizations, scientific research community, public through improved geological knowledge

name: Contributed Funds, United States Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Receives contributions from non-federal sources for fish and wildlife conservation activities conducted cooperatively with the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service. Supports research, habitat management, and species conservation projects.
beneficiaries: Fish and wildlife conservation, contributing partners, the public through enhanced species protection

name: Contribution for Annuity Benefits, National Park Service, Interior
description: Funds the federal government's contribution toward retirement annuity benefits for eligible National Park Service employees, particularly those in law enforcement and firefighting positions with enhanced retirement provisions.
beneficiaries: National Park Service employees, particularly law enforcement rangers and firefighters

name: Contribution to the African Development Bank, Treasury
description: Funds the U.S. capital subscription and periodic replenishments to the African Development Bank, a multilateral development bank that finances economic and social development projects in Africa.
beneficiaries: African nations receiving development financing, U.S. interests in African economic development

name: Contribution to the Asian Development Fund, Treasury
description: Provides the U.S. contribution to the Asian Development Fund, the concessional lending facility of the Asian Development Bank that provides low-interest loans and grants to the poorest countries in Asia and the Pacific.
beneficiaries: Low-income countries in Asia and the Pacific, vulnerable populations in developing Asian nations

name: Contribution to the Clean Technology Fund, Treasury
description: Provides the U.S. contribution to the Clean Technology Fund, one of the Climate Investment Funds that finances large-scale clean energy projects in developing countries to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
beneficiaries: Developing countries investing in clean energy, global climate mitigation efforts

name: Contribution to the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development, Treasury
description: Funds the U.S. capital subscription to the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development, established to support the transition of former communist countries in Central and Eastern Europe to market economies.
beneficiaries: Transition economies in Central and Eastern Europe, private sector development in former communist states

name: Contribution to the International Fund for Agricultural Development, Treasury
description: Provides the U.S. contribution to IFAD, a specialized UN agency that finances agricultural development projects in the poorest rural areas of developing countries. Supports smallholder farmers, food security, and rural livelihoods.
beneficiaries: Rural poor in developing countries, smallholder farmers, food-insecure populations

name: Contribution to the Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency, Treasury
description: Funds the U.S. capital subscription to MIGA, a member of the World Bank Group that provides political risk insurance and credit enhancement to foreign investors in developing countries.
beneficiaries: Foreign investors in developing countries, developing nations seeking to attract foreign investment

name: Contributions for Highway Research Program, Federal Highway Administration, Transportation
description: Receives contributions from state highway agencies and other entities for cooperative highway research conducted by the Federal Highway Administration. Supports the Strategic Highway Research Program and related activities.
beneficiaries: State highway departments, transportation researchers, road users through improved highway technology

name: Contributions for Renewable Energy Impact Assessments and Mitigation, Defense
description: Receives contributions from renewable energy developers to fund assessments of potential impacts from renewable energy projects on military operations, including radar interference, and mitigation measures. Ensures military readiness while supporting clean energy development.
beneficiaries: Military operations potentially affected by renewable energy installations, renewable energy developers

name: Contributions to International Organizations, State
description: Funds U.S. assessed contributions to international organizations of which the United States is a member, including the United Nations, NATO, WHO, and dozens of other multilateral bodies. Covers mandatory membership dues.
beneficiaries: International organizations, global governance and cooperation, U.S. diplomatic interests

name: Contributions to the Cooperative Threat Reduction Program, Defense
description: Receives contributions from foreign governments and international organizations to supplement the Nunn-Lugar Cooperative Threat Reduction Program, which secures and dismantles weapons of mass destruction in former Soviet states.
beneficiaries: Global nonproliferation and security, former Soviet states, international security cooperation

name: Contributions to the International Monetary Fund Facilities and Trust Funds, Treasury
description: Funds U.S. contributions to IMF lending facilities and trust funds that provide financial assistance to member countries experiencing balance of payments difficulties. Supports global financial stability.
beneficiaries: IMF member countries facing economic crises, global financial stability, the international monetary system

name: Contributions, Education
description: Receives voluntary contributions and donations from non-federal sources for educational purposes administered by the Department of Education. Supports specific educational programs and initiatives designated by donors.
beneficiaries: Educational programs receiving donated funds, students and educators

name: Contributions, American Battle Monuments Commission
description: Receives private contributions and donations to the American Battle Monuments Commission for the construction, maintenance, and improvement of overseas military memorials and cemeteries. Supplements federal appropriations.
beneficiaries: Overseas military memorials and cemeteries, families of fallen service members

name: Contributions, Indian Health Facilities, Indian Health Services, Health and Human Services
description: Receives contributions from tribal governments and other sources for the construction, maintenance, and improvement of Indian health facilities. Supplements federal funding for healthcare infrastructure serving Native American communities.
beneficiaries: Indian tribes, Alaska Natives, Native American communities receiving health services

name: Cooperative Endangered Species Conservation Fund, from Land and Water Conservation Fund, U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Provides additional funding from the Land and Water Conservation Fund for cooperative endangered species conservation grants to states and territories. Supplements the primary Cooperative Endangered Species Conservation Fund for land acquisition and habitat conservation.
beneficiaries: Endangered species, state conservation programs, landowners participating in habitat conservation

name: Cooperative Research and Development Agreements, Centers for Disease Control, Health and Human Services
description: Manages cooperative research partnerships between CDC and private sector entities to develop and commercialize public health technologies, diagnostics, and treatments. Facilitates technology transfer from CDC laboratories.
beneficiaries: Private sector research partners, public health through commercialized CDC innovations

name: Cooperative Research and Development Agreements, Food and Drug Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Manages cooperative research partnerships between FDA and private sector entities to advance regulatory science, develop new analytical methods, and improve food and drug safety. Facilitates technology transfer and collaborative research.
beneficiaries: Pharmaceutical companies, food safety researchers, consumers through improved regulatory science

name: Cooperative Research and Development Agreements, National Institutes of Health, Health and Human Services
description: Manages cooperative research partnerships between NIH intramural scientists and private sector entities to advance biomedical research and bring discoveries to market. A key mechanism for NIH technology transfer.
beneficiaries: Biotechnology and pharmaceutical companies, patients through accelerated drug development, NIH researchers

name: Cooperative Work, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Receives deposits from cooperating entities for work performed by the Forest Service on behalf of states, counties, municipalities, and other organizations on National Forest System lands. Supports collaborative land management projects.
beneficiaries: Cooperating state and local agencies, forest management, communities near national forests

name: Coronavirus Capital Projects Fund, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Provides payments to states, territories, and tribal governments to carry out critical capital projects that enable work, education, and health monitoring in response to the COVID-19 pandemic. Focuses on broadband infrastructure and digital connectivity.
beneficiaries: State, territorial, and tribal governments, communities lacking broadband and digital infrastructure

name: Coronavirus Relief Fund, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Provided $150 billion in direct payments to state, local, and tribal governments under the CARES Act to cover expenditures incurred due to the COVID-19 public health emergency. One of the largest pandemic relief programs.
beneficiaries: State, local, and tribal governments, residents affected by COVID-19 pandemic

name: Corporation for National and Community Service
description: A federal agency (also known as AmeriCorps) that supports community service and volunteerism programs including AmeriCorps, Senior Corps, and the Social Innovation Fund. Engages Americans in service to address critical community needs.
beneficiaries: AmeriCorps members, community organizations, communities receiving volunteer services, seniors in Senior Corps

name: Council of the Inspectors General on Integrity and Efficiency
description: A coordinating body for the federal Inspectors General community, established by the Inspector General Reform Act. Promotes integrity, economy, and effectiveness across the federal government through coordination of IG activities.
beneficiaries: Federal Inspectors General, government accountability and oversight, taxpayers

name: Council on Environmental Quality and Office of Environmental Quality, Executive
description: Established by the National Environmental Policy Act, advises the President on environmental policy and oversees federal agency compliance with NEPA, including environmental impact assessments. Coordinates the federal government's environmental efforts.
beneficiaries: The environment, federal agencies implementing NEPA, the public through environmental protection oversight

name: Covered Countermeasure Process Fund, Health Resources and Services Administration, Health and Human Services
description: Funds the Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program, which provides benefits to individuals injured by covered countermeasures (vaccines, medications, devices) administered during a declared public health emergency. Established under the Public Readiness and Emergency Preparedness Act.
beneficiaries: Individuals injured by medical countermeasures during public health emergencies

name: Crash Data, National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, Transportation
description: Funds the collection, analysis, and dissemination of motor vehicle crash data through systems such as the Fatality Analysis Reporting System (FARS) and the National Automotive Sampling System. Supports evidence-based highway safety policy.
beneficiaries: Highway safety researchers, policymakers, vehicle manufacturers, the driving public

name: Creating Helpful Incentives to Produce Semiconductors (CHIPS) for America Fund, NIST, Commerce
description: Provides substantial direct funding to incentivize the construction, expansion, and modernization of semiconductor manufacturing facilities in the United States under the CHIPS and Science Act. Includes funding for manufacturing incentives and R&D programs.
beneficiaries: Semiconductor manufacturers, U.S. technology sector, national security, manufacturing workers

name: Crime Victims Fund, Justice
description: Collects fines, penalties, and forfeited bonds from convicted federal offenders and distributes funds to state victim compensation and assistance programs under the Victims of Crime Act (VOCA). One of the largest funding sources for crime victim services.
beneficiaries: Crime victims, state victim compensation programs, domestic violence shelters, child advocacy centers

name: Customs and Border Protection Services at User Fee Facilities, U.S. Customs and Border Protection, Homeland Security
description: Provides customs, immigration, and agricultural inspection services at designated user fee facilities (airports, seaports, and land ports of entry) that reimburse CBP for the cost of services. Funded through user fees from facility operators.
beneficiaries: International travelers, importers, facility operators at user-fee ports of entry

name: Customs and Border Protection, Customs User Fees Account, USCS, Homeland Security
description: Collects user fees from commercial merchandise processing at U.S. ports of entry to fund customs inspections, trade enforcement, and border security operations. Known as the Merchandise Processing Fee account.
beneficiaries: Importers and exporters through trade facilitation, CBP operations, national security

name: Customs and Border Protection, Immigration User Fees, Border and Transportation Security, Homeland Security
description: Collects inspection fees from international air and sea passengers and commercial vessels to fund immigration inspection services at U.S. ports of entry. Supports CBP immigration officer staffing and operations.
beneficiaries: International travelers, CBP immigration inspection operations

name: Customs and Border Protection, Land Border Inspection Fees, Border and Transportation Security, Homeland Security
description: Collects fees for dedicated commuter lane and SENTRI/NEXUS trusted traveler programs at land border ports of entry with Mexico and Canada. Funds expedited processing for pre-approved low-risk travelers.
beneficiaries: Frequent border crossers, trusted traveler program participants, land border communities

name: Customs and Border Protection, Transfer and Expenses of Operation, Puerto Rico, USCS, Homeland Security
description: Manages customs operations and the transfer of customs duties collected in Puerto Rico, which are returned to the Puerto Rican treasury under federal law. Funds CBP operations on the island.
beneficiaries: Puerto Rico treasury (receiving customs revenue), businesses and travelers in Puerto Rico

name: Cyber Security Initiatives, Office of the Secretary, Transportation
description: Funds cybersecurity programs and initiatives to protect the Department of Transportation's information systems and the broader transportation infrastructure from cyber threats. Supports network defense, vulnerability assessment, and incident response.
beneficiaries: Transportation systems and infrastructure, DOT information systems, the traveling public

name: Cybersecurity Enhancement Account, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Funds cybersecurity improvements and modernization of information technology security at the Department of the Treasury. Protects financial systems, taxpayer data, and critical Treasury infrastructure from cyber threats.
beneficiaries: Treasury Department operations, taxpayers whose data is protected, the financial system

name: DOD-VA Health Care Sharing Incentive Fund, Veterans Affairs
description: Provides incentive funding to encourage health care resource sharing between the Department of Defense and Department of Veterans Affairs. Supports joint ventures, shared medical facilities, and coordinated care delivery.
beneficiaries: Veterans and active-duty military receiving healthcare, VA and DoD medical facilities

name: Dairy Indemnity Program, Farm Service Agency, Agriculture
description: Provides indemnity payments to dairy farmers and manufacturers of dairy products whose milk has been contaminated by pesticides, chemicals, or nuclear radiation through no fault of their own. Protects dairy farmers from losses due to contamination events.
beneficiaries: Dairy farmers and dairy product manufacturers affected by contamination, consumers through food safety protection

name: Damage Assessment and Restoration Revolving Fund, Agriculture
description: Funds the assessment of natural resource damages and restoration of resources on USDA-managed lands injured by hazardous substance releases, oil spills, or other environmental incidents. Recovers costs from responsible parties.
beneficiaries: Natural resources on agricultural lands, communities affected by environmental contamination

name: Damage Assessment and Restoration Revolving Fund, Environmental Protection Agency
description: Funds EPA's natural resource damage assessment activities and environmental restoration following hazardous substance releases or oil spills. Recovered funds from responsible parties are recycled for additional restoration work.
beneficiaries: Damaged ecosystems, communities affected by hazardous releases, environmental restoration

name: Damage Assessment and Restoration Revolving Fund, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, Commerce
description: Funds NOAA's assessment and restoration of natural resources injured by oil spills, hazardous substance releases, and vessel groundings in coastal and marine environments. Recovers costs from responsible parties for reinvestment in restoration.
beneficiaries: Coastal and marine ecosystems, fisheries, coastal communities, marine wildlife

name: Data-Driven Innovation, Funds Appropriated to the President, Executive Office of the President
description: Funded cross-agency initiatives to leverage government data and analytics to improve decision-making, service delivery, and operational efficiency across the federal government. Supported open data and evidence-based policymaking.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies, the public through improved government services, data-driven policymaking

name: Debt Collection Fund, Office of the Secretary, Health and Human Services
description: Funds debt collection activities for debts owed to the Department of Health and Human Services, including overpayments from Medicare, Medicaid, and other HHS programs. Supports recovery of improperly spent federal health funds.
beneficiaries: HHS programs through recovered funds, taxpayers through reduced improper payments

name: Debt Restructuring, Treasury
description: Manages the restructuring of bilateral official debt owed by developing countries to the United States, including debt reduction and rescheduling agreements negotiated through the Paris Club. Supports international development through debt relief.
beneficiaries: Heavily indebted developing countries, international debt relief efforts

name: Defense Emergency Response Fund, Defense
description: Provides rapid-response funding for the Department of Defense to address unforeseen military emergencies and contingency operations. Allows DoD to fund urgent operational needs before regular appropriations are available.
beneficiaries: U.S. military operations, troops deployed in emergency situations, national defense

name: Defense Nuclear Facilities Safety Board
description: An independent federal agency that provides technical oversight of nuclear weapons facilities operated by the Department of Energy. Reviews safety standards and practices at DOE defense nuclear facilities to protect public health and safety.
beneficiaries: Workers at DOE nuclear facilities, communities near defense nuclear sites, public safety

name: Defense Production Act Program Account, Defense
description: Funds the Defense Production Act Title III program, which provides loans, loan guarantees, purchase commitments, and other incentives to develop and maintain domestic industrial capabilities critical to national defense.
beneficiaries: Defense industrial base, manufacturers of defense-critical materials and components, national security

name: Delaware Water Gap Route 209 Operations, National Park Service
description: Funds the operation and maintenance of Route 209 through the Delaware Water Gap National Recreation Area in Pennsylvania and New Jersey. Covers road maintenance, safety improvements, and traffic management on this federally managed highway.
beneficiaries: Motorists using Route 209, visitors to Delaware Water Gap National Recreation Area, local communities

name: Delta Regional Authority
description: A federal-state partnership that promotes economic development in the Mississippi River Delta region spanning 252 counties across eight states from Illinois to Louisiana. Invests in infrastructure, workforce development, and community improvement in distressed areas.
beneficiaries: Residents of the Delta region, particularly economically distressed communities in the lower Mississippi River area

name: Denali Commission
description: An independent federal agency that provides critical infrastructure and economic development support to remote communities in Alaska, particularly Alaska Native villages. Addresses the unique infrastructure challenges of rural Alaska.
beneficiaries: Remote Alaska communities, Alaska Native villages, rural Alaskans

name: Denali Commission Trust Fund
description: The trust fund that finances the Denali Commission's operations and grants for infrastructure, energy, health care, and economic development in rural Alaska. Receives deposits from federal agencies and other sources.
beneficiaries: Rural Alaska communities, Alaska Native villages, the Denali Commission's grant programs

name: Department of Defense Acquisition Workforce Development Account, Defense
description: Funds the recruitment, training, and retention of the Department of Defense acquisition workforce to improve management of defense contracts and procurement. Finances education, training, and strategic human capital initiatives.
beneficiaries: DoD acquisition professionals, defense contracting operations, taxpayers through improved procurement

name: Department of Defense Base Closure Account 2005, Defense
description: Funds the implementation of military base closure and realignment decisions from the 2005 Base Realignment and Closure (BRAC) round. Covers environmental cleanup, facility construction, and property disposal costs.
beneficiaries: Military communities affected by BRAC 2005, receiving communities, environmental cleanup

name: Department of Defense Base Closure Account, Defense
description: Funds the implementation of military base closures and realignments from the 1988, 1991, 1993, and 1995 BRAC rounds. Covers ongoing environmental remediation, caretaker costs, and property conveyance at closed bases.
beneficiaries: Communities near closed military bases, environmental remediation, redevelopment authorities

name: Department of Defense Family Housing Improvement Fund, Defense
description: Provides alternative financing mechanisms for military family housing through public-private partnerships under the Military Housing Privatization Initiative. Allows DoD to leverage private capital to improve on-base family housing.
beneficiaries: Military families living in on-base housing, private housing partners

name: Department of Defense General Gift Fund, Defense
description: Receives gifts, bequests, and donations from individuals and organizations for the benefit of the Department of Defense, its personnel, and their dependents. Used for morale, welfare, and recreation purposes.
beneficiaries: Military personnel and their families, DoD morale, welfare, and recreation programs

name: Department of Defense Military Unaccompanied Housing Improvement Fund, Defense
description: Provides funding for public-private partnerships to construct, renovate, and maintain unaccompanied military housing (barracks and dormitories) for service members without dependents. Mirrors the family housing privatization initiative.
beneficiaries: Single military service members and those stationed without families

name: Department of Defense Rapid Prototyping Fund, Defense
description: Funds the rapid prototyping and fielding of innovative technologies to address emerging military threats. Accelerates the transition of promising technologies from development to operational use.
beneficiaries: U.S. military forces, defense technology developers, national defense capabilities

name: Department of Defense Vietnam War Commemoration Fund, Defense
description: Receives donations to support the 50th Anniversary Vietnam War Commemoration program honoring Vietnam veterans and their families. Funds commemorative events, outreach, and recognition activities.
beneficiaries: Vietnam War veterans and their families, the public through historical remembrance

name: Department of Defense, Forest Products Program, Army
description: Manages timber sales and forest products on military installations, with proceeds used for reforestation, road maintenance, and natural resource management on Army lands. Supports sustainable forestry on military bases.
beneficiaries: Military installations, timber purchasers, natural resource management on Army lands

name: Department of Education Nonrecurring Expenses Fund
description: Receives unobligated balances from expired Department of Education appropriations and repurposes them for information technology infrastructure, acquisition of capital equipment, and facility improvements. Reduces waste from expired but unspent funds.
beneficiaries: Department of Education operations, IT modernization, administrative efficiency

name: Department of Veterans Affairs Cemetery Gift Fund, Veterans Affairs
description: Receives gifts, bequests, and donations for the benefit of VA national cemeteries, including cemetery beautification, memorial programs, and historic preservation. Supplements appropriated funds for cemetery operations.
beneficiaries: Veterans' families, national cemetery visitors, cemetery beautification and preservation

name: Department of the Air Force General Gift Fund, Air Force
description: Receives gifts, bequests, and donations from individuals and organizations for the benefit of Air Force and Space Force personnel, their dependents, and morale, welfare, and recreation programs.
beneficiaries: Air Force and Space Force personnel and their families, morale and welfare programs

name: Department of the Army General Gift Fund, Army
description: Receives gifts, bequests, and donations from individuals and organizations for the benefit of Army personnel, their dependents, and morale, welfare, and recreation programs.
beneficiaries: Army personnel and their families, morale, welfare, and recreation programs

name: Department of the Navy General Gift Fund, Navy
description: Receives gifts, bequests, and donations from individuals and organizations for the benefit of Navy and Marine Corps personnel, their dependents, and morale, welfare, and recreation programs.
beneficiaries: Navy and Marine Corps personnel and their families, morale and welfare programs

name: Department-Wide Systems and Capital Investments Programs, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Funds major information technology investments and capital improvement projects that serve multiple bureaus and offices across the Department of the Treasury. Supports enterprise-wide IT modernization and infrastructure.
beneficiaries: Treasury Department bureaus, taxpayers through improved government efficiency and services

name: Department-Wide Technology Investments, Departmental Management, Intelligence, Situational Awareness, and Oversight, Homeland Security
description: Funds enterprise-wide information technology investments across the Department of Homeland Security, including shared IT infrastructure, cybersecurity, and data analytics capabilities. Supports departmental integration and information sharing.
beneficiaries: DHS component agencies, national security and homeland security operations

name: Departmental Operations, Land and Water Conservation Fund, Departmental Offices, Interior
description: Funds the Department of the Interior's administration of the Land and Water Conservation Fund, including land acquisition for federal conservation purposes and grants to states for outdoor recreation. Supported by offshore oil and gas revenues.
beneficiaries: Public lands, state and local parks, outdoor recreation, conservation

name: Detention and Removal Operations, U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, Homeland Security
description: Funds ICE's detention of aliens in removal proceedings and the execution of removal orders for those found to be unlawfully present or deportable. Covers detention facility operations, transportation, and removal flights.
beneficiaries: Immigration enforcement operations, public safety through removal of deportable individuals

name: Digital Equity, National Telecommunications and Information Administration, Commerce
description: Funds programs to promote digital equity and inclusion by ensuring all individuals and communities have the skills, technology, and capacity needed to benefit from broadband internet. Established under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.
beneficiaries: Underserved communities, digital literacy programs, populations on the wrong side of the digital divide

name: Disaster Loan Fund, Liquidating Account, Small Business Administration
description: Manages the legacy portfolio of SBA disaster loans made before the Federal Credit Reform Act of 1990. Collects repayments and handles defaults on pre-reform disaster loans to homeowners, renters, and businesses.
beneficiaries: Disaster-affected homeowners, renters, and businesses with pre-1992 SBA disaster loans

name: Disposal of Department of Defense Real Property, Defense
description: Manages the disposal and conveyance of excess and surplus military real property, including land, buildings, and facilities no longer needed by the Department of Defense. Facilitates property transfer for community redevelopment.
beneficiaries: Communities receiving former military properties, redevelopment authorities, DoD through cost avoidance

name: Distance Learning, Telemedicine and Broadband Program, Rural Utilities Service, Agriculture
description: Provides grants and loans to rural communities for distance learning and telemedicine technologies, and broadband infrastructure. Connects rural schools, libraries, and healthcare facilities with advanced telecommunications.
beneficiaries: Rural students, rural healthcare patients, rural communities lacking broadband access

name: District of Columbia Crime Victims Compensation Fund, District of Columbia Courts
description: Provides financial compensation to victims of violent crime in the District of Columbia for unreimbursed medical expenses, lost wages, and other costs resulting from criminal victimization. Administered by the D.C. courts.
beneficiaries: Victims of violent crime in the District of Columbia

name: District of Columbia Federal Pension Fund, Treasury
description: Manages the federal government's assumption of pension liabilities for District of Columbia police, firefighters, teachers, and judges hired before July 1, 1997. Established when the federal government took over pre-existing D.C. pension obligations.
beneficiaries: Retired D.C. police, firefighters, teachers, and judges hired before 1997

name: District of Columbia Judicial Retirement and Survivor's Annuity Fund, Treasury
description: Funds retirement annuities and survivor benefits for judges of the District of Columbia courts. Managed by Treasury as part of the federal government's financial responsibilities for D.C. governance.
beneficiaries: Retired D.C. judges and their survivors

name: Domestic Trafficking Victims' Fund, Office of Justice Programs, Justice
description: Provides funding for services and assistance to domestic victims of human trafficking, including housing, legal aid, mental health services, and case management. Funded by special assessments on convicted sex traffickers and purchasers of commercial sex.
beneficiaries: Victims of domestic human trafficking, organizations providing anti-trafficking services

name: Donations for Forest and Rangeland Research, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Receives donations from individuals, corporations, and other entities for forest and rangeland research conducted by the Forest Service's Research and Development branch. Supplements federal research funding with private contributions.
beneficiaries: Forest and rangeland research, Forest Service scientists, forest management practices

name: Donations, Advisory Council on Historic Preservation
description: Receives voluntary donations to the Advisory Council on Historic Preservation for the promotion of historic preservation activities. Supplements appropriated funds for the council's operations and programs.
beneficiaries: Historic preservation programs, the Advisory Council's mission

name: Donations, National Park Service
description: Receives and manages donations of money, land, and other assets given to the National Park Service for the benefit of the national park system. Supports specific park units, programs, and conservation activities as designated by donors.
beneficiaries: National parks, park visitors, conservation and preservation efforts

name: Donations, National Science Foundation
description: Receives donations from individuals, corporations, and foundations to support scientific research and education programs administered by the National Science Foundation. Supplements federal research funding.
beneficiaries: Scientific research, NSF programs, researchers, students

name: Dual Benefits Payments Account, Railroad Retirement Board
description: Funds the "windfall" dual benefit payments to retired railroad workers who qualified for both railroad retirement and Social Security benefits based on work in both the railroad industry and covered employment. A legacy obligation being paid down.
beneficiaries: Retired railroad workers eligible for both railroad retirement and Social Security benefits

name: EEOC Education, Technical Assistance and Training Revolving Fund, Equal Employment Opportunity Commission
description: A revolving fund that provides education, technical assistance, and training programs on equal employment opportunity laws on a fee-for-service basis. Supports employer compliance training and anti-discrimination education.
beneficiaries: Employers seeking EEO compliance training, employees, HR professionals

name: Early Retiree Reinsurance Program, Office of the Secretary, Health and Human Services
description: Provided temporary reinsurance to employer-sponsored health plans covering early retirees (aged 55-64) to help reduce premium costs before ACA health insurance exchanges became operational. The program is fully expended.
beneficiaries: Early retirees aged 55-64, employer-sponsored health plans covering retirees (program exhausted)

name: East-West Center, State
description: Funds the East-West Center in Honolulu, Hawaii, which promotes understanding and cooperation among the peoples and nations of Asia, the Pacific, and the United States through research, education, and dialogue programs.
beneficiaries: Scholars, students, and policymakers from the U.S. and Asia-Pacific region

name: Economic Development Assistance Programs, Economic Development Administration, Commerce
description: Provides grants for public works, economic adjustment, planning, and technical assistance to economically distressed communities through the Economic Development Administration. Supports infrastructure, innovation, and regional economic diversification.
beneficiaries: Economically distressed communities, regional development organizations, local governments

name: Economic Research Service, Agriculture
description: Funds the operations of the USDA's Economic Research Service, which conducts economic analysis and research on agriculture, food, natural resources, and rural development. Provides data and analysis to inform farm and food policy.
beneficiaries: Agricultural policymakers, farmers, food industry, researchers, rural communities

name: Economic Stabilization Program Account, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Managed the Troubled Asset Relief Program (TARP) and related financial stabilization programs during the 2008 financial crisis. Authorized under the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act to purchase troubled assets and inject capital into financial institutions.
beneficiaries: Financial system stability, banks and financial institutions during the crisis, the broader economy

name: Election Security Grants, Election Assistance Commission
description: Provides federal grants to states to improve the administration and security of federal elections, including upgrading voting equipment, implementing post-election audits, and enhancing cybersecurity of election infrastructure.
beneficiaries: State and local election administrators, voters, election infrastructure security

name: Elective Payment for Energy Property and Electricity Produced from Certain Renewable Resources, Etc., Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Provides direct elective payments (direct pay) to tax-exempt entities, state and local governments, and other eligible organizations for clean energy tax credits under the Inflation Reduction Act. Allows entities without tax liability to benefit from energy credits.
beneficiaries: Tax-exempt organizations, state and local governments, tribal entities investing in clean energy

name: Electric or Low-Emitting Ferry Program, Federal Transit Administration, Transportation
description: Provides competitive grants for the purchase of electric or low-emitting ferry vessels and related charging or fueling infrastructure. Established by the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law to reduce emissions from ferry operations.
beneficiaries: Ferry operators, coastal and island communities, the environment through reduced maritime emissions

name: Electronic System for Travel Authorization, U.S. Customs and Border Protection, Homeland Security
description: Operates the ESTA system that screens Visa Waiver Program travelers before they board U.S.-bound flights or vessels. Collects travel promotion fees to fund the system and Brand USA tourism marketing.
beneficiaries: Visa Waiver Program travelers, U.S. border security, tourism promotion

name: Emergencies in the Diplomatic and Consular Service, State
description: Provides emergency funding for unforeseen diplomatic and consular emergencies, including evacuation of U.S. citizens abroad, emergency security at diplomatic posts, and emergency assistance to Americans overseas.
beneficiaries: U.S. citizens abroad needing emergency assistance, diplomatic personnel, consular operations

name: Emergency Capital Investment Fund, Departmental Offices, Treasury
description: Provides capital investments in Community Development Financial Institutions and Minority Depository Institutions to support lending in low- and moderate-income and minority communities. Established under the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2021.
beneficiaries: CDFIs, Minority Depository Institutions, low-income and minority communities, small businesses

name: Emergency Citrus Disease Research and Development Trust Fund, National Institute of Food and Agriculture, Agriculture
description: Funds emergency research to combat citrus greening disease (Huanglongbing), which threatens the U.S. citrus industry. Supports development of disease-resistant citrus varieties, treatments, and management strategies.
beneficiaries: Citrus growers, the citrus industry, agricultural researchers, consumers

name: Emergency EIDL Grants, Small Business Administration
description: Provided emergency Economic Injury Disaster Loan (EIDL) advance grants to small businesses and nonprofits experiencing economic harm from the COVID-19 pandemic. Grants of up to $10,000 did not need to be repaid.
beneficiaries: Small businesses, nonprofits, and sole proprietors affected by the COVID-19 pandemic

name: Emergency Forest Restoration Program, Farm Service Agency, Agriculture
description: Provides cost-share payments to private forest landowners for emergency restoration of forest resources damaged by natural disasters such as wildfires, hurricanes, and ice storms. Helps restore non-industrial private forest lands.
beneficiaries: Private forest landowners whose forests were damaged by natural disasters

name: Emergency Homeowner's Relief Fund, Housing Programs, Housing and Urban Development
description: Provided emergency assistance to homeowners at risk of foreclosure due to involuntary unemployment or underemployment, covering mortgage payments, insurance, and taxes. Established under the Dodd-Frank Act.
beneficiaries: Homeowners at risk of foreclosure due to unemployment (program largely expired)

name: Emergency Preparedness Grant, Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration, Transportation
description: Provides grants to states, territories, and tribal governments to develop and improve emergency response plans and training for hazardous materials transportation incidents. Supports first responder preparedness for hazmat emergencies.
beneficiaries: State and local emergency responders, communities along hazardous materials transportation routes

name: Emergency Relief Program, Federal Highway Administration, Transportation
description: Provides federal funding for the repair and reconstruction of federal-aid highways and roads on federal lands that have been damaged by natural disasters or catastrophic failures. Supplements regular highway funding after declared emergencies.
beneficiaries: State and local transportation agencies, motorists, communities with disaster-damaged roads
